+**Anti-synergy with Burns saturation:** Dark Embrace draws from whatever is in the draw pile. Against [[bosses/Hexaghost]], Burns accumulate faster than True Grit+ can exhaust them. Once the deck has 3+ Burns, exhausting a Burn draws another Burn -- the "engine" becomes a wash. Dark Embrace is strong with finite status cards (Wounds, Slimed, Dazed) that don't replenish, but weak against Hexaghost where Burns are continuously added. Do NOT buy Dark Embrace specifically to handle Hexaghost Burns.
-**Matchups:** Strong vs Book of Stabbing -- Wounds are exhaust fodder that convert into real draws. Dark Embrace + Burning Pact/True Grit+ turns every Wound into card advantage. Weak vs Hexaghost -- see anti-synergy note above.
+**Matchups:** Strong vs [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]] -- Wounds are exhaust fodder that convert into real draws. Dark Embrace + [[cards/Burning Pact]]/True Grit+ turns every Wound into card advantage. [[debuffs/Weak]] vs Hexaghost -- see anti-synergy note above.
heuristics/cards/dark-shackles.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Dark Shackles
-Emergency defensive tool. Reduces enemy Strength for 1 turn only, then it returns. Costs 0 energy.
+Emergency defensive tool. Reduces enemy [[buffs/Strength]] for 1 turn only, then it returns. Costs 0 energy.
-**When to play:** Time it for the enemy's highest-damage turn. Using it on a low-damage turn wastes the exhaust. Against bosses with phase transitions (The Champ Execute), save for the first big hit after transition.
+**When to play:** Time it for the enemy's highest-damage turn. Using it on a low-damage turn wastes the exhaust. Against bosses with phase transitions ([[bosses/The Champ]] Execute), save for the first big hit after transition.
**Decision points:** Because the Strength reduction is temporary (1 turn only), do not waste it on routine damage turns. Save for lethal-damage turns.
-**Matchups:** The Champ -- strong post-Execute when Champ has +8 Strength. Time Eater -- save for late-fight turns when Time Warp Str stacks reach +4-6 and incoming damage exceeds block capacity. Boss fights generally -- 0E emergency button for any turn where incoming would be lethal.
+**Matchups:** The Champ -- strong post-Execute when Champ has +8 Strength. [[bosses/Time Eater]] -- save for late-fight turns when [[debuffs/Time Warp]] Str stacks reach +4-6 and incoming damage exceeds block capacity. Boss fights generally -- 0E emergency button for any turn where incoming would be lethal.
-**Protect from Fiend Fire:** Dark Shackles is a one-use exhaust card. If Fiend Fire exhausts it early in a boss fight, its -15 Str emergency defense is permanently lost. In long fights (Time Eater, 10+ turns), saving Dark Shackles for the endgame is far more valuable than the 10 extra Fiend Fire damage from exhausting it.
+**Protect from [[cards/Fiend Fire]]:** Dark Shackles is a one-use exhaust card. If Fiend Fire exhausts it early in a boss fight, its -15 Str emergency defense is permanently lost. In long fights (Time Eater, 10+ turns), saving Dark Shackles for the endgame is far more valuable than the 10 extra Fiend Fire damage from exhausting it.
-**Synergies:** Works in any deck since it costs 0E. Pairs with other Strength-reduction effects (Disarm) for layered defense.
+**Synergies:** Works in any deck since it costs 0E. Pairs with other Strength-reduction effects ([[cards/Disarm]]) for layered defense.
heuristics/cards/defend.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Defend
-Basic block. Frail reduces this to 3, which is terrible.
+Basic block. [[debuffs/Frail]] reduces this to 3, which is terrible.
**When to play:** When you need block and have nothing better. Defend+ at 8 block is actually decent.
-**Decision points:** Against Chosen with Hex active, every Defend adds a Dazed to your draw pile. Against Gremlin Nob, Defend triggers Enrage (+2 Str). Consider whether block is worth the cost.
+**Decision points:** Against [[enemies/Chosen]] with [[debuffs/Hex]] active, every Defend adds a [[cards/Dazed]] to your draw pile. Against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], Defend triggers [[buffs/Enrage]] (+2 Str). Consider whether block is worth the cost.
-**Upgrade priority:** Low individual priority but Ancient Writing mass upgrade is excellent. 5->8 block is a 60% increase.
+**Upgrade priority:** Low individual priority but [[events/Ancient Writing]] mass upgrade is excellent. 5->8 block is a 60% increase.
heuristics/cards/demon-form.md
@@ -6,10 +6,10 @@ Long-fight scaling engine. Provides compounding damage over many turns.
**Decision points:** 3E cost means the setup turn produces ZERO block and ZERO damage. Needs 2-3 turns after playing to meaningfully impact outcome. Useless when entering a fight at low HP vs fast-scaling enemies.
-**Reaper synergy warning:** Demon Form as the ONLY Strength source makes Reaper healing too slow in early turns. If Reaper is in the deck, pair Demon Form with a front-loaded Strength source (Inflame, Spot Weakness) so Reaper provides meaningful healing from the first play.
+**[[cards/Reaper]] synergy warning:** Demon Form as the ONLY [[buffs/Strength]] source makes Reaper healing too slow in early turns. If Reaper is in the deck, pair Demon Form with a front-loaded Strength source ([[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Spot Weakness]]) so Reaper provides meaningful healing from the first play.
-**Synergies:** Heavy Blade+ (5x Strength bonus -- at +12 Str, Heavy Blade+ deals 74 damage), Limit Break (exponential Strength growth), Shuriken + Anger (combined Str from both sources), Evolve (maintains hand quality while Demon Form shortens fights).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (5x Strength bonus -- at +12 Str, Heavy Blade+ deals 74 damage), [[cards/Limit Break]] (exponential Strength growth), [[relics/Shuriken]] + [[cards/Anger]] (combined Str from both sources), [[cards/Evolve]] (maintains hand quality while Demon Form shortens fights).
-**Matchups:** The Champ -- needs to be played Turn 1 to accumulate enough before Execute. Dominates 10-15 turn boss fights.
+**Matchups:** [[bosses/The Champ]] -- needs to be played Turn 1 to accumulate enough before Execute. Dominates 10-15 turn boss fights.
**Deck building note:** If Demon Form is primary scaling, the deck still needs fast burst for emergency hallway fights at low HP.
heuristics/cards/deus-ex-machina.md
@@ -6,8 +6,8 @@ Unplayable card that generates 2/3 Miracles on draw and exhausts itself. Free en
**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 3 Miracles instead of 2 is significant extra energy over a fight.
-**Miracle management:** The generated Miracles follow all Miracle rules -- hold them for turns where the energy matters. Do not waste them. See miracle.md.
+**[[cards/Miracle]] management:** The generated Miracles follow all Miracle rules -- hold them for turns where the energy matters. Do not waste them. See miracle.md.
**Deck thinning:** Exhausts on draw, permanently removing one card from the combat deck. Improves future draw quality.
-**Draft priority:** Medium-high. Free energy generation and deck thinning works well in any Watcher deck. No setup required.
+**Draft priority:** Medium-high. Free energy generation and deck thinning works well in any [[characters/Watcher]] deck. No setup required.
heuristics/cards/disarm.md
@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
# Disarm
-Permanent Strength reduction. Extremely powerful against scaling enemies.
+Permanent [[buffs/Strength]] reduction. Extremely powerful against scaling enemies.
**When to play:** Save for the enemy with the most dangerous Strength scaling. Against multi-hit enemies, the value compounds (5-hit attack loses 10 total damage).
-**Decision points:** Against Gremlin Nob, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage). Against Chosen, playing Disarm adds a Dazed via Hex -- weigh permanent -2 Str against deck clog. If fight lasts 4+ more turns, the Str reduction is usually worth one Dazed.
+**Decision points:** Against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], do NOT play (Skill triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]). Against [[enemies/Chosen]], playing Disarm adds a [[cards/Dazed]] via [[debuffs/Hex]] -- weigh permanent -2 Str against deck clog. If fight lasts 4+ more turns, the Str reduction is usually worth one Dazed.
-**Matchups:** Gremlin Leader -- reduces Rally effectiveness. Hexaghost -- permanently reduces all attacks including each Inferno hit. Slavers -- play on the hardest-hitting Slaver. Collector -- play Turn 1 for maximum cumulative value. Transient -- essential for survival; two Disarms makes the fight trivial.
+**Matchups:** [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] -- reduces Rally effectiveness. [[bosses/Hexaghost]] -- permanently reduces all attacks including each Inferno hit. Slavers -- play on the hardest-hitting Slaver. Collector -- play Turn 1 for maximum cumulative value. [[encounters/Transient]] -- essential for survival; two Disarms makes the fight trivial.
-**Slime Boss:** Disarm applied pre-split does NOT carry over to split slimes. Play post-split on the higher-damage slime.
+**[[bosses/Slime Boss]]:** Disarm applied pre-split does NOT carry over to split slimes. Play post-split on the higher-damage slime.
-**Artifact interaction:** Against enemies with Artifact, Disarm strips 1 Artifact instead of applying -2 Str.
+**[[buffs/Artifact]] interaction:** Against enemies with Artifact, Disarm strips 1 Artifact instead of applying -2 Str.
-**The Champ:** Disarm applied before Anger is completely wasted (Anger cleanses all debuffs). Hold Disarm for Phase 2 -- the -2 Str reduction is critical against post-Anger +6/+9/+12 Str. With Runic Pyramid, Disarm stays in hand; if Fiend Fire is also in deck, Disarm will be exhausted by Fiend Fire unless played first. Protect Disarm from Fiend Fire during Phase 1 burst.
+**[[bosses/The Champ]]:** Disarm applied before Anger is completely wasted (Anger cleanses all debuffs). Hold Disarm for Phase 2 -- the -2 Str reduction is critical against post-Anger +6/+9/+12 Str. With [[relics/Runic Pyramid]], Disarm stays in hand; if [[cards/Fiend Fire]] is also in deck, Disarm will be exhausted by Fiend Fire unless played first. Protect Disarm from Fiend Fire during Phase 1 burst.
heuristics/cards/doom-and-gloom.md
@@ -8,4 +8,4 @@ Damage card that also channels a Dark orb for scaling.
**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. 10->14 damage is useful but the Dark orb is the main value regardless.
-**Synergies:** Barrage (Dark orbs count toward orb count = +4/7 damage per Barrage play).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Barrage]] (Dark orbs count toward orb count = +4/7 damage per Barrage play).
heuristics/cards/double-tap.md
@@ -4,6 +4,6 @@ Doubles the next Attack. The duplicated play counts as a separate play for all p
**When to play:** Play BEFORE your strongest Attack card. At 1E cost, only worth it when the doubled Attack deals significant damage.
-**Synergies:** Rampage (double play = double scaling acceleration + massive damage), Pommel Strike+ (20 damage + draw 4), Heavy Blade (at Str 6: 32 x 2 = 64 damage for 3E total), Bash+ (first hit 10 + 3 Vuln, second hit 15 with Vuln active = 25 total).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Rampage]] (double play = double scaling acceleration + massive damage), [[cards/Pommel Strike]] (20 damage + draw 4), [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (at Str 6: 32 x 2 = 64 damage for 3E total), [[cards/Bash]] (first hit 10 + 3 Vuln, second hit 15 with Vuln active = 25 total).
-**Matchups:** Bronze Automaton -- Double Tap is a high-value Stasis target. Orbs can steal it from your hand. Consider playing early before Orbs get a chance to steal it.
+**Matchups:** [[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] -- Double Tap is a high-value Stasis target. Orbs can steal it from your hand. Consider playing early before Orbs get a chance to steal it.
heuristics/cards/dropkick.md
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
# Dropkick
-If target is Vulnerable: gain 1 energy + draw 1 card. Effectively FREE when target is Vulnerable.
+If target is [[debuffs/Vulnerable]]: gain 1 energy + draw 1 card. Effectively FREE when target is Vulnerable.
**When to play:** Is the target Vulnerable? If yes, always play -- it is free damage + cycling. If no, it is a terrible card (5/8 damage, no benefit). Do NOT play against non-Vulnerable targets.
**Upgrade priority:** 5->8 damage. With Vulnerable: 7->12 damage. The free damage is significantly higher upgraded.
-**Synergies:** Bash+ (3 turns Vulnerable = 3 free Dropkick plays), Thunderclap (mass Vulnerable = Dropkick any target).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Bash]] (3 turns Vulnerable = 3 free Dropkick plays), [[cards/Thunderclap]] (mass Vulnerable = Dropkick any target).
-**Matchups:** Book of Stabbing -- Dropkick+ dealt 12 Vuln damage three times as free actions = 36 total for 0 net energy. Slime Boss pre-split -- excellent with Bash+ providing 3 turns of Vulnerable. Post-split -- requires re-applying Vulnerable to each slime individually.
+**Matchups:** [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]] -- Dropkick+ dealt 12 Vuln damage three times as free actions = 36 total for 0 net energy. [[bosses/Slime Boss]] pre-split -- excellent with Bash+ providing 3 turns of Vulnerable. Post-split -- requires re-applying Vulnerable to each slime individually.
heuristics/cards/dual-wield.md
@@ -4,10 +4,10 @@ Creates copies of an Attack or Power card in your hand. Copies go to hand and ca
**When to play:** In boss fights and tough hallway fights on a high-value Attack. Timing: play mid-fight when the target card is in hand and you have energy to play the copies.
-**Best targets:** Immolate+ (84-102 AOE across 3 copies -- won Donu and Deca), Heavy Blade+ (triple at high Strength), Impervious (90-120 block in one turn), Flame Barrier (stacking block and return damage).
+**Best targets:** [[cards/Immolate]] (84-102 AOE across 3 copies -- won [[bosses/Donu and Deca]]), [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (triple at high [[buffs/Strength]]), [[cards/Impervious]] (90-120 block in one turn), [[cards/Flame Barrier]] (stacking block and return damage).
-**Snecko Eye synergy:** Under Confusion, each copy gets a new random cost. ~44% chance at least one costs 0E with 2 copies.
+**[[relics/Snecko Eye]] synergy:** Under Confusion, each copy gets a new random cost. ~44% chance at least one costs 0E with 2 copies.
-**Decision points:** A Skill card -- triggers Gremlin Nob's Enrage and Chosen's Hex. In those fights, do not play.
+**Decision points:** A Skill card -- triggers [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]]'s [[buffs/Enrage]] and [[enemies/Chosen]]'s [[debuffs/Hex]]. In those fights, do not play.
**Upgrade priority:** Very high. 1 copy vs 2 copies is the difference between "good" and "game-winning."
heuristics/cards/echo-form.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Echo Form
-The single most powerful card in the Defect's pool. Doubles the first card played each turn.
+The single most powerful card in the [[characters/Defect]]'s pool. Doubles the first card played each turn.
**When to play:** As early as possible in long fights. The doubling compounds -- Turn 1 doubles 9+ cards across 10 turns. Plan for a low-pressure turn (after building Frost wall) to deploy.
@@ -8,10 +8,10 @@ The single most powerful card in the Defect's pool. Doubles the first card playe
**Upgrade priority:** Arguably the most important in the Defect pool. Removes Ethereal -- avoids permanent loss from bad draw timing.
-**Seek synergy:** Seek guarantees Echo Form in hand Turn 1 (or whenever needed). This Seek + Echo Form combination is the core of the Defect's most consistent winning engine.
+**[[cards/Seek]] synergy:** Seek guarantees Echo Form in hand Turn 1 (or whenever needed). This Seek + Echo Form combination is the core of the Defect's most consistent winning engine.
-**Mummified Hand synergy:** Playing Echo Form triggers Mummified Hand, making a random card cost 0. Partially offsets 3E setup cost.
+**[[relics/Mummified Hand]] synergy:** Playing Echo Form triggers Mummified Hand, making a random card cost 0. Partially offsets 3E setup cost.
-**Time Eater warning:** Echo Form copies count toward 12-card counter. Each doubled card = 2 plays counted. Effective budget is ~6 real card plays per cycle.
+**[[bosses/Time Eater]] warning:** Echo Form copies count toward 12-card counter. Each doubled card = 2 plays counted. Effective budget is ~6 real card plays per cycle.
-**Bottled Lightning interaction:** Can make Echo Form innate, but Bottled Lightning on Seek is often better (Seek adapts to specific fights).
+**[[relics/Bottled Lightning]] interaction:** Can make Echo Form innate, but Bottled Lightning on Seek is often better (Seek adapts to specific fights).
heuristics/cards/empty-body.md
@@ -6,8 +6,8 @@ Wrath exit that leaves you in no stance (neutral). Provides block without enteri
**Upgrade priority:** Low-medium. 7->10 block is a solid improvement but does not change the card's role.
-**Play order (CRITICAL):** Play Flurry of Blows BEFORE Empty Body. Leaving any stance triggers Flurry's return from discard.
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** Play [[cards/Flurry of Blows]] BEFORE Empty Body. Leaving any stance triggers Flurry's return from discard.
-**Vigilance comparison:** Vigilance is generally better (enters Calm for +2E next turn). Empty Body provides flexibility when you do not want to be locked into the Calm->Wrath cycle.
+**[[cards/Vigilance]] comparison:** Vigilance is generally better (enters Calm for +2E next turn). Empty Body provides flexibility when you do not want to be locked into the Calm->Wrath cycle.
**Draft priority:** Low-medium. Take only if Vigilance copies are insufficient for Wrath exits.
heuristics/cards/entrench.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Entrench
-Doubles all accumulated block. The single most powerful block-scaling card with Barricade.
+Doubles all accumulated block. The single most powerful block-scaling card with [[cards/Barricade]].
-**Upgrade priority:** VERY HIGH. Entrench+ costs 1E (from 2E). Under Corruption, Entrench+ costs 0E and exhausts -- game-breaking.
+**Upgrade priority:** VERY HIGH. Entrench+ costs 1E (from 2E). Under [[cards/Corruption]], Entrench+ costs 0E and exhausts -- game-breaking.
**When to play:** With Barricade. Block persists, so Entrench doubles the accumulated total. Typical sequence: Turn 1 generate 20, Turn 2 add 15 (35 total), Turn 3 Entrench doubles to 70. Without Barricade, Entrench doubles only this turn's block (typically 10-20) -- mediocre for 2E.
-**Synergies:** Barricade (mandatory pairing), Corruption (becomes 0E + FNP trigger on exhaust), Feel No Pain (exhaust under Corruption = +3-4 block on top), Body Slam+ (doubled block = doubled damage for free), Impervious (30-40 base doubled to 60-80).
+**Synergies:** Barricade (mandatory pairing), Corruption (becomes 0E + FNP trigger on exhaust), [[cards/Feel No Pain]] (exhaust under Corruption = +3-4 block on top), [[cards/Body Slam]] (doubled block = doubled damage for free), [[cards/Impervious]] (30-40 base doubled to 60-80).
-**Anti-synergies:** Decks without Barricade (poor value), fights with Frail.
+**Anti-synergies:** Decks without Barricade (poor value), fights with [[debuffs/Frail]].
**Do NOT take** without Barricade or a clear path to obtaining Barricade.
heuristics/cards/eruption.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Eruption
-Watcher's primary Wrath entry. The core of the Calm energy loop.
+[[characters/Watcher]]'s primary Wrath entry. The core of the Calm energy loop.
**When to play:** On burst turns when you have attacks ready to benefit from doubled damage. In Calm, Eruption+ is effectively free (spend 1E, gain 2E from Calm exit = net +1E).
**Upgrade priority:** Very high. 2E->1E doubles the efficiency of the Calm energy loop and allows more cards to be played on burst turns. One of the most important Watcher upgrades.
-**Play order (CRITICAL):** If Flurry of Blows is in hand, play it BEFORE Eruption. Flurry needs to be in the discard pile to trigger its return when Eruption changes stance.
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** If [[cards/Flurry of Blows]] is in hand, play it BEFORE Eruption. Flurry needs to be in the discard pile to trigger its return when Eruption changes stance.
-**When NOT to play:** Do NOT play Eruption if you cannot exit Wrath this turn and the enemy is attacking. Doubled incoming damage at low HP is lethal. Hexaghost Turn 1 (free turn): Do NOT enter Wrath -- Turn 2 Divider would be doubled = instant death.
+**When NOT to play:** Do NOT play Eruption if you cannot exit Wrath this turn and the enemy is attacking. Doubled incoming damage at low HP is lethal. [[bosses/Hexaghost]] Turn 1 (free turn): Do NOT enter Wrath -- Turn 2 Divider would be doubled = instant death.
**Calm energy loop:** In Calm: play Eruption+ (1E) -> exit Calm (+2E) -> enter Wrath (double damage). Net gain: +1E and doubled attacks. This is the Watcher's fundamental engine.
heuristics/cards/evolve.md
@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
Neutralizes hand-clogging from Status cards. Each Status drawn is immediately replaced by a real card.
-**When to play:** Play on free turns. At 1E, it competes with block/damage on attack turns. Best set up early in fights where Status cards are expected. Without Mark of Pain, zero value in fights without Status card generation -- do NOT play in non-Status fights as it wastes 1E.
+**When to play:** Play on free turns. At 1E, it competes with block/damage on attack turns. Best set up early in fights where Status cards are expected. Without [[relics/Mark of Pain]], zero value in fights without Status card generation -- do NOT play in non-Status fights as it wastes 1E.
-**Synergies:** Mark of Pain + Evolve+ (Wounds from attacks become massive card advantage -- 4E + bonus draws = 5-7 cards/turn), Hexaghost Burns / Immolate Burns (Burns become cycle-neutral), Sentries Dazed (draw replacement, Dazed exhausts itself), Fire Breathing (drawing Status = damage + replacement).
+**Synergies:** Mark of Pain + Evolve+ (Wounds from attacks become massive card advantage -- 4E + bonus draws = 5-7 cards/turn), [[bosses/Hexaghost]] Burns / [[cards/Immolate]] Burns (Burns become cycle-neutral), Sentries [[cards/Dazed]] (draw replacement, Dazed exhausts itself), [[cards/Fire Breathing]] (drawing Status = damage + replacement).
**Upgrade priority:** HIGH with Mark of Pain (2 draws per Wound = dominant card advantage). Moderate without Mark of Pain.
-**Matchups:** Excellent vs Hexaghost (top-priority Turn 1 setup). Excellent vs Sentries, Slime Boss, Book of Stabbing. With Mark of Pain, has value in EVERY fight. Chosen adds Dazed via Hex, so Evolve has value after Hex activation.
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Hexaghost (top-priority Turn 1 setup). Excellent vs Sentries, [[bosses/Slime Boss]], [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]]. With Mark of Pain, has value in EVERY fight. [[enemies/Chosen]] adds Dazed via [[debuffs/Hex]], so Evolve has value after Hex activation.
heuristics/cards/exhume.md
@@ -2,12 +2,12 @@
Recovers one card from the exhaust pile. The only way to reuse exhausted cards. Exhausts itself on play.
-**When to play:** When a critical exhausted card is needed again -- Shockwave, Disarm, Offering, Impervious. Best as a mid-fight recovery tool, not a Turn 1 play.
+**When to play:** When a critical exhausted card is needed again -- [[cards/Shockwave]], [[cards/Disarm]], [[cards/Offering]], [[cards/Impervious]]. Best as a mid-fight recovery tool, not a Turn 1 play.
**Upgrade priority:** Very high. 1E to 0E makes Exhume free, allowing recovery + play of the recovered card in the same turn.
-**Fiend Fire interaction (CRITICAL):** Exhume is a Skill, so Fiend Fire exhausts it along with everything else in hand. If Fiend Fire exhausts Exhume unused, Exhume sits in the exhaust pile with no way to activate it (unless a second Exhume exists). The solution is NOT to play Exhume before Fiend Fire (nothing useful may be in the exhaust pile yet). The solution is: do not play Fiend Fire while Exhume is in hand. Wait until Exhume is safely in the draw pile, then use Fiend Fire. With Runic Pyramid, this means Exhume must be played on a prior turn for some purpose (even a marginal one) before Fiend Fire can safely fire.
+**[[cards/Fiend Fire]] interaction (CRITICAL):** Exhume is a Skill, so Fiend Fire exhausts it along with everything else in hand. If Fiend Fire exhausts Exhume unused, Exhume sits in the exhaust pile with no way to activate it (unless a second Exhume exists). The solution is NOT to play Exhume before Fiend Fire (nothing useful may be in the exhaust pile yet). The solution is: do not play Fiend Fire while Exhume is in hand. Wait until Exhume is safely in the draw pile, then use Fiend Fire. With [[relics/Runic Pyramid]], this means Exhume must be played on a prior turn for some purpose (even a marginal one) before Fiend Fire can safely fire.
-**Phased boss fights (The Champ, Awakened One):** Exhume's highest value is recovering debuff cards after a phase transition. Against The Champ: Exhume after Anger can recover Disarm or Shockwave for Phase 2. Plan the fight around keeping Exhume available for this purpose.
+**Phased boss fights ([[bosses/The Champ]], [[bosses/Awakened One]]):** Exhume's highest value is recovering debuff cards after a phase transition. Against The Champ: Exhume after Anger can recover Disarm or Shockwave for Phase 2. Plan the fight around keeping Exhume available for this purpose.
-**Synergies:** Feel No Pain (Exhume triggers FNP on play since it exhausts), Corruption (Exhume becomes free), any exhaust-heavy deck where key cards get consumed.
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Feel No Pain]] (Exhume triggers FNP on play since it exhausts), [[cards/Corruption]] (Exhume becomes free), any exhaust-heavy deck where key cards get consumed.
heuristics/cards/fear-no-evil.md
@@ -7,7 +7,7 @@ Conditional Calm entry that doubles as an attack. Only enters Calm when the enem
**Upgrade priority:** Medium. +3 damage (8->11) is solid but not game-changing.
**Play order (CRITICAL):** On attack turns:
-1. Play Flurry of Blows first (goes to discard)
+1. Play [[cards/Flurry of Blows]] first (goes to discard)
2. Play Fear No Evil (deals damage, enters Calm, Flurry returns to hand)
3. Play returned Flurry of Blows again
heuristics/cards/feed.md
@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
# Feed
-One of the strongest Ironclad cards. Permanent Max HP scaling.
+One of the strongest [[characters/Ironclad]] cards. Permanent Max HP scaling.
**When to play:** Whenever you can land the killing blow on an enemy. Each kill adds 3-4 Max HP permanently. Plan other attacks to soften the target, then Feed for the kill.
**Critical:** Feed exhausts on play regardless of whether it kills. If the enemy survives, you lose the card permanently with zero Max HP gained. Calculate exact damage before committing.
-**Common mistake:** Feed in the discard pile when an enemy is at killable HP. Feed only works if YOU play it as the killing blow. Plan Feed kills in advance -- hold Feed in hand when an enemy approaches kill range, or use Headbutt to place Feed on top.
+**Common mistake:** Feed in the discard pile when an enemy is at killable HP. Feed only works if YOU play it as the killing blow. Plan Feed kills in advance -- hold Feed in hand when an enemy approaches kill range, or use [[cards/Headbutt]] to place Feed on top.
**Take early, use every fight:** Feed's value compounds over the run. In every multi-enemy fight, plan to Feed-kill at least one enemy. Do NOT save Feed for "the perfect moment."
-**Synergies:** Headbutt (place Feed on top when enemy is low), Vulnerable (15/18 damage kill threshold), Strength (+2 Str makes Feed+ deal 14 base / 21 with Vuln).
+**Synergies:** Headbutt (place Feed on top when enemy is low), [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (15/18 damage kill threshold), [[buffs/Strength]] (+2 Str makes Feed+ deal 14 base / 21 with Vuln).
-**Matchups:** Best targets are low-HP enemies: Byrds (~25 HP), Mystic (40 HP), Gremlins, Cultists. Slime Boss split is the best Feed+ fight in Act 1.
+**Matchups:** Best targets are low-HP enemies: Byrds (~25 HP), [[enemies/Mystic]] (40 HP), Gremlins, Cultists. [[bosses/Slime Boss]] split is the best Feed+ fight in [[acts/Act 1]].
heuristics/cards/feel-no-pain.md
@@ -4,12 +4,12 @@ Passive block engine that triggers on every exhaust. Permanent for the rest of c
**When to play:** On a free turn for zero opportunity cost. At 1E, worth playing even when only 2-3 exhaust triggers are expected. Priority increases with more exhaust sources.
-**Synergies:** True Grit+ (exhaust + block + 3 FNP), Burning Pact (exhaust + draw + 3 block), Fiend Fire (exhaust entire hand = 3 per card), Corruption (all Skills exhaust = constant triggers -- FNP is critical for surviving Corruption), Corruption + Dead Branch (triple combo: free exhaust + 3 block + random card), Second Wind (mass exhaust = massive FNP burst).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/True Grit]] (exhaust + block + 3 FNP), [[cards/Burning Pact]] (exhaust + draw + 3 block), [[cards/Fiend Fire]] (exhaust entire hand = 3 per card), [[cards/Corruption]] (all Skills exhaust = constant triggers -- FNP is critical for surviving Corruption), Corruption + [[relics/Dead Branch]] (triple combo: free exhaust + 3 block + random card), [[cards/Second Wind]] (mass exhaust = massive FNP burst).
-**Slime Boss synergy:** Each Slimed exhausted generates 3 block, partially offsetting 1E tempo cost. 5+ Slimed = 15+ block across the fight.
+**[[bosses/Slime Boss]] synergy:** Each [[cards/Slimed]] exhausted generates 3 block, partially offsetting 1E tempo cost. 5+ Slimed = 15+ block across the fight.
-**Spheric Guardian:** Corruption + FNP successfully defeated Spheric Guardian with only 12 HP lost. Recommended configuration for the hardest Act 2 hallway fight.
+**[[enemies/Spheric Guardian]]:** Corruption + FNP successfully defeated Spheric Guardian with only 12 HP lost. Recommended configuration for the hardest [[acts/Act 2]] hallway fight.
-**Victory engine:** Mandatory component of Barricade + Corruption engine. FNP+ (4 block/exhaust) generated 40-60+ block per fight from exhaust triggers alone.
+**Victory engine:** Mandatory component of [[cards/Barricade]] + Corruption engine. FNP+ (4 block/exhaust) generated 40-60+ block per fight from exhaust triggers alone.
-**Anti-synergy:** Weak in decks with few exhaust sources.
+**Anti-synergy:** [[debuffs/Weak]] in decks with few exhaust sources.
heuristics/cards/fiend-fire.md
@@ -2,19 +2,19 @@
Exhausts ALL other cards in hand. Deals 7 damage per card exhausted to a single target.
-**When to play:** When burst will kill the target, when remaining cards are expendable, or on free turns. Turns Slimed/Decay/Dazed/Burn cards into 7 damage each. After playing, your hand is EMPTY. Only play when: (1) the burst kills, (2) it is a free turn, or (3) remaining cards are expendable.
+**When to play:** When burst will kill the target, when remaining cards are expendable, or on free turns. Turns [[cards/Slimed]]/Decay/[[cards/Dazed]]/Burn cards into 7 damage each. After playing, your hand is EMPTY. Only play when: (1) the burst kills, (2) it is a free turn, or (3) remaining cards are expendable.
-**Synergies:** Status/Curse cards (become 7+ damage instead of dead draws), Strength (each card's damage scales), Vulnerable (each hit gets 50% bonus), Unceasing Top (empties hand, triggers Top draw engine), Snecko Oil (randomize costs before Fiend Fire), Feel No Pain (Fiend Fire self-exhausts too, so total FNP triggers = other cards + 1. With N other cards in hand: FNP block = (N+1) x 3).
+**Synergies:** Status/Curse cards (become 7+ damage instead of dead draws), [[buffs/Strength]] (each card's damage scales), [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (each hit gets 50% bonus), [[relics/Unceasing Top]] (empties hand, triggers Top draw engine), [[potions/Snecko Oil]] (randomize costs before Fiend Fire), [[cards/Feel No Pain]] (Fiend Fire self-exhausts too, so total FNP triggers = other cards + 1. With N other cards in hand: FNP block = (N+1) x 3).
-**Matchups:** Slime Boss -- burst + Slimed exhaust. Chosen -- only use when burst will kill or exhausted cards are genuinely expendable. If Chosen survives, remaining turns have zero block sources.
+**Matchups:** [[bosses/Slime Boss]] -- burst + Slimed exhaust. [[enemies/Chosen]] -- only use when burst will kill or exhausted cards are genuinely expendable. If Chosen survives, remaining turns have zero block sources.
-**Caution:** Exhausting key cards (Barricade, Clothesline) is permanent. At critically low HP, exhausting everything for survival is correct -- survival > future value.
+**Caution:** Exhausting key cards ([[cards/Barricade]], [[cards/Clothesline]]) is permanent. At critically low HP, exhausting everything for survival is correct -- survival > future value.
-**Phased boss warning (The Champ, Awakened One):** Against bosses with phase transitions that cleanse debuffs, Fiend Fire can be run-ending. If debuff cards (Disarm, Shockwave, Exhume) are in hand when Fiend Fire fires, they are permanently exhausted. Against The Champ specifically: debuffs applied before Anger are wasted AND debuffs exhausted by Fiend Fire are gone forever. Before playing Fiend Fire in a phased boss fight, verify that ALL Phase 2 cards have already been played or are safely in the draw/discard pile -- not in hand.
+**Phased boss warning ([[bosses/The Champ]], [[bosses/Awakened One]]):** Against bosses with phase transitions that cleanse debuffs, Fiend Fire can be run-ending. If debuff cards ([[cards/Disarm]], [[cards/Shockwave]], [[cards/Exhume]]) are in hand when Fiend Fire fires, they are permanently exhausted. Against The Champ specifically: debuffs applied before Anger are wasted AND debuffs exhausted by Fiend Fire are gone forever. Before playing Fiend Fire in a phased boss fight, verify that ALL Phase 2 cards have already been played or are safely in the draw/discard pile -- not in hand.
-**Long boss fight warning (Time Eater, any 10+ turn fight):** In fights lasting 10+ turns, Fiend Fire exhaust is permanent resource destruction. Every card exhausted is gone for the remaining 8-10 turns. Cards that seem expendable on Turn 2 become critical by Turn 10 when the enemy has +4-6 Strength from Time Warp stacks. Before playing Fiend Fire in a long fight, categorize every card in hand: (1) truly expendable (Strikes, status cards, duplicate copies), (2) needed later (emergency defense like Dark Shackles, Weak sources like Intimidate, block cards). If category 2 cards are in hand, do NOT play Fiend Fire -- play those cards first or wait for a turn where only expendable cards remain. The Time Warp card count pressure makes Fiend Fire tempting (2 cards for 60+ damage is efficient), but the efficiency is an illusion if you lose your only Weak source or your emergency -15 Str button for the endgame.
+**Long boss fight warning ([[bosses/Time Eater]], any 10+ turn fight):** In fights lasting 10+ turns, Fiend Fire exhaust is permanent resource destruction. Every card exhausted is gone for the remaining 8-10 turns. Cards that seem expendable on Turn 2 become critical by Turn 10 when the enemy has +4-6 Strength from [[debuffs/Time Warp]] stacks. Before playing Fiend Fire in a long fight, categorize every card in hand: (1) truly expendable (Strikes, status cards, duplicate copies), (2) needed later (emergency defense like [[cards/Dark Shackles]], [[debuffs/Weak]] sources like [[cards/Intimidate]], block cards). If category 2 cards are in hand, do NOT play Fiend Fire -- play those cards first or wait for a turn where only expendable cards remain. The Time Warp card count pressure makes Fiend Fire tempting (2 cards for 60+ damage is efficient), but the efficiency is an illusion if you lose your only Weak source or your emergency -15 Str button for the endgame.
-**Runic Pyramid trap:** Runic Pyramid retains the entire hand across turns, so the hand grows. Fiend Fire exhausts MORE cards (more damage) but also exhausts every retained card. In boss fights requiring card conservation, do NOT play Fiend Fire while critical cards are in hand. With Runic Pyramid, those cards will NOT leave your hand naturally -- you must either play them first or accept that Fiend Fire cannot be used that turn.
+**[[relics/Runic Pyramid]] trap:** Runic Pyramid retains the entire hand across turns, so the hand grows. Fiend Fire exhausts MORE cards (more damage) but also exhausts every retained card. In boss fights requiring card conservation, do NOT play Fiend Fire while critical cards are in hand. With Runic Pyramid, those cards will NOT leave your hand naturally -- you must either play them first or accept that Fiend Fire cannot be used that turn.
**Guardian warning:** Fiend Fire as a primary engine drains the deck unsustainably in 12-14 turn fights. Use on free turns for burst, do not plan the deck around it as sole damage engine.
heuristics/cards/finisher.md
@@ -2,8 +2,8 @@
Deals damage multiplied by number of Attacks played this turn. Shivs count as Attacks.
-**When to play:** Play as the LAST Attack card of the turn. After 4 Shivs: 4 * (6 + Str) damage for 1E. With Blade Dance (3 Shivs) + 1 extra Shiv: excellent burst.
+**When to play:** Play as the LAST Attack card of the turn. After 4 Shivs: 4 * (6 + Str) damage for 1E. With [[cards/Blade Dance]] (3 Shivs) + 1 extra [[cards/Shiv]]: excellent burst.
-**Synergies:** Blade Dance (creates 3 Shivs that build the Attack count), Phantasmal Killer (Finisher is an Attack -- Double Damage doubles its total output), Accuracy (enables Shiv plays that build the count).
+**Synergies:** Blade Dance (creates 3 Shivs that build the Attack count), [[cards/Phantasmal Killer]] (Finisher is an Attack -- Double Damage doubles its total output), [[cards/Accuracy]] (enables Shiv plays that build the count).
**Draft priority:** Mid-to-high in Shiv decks. Without Shiv support, deals only 6-12 damage (1-2 attacks before it) -- mediocre.
heuristics/cards/fire-breathing.md
@@ -4,12 +4,12 @@ Passive AOE damage triggered by drawing Status or Curse cards.
**When to play:** Play on free turns (enemy buff turns). At 1E, it competes with block and damage on attack turns. Best set up early when Status cards are expected to accumulate. In fights with no Status cards, Fire Breathing does nothing.
-**Key synergy -- Evolve + Fire Breathing:** The strongest anti-Status engine. Evolve draws replacement on Status draw. Fire Breathing deals damage on Status draw. Together, Status cards become pure upside: free damage with no hand clog. Confirmed Hexaghost counter.
+**Key synergy -- [[cards/Evolve]] + Fire Breathing:** The strongest anti-Status engine. Evolve draws replacement on Status draw. Fire Breathing deals damage on Status draw. Together, Status cards become pure upside: free damage with no hand clog. Confirmed [[bosses/Hexaghost]] counter.
-**Do NOT exhaust Fire Breathing+ when Immolate is in the deck.** Burns from Immolate trigger 10 damage on draw -- bypasses Plated Armor entirely. Exhausting Fire Breathing+ throws away 30-50+ free damage.
+**Do NOT exhaust Fire Breathing+ when [[cards/Immolate]] is in the deck.** Burns from Immolate trigger 10 damage on draw -- bypasses [[buffs/Plated Armor]] entirely. Exhausting Fire Breathing+ throws away 30-50+ free damage.
-**Synergies:** Power Through (Wounds trigger on draw), Evolve (Status = damage + replacement draw), Immolate Burns.
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Power Through]] (Wounds trigger on draw), Evolve (Status = damage + replacement draw), Immolate Burns.
**Upgrade priority:** Significant. 6->10 damage per trigger is 67% more damage.
-**Matchups:** Excellent vs Hexaghost (neutralizes Burns), Sentries (Dazed triggers), Slime Boss (Slimed triggers), Book of Stabbing (Wounds trigger). Irrelevant in fights with no Status cards.
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Hexaghost (neutralizes Burns), Sentries ([[cards/Dazed]] triggers), [[bosses/Slime Boss]] ([[cards/Slimed]] triggers), [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]] (Wounds trigger). Irrelevant in fights with no Status cards.
heuristics/cards/fission.md
@@ -6,6 +6,6 @@ Critical upgrade difference between removing orbs (unupgraded) and evoking them
**Upgraded (EXCELLENT):** EVOKES all orbs, triggering effects. With 5 orbs: 5 Frost evokes (25 block), 5 Energy, 5 draws. Massive defensive burst AND enables playing drawn cards with gained energy.
-**Upgrade priority:** Extremely high -- top-3 Defect upgrade target. The difference between removing and evoking is the difference between destroying your own defense and generating a massive burst turn.
+**Upgrade priority:** Extremely high -- top-3 [[characters/Defect]] upgrade target. The difference between removing and evoking is the difference between destroying your own defense and generating a massive burst turn.
**Decision points:** At 0 orbs, does nothing. At 1 orb, minimal. Shines at 3+ orbs. Fission+ is a burst finisher when evoke effects + Energy/draws enable killing or massive block.
heuristics/cards/flame-barrier.md
@@ -2,12 +2,12 @@
Block + thorns effect. Punishes multi-hit attacks.
-**When to play:** Excellent against multi-hit enemies. Flame Barrier+ against Hexaghost Inferno (7 hits) = 16 block + 42 counter damage. Against multi-enemy fights (Slavers, Gremlins), each attacker triggers counter damage.
+**When to play:** Excellent against multi-hit enemies. Flame Barrier+ against [[bosses/Hexaghost]] Inferno (7 hits) = 16 block + 42 counter damage. Against multi-enemy fights (Slavers, Gremlins), each attacker triggers counter damage.
**Decision points:** At 2E, competes with other defensive plays. Worth it when facing multi-hit attacks or multiple attackers. On single-hit attack turns, a Defend may be more energy-efficient.
**Upgrade priority:** High when facing Hexaghost. 12->16 block, 4->6 counter per hit. Single best defensive card for Inferno turns.
-**Synergies:** Hexaghost Inferno (7 hits x 6 counter = 42 free damage), multi-enemy fights (each attacker triggers counter), Book of Stabbing (multi-hit attacks trigger counter on every hit, and hit count increases each turn).
+**Synergies:** Hexaghost Inferno (7 hits x 6 counter = 42 free damage), multi-enemy fights (each attacker triggers counter), [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]] (multi-hit attacks trigger counter on every hit, and hit count increases each turn).
**Matchups:** Best-in-class vs Hexaghost. Strong vs Book of Stabbing. Moderate vs single large attackers.
heuristics/cards/flex.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Flex
-Temporary Strength that disappears at end of turn. Standalone, almost never good.
+Temporary [[buffs/Strength]] that disappears at end of turn. Standalone, almost never good.
-**When to play:** Do not take unless Limit Break+ is already in the deck or planned. If already in deck without Limit Break+, remove at next shop.
+**When to play:** Do not take unless [[cards/Limit Break]] is already in the deck or planned. If already in deck without Limit Break+, remove at next shop.
**Exception -- Limit Break+ combo:** Flex+ becomes a legitimate combo piece with Limit Break+. Play Flex+ for +4 temporary Str, then Limit Break+ to double total. The doubled amount is permanent -- only the 4 temporary Str is lost at end of turn. This turned Flex+ from a dead card into a key boss-kill contributor.
-**Decision points:** Without Limit Break+, Inflame (1E, permanent +2 Str) is strictly better in any fight lasting 2+ turns.
+**Decision points:** Without Limit Break+, [[cards/Inflame]] (1E, permanent +2 Str) is strictly better in any fight lasting 2+ turns.
heuristics/cards/flurry-of-blows.md
@@ -1,10 +1,10 @@
# Flurry of Blows
-The Watcher's core free-damage engine. Returns to hand from discard on every stance change for another free 0-cost play.
+The [[characters/Watcher]]'s core free-damage engine. Returns to hand from discard on every stance change for another free 0-cost play.
**Play order (CRITICAL -- SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT WATCHER RULE):** ALWAYS play Flurry of Blows BEFORE any stance-change card. Flurry must be in the DISCARD PILE when a stance change occurs for the return trigger to work.
-**WRONG:** Eruption -> Flurry. Flurry was in hand during the stance change. One play only (4 damage).
+**WRONG:** [[cards/Eruption]] -> Flurry. Flurry was in hand during the stance change. One play only (4 damage).
**CORRECT:** Flurry (4 damage, goes to discard) -> Eruption (Flurry returns to hand) -> Flurry again (4 more damage). Two plays for 8 total.
@@ -14,8 +14,8 @@ With 2 stance changes per turn: correct order yields 3 plays (12 damage) vs wron
**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 4->6 per play. With 3 plays per turn: 12->18 damage difference.
-**Shuriken/Kunai synergy:** Each Flurry play counts as an attack. With 2-3 plays per turn, relic thresholds trigger frequently.
+**[[relics/Shuriken]]/[[relics/Kunai]] synergy:** Each Flurry play counts as an attack. With 2-3 plays per turn, relic thresholds trigger frequently.
**Multiple copies:** Two Flurry in the deck means two returns per stance change. With 2 copies and 2 stance changes: 6 free plays for 24 damage.
-**Draft priority:** High. Take in Act 1 whenever offered. Best common card for the stance-cycling damage engine. A second copy doubles output.
+**Draft priority:** High. Take in [[acts/Act 1]] whenever offered. Best common card for the stance-cycling damage engine. A second copy doubles output.
heuristics/cards/flying-sleeves.md
@@ -8,6 +8,6 @@ Multi-hit Retain attack. Hold for Wrath turns to deal massive doubled damage.
**Retain strategy:** Draw Flying Sleeves on a defensive turn, hold it for the next Wrath turn. No wasted draws.
-**Shuriken/Kunai synergy:** 2 hits = 2 counts toward the 3-attack threshold. Pairs well with Flurry of Blows for quick relic triggers.
+**[[relics/Shuriken]]/[[relics/Kunai]] synergy:** 2 hits = 2 counts toward the 3-attack threshold. Pairs well with [[cards/Flurry of Blows]] for quick relic triggers.
-**Snake Plant warning:** Multi-hit attacks trigger Malleable block multiple times. Against Snake Plant, Flying Sleeves gives the enemy 3+4 = 7 block from Malleable (two triggers). Use single large hits against Snake Plant when possible.
+**[[enemies/Snake Plant]] warning:** Multi-hit attacks trigger [[buffs/Malleable]] block multiple times. Against Snake Plant, Flying Sleeves gives the enemy 3+4 = 7 block from Malleable (two triggers). Use single large hits against Snake Plant when possible.
heuristics/cards/follow-up.md
@@ -8,6 +8,6 @@ Conditional 0-cost attack. Free when played immediately after another Attack car
**Attack chaining:** Strike -> Follow-Up (free) -> Flurry (free) = 3 attacks for 1E total. Enables long burst sequences.
-**Kunai/Shuriken synergy:** Free attacks count toward the 3-attack threshold. Follow-Up + Flurry after any attack = 3 attacks for minimal energy, triggering both relics.
+**[[relics/Kunai]]/[[relics/Shuriken]] synergy:** Free attacks count toward the 3-attack threshold. Follow-Up + Flurry after any attack = 3 attacks for minimal energy, triggering both relics.
**In Wrath:** A free 14/22 damage attack in Wrath is exceptional. Chain attacks to maximize doubled damage output.
heuristics/cards/ftl.md
@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
0-cost attack with conditional draw. When played first each turn, replaces itself (or generates +1 card upgraded).
-**When to play:** Always play FTL first if possible to get the draw bonus. If another card is more important first (especially with Echo Form), the draw bonus is lost and FTL becomes simple 5 damage for 0E.
+**When to play:** Always play FTL first if possible to get the draw bonus. If another card is more important first (especially with [[cards/Echo Form]]), the draw bonus is lost and FTL becomes simple 5 damage for 0E.
**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. Draw 1->2 makes FTL+ a strong opening play each turn.
**Echo Form note:** Interaction with "first card played" mechanic and Echo Form copies requires careful sequencing.
-**Safe against Gremlin Nob** (Attack card).
+**Safe against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]]** (Attack card).
heuristics/cards/havoc.md
@@ -2,9 +2,9 @@
Plays the top card of the draw pile for free, then exhausts it. The played card's energy cost is ignored.
-**When to play:** When the draw pile contains expendable cards (Strikes, status, Wounds, Burns, Dazed) and you want a free action. Best when you know what is on top (e.g., after Headbutt).
+**When to play:** When the draw pile contains expendable cards (Strikes, status, Wounds, Burns, [[cards/Dazed]]) and you want a free action. Best when you know what is on top (e.g., after [[cards/Headbutt]]).
-**Critical danger:** Havoc exhausts the played card permanently. If the top card is a key scaling card (Rampage+, Fiend Fire+, Bash+), it is gone forever.
+**Critical danger:** Havoc exhausts the played card permanently. If the top card is a key scaling card ([[cards/Rampage]], [[cards/Fiend Fire]], [[cards/Bash]]), it is gone forever.
**Decision points:** Before playing, consider what is in the draw pile. Havoc is safe when: (1) draw pile is mostly Strikes/Defends, (2) you tracked what remains, (3) the fight is trivial. Havoc is dangerous when: (1) deck contains irreplaceable cards, (2) you have not tracked draw pile contents.
@@ -12,4 +12,4 @@ Plays the top card of the draw pile for free, then exhausts it. The played card'
**Anti-synergy:** Any deck with irreplaceable scaling cards (Rampage, Fiend Fire, Feed). DO NOT take if the deck depends on specific cards.
-**Matchups:** Gremlin Nob -- Havoc is a Skill, triggers Enrage. Do NOT play.
+**Matchups:** [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] -- Havoc is a Skill, triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]. Do NOT play.
heuristics/cards/headbutt.md
@@ -4,10 +4,10 @@ Put a card from your discard pile on top of your draw pile. Strongest with predi
**When to play:** When you have a high-value card in discard you need next turn. Check your discard pile -- what do you need next turn given enemy intent?
-**Upgrade priority:** High if Headbutt is a regular play. 9->12 damage. With Vulnerable: 13->18. In one fight, unupgraded left Mystic at 1 HP, extending the fight 2+ turns.
+**Upgrade priority:** High if Headbutt is a regular play. 9->12 damage. With [[debuffs/Vulnerable]]: 13->18. In one fight, unupgraded left [[enemies/Mystic]] at 1 HP, extending the fight 2+ turns.
-**Synergies:** Rampage (top-deck to keep scaling chain unbroken), Carnage+ (guarantee 28-42 next turn), Feed (guarantee kill shot), Bash+ (refresh Vulnerable on demand), Immolate+ (top-deck for guaranteed 28+ AOE), Heavy Blade (top-deck for guaranteed finisher).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Rampage]] (top-deck to keep scaling chain unbroken), [[cards/Carnage]] (guarantee 28-42 next turn), Feed (guarantee kill shot), [[cards/Bash]] (refresh Vulnerable on demand), [[cards/Immolate]] (top-deck for guaranteed 28+ AOE), [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (top-deck for guaranteed finisher).
-**Snecko Eye interaction:** Top-decked cards receive a new random cost when drawn. Top-deck a 2E card and it has 25% chance of being free next turn.
+**[[relics/Snecko Eye]] interaction:** Top-decked cards receive a new random cost when drawn. Top-deck a 2E card and it has 25% chance of being free next turn.
-**Matchups:** Lagavulin -- top Bash+ to refresh Vulnerable. Guardian -- guarantee burst for Mode Shift phases. Bronze Automaton -- top-deck Heavy Blade for post-Hyper Beam stun turn. Donu and Deca -- top-deck Immolate+ repeatedly.
+**Matchups:** [[enemies/Lagavulin]] -- top Bash+ to refresh Vulnerable. Guardian -- guarantee burst for [[buffs/Mode Shift]] phases. [[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] -- top-deck Heavy Blade for post-Hyper Beam stun turn. [[bosses/Donu and Deca]] -- top-deck Immolate+ repeatedly.
heuristics/cards/heavy-blade.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Heavy Blade
-High-cost Strength multiplier. Worthless without Strength, game-winning with it.
+High-cost [[buffs/Strength]] multiplier. Worthless without Strength, game-winning with it.
-**When to play:** Only take when the deck has Strength sources (Vajra, Inflame, Spot Weakness, Limit Break, Demon Form). At 0 Strength, just 14 damage for 2E (terrible). At Str 6+, highest damage-per-play available.
+**When to play:** Only take when the deck has Strength sources ([[relics/Vajra]], [[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Spot Weakness]], [[cards/Limit Break]], [[cards/Demon Form]]). At 0 Strength, just 14 damage for 2E (terrible). At Str 6+, highest damage-per-play available.
**Upgrade priority:** High in Strength decks. 3x to 5x Strength multiplier is transformative. At Str 6: 32 -> 44 damage.
-**Synergies:** Demon Form+ (by turn 4 at +12 Str, deals 74 damage; by turn 6 at +18 Str, deals 104), Limit Break+ (doubling Str before Heavy Blade = exponential), Double Tap (plays twice for 64+ damage), Spot Weakness (+3 Str becomes +9/+15 damage), Vulnerable (applies to full total).
+**Synergies:** Demon Form+ (by turn 4 at +12 Str, deals 74 damage; by turn 6 at +18 Str, deals 104), Limit Break+ (doubling Str before Heavy Blade = exponential), [[cards/Double Tap]] (plays twice for 64+ damage), Spot Weakness (+3 Str becomes +9/+15 damage), [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (applies to full total).
-**Snecko Eye synergy:** 25% chance of costing 0E under Confusion. Free 30-94 damage play.
+**[[relics/Snecko Eye]] synergy:** 25% chance of costing 0E under Confusion. Free 30-94 damage play.
**Matchups:** Excellent in boss fights where Strength has time to build. Less effective in quick hallway fights where 2E without setup is too slow.
heuristics/cards/hemokinesis.md
@@ -2,8 +2,8 @@
High burst for 1 energy. The 2 HP self-damage is minor when healthy.
-**When to play:** Opening turns, boss burst damage, finishing blows where HP cost is irrelevant. With Vulnerable on target: floor(15*1.5) = 22 damage for 1E -- exceptional burst.
+**When to play:** Opening turns, boss burst damage, finishing blows where HP cost is irrelevant. With [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] on target: floor(15*1.5) = 22 damage for 1E -- exceptional burst.
-**Decision points:** Check current HP. At low HP in a long fight, the 2 HP per play adds up. Against Slime Boss split phase, the 2 HP cost is especially dangerous -- combined with split damage, even one Hemokinesis can be the difference between survival and death.
+**Decision points:** Check current HP. At low HP in a long fight, the 2 HP per play adds up. Against [[bosses/Slime Boss]] split phase, the 2 HP cost is especially dangerous -- combined with split damage, even one Hemokinesis can be the difference between survival and death.
-**Synergies:** Vulnerable (22 damage for 1E), Rupture (+1 Str per play from self-damage), kill shots where HP cost is irrelevant.
+**Synergies:** Vulnerable (22 damage for 1E), [[cards/Rupture]] (+1 Str per play from self-damage), kill shots where HP cost is irrelevant.
heuristics/cards/immolate.md
@@ -2,14 +2,14 @@
High-damage AOE attack. Hits ALL enemies. The Burn downside is minor.
-**When to play:** Multi-enemy fights (Byrds, Gremlin groups, Slime Boss split, Collector + Torch Heads). Play freely -- 21+ AOE is worth 2-4 HP of Burn damage across the fight.
+**When to play:** Multi-enemy fights (Byrds, Gremlin groups, [[bosses/Slime Boss]] split, Collector + Torch Heads). Play freely -- 21+ AOE is worth 2-4 HP of Burn damage across the fight.
-**Upgrade priority:** High. 21->28 AOE. With Vulnerable: 42 AOE. With Paper Phrog (75%): 49 AOE. Most impactful card in multi-enemy boss fights.
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 21->28 AOE. With [[debuffs/Vulnerable]]: 42 AOE. With [[relics/Paper Phrog]] (75%): 49 AOE. Most impactful card in multi-enemy boss fights.
-**Synergies:** Thunderclap (applies Vulnerable to all, then Immolate for 31+ AOE), Fire Breathing+ (Burns trigger 10 free damage on draw -- bypasses Plated Armor; do NOT exhaust Fire Breathing when Immolate is in deck), Evolve (Burns draw replacement cards -- makes Burn downside pure upside), Dual Wield+ (3 copies = 84-102 AOE damage in one turn -- won Donu and Deca), Headbutt (top-deck for guaranteed next turn).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Thunderclap]] (applies Vulnerable to all, then Immolate for 31+ AOE), [[cards/Fire Breathing]] (Burns trigger 10 free damage on draw -- bypasses [[buffs/Plated Armor]]; do NOT exhaust Fire Breathing when Immolate is in deck), [[cards/Evolve]] (Burns draw replacement cards -- makes Burn downside pure upside), [[cards/Dual Wield]] (3 copies = 84-102 AOE damage in one turn -- won [[bosses/Donu and Deca]]), [[cards/Headbutt]] (top-deck for guaranteed next turn).
-**Snecko Eye synergy:** Frequently costs 0-1E instead of 2E, enabling multiple plays per turn.
+**[[relics/Snecko Eye]] synergy:** Frequently costs 0-1E instead of 2E, enabling multiple plays per turn.
-**Matchups:** Excellent vs Byrds, Slime Boss split, 3 Cultists, Guardian, The Collector. Primary damage source in both consecutive victories.
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Byrds, Slime Boss split, 3 Cultists, Guardian, [[bosses/The Collector]]. Primary damage source in both consecutive victories.
-**Flight damage math:** Against Byrds (Flight 3), damage is halved and rounded down. Include Strength in the base BEFORE halving: (21 + Str) / 2 floored. With +2 Str from Inflame: floor(23/2) = 11 per Byrd, not 10. Forgetting Strength underestimates damage by 1 per Byrd.
+**Flight damage math:** Against Byrds (Flight 3), damage is halved and rounded down. Include [[buffs/Strength]] in the base BEFORE halving: (21 + Str) / 2 floored. With +2 Str from [[cards/Inflame]]: floor(23/2) = 11 per [[enemies/Byrd]], not 10. Forgetting Strength underestimates damage by 1 per Byrd.
heuristics/cards/impervious.md
@@ -4,10 +4,10 @@ Best single-card block in the game. One-time use (Exhaust).
**When to play:** Save for the biggest incoming hit of the fight. Using 30-40 block on a 16-damage turn is wasteful -- Defends handle that. Calculate: "If I don't play Impervious now, will I die? If yes, play it. If no, save it."
-**Timing discipline:** NEVER play on Guardian's Sharp Hide attacks (9-16 damage) unless you would die. Save for the 32-damage single hit. Against Gremlin Nob, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage).
+**Timing discipline:** NEVER play on Guardian's [[buffs/Sharp Hide]] attacks (9-16 damage) unless you would die. Save for the 32-damage single hit. Against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], do NOT play (Skill triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]).
**Upgrade priority:** High. 30->40 block. The extra 10 block is the difference between absorbing a big hit cleanly and taking chip.
-**Matchups:** Bronze Automaton -- THE most important card. Hyper Beam deals 45-57 damage. Save for Hyper Beam turns. Guardian -- absorbs the 32-damage attack completely (upgraded takes 0). Gremlin Leader -- save for Turn 4-5 when Rally-scaled damage reaches 34+. Collector -- critical for post-STRONG_DEBUFF turns (37-50+ incoming). Transient -- save for Turn 3+ (50+ damage), NOT Turn 1. Snake Plant -- absorbs 9x3=27 multi-hit. Hexaghost -- save for Divider (Turn 2) or Inferno. In a 16-card deck, there is a ~31% chance Impervious is NOT in your Turn 2 hand. Headbutt can place Impervious on top of draw pile the turn before a predicted Divider/Inferno. If Impervious is the only plan for a specific high-damage turn, the plan is unreliable -- need backup block (Weak, Flame Barrier, multiple Defends).
+**Matchups:** [[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] -- THE most important card. Hyper Beam deals 45-57 damage. Save for Hyper Beam turns. Guardian -- absorbs the 32-damage attack completely (upgraded takes 0). [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] -- save for Turn 4-5 when Rally-scaled damage reaches 34+. Collector -- critical for post-STRONG_DEBUFF turns (37-50+ incoming). [[encounters/Transient]] -- save for Turn 3+ (50+ damage), NOT Turn 1. [[enemies/Snake Plant]] -- absorbs 9x3=27 multi-hit. [[bosses/Hexaghost]] -- save for Divider (Turn 2) or Inferno. In a 16-card deck, there is a ~31% chance Impervious is NOT in your Turn 2 hand. [[cards/Headbutt]] can place Impervious on top of draw pile the turn before a predicted Divider/Inferno. If Impervious is the only plan for a specific high-damage turn, the plan is unreliable -- need backup block ([[debuffs/Weak]], [[cards/Flame Barrier]], multiple Defends).
-**Synergies:** Calipers (retains 25 block next turn upgraded), Barricade (retained permanently), Spot Weakness (use the safe turn to set up Strength).
+**Synergies:** [[relics/Calipers]] (retains 25 block next turn upgraded), [[cards/Barricade]] (retained permanently), [[cards/Spot Weakness]] (use the safe turn to set up [[buffs/Strength]]).
heuristics/cards/inflame.md
@@ -2,8 +2,8 @@
Cheap, permanent damage scaling. Every Attack deals 2 more damage for the entire fight.
-**When to play:** Play as early as possible in long fights (bosses, elites). On free turns, always play. On attack turns with high incoming, play Inflame (1E) and use remaining 2E on block. Do not stack multiple setup cards in one turn. Powers do NOT trigger Gremlin Nob Enrage.
+**When to play:** Play as early as possible in long fights (bosses, elites). On free turns, always play. On attack turns with high incoming, play Inflame (1E) and use remaining 2E on block. Do not stack multiple setup cards in one turn. Powers do NOT trigger [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] [[buffs/Enrage]].
-**Synergies:** Reaper (Strength increases damage to ALL enemies = more healing), Heavy Blade (+2 Str becomes +6 or +10 damage due to multiplier), Whirlwind (each hit gains +2), Twin Strike (+2 per hit = +4 total), every Attack card.
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Reaper]] ([[buffs/Strength]] increases damage to ALL enemies = more healing), [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (+2 Str becomes +6 or +10 damage due to multiplier), [[cards/Whirlwind]] (each hit gains +2), [[cards/Twin Strike]] (+2 per hit = +4 total), every Attack card.
-**Matchups:** Guardian Defensive Mode -- safe (Power, not Attack, no Sharp Hide retaliation). Bronze Automaton -- play immediately to avoid Stasis theft. Spheric Guardian -- CRITICAL. Without Strength scaling, this fight is mathematically unwinnable. Inflame is the single best card for breaking through Spheric Guardian's defenses.
+**Matchups:** Guardian Defensive Mode -- safe (Power, not Attack, no [[buffs/Sharp Hide]] retaliation). [[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] -- play immediately to avoid Stasis theft. [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] -- CRITICAL. Without Strength scaling, this fight is mathematically unwinnable. Inflame is the single best card for breaking through Spheric Guardian's defenses.
heuristics/cards/inner-peace.md
@@ -4,11 +4,11 @@ Dual-purpose card: draws 3/4 cards when in Calm, enters Calm when not.
**When to play:** Depends on current stance.
-**In Calm (draw mode):** Draw 3/4 cards. Use when already in Calm and need to find specific cards (Eruption for burst, Defend for blocking). Play early in the turn to maximize use of drawn cards.
+**In Calm (draw mode):** Draw 3/4 cards. Use when already in Calm and need to find specific cards ([[cards/Eruption]] for burst, Defend for blocking). Play early in the turn to maximize use of drawn cards.
-**In Wrath or no stance (Calm entry mode):** Enters Calm. Provides safe exit from Wrath and sets up +2E energy loop for next turn. Use when stuck in Wrath with no Vigilance available.
+**In Wrath or no stance (Calm entry mode):** Enters Calm. Provides safe exit from Wrath and sets up +2E energy loop for next turn. Use when stuck in Wrath with no [[cards/Vigilance]] available.
-**Play order (CRITICAL):** If Flurry of Blows is in hand and you are NOT in Calm, play Flurry BEFORE Inner Peace. Inner Peace entering Calm triggers Flurry's return from discard. If already in Calm, play Inner Peace first for the draw.
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** If [[cards/Flurry of Blows]] is in hand and you are NOT in Calm, play Flurry BEFORE Inner Peace. Inner Peace entering Calm triggers Flurry's return from discard. If already in Calm, play Inner Peace first for the draw.
**Upgrade priority:** High. Drawing 4 instead of 3 significantly improves the chance of finding specific combo pieces.
heuristics/cards/intimidate.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Intimidate
-Free mass Weak. Zero downside at 0 energy cost.
+Free mass [[debuffs/Weak]]. Zero downside at 0 energy cost.
**When to play:** ALWAYS play when drawn in most fights. Reduces all enemy attack damage by 25% for 1-2 turns.
-**Decision points:** Against Gremlin Nob and Chosen, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage/Hex). Bronze Automaton: Do NOT use for Artifact stripping. SAVE for the Hyper Beam turn -- Weak reduces Hyper Beam damage by 25%. Intimidate exhausts after use, so if played early for Artifact stripping, no Weak source exists when Hyper Beam fires.
+**Decision points:** Against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] and [[enemies/Chosen]], do NOT play (Skill triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]/[[debuffs/Hex]]). [[bosses/Bronze Automaton]]: Do NOT use for [[buffs/Artifact]] stripping. SAVE for the Hyper Beam turn -- Weak reduces Hyper Beam damage by 25%. Intimidate exhausts after use, so if played early for Artifact stripping, no Weak source exists when Hyper Beam fires.
**Upgrade priority:** Worth upgrading when the deck has few other debuff sources. 1->2 Weak doubles duration.
-**Synergies:** Letter Opener (free 5 damage + mass Weak), Corruption (free AND exhausts), Feel No Pain (exhaust triggers 3 block).
+**Synergies:** [[relics/Letter Opener]] (free 5 damage + mass Weak), [[cards/Corruption]] (free AND exhausts), [[cards/Feel No Pain]] (exhaust triggers 3 block).
-**Matchups:** Guardian -- Weak reduces 32-damage attack to 24. Gremlin Leader -- mass Weak on all gremlins + Leader. Bronze Automaton -- SAVE for Hyper Beam turn. Weak on Hyper Beam reduces 51 damage to 38.
+**Matchups:** Guardian -- Weak reduces 32-damage attack to 24. [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] -- mass Weak on all gremlins + Leader. Bronze Automaton -- SAVE for Hyper Beam turn. Weak on Hyper Beam reduces 51 damage to 38.
heuristics/cards/iron-wave.md
@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
Dual-purpose card. Does two things at once for 1 energy.
-**When to play:** When you need both offense and defense but only have 1 energy to spare. Against Chosen with Hex (Attack card, no Dazed penalty). During Guardian Sharp Hide (block absorbs retaliation, then deal damage).
+**When to play:** When you need both offense and defense but only have 1 energy to spare. Against [[enemies/Chosen]] with [[debuffs/Hex]] (Attack card, no [[cards/Dazed]] penalty). During Guardian [[buffs/Sharp Hide]] (block absorbs retaliation, then deal damage).
-**Decision points:** Is splitting between 5 damage and 5 block better than fully committing to one? Usually yes when you need both. With Strength, only the damage scales. Block is affected by Frail and Dexterity, not Strength.
+**Decision points:** Is splitting between 5 damage and 5 block better than fully committing to one? Usually yes when you need both. With [[buffs/Strength]], only the damage scales. Block is affected by [[debuffs/Frail]] and [[buffs/Dexterity]], not Strength.
**Upgrade priority:** High. 5/5->7/7 is a 40% improvement on both halves.
-**Synergies:** Metallicize (passive block + Iron Wave block = solid defense per turn), Strength (damage half scales).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Metallicize]] (passive block + Iron Wave block = solid defense per turn), Strength (damage half scales).
heuristics/cards/judgment.md
@@ -10,4 +10,4 @@ Conditional instant-kill finisher. Sets enemy HP to 0 if at or below 30/40 HP th
**Useless above threshold:** Against enemies above 30/40 HP, Judgment does literally nothing. Do not waste 1E playing it on a full-health enemy.
-**Boss fights:** Excellent finisher. Against Hexaghost, getting the boss to 30-40 HP then using Judgment avoids 1-2 additional turns of Burns damage.
+**Boss fights:** Excellent finisher. Against [[bosses/Hexaghost]], getting the boss to 30-40 HP then using Judgment avoids 1-2 additional turns of Burns damage.
heuristics/cards/just-lucky.md
@@ -6,10 +6,10 @@ Free card that does a little of everything: damage, block, and Scry. Always play
**Upgrade priority:** Low-medium. 3->4 damage, 2->3 block. Small improvements across the board.
-**Wave of the Hand synergy:** The 2-3 block triggers Wave of the Hand's Weak application. Playing Just Lucky after Wave of the Hand = free Weak application. This is a key interaction for the Watcher's Weak strategy.
+**[[cards/Wave of the Hand]] synergy:** The 2-3 block triggers Wave of the Hand's [[debuffs/Weak]] application. Playing Just Lucky after Wave of the Hand = free Weak application. This is a key interaction for the [[characters/Watcher]]'s Weak strategy.
**Scry 1:** Small but consistent deck filtering. Discard Burns or Curses from the top of the draw pile.
-**Shuriken/Kunai:** Counts as an Attack for relic triggers despite its small damage.
+**[[relics/Shuriken]]/[[relics/Kunai]]:** Counts as an Attack for relic triggers despite its small damage.
**Play order:** Play after Wave of the Hand if active (to trigger Weak). Otherwise, play freely as a bonus before ending turn.
heuristics/cards/leap.md
@@ -2,8 +2,8 @@
Straightforward block card. 9 block for 1E is good value (better than Defend's 5).
-**When to play:** When you need block. No special mechanics. Fills the Defect's need for reliable turn-by-turn block before Frost orbs + Focus are online.
+**When to play:** When you need block. No special mechanics. Fills the [[characters/Defect]]'s need for reliable turn-by-turn block before Frost orbs + Focus are online.
-**Upgrade priority:** Decent. 9->12 block. Lower priority than Focus cards or Coolheaded.
+**Upgrade priority:** Decent. 9->12 block. Lower priority than Focus cards or [[cards/Coolheaded]].
-**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers Enrage. Against Nob, only play if survival requires it.
+**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]. Against Nob, only play if survival requires it.
heuristics/cards/limit-break.md
@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
# Limit Break
-Strength multiplier. Converts existing Strength into exponential scaling.
+[[buffs/Strength]] multiplier. Converts existing Strength into exponential scaling.
-**When to play:** After Inflame or Demon Form has provided base Strength. Mid-fight when Strength is already 3+ for meaningful doubling. Does NOTHING if Strength is 0.
+**When to play:** After [[cards/Inflame]] or [[cards/Demon Form]] has provided base Strength. Mid-fight when Strength is already 3+ for meaningful doubling. Does NOTHING if Strength is 0.
**Upgrade priority:** The most impactful upgrade in Str-scaling decks. Limit Break+ (no exhaust) enables repeated doubling for infinite scaling.
**Flex+ combo:** Limit Break+ doubles ALL current Str including temporary from Flex+. Play Flex+ (4 temp Str) then Limit Break+ to convert into permanent. Example: 1 base + 4 from Flex+ = 5 total, doubled to 10. End of turn lose 4 = 6 permanent.
-**Synergies:** Vajra (+1) + Inflame (+2) + Spot Weakness (+3) = Str 6 base. Limit Break+ doubles to 12, then 24. Heavy Blade at Str 6 deals 32 (or 44 upgraded). Demon Form + Limit Break dominates long fights but requires 3+ setup turns.
+**Synergies:** [[relics/Vajra]] (+1) + Inflame (+2) + [[cards/Spot Weakness]] (+3) = Str 6 base. Limit Break+ doubles to 12, then 24. [[cards/Heavy Blade]] at Str 6 deals 32 (or 44 upgraded). Demon Form + Limit Break dominates long fights but requires 3+ setup turns.
-**Weaknesses:** Requires setup (Inflame or Demon Form first), a Skill card (triggers Hex/Enrage), at low HP vs fast enemies the setup window does not exist. As a Neow random rare, dead until a Str source is found.
+**Weaknesses:** Requires setup (Inflame or Demon Form first), a Skill card (triggers [[debuffs/Hex]]/[[buffs/Enrage]]), at low HP vs fast enemies the setup window does not exist. As a Neow random rare, dead until a Str source is found.
**Deck building note:** Always pair with immediate damage/block sources for fights where the engine cannot activate.
heuristics/cards/loop.md
@@ -8,4 +8,4 @@ Doubles the passive output of the first orb slot. Value scales directly with Foc
**Focus scaling:** At Focus 3, a Frost orb passive gives 5 block. Loop adds another 5 at start of turn = 10 passive block/turn from a single orb.
-**Emotion Chip interaction:** If Emotion Chip is active, the first orb triggers three times per cycle -- massive passive value.
+**[[relics/Emotion Chip]] interaction:** If Emotion Chip is active, the first orb triggers three times per cycle -- massive passive value.
heuristics/cards/machine-learning.md
@@ -6,4 +6,4 @@ Permanent +1 card draw per turn. Subtle but compounds significantly in long figh
**Upgrade priority:** Extremely high. At 0E Innate, Machine Learning+ is always free to play Turn 1, guaranteeing +1 draw from Turn 2 onward with zero opportunity cost.
-**Mummified Hand synergy:** As a Power, triggers Mummified Hand. At 0E upgraded, already free -- Mummified Hand trigger is pure bonus value.
+**[[relics/Mummified Hand]] synergy:** As a Power, triggers Mummified Hand. At 0E upgraded, already free -- Mummified Hand trigger is pure bonus value.
heuristics/cards/malaise.md
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
# Malaise
-Silent's strongest boss debuff. Permanent Strength reduction + Weak.
+[[characters/Silent]]'s strongest boss debuff. Permanent [[buffs/Strength]] reduction + [[debuffs/Weak]].
**Critical -- X-cost energy trap:** Malaise automatically uses ALL remaining energy. You CANNOT specify X manually. Play block cards and other spells FIRST, then Malaise LAST with only the desired energy remaining. A confirmed death occurred when Malaise consumed all 4E leaving 0E for Defend at 19 HP.
-**When to play:** Save for boss fights. Exhaust means single use per combat. Do not waste on hallway fights unless genuinely lethal. Play LAST after all other non-X-cost cards. Do NOT use for Artifact stripping.
+**When to play:** Save for boss fights. Exhaust means single use per combat. Do not waste on hallway fights unless genuinely lethal. Play LAST after all other non-X-cost cards. Do NOT use for [[buffs/Artifact]] stripping.
-**Synergies:** High energy decks (more energy = larger X = bigger debuff), Wraith Form+ (reduces damage post-Intangible), Shiv burst (neuters offense while Shiv damage scales).
+**Synergies:** High energy decks (more energy = larger X = bigger debuff), [[cards/Wraith Form]] (reduces damage post-[[buffs/Intangible]]), [[cards/Shiv]] burst (neuters offense while Shiv damage scales).
**Draft priority:** High for boss fights. One of the best Silent cards against any single-target boss.
heuristics/cards/masterful-stab.md
@@ -4,8 +4,8 @@
**When to play:** Excellent in decks that take zero damage. Dead weight in long fights with chip damage.
-**Synergies:** Footwork+/high Dex builds (zero damage taken = permanent 0E), short hallway fights with no chip damage.
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Footwork]]/high Dex builds (zero damage taken = permanent 0E), short hallway fights with no chip damage.
-**Anti-synergies:** Hexaghost (Burns deal unblockable damage, cost rises 2-4 per turn -- completely unplayable by Turn 12), any fight with unavoidable chip damage (Poison, Burns, Decay curse, Brutality), long fights generally.
+**Anti-synergies:** [[bosses/Hexaghost]] (Burns deal unblockable damage, cost rises 2-4 per turn -- completely unplayable by Turn 12), any fight with unavoidable chip damage ([[debuffs/Poison]], Burns, Decay curse, [[cards/Brutality]]), long fights generally.
-**Decision framework:** Take when the deck reliably achieves zero damage in hallway fights AND the Act 1 boss is NOT Hexaghost. If Hexaghost is the boss, Masterful Stab is a trap.
+**Decision framework:** Take when the deck reliably achieves zero damage in hallway fights AND the [[acts/Act 1]] boss is NOT Hexaghost. If Hexaghost is the boss, Masterful Stab is a trap.
heuristics/cards/meditate.md
@@ -6,11 +6,11 @@ Powerful card retrieval that ends your turn. Play LAST -- always.
**Upgrade priority:** High. Retrieving 2 cards instead of 1 lets you set up both attack and defense for next turn, or retrieve two combo pieces.
-**Card retrieval with Retain:** Retrieved cards stay in hand through end of turn (Retain). Guarantees specific cards for next turn -- retrieve Eruption+ for burst, or Vigilance for blocking.
+**Card retrieval with Retain:** Retrieved cards stay in hand through end of turn (Retain). Guarantees specific cards for next turn -- retrieve [[cards/Eruption]] for burst, or [[cards/Vigilance]] for blocking.
**Enters Calm:** Sets up +2E energy bonus for next turn. Combined with retrieved Eruption+, this guarantees the full Calm energy loop.
-**Flurry interaction:** Entering Calm triggers Flurry of Blows return from discard. But since Meditate ends your turn, you cannot play returned Flurry this turn. However, Flurry Retains, so it is available next turn.
+**Flurry interaction:** Entering Calm triggers [[cards/Flurry of Blows]] return from discard. But since Meditate ends your turn, you cannot play returned Flurry this turn. However, Flurry Retains, so it is available next turn.
**Common mistakes:**
- Playing Meditate early and losing the rest of your plays for the turn.
heuristics/cards/melter.md
@@ -2,12 +2,12 @@
Bypasses enemy Block entirely. Play before other attacks on blocked enemies.
-**When to play:** Against enemies with Block (Spheric Guardian, Shield Gremlin, enemies using Defensive Stance). Without Melter, damage into Block is wasted -- Melter removes all Block then deals its damage directly to HP.
+**When to play:** Against enemies with Block ([[enemies/Spheric Guardian]], [[enemies/Shield Gremlin]], enemies using Defensive Stance). Without Melter, damage into Block is wasted -- Melter removes all Block then deals its damage directly to HP.
**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. 10->14 damage. The Block removal is the main value regardless of upgrade.
**Play order:** Play Melter BEFORE other attacks on blocked enemies. Block removal applies first, then Melter's damage hits HP, then subsequent attacks hit unblocked HP.
-**Barrage synergy:** Melter strips Block, then Barrage's full multi-orb damage hits HP. Critical in Spheric Guardian and Gremlin Leader fights.
+**[[cards/Barrage]] synergy:** Melter strips Block, then Barrage's full multi-orb damage hits HP. Critical in Spheric Guardian and [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] fights.
**Decision points:** If enemy has 15 Block, a normal 10-damage attack does 0 HP damage. Melter removes 15 Block then deals 10 HP damage -- effectively 25 damage advantage over playing another attack first.
heuristics/cards/metallicize.md
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
# Metallicize
-Passive block every turn. The block equivalent of Strength.
+Passive block every turn. The block equivalent of [[buffs/Strength]].
-**When to play:** Play as early as possible in long fights. Over 10 turns: 30 free block. Two copies provide 6 block/turn. Powers do NOT trigger Gremlin Nob Enrage.
+**When to play:** Play as early as possible in long fights. Over 10 turns: 30 free block. Two copies provide 6 block/turn. Powers do NOT trigger [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] [[buffs/Enrage]].
-**Key interaction:** Metallicize block is NOT affected by Frail. Frail only reduces block from cards. Metallicize's end-of-turn block is flat regardless of debuffs -- especially valuable against Spheric Guardian (Frail 5).
+**Key interaction:** Metallicize block is NOT affected by [[debuffs/Frail]]. Frail only reduces block from cards. Metallicize's end-of-turn block is flat regardless of debuffs -- especially valuable against [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] (Frail 5).
-**Synergies:** Iron Wave (passive + card block = solid defense), second Metallicize (6-7 block/turn is exceptional), Barricade (Metallicize block carries over and stacks -- with Barricade + 2x Metallicize, passive alone reaches 21 by Turn 3, 35 by Turn 5).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Iron Wave]] (passive + card block = solid defense), second Metallicize (6-7 block/turn is exceptional), [[cards/Barricade]] (Metallicize block carries over and stacks -- with Barricade + 2x Metallicize, passive alone reaches 21 by Turn 3, 35 by Turn 5).
-**Matchups:** Guardian -- handles many attack patterns with only 1 Defend needed. Hexaghost -- critical for the 13-turn fight. Byrds -- essential for long attrition. Spheric Guardian -- Frail-immune block.
