+**Matchups:** Guardian -- handles many attack patterns with only 1 Defend needed. [[bosses/Hexaghost]] -- critical for the 13-turn fight. Byrds -- essential for long attrition. Spheric Guardian -- Frail-immune block.
heuristics/cards/miracle.md
@@ -10,7 +10,7 @@ Free +1E that Retains and Exhausts. ONLY play when you have a useful way to spen
- Funds the Calm -> Wrath energy loop when short 1E.
- Enables a Power setup on a free turn.
-**BAD use:** Playing all Miracles with no plan for the energy. A confirmed death occurred with 4 Miracles in hand -- player played all 3 Miracles for +3E, had no useful cards to spend energy on (only Meditate+ and Rushdown), and died with 0 block against Snake Plant's 21-damage attack.
+**BAD use:** Playing all Miracles with no plan for the energy. A confirmed death occurred with 4 Miracles in hand -- player played all 3 Miracles for +3E, had no useful cards to spend energy on (only [[cards/Meditate]] and [[cards/Rushdown]]), and died with 0 block against [[enemies/Snake Plant]]'s 21-damage attack.
**Upgrade priority:** N/A (generated card, cannot be upgraded directly).
heuristics/cards/offering.md
@@ -5,8 +5,8 @@ One of the best cards in the game. Trade HP for a massive tempo burst.
**When to play:** Turn 1 of any fight where you need to set up quickly (play a Power + attack in the same turn). At high HP (50+), the cost is negligible. At low HP (15-), the 6 HP loss can be lethal — never play when 6 HP puts you below incoming damage minus available block.
**Synergies:**
-- Expensive cards (Bludgeon, Fiend Fire, Demon Form) — makes 3E cards playable alongside other cards
-- Unceasing Top — Offering draws 3 and gives 2E; if you play down to empty hand, Top triggers for additional draws
+- Expensive cards ([[cards/Bludgeon]], [[cards/Fiend Fire]], [[cards/Demon Form]]) — makes 3E cards playable alongside other cards
+- [[relics/Unceasing Top]] — Offering draws 3 and gives 2E; if you play down to empty hand, Top triggers for additional draws
- Fiend Fire — 2 extra energy means Fiend Fire still leaves energy for more plays
**Upgrade priority:** Low. Base version already provides maximum value.
heuristics/cards/overclock.md
@@ -6,6 +6,6 @@ Free cycling card -- draws 2/3 cards for 0E. The Burn downside is minor.
**Upgrade priority:** High. Draw 2->3 significantly improves the card.
-**Synergies:** Fire Breathing (Burns trigger damage on draw), Evolve (Burns draw replacement cards -- makes Burn downside upside).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Fire Breathing]] (Burns trigger damage on draw), [[cards/Evolve]] (Burns draw replacement cards -- makes Burn downside upside).
-**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers Enrage.
+**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers [[buffs/Enrage]].
heuristics/cards/phantasmal-killer.md
@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
Grants Double Damage buff for the NEXT Attack card played. Buff persists until consumed.
-**When to play:** The turn BEFORE a burst turn. On a free turn (enemy buffing/defending), play PK. Next turn, play the highest-value Attack for doubled damage. With Shivs: PK then Blade Dance next turn for 3 doubled Shivs.
+**When to play:** The turn BEFORE a burst turn. On a free turn (enemy buffing/defending), play PK. Next turn, play the highest-value Attack for doubled damage. With Shivs: PK then [[cards/Blade Dance]] next turn for 3 doubled Shivs.
-**Synergies:** Blade Dance (3 Shivs, each doubled), Accuracy (+4/+8 per Shiv, all doubled), Finisher (as an Attack, its total is also doubled), Vulnerable (multiplicative with Double Damage).
+**Synergies:** Blade Dance (3 Shivs, each doubled), [[cards/Accuracy]] (+4/+8 per [[cards/Shiv]], all doubled), [[cards/Finisher]] (as an Attack, its total is also doubled), [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (multiplicative with Double Damage).
-**Time Warp trick:** If counter is at 11/12, play PK as the 12th card on a free turn. Time Warp triggers, but Double Damage persists for the fresh cycle's first attack.
+**[[debuffs/Time Warp]] trick:** If counter is at 11/12, play PK as the 12th card on a free turn. Time Warp triggers, but Double Damage persists for the fresh cycle's first attack.
**Draft priority:** High when deck has Blade Dance and/or Accuracy. Upgraded version (0E) is strictly better -- free setup any turn.
heuristics/cards/pommel-strike.md
@@ -6,6 +6,6 @@ Damage + draw. Replaces itself in hand while dealing more than Strike.
**Upgrade priority:** High. The double draw (1->2) makes this one of the best cycling cards in the game.
-**Synergies:** Any deck that wants to find specific cards (Bash+, Fiend Fire, Feed). Snecko Oil (draw into 0-cost cards).
+**Synergies:** Any deck that wants to find specific cards ([[cards/Bash]], [[cards/Fiend Fire]], Feed). [[potions/Snecko Oil]] (draw into 0-cost cards).
**Matchups:** Against Byrds, the 9 damage is halved by Flight but the draw still finds better answers.
heuristics/cards/power-through.md
@@ -2,12 +2,12 @@
Massive block (triple Defend) but pollutes hand with 2 unplayable Wounds.
-**When to play:** Must-survive turns with massive incoming damage. When you have exhaust tools (True Grit+, Fiend Fire, Second Wind) to remove the Wounds afterward. Short fights (3-4 turns) where the Wounds will not cycle back.
+**When to play:** Must-survive turns with massive incoming damage. When you have exhaust tools ([[cards/True Grit]], [[cards/Fiend Fire]], [[cards/Second Wind]]) to remove the Wounds afterward. Short fights (3-4 turns) where the Wounds will not cycle back.
**When it is a trap:** In long fights without exhaust tools. The Wounds reduce effective hand size, meaning fewer block AND fewer damage cards per turn. Over 8+ turns, playing Power Through twice adds 4 Wounds, reducing block density for all subsequent turns.
-**Synergy -- Fire Breathing:** The 2 Wounds trigger Fire Breathing when drawn, dealing 6-10 damage to all enemies per Wound. With Evolve, each Wound drawn deals damage AND draws a replacement.
+**Synergy -- [[cards/Fire Breathing]]:** The 2 Wounds trigger Fire Breathing when drawn, dealing 6-10 damage to all enemies per Wound. With [[cards/Evolve]], each Wound drawn deals damage AND draws a replacement.
-**Slime Boss caution:** Adds 2 Wounds on top of Slimed cards. Without exhaust tools, Wound + Slimed accumulation can make 4 of 5 cards unplayable.
+**[[bosses/Slime Boss]] caution:** Adds 2 Wounds on top of [[cards/Slimed]] cards. Without exhaust tools, Wound + Slimed accumulation can make 4 of 5 cards unplayable.
**Draft rule:** Do not take unless the deck has at least one exhaust tool, Fire Breathing, or the deck is fast enough to end fights in 4-5 turns.
heuristics/cards/pummel.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Pummel
-Multi-hit Attack. Excellent Strength-scaling finisher. Exhausts after use.
+Multi-hit Attack. Excellent [[buffs/Strength]]-scaling finisher. Exhausts after use.
-**When to play:** Only when Strength is high enough to make the hits meaningful. At base 2x4 = 8 for 1E, mediocre. Save for mid-to-late fight after Inflame or Demon Form have built Strength. Since it Exhausts, you get one shot per fight -- make it count. Play on a Vulnerable target.
+**When to play:** Only when Strength is high enough to make the hits meaningful. At base 2x4 = 8 for 1E, mediocre. Save for mid-to-late fight after [[cards/Inflame]] or [[cards/Demon Form]] have built Strength. Since it Exhausts, you get one shot per fight -- make it count. Play on a [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] target.
**Decision points:** At +5 Str: (2+5) x 4 = 28 for 1E. At +7 Str: (2+7) x 4 = 36 for 1E.
-**Synergies:** Inflame/Demon Form (Strength multiplied across 4-5 hits), Vulnerable (1.5x on each hit), Pen Nib, Shuriken.
+**Synergies:** Inflame/Demon Form (Strength multiplied across 4-5 hits), Vulnerable (1.5x on each hit), [[relics/Pen Nib]], [[relics/Shuriken]].
-**Anti-synergy:** Snake Plant Malleable -- each hit triggers Malleable, causing increasing block (4-hit Pummel can give 3+4+5+6 = 18 block). Avoid against enemies with Malleable.
+**Anti-synergy:** [[enemies/Snake Plant]] [[buffs/Malleable]] -- each hit triggers Malleable, causing increasing block (4-hit Pummel can give 3+4+5+6 = 18 block). Avoid against enemies with Malleable.
-**Comparison:** Sword Boomerang has higher base per-hit damage and does not exhaust, but Pummel has more hits for better Strength scaling in single-target burst.
+**Comparison:** [[cards/Sword Boomerang]] has higher base per-hit damage and does not exhaust, but Pummel has more hits for better Strength scaling in single-target burst.
heuristics/cards/rage.md
@@ -8,8 +8,8 @@ Gain block whenever you play an Attack this turn. Lasts until end of turn only -
**Decision points:** If you draw Rage on a turn where you play few attacks, it provides minimal value.
-**Anti-synergy with Barricade:** Despite appearing to synergize, Rage's per-turn expiration limits the combo. You must redraw and replay Rage each turn to benefit.
+**Anti-synergy with [[cards/Barricade]]:** Despite appearing to synergize, Rage's per-turn expiration limits the combo. You must redraw and replay Rage each turn to benefit.
-**Synergies:** Attack-heavy decks, Unceasing Top, Anger (0E Attack that triggers Rage for free block), Shuriken (both reward playing many Attacks per turn).
+**Synergies:** Attack-heavy decks, [[relics/Unceasing Top]], [[cards/Anger]] (0E Attack that triggers Rage for free block), [[relics/Shuriken]] (both reward playing many Attacks per turn).
-**Matchups:** Excellent vs Gremlin Leader (block while killing gremlins). Against The Collector, Rage+ combined with Thunderclap provided exactly the block needed to survive a lethal turn at 2 HP.
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] (block while killing gremlins). Against [[bosses/The Collector]], Rage+ combined with [[cards/Thunderclap]] provided exactly the block needed to survive a lethal turn at 2 HP.
heuristics/cards/rampage.md
@@ -4,10 +4,10 @@ Scaling attack that gets stronger every time you play it. +8 damage per play (cu
**When to play:** Play every turn you can afford it. Each play increases all future plays. In short fights (3 turns), only reaches 16-24 total -- mediocre. In long fights, becomes highest damage-per-energy in the deck. On free turns, always play for the scaling even if targeting a full-HP enemy.
-**Scaling math:** Play 1: 8, Play 2: 16, Play 3: 24, Play 4: 32, Play 5: 40. The +8 accumulates on the BASE, then Strength and Vulnerable apply ON TOP (multiplicative scaling).
+**Scaling math:** Play 1: 8, Play 2: 16, Play 3: 24, Play 4: 32, Play 5: 40. The +8 accumulates on the BASE, then [[buffs/Strength]] and [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] apply ON TOP (multiplicative scaling).
-**Synergies:** Double Tap (double play = double scaling acceleration), Headbutt (top-deck Rampage to keep scaling chain unbroken), Vulnerable (50% bonus on already-high damage), Inflame (compounds with scaling base).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Double Tap]] (double play = double scaling acceleration), [[cards/Headbutt]] (top-deck Rampage to keep scaling chain unbroken), Vulnerable (50% bonus on already-high damage), [[cards/Inflame]] (compounds with scaling base).
-**Anti-synergy:** Havoc (can randomly exhaust Rampage permanently), unupgraded True Grit (random exhaust risk). Any random-exhaust effect is devastating because Rampage's value is cumulative.
+**Anti-synergy:** Havoc (can randomly exhaust Rampage permanently), unupgraded [[cards/True Grit]] (random exhaust risk). Any random-exhaust effect is devastating because Rampage's value is cumulative.
-**Matchups:** Hexaghost -- exceptional in the 13-turn fight (scales to 60+ damage). Guardian -- strong for 10+ turns. 3 Cultists -- play turn 1 (free) to start scaling.
+**Matchups:** [[bosses/Hexaghost]] -- exceptional in the 13-turn fight (scales to 60+ damage). Guardian -- strong for 10+ turns. 3 Cultists -- play turn 1 (free) to start scaling.
heuristics/cards/reaper.md
@@ -2,16 +2,16 @@
Essential sustain card. Damage + healing in one. Exhausts after one use per fight.
-**When to play:** Multi-enemy fights where you heal from hitting all enemies. With Strength, both damage and healing scale. Timing is critical since it exhausts.
+**When to play:** Multi-enemy fights where you heal from hitting all enemies. With [[buffs/Strength]], both damage and healing scale. Timing is critical since it exhausts.
-**Decision points:** Reaper heals based on UNBLOCKED damage -- enemies with block reduce healing. In scaling fights, Shockwave+ on the first attack turn is higher priority than Reaper healing. Prevention > healing.
+**Decision points:** Reaper heals based on UNBLOCKED damage -- enemies with block reduce healing. In scaling fights, [[cards/Shockwave]] on the first attack turn is higher priority than Reaper healing. Prevention > healing.
-**Critical limitation:** Cannot sustain across consecutive combat rooms. A deck relying solely on Reaper will lose 25-40 HP per fight in Act 2 and recover only 6 from Burning Blood. Pair with Feed, Blood Potion, Regen Potion, or Toy Ornithopter.
+**Critical limitation:** Cannot sustain across consecutive combat rooms. A deck relying solely on Reaper will lose 25-40 HP per fight in [[acts/Act 2]] and recover only 6 from [[relics/Burning Blood]]. Pair with Feed, [[potions/Blood Potion]], [[potions/Regen Potion]], or [[relics/Toy Ornithopter]].
-**Exhaust warning:** Reaper can be permanently lost through random exhaust effects (unupgraded True Grit, Havoc). Protect Reaper from random exhaust at all costs.
+**Exhaust warning:** Reaper can be permanently lost through random exhaust effects (unupgraded [[cards/True Grit]], Havoc). Protect Reaper from random exhaust at all costs.
-**Synergies:** Inflame (+2 Str boosts damage to all enemies = more healing), Vulnerable (bonus damage = bonus healing), multi-enemy fights.
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Inflame]] (+2 Str boosts damage to all enemies = more healing), [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (bonus damage = bonus healing), multi-enemy fights.
-**Anti-synergy:** Demon Form as the sole Strength source. Reaper needs Strength NOW, not later.
+**Anti-synergy:** [[cards/Demon Form]] as the sole Strength source. Reaper needs Strength NOW, not later.
-**Matchups:** The Collector -- 3 targets, excellent healing. Spheric Guardian -- WORST MATCHUP (Barricade block means Reaper heals zero). The Champ -- single target, limited healing; save for after Execute.
+**Matchups:** [[bosses/The Collector]] -- 3 targets, excellent healing. [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] -- WORST MATCHUP ([[cards/Barricade]] block means Reaper heals zero). [[bosses/The Champ]] -- single target, limited healing; save for after Execute.
heuristics/cards/rupture.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Rupture
-Converts self-damage into permanent Strength scaling.
+Converts self-damage into permanent [[buffs/Strength]] scaling.
**When to play:** As early as possible to maximize Strength accumulation. Rupture does nothing without self-damage cards.
-**Decision points:** Without Brutality or Hemokinesis, it is a dead card. With Brutality, it creates a scaling engine that makes long fights easier over time.
+**Decision points:** Without [[cards/Brutality]] or [[cards/Hemokinesis]], it is a dead card. With Brutality, it creates a scaling engine that makes long fights easier over time.
-**Synergies:** Brutality (automatic +1 Str/turn), Hemokinesis (+1 Str per play), Offering (+1 Str per play). Multiple Ruptures stack (2 Ruptures + Brutality = +2 Str/turn).
+**Synergies:** Brutality (automatic +1 Str/turn), Hemokinesis (+1 Str per play), [[cards/Offering]] (+1 Str per play). Multiple Ruptures stack (2 Ruptures + Brutality = +2 Str/turn).
heuristics/cards/rushdown.md
@@ -6,8 +6,8 @@ Engine Power that draws 2/3 cards on every Wrath entry. Enables longer, more exp
**Upgrade priority:** High. Drawing 3 instead of 2 per Wrath entry significantly increases burst potential and consistency.
-**Triggers every Wrath entry:** Every Eruption, Crescendo, or other Wrath-entry card draws 2-3 extra cards. In a stance-cycling turn with 2 Wrath entries: 4-6 extra draws.
+**Triggers every Wrath entry:** Every [[cards/Eruption]], [[cards/Crescendo]], or other Wrath-entry card draws 2-3 extra cards. In a stance-cycling turn with 2 Wrath entries: 4-6 extra draws.
-**Flurry of Blows combo:** Wrath entry triggers both Rushdown (draw 2-3) and Flurry return (from discard). The drawn cards may include more attacks to play in Wrath at doubled damage. This combination is the Watcher's primary burst engine.
+**[[cards/Flurry of Blows]] combo:** Wrath entry triggers both Rushdown (draw 2-3) and Flurry return (from discard). The drawn cards may include more attacks to play in Wrath at doubled damage. This combination is the [[characters/Watcher]]'s primary burst engine.
**Draft priority:** High. Take Rushdown whenever offered. Core engine piece for the stance-cycling strategy.
heuristics/cards/searing-blow.md
@@ -4,4 +4,4 @@ Can be upgraded multiple times (unique mechanic). Worth building around only if
**When to play:** If upgraded 3+ times, becomes a primary damage source. Otherwise, a mediocre 2E attack.
-**Decision points:** Only take if you plan to upgrade at every rest site. At 1-2 upgrades, worse than Carnage or Pommel Strike.
+**Decision points:** Only take if you plan to upgrade at every rest site. At 1-2 upgrades, worse than [[cards/Carnage]] or [[cards/Pommel Strike]].
heuristics/cards/second-wind.md
@@ -2,13 +2,13 @@
Mass exhaust for block. Clears status cards while gaining significant block.
-**When to play:** When hand is full of status cards (Burns, Wounds, Slimed, Dazed). Each exhausted becomes 5-7 block. Also exhausts Defends and other Skills.
+**When to play:** When hand is full of status cards (Burns, Wounds, [[cards/Slimed]], [[cards/Dazed]]). Each exhausted becomes 5-7 block. Also exhausts Defends and other Skills.
-**Decision points:** Exhausts ALL non-Attack cards including useful Skills. Check what you are losing before playing. In long attrition fights, exhausting irreplaceable Skills (Shockwave+, True Grit+) sealed losses.
+**Decision points:** Exhausts ALL non-Attack cards including useful Skills. Check what you are losing before playing. In long attrition fights, exhausting irreplaceable Skills ([[cards/Shockwave]], [[cards/True Grit]]) sealed losses.
**Critical warning:** Only play when you are certain the exhausted Skills are no longer needed.
-**Synergies:** Corruption (Skills cost 0 but exhaust -- Second Wind exhausts them for block value), Dark Embrace (draw per exhaust), Barricade (block persists), Feel No Pain (each card exhausted by SW+ triggers FNP -- hand with 5 status = 25-35 block from SW+ + 15-20 from FNP = 40-55 total).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Corruption]] (Skills cost 0 but exhaust -- Second Wind exhausts them for block value), [[cards/Dark Embrace]] (draw per exhaust), [[cards/Barricade]] (block persists), [[cards/Feel No Pain]] (each card exhausted by SW+ triggers FNP -- hand with 5 status = 25-35 block from SW+ + 15-20 from FNP = 40-55 total).
**Normality curse removal:** Under Corruption, Second Wind+ can permanently exhaust Normality from combat. High-priority play.
heuristics/cards/seeing-red.md
@@ -4,4 +4,4 @@ Free energy. Costs 0 to play (upgraded), gains 2E, exhausts.
**When to play:** Any turn where you have cards to play but not enough energy. Play early in the turn so you can use the 2E on remaining cards.
-**Synergies:** Expensive cards (Carnage 2E, Shockwave 2E, Fiend Fire 2E). Enables playing them alongside other cards. Excellent for enabling Bash+ 2E + another 2E card in the same turn.
+**Synergies:** Expensive cards ([[cards/Carnage]] 2E, [[cards/Shockwave]] 2E, [[cards/Fiend Fire]] 2E). Enables playing them alongside other cards. Excellent for enabling [[cards/Bash]] 2E + another 2E card in the same turn.
heuristics/cards/seek.md
@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
# Seek
-The most consistent card in the Defect's pool. Guarantees access to the exact card needed for the current situation.
+The most consistent card in the [[characters/Defect]]'s pool. Guarantees access to the exact card needed for the current situation.
-**When to play:** Turn 1 of boss fights: find Echo Form. Hallway fights: find the kill card (Barrage+ with full orbs).
+**When to play:** Turn 1 of boss fights: find [[cards/Echo Form]]. Hallway fights: find the kill card ([[cards/Barrage]] with full orbs).
**Upgrade priority:** Extremely high. 1 card->2 cards. Seek+ finds 2 cards for 0E -- functionally a perfect draw ignoring deck dilution.
-**Bottled Lightning synergy:** Making Seek innate guarantees a tutor Turn 1 of every combat. The single most consistent opener for the Defect.
+**[[relics/Bottled Lightning]] synergy:** Making Seek innate guarantees a tutor Turn 1 of every combat. The single most consistent opener for the Defect.
**Echo Form synergy:** Echo Form doubles Seek, tutoring 2 (or 4 with Seek+). Enables assembling a complete hand every turn.
**Exhaust note:** One-time tutor per combat. Almost always sufficient because it sets up the engine on Turn 1.
-**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers Enrage. Play only if tutored card enables a kill within 1-2 turns.
+**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]. Play only if tutored card enables a kill within 1-2 turns.
heuristics/cards/sentinel.md
@@ -2,12 +2,12 @@
Block card that refunds energy when exhausted.
-**When to play:** In exhaust-heavy decks. Sentinel provides block AND energy when fed to Fiend Fire, True Grit+, Second Wind, or Burning Pact.
+**When to play:** In exhaust-heavy decks. Sentinel provides block AND energy when fed to [[cards/Fiend Fire]], [[cards/True Grit]], [[cards/Second Wind]], or [[cards/Burning Pact]].
-**Decision points:** Base block (5/8) is modest. The value is in the exhaust payoff. Without exhaust tools, Sentinel is just a worse Shrug It Off. With exhaust tools, it is a net-positive play: block + energy recovery.
+**Decision points:** Base block (5/8) is modest. The value is in the exhaust payoff. Without exhaust tools, Sentinel is just a worse [[cards/Shrug It Off]]. With exhaust tools, it is a net-positive play: block + energy recovery.
-**Upgrade priority:** Both halves improve significantly. Upgraded Sentinel exhausted via True Grit+ or Second Wind gives 8 block + 3 energy -- equivalent to a free Offering without the HP loss.
+**Upgrade priority:** Both halves improve significantly. Upgraded Sentinel exhausted via True Grit+ or Second Wind gives 8 block + 3 energy -- equivalent to a free [[cards/Offering]] without the HP loss.
-**Synergies:** Fiend Fire (exhausted for 7+ damage AND gains 2-3E), True Grit+ (exhaust for block + energy), Dark Embrace (exhaust draws a card), Corruption (all Skills cost 0 and exhaust -- Sentinel becomes 0E for 5-8 block + 2-3E, a net energy gain).
+**Synergies:** Fiend Fire (exhausted for 7+ damage AND gains 2-3E), True Grit+ (exhaust for block + energy), [[cards/Dark Embrace]] (exhaust draws a card), [[cards/Corruption]] (all Skills cost 0 and exhaust -- Sentinel becomes 0E for 5-8 block + 2-3E, a net energy gain).
-**Matchups:** Gremlin Nob -- do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage).
+**Matchups:** [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] -- do NOT play (Skill triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]).
heuristics/cards/sever-soul.md
@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
High damage attack that exhausts every non-Attack card currently in hand.
-**When to play:** In exhaust-synergy decks with Feel No Pain or Dark Embrace. When hand is mostly Status cards and you want to purge them all while dealing damage.
+**When to play:** In exhaust-synergy decks with [[cards/Feel No Pain]] or [[cards/Dark Embrace]]. When hand is mostly Status cards and you want to purge them all while dealing damage.
**Decision points:** Before playing, count how many non-Attack cards are in hand and evaluate whether you can afford to lose them ALL for the rest of this fight. Play Defends/Skills FIRST, then Sever Soul last to minimize cards exhausted. If any Defend or key Skill is in hand, do NOT play unless the fight will end this turn.
-**Critical warning:** Do not take this card as a generic damage source. The exhaust-all-non-attacks cost is severe. It is NOT a substitute for Hemokinesis or Carnage. Only take it in dedicated exhaust decks (Feel No Pain, Dark Embrace, Corruption).
+**Critical warning:** Do not take this card as a generic damage source. The exhaust-all-non-attacks cost is severe. It is NOT a substitute for [[cards/Hemokinesis]] or [[cards/Carnage]]. Only take it in dedicated exhaust decks (Feel No Pain, Dark Embrace, [[cards/Corruption]]).
**Matchups:** In long fights (Guardian, bosses), losing Defend cards cripples block density for the rest of combat.
heuristics/cards/shockwave.md
@@ -2,14 +2,14 @@
Mass debuff. One of the most powerful cards in the game. Exhausts but returns to deck next fight.
-**When to play:** Play on the FIRST ATTACK TURN of multi-enemy fights. For 3 Cultists, that is turn 2. For Slavers, that is turn 1. Never save for turn 4+ -- preventing damage with early Weak far exceeds healing from Reaper. Never hold for "a harder fight" -- exhaust resets between fights.
+**When to play:** Play on the FIRST ATTACK TURN of multi-enemy fights. For 3 Cultists, that is turn 2. For Slavers, that is turn 1. Never save for turn 4+ -- preventing damage with early [[debuffs/Weak]] far exceeds healing from [[cards/Reaper]]. Never hold for "a harder fight" -- exhaust resets between fights.
-**Critical:** After Shockwave (2E), the remaining 1E MUST go to block at critical HP, NOT damage. Confirmed fatal: Shockwave + Pommel Strike instead of Shockwave + Defend at 19 HP vs Slavers resulted in Turn 1 death.
+**Critical:** After Shockwave (2E), the remaining 1E MUST go to block at critical HP, NOT damage. Confirmed fatal: Shockwave + [[cards/Pommel Strike]] instead of Shockwave + Defend at 19 HP vs Slavers resulted in Turn 1 death.
**Upgrade priority:** High. 2->3 turns of each debuff is 50% more duration.
-**Synergies:** Multi-enemy fights (debuffs apply to ALL), Dropkick (Vulnerable on all targets = free cycling).
+**Synergies:** Multi-enemy fights (debuffs apply to ALL), [[cards/Dropkick]] ([[debuffs/Vulnerable]] on all targets = free cycling).
-**Matchups:** Hexaghost -- Weak reduces Inferno from 42 to 28. MANDATORY for survival. Gremlin Leader -- mass debuff. Slime Boss split -- mass debuff on both slimes. The Collector -- play when Torch Heads are present.
+**Matchups:** [[bosses/Hexaghost]] -- Weak reduces Inferno from 42 to 28. MANDATORY for survival. [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] -- mass debuff. [[bosses/Slime Boss]] split -- mass debuff on both slimes. [[bosses/The Collector]] -- play when Torch Heads are present.
-**Warning -- The Champ:** Shockwave debuffs applied BEFORE Anger are WASTED. Anger cleanses ALL debuffs at 50% HP. Hold for after Anger. With Runic Pyramid, Shockwave stays in hand across turns -- if Fiend Fire is also in the deck, Shockwave will be permanently exhausted by Fiend Fire along with the rest of the hand. Do NOT fire Fiend Fire while Shockwave is in hand during a Champ fight. Wait until Shockwave has been played post-Anger or is safely in the draw pile.
+**Warning -- [[bosses/The Champ]]:** Shockwave debuffs applied BEFORE [[cards/Anger]] are WASTED. Anger cleanses ALL debuffs at 50% HP. Hold for after Anger. With [[relics/Runic Pyramid]], Shockwave stays in hand across turns -- if [[cards/Fiend Fire]] is also in the deck, Shockwave will be permanently exhausted by Fiend Fire along with the rest of the hand. Do NOT fire Fiend Fire while Shockwave is in hand during a Champ fight. Wait until Shockwave has been played post-Anger or is safely in the draw pile.
heuristics/cards/shrug-it-off.md
@@ -6,4 +6,4 @@ Block + draw. Replaces itself while providing defense.
**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 8->11 block. After upgrading, always use 11 in calculations, not the base 8. Miscalculating SIO+ block as 8 instead of 11 costs 3 block per play -- this exact error has been the survival margin in a boss fight.
-**Matchups:** Against Gremlin Nob, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage).
+**Matchups:** Against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], do NOT play (Skill triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]).
heuristics/cards/skewer.md
@@ -6,6 +6,6 @@ X-cost attack that scales with remaining energy. High energy produces massive si
**When to play:** Best on free turns (enemy buffing/defending) where all energy can go to damage. Pairs with Energy Potions for massive burst (5E = 35/50 damage).
-**Synergies:** Vulnerable (50% more damage per hit), high energy sources.
+**Synergies:** [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (50% more damage per hit), high energy sources.
**Draft priority:** Moderate. Strong burst finisher for boss fights and elites. Less useful in hallway fights where energy must split between block and damage.
heuristics/cards/spot-weakness.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Spot Weakness
-Conditional Strength gain. Only works when enemy's current intent is an Attack.
+Conditional [[buffs/Strength]] gain. Only works when enemy's current intent is an Attack.
-**When to play:** Only play when enemy intent is an Attack. On non-attack turns, this does nothing and wastes 1 energy. Against enemies where intent is hidden (Runic Dome), this is a gamble.
+**When to play:** Only play when enemy intent is an Attack. On non-attack turns, this does nothing and wastes 1 energy. Against enemies where intent is hidden ([[relics/Runic Dome]]), this is a gamble.
**Upgrade priority:** Worth upgrading for scaling fights. +3 -> +4 STR.
-**Synergies:** Multi-hit attacks (Pummel, Twin Strike, Whirlwind -- Strength applies per hit), Heavy Blade (+3 Str becomes +9/+15 due to multiplier), Limit Break (doubling after Spot Weakness gives massive scaling), long fights where Strength compounds.
+**Synergies:** Multi-hit attacks ([[cards/Pummel]], [[cards/Twin Strike]], [[cards/Whirlwind]] -- Strength applies per hit), [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (+3 Str becomes +9/+15 due to multiplier), [[cards/Limit Break]] (doubling after Spot Weakness gives massive scaling), long fights where Strength compounds.
-**Matchups:** Excellent vs Guardian (predictable attack turns, long fight). Excellent vs Hexaghost (frequent attack turns). Hexaghost Turn 2 timing: playing it alongside Inflame+ leaves only 1E for block against ~30 damage. Consider playing on Turn 3+ instead. Marginally useful vs Spheric Guardian (alternates attack/defend).
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Guardian (predictable attack turns, long fight). Excellent vs [[bosses/Hexaghost]] (frequent attack turns). Hexaghost Turn 2 timing: playing it alongside [[cards/Inflame]] leaves only 1E for block against ~30 damage. Consider playing on Turn 3+ instead. Marginally useful vs [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] (alternates attack/defend).
-**Snecko Eye synergy:** Even at 2-3E cost, the permanent Str gain is often worth it in boss fights.
+**[[relics/Snecko Eye]] synergy:** Even at 2-3E cost, the permanent Str gain is often worth it in boss fights.
heuristics/cards/strike.md
@@ -2,8 +2,8 @@
Baseline damage card that gets outclassed quickly.
-**When to play:** Early floors before better attacks are available. Safe against Gremlin Nob (Attack card).
+**When to play:** Early floors before better attacks are available. Safe against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] (Attack card).
**Decision points:** Is there a better attack to play instead? Could this energy be spent on block or setup?
-**Upgrade priority:** Low. 6->9 damage (13 with Vulnerable). Only worth mass-upgrading via Ancient Writing event.
+**Upgrade priority:** Low. 6->9 damage (13 with [[debuffs/Vulnerable]]). Only worth mass-upgrading via [[events/Ancient Writing]] event.
heuristics/cards/sweeping-beam.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Sweeping Beam
-The Defect's best early AOE option. Hits all enemies and replaces itself with a draw.
+The [[characters/Defect]]'s best early AOE option. Hits all enemies and replaces itself with a draw.
-**When to play:** Always in multi-enemy fights for the AOE. In single-enemy fights, slightly below Strike in damage but the draw makes it net-positive. Safe against Gremlin Nob as an Attack.
+**When to play:** Always in multi-enemy fights for the AOE. In single-enemy fights, slightly below Strike in damage but the draw makes it net-positive. Safe against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] as an Attack.
-**Early Defect priority:** High. Addresses the Attack deficit and provides AOE damage, critical for Act 1 Slime Boss and Act 2 multi-enemy fights.
+**Early Defect priority:** High. Addresses the Attack deficit and provides AOE damage, critical for [[acts/Act 1]] [[bosses/Slime Boss]] and [[acts/Act 2]] multi-enemy fights.
**Upgrade priority:** 6->9 damage AOE. Solid improvement.
heuristics/cards/sword-boomerang.md
@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
Hits random enemies 3-4 times for 3 damage each. Targets are random across all enemies.
-**When to play:** In single-enemy fights, all hits land on the same target. With high Strength, each hit gains +Str damage. At +5 Str: (3+5) x 3 = 24 for 1E. Does not Exhaust, so it can be played every cycle.
+**When to play:** In single-enemy fights, all hits land on the same target. With high [[buffs/Strength]], each hit gains +Str damage. At +5 Str: (3+5) x 3 = 24 for 1E. Does not Exhaust, so it can be played every cycle.
**Decision points:** At base (3x3 = 9 for 1E), mediocre. Becomes strong at Strength +3 or higher. Against multiple enemies, random targeting makes it unreliable for focus-firing a priority target.
-**Synergies:** Inflame/Demon Form/Brimstone (Strength multiplied across 3-4 hits), Vulnerable (1.5x on each hit), Pen Nib.
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Inflame]]/[[cards/Demon Form]]/[[relics/Brimstone]] (Strength multiplied across 3-4 hits), [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (1.5x on each hit), [[relics/Pen Nib]].
-**Anti-synergy:** Snake Plant Malleable -- each hit triggers Malleable, granting increasing block. Avoid against enemies with Malleable. Random targeting in multi-enemy fights is unreliable.
+**Anti-synergy:** [[enemies/Snake Plant]] [[buffs/Malleable]] -- each hit triggers Malleable, granting increasing block. Avoid against enemies with Malleable. Random targeting in multi-enemy fights is unreliable.
heuristics/cards/talk-to-the-hand.md
@@ -6,8 +6,8 @@ Block Return per hit. Excellent against multi-hit enemies. Play early since it e
**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. Block Return 2->3 is 50% more free block per hit. Against multi-hit enemies, this scales rapidly.
-**Snake Plant (7x3):** Block Return 2 triggers 3 times per Chomp = 6 free block per attack. Upgraded: 9 free block. Extremely strong in this matchup.
+**[[enemies/Snake Plant]] (7x3):** Block Return 2 triggers 3 times per Chomp = 6 free block per attack. Upgraded: 9 free block. Extremely strong in this matchup.
-**Multi-hit enemies:** The more hits an enemy does, the more value Block Return provides. Excellent against Hexaghost Inferno (6 hits), Book of Stabbing, and any multi-attack enemy.
+**Multi-hit enemies:** The more hits an enemy does, the more value Block Return provides. Excellent against [[bosses/Hexaghost]] Inferno (6 hits), [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]], and any multi-attack enemy.
**Exhausts:** Only one use per combat. Apply to the enemy that attacks most frequently or with the most hits.
heuristics/cards/terror.md
@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
# Terror
-99 Vulnerable = permanent 50% more damage from ALL attacks for the rest of combat.
+99 [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] = permanent 50% more damage from ALL attacks for the rest of combat.
-**When to play:** Play on the first turn you can spare 1E (0E upgraded), then focus on damage. Earlier application = more total bonus damage. Against Awakened One: Terror in Phase 1 accelerates kill before Curiosity Str accumulates. Terror in Phase 2 enables the kill without Powers.
+**When to play:** Play on the first turn you can spare 1E (0E upgraded), then focus on damage. Earlier application = more total bonus damage. Against [[bosses/Awakened One]]: Terror in Phase 1 accelerates kill before Curiosity Str accumulates. Terror in Phase 2 enables the kill without Powers.
**Boss fight value:** Against bosses with 250-300+ HP, the 50% increase saves 3-5 turns of combat, translating to 3-5 fewer turns of incoming damage.
-**Dead Branch synergy:** Terror exhausts, triggering Dead Branch for a random card -- "free" proc on top of devastating debuff.
+**[[relics/Dead Branch]] synergy:** Terror exhausts, triggering Dead Branch for a random card -- "free" proc on top of devastating debuff.
**Bane synergy:** Bane deals double on Poisoned. With Terror's Vuln 99: (base * 2) * 1.5 = 3x effective damage.
heuristics/cards/thunderclap.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Thunderclap
-Cheap mass Vulnerable. The best setup card for multi-enemy fights.
+Cheap mass [[debuffs/Vulnerable]]. The best setup card for multi-enemy fights.
-**When to play:** Always in multi-enemy fights. Turn 1 opener to set up 50% more damage on everything. Strips 1 Flight per Byrd hit and 1 Artifact per enemy.
+**When to play:** Always in multi-enemy fights. Turn 1 opener to set up 50% more damage on everything. Strips 1 Flight per [[enemies/Byrd]] hit and 1 [[buffs/Artifact]] per enemy.
-**Decision points:** Against single enemies, worse than Bash+ (1 turn Vulnerable vs 3 turns). Against multiple enemies, one of the best cards in the game.
+**Decision points:** Against single enemies, worse than [[cards/Bash]] (1 turn Vulnerable vs 3 turns). Against multiple enemies, one of the best cards in the game.
**Upgrade priority:** Worth upgrading when it is a regular play. 4->7 AOE damage with Vulnerable: ~6->10 damage each.
-**Synergies:** Dropkick (sets up free cycling on all Vulnerable targets), all Attack cards (50% more damage to all enemies).
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Dropkick]] (sets up free cycling on all Vulnerable targets), all Attack cards (50% more damage to all enemies).
-**Matchups:** Byrds -- THE single most important card. Strips 1 Flight from each + applies Vulnerable. Without Thunderclap, Flight 3 on three Byrds requires 9 separate hits. Having Thunderclap before Act 2 is essential. Sentries -- strips 1 Artifact from each. Slime Boss split -- mass Vulnerable on both slimes. Bronze Automaton -- strips Artifact charges (Automaton starts with Artifact 3).
+**Matchups:** Byrds -- THE single most important card. Strips 1 Flight from each + applies Vulnerable. Without Thunderclap, Flight 3 on three Byrds requires 9 separate hits. Having Thunderclap before [[acts/Act 2]] is essential. Sentries -- strips 1 Artifact from each. [[bosses/Slime Boss]] split -- mass Vulnerable on both slimes. [[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] -- strips Artifact charges (Automaton starts with Artifact 3).
heuristics/cards/true-grit.md
@@ -2,14 +2,14 @@
Upgraded version lets you CHOOSE which card to exhaust. Unupgraded version exhausts RANDOMLY.
-**When to play:** True Grit+ is excellent -- block + controlled deck thinning. Exhaust status cards (Slimed, Wound, Burn) while gaining block.
+**When to play:** True Grit+ is excellent -- block + controlled deck thinning. Exhaust status cards ([[cards/Slimed]], Wound, Burn) while gaining block.
**Decision points:** CRITICAL -- is this upgraded? Unupgraded True Grit is a ticking time bomb. In a thin deck, random exhaust may destroy your best card. True Grit+ lets you choose expendable cards (Strikes, status cards). Before playing unupgraded, check: are there cards in hand you absolutely cannot lose?
**Upgrade priority:** THE most important upgrade in the game. Random->chosen exhaust completely changes the card. Upgrade priority #1 whenever True Grit is in the deck.
-**Synergies:** Dark Embrace (draw on exhaust), Charon's Ashes (3 AOE on exhaust). Be careful of over-exhausting.
+**Synergies:** [[cards/Dark Embrace]] (draw on exhaust), [[relics/Charon's Ashes]] (3 AOE on exhaust). Be careful of over-exhausting.
-**Anti-synergy:** Unupgraded True Grit with Fiend Fire+ or any irreplaceable card. Random exhaust can destroy the deck's best card mid-combat.
+**Anti-synergy:** Unupgraded True Grit with [[cards/Fiend Fire]] or any irreplaceable card. Random exhaust can destroy the deck's best card mid-combat.
-**Matchups:** Gremlin Nob -- do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage). Guardian -- unupgraded True Grit is particularly dangerous; random exhaust removes key cards from a hand already under pressure.
+**Matchups:** [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] -- do NOT play (Skill triggers [[buffs/Enrage]]). Guardian -- unupgraded True Grit is particularly dangerous; random exhaust removes key cards from a hand already under pressure.
heuristics/cards/twin-strike.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Twin Strike
-Multi-hit attack. Each hit applies Strength separately, making this one of the best Strength-scaling cheap attacks.
+Multi-hit attack. Each hit applies [[buffs/Strength]] separately, making this one of the best Strength-scaling cheap attacks.
-**When to play:** Against Flight enemies (strips 2 Flight). For Pen Nib counting (each hit counts toward the 10-hit counter). In Strength-stacking decks -- at Str 6, Twin Strike deals (5+6)*2 = 22 for 1E (or 26 upgraded).
+**When to play:** Against Flight enemies (strips 2 Flight). For [[relics/Pen Nib]] counting (each hit counts toward the 10-hit counter). In Strength-stacking decks -- at Str 6, Twin Strike deals (5+6)*2 = 22 for 1E (or 26 upgraded).
**Decision points:** Against Flight enemies, multi-hit is more valuable than single-hit for the same total. In Strength decks, Twin Strike is often the best finisher for its energy cost.
-**Synergies:** Vajra/Inflame/Spot Weakness (Strength applies per hit = double benefit), Vulnerable (50% bonus on each hit), Double Tap (plays twice for massive burst).
+**Synergies:** [[relics/Vajra]]/[[cards/Inflame]]/[[cards/Spot Weakness]] (Strength applies per hit = double benefit), [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (50% bonus on each hit), [[cards/Double Tap]] (plays twice for massive burst).
heuristics/cards/uppercut.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Uppercut
-Applies BOTH Weak and Vulnerable in one card. The best single-card Artifact stripper in the Ironclad pool.
+Applies BOTH [[debuffs/Weak]] and [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] in one card. The best single-card [[buffs/Artifact]] stripper in the [[characters/Ironclad]] pool.
**When to play:** Against single targets where both debuffs matter. Against Artifact enemies (strips 2 charges). Upgraded version applies 2 turns of each.
-**Decision points:** At 2E, is dual-debuff worth the cost? Against Spheric Guardian (3 Artifact), strips 2 charges efficiently. Against Gremlin Nob, safe (Attack card).
+**Decision points:** At 2E, is dual-debuff worth the cost? Against [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] (3 Artifact), strips 2 charges efficiently. Against [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], safe (Attack card).
**Upgrade priority:** High against Artifact enemies. Extends debuff duration to 2 turns each -- doubles value.
-**Synergies:** Artifact stripping (each debuff consumes 1 charge separately), Spot Weakness (Vulnerable amplifies Strength-boosted attacks), Demon Form (sustained Vulnerability keeps damage high as Strength ramps).
+**Synergies:** Artifact stripping (each debuff consumes 1 charge separately), [[cards/Spot Weakness]] (Vulnerable amplifies [[buffs/Strength]]-boosted attacks), [[cards/Demon Form]] (sustained Vulnerability keeps damage high as Strength ramps).
**Matchups:** Spheric Guardian -- strips 2 of 3 Artifact charges in one play.
heuristics/cards/vigilance.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Vigilance
-Watcher's primary Calm entry and Wrath exit. Provides block and enters Calm for the energy loop.
+[[characters/Watcher]]'s primary Calm entry and Wrath exit. Provides block and enters Calm for the energy loop.
**When to play:**
- Emergency Wrath exit when enemy is about to attack (most critical use).
@@ -9,8 +9,8 @@ Watcher's primary Calm entry and Wrath exit. Provides block and enters Calm for
**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 8->12 block makes it significantly better for surviving Inferno-type multi-hit attacks and provides a meaningful buffer when exiting Wrath.
-**Play order (CRITICAL):** If Flurry of Blows is in hand, play it BEFORE Vigilance. The stance change (entering Calm) triggers Flurry's return from discard.
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** If [[cards/Flurry of Blows]] is in hand, play it BEFORE Vigilance. The stance change (entering Calm) triggers Flurry's return from discard.
**Exits Wrath safely:** Without an exit from Wrath, doubled incoming damage is lethal. Vigilance is the Watcher's primary safety valve. Always know where your Wrath exit is before entering Wrath.
-**Enters Calm:** Gain +2 energy when you later leave Calm (via Eruption, Crescendo, or other Wrath-entry cards). This is the setup half of the Calm energy loop.
+**Enters Calm:** Gain +2 energy when you later leave Calm (via [[cards/Eruption]], [[cards/Crescendo]], or other Wrath-entry cards). This is the setup half of the Calm energy loop.
heuristics/cards/wave-of-the-hand.md
@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
# Wave of the Hand
-Play FIRST before any block cards. Converts block generation into Weak application for the rest of the turn.
+Play FIRST before any block cards. Converts block generation into [[debuffs/Weak]] application for the rest of the turn.
**When to play:** FIRST, before any block-generating cards. It must be active before block is gained to trigger Weak. Playing it after block cards wastes the triggers.
**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 1->2 Weak per trigger doubles the Weak output. With 2-3 block sources per turn, this is 4-6 Weak instead of 2-3.
-**Multiple triggers:** Every block source triggers a separate Weak application. Wave of the Hand + 2 Defends = 2 Weak applications. Wave + Vigilance + Halt = 3 applications.
+**Multiple triggers:** Every block source triggers a separate Weak application. Wave of the Hand + 2 Defends = 2 Weak applications. Wave + [[cards/Vigilance]] + Halt = 3 applications.
-**Just Lucky synergy:** Just Lucky gives 2-3 block, triggering Wave. Free Weak application from a 0E card.
+**[[cards/Just Lucky]] synergy:** Just Lucky gives 2-3 block, triggering Wave. Free Weak application from a 0E card.
-**Hexaghost Inferno:** Play Wave before block cards on the Inferno turn. Each block card applies Weak, reducing Inferno's multi-hit damage significantly.
+**[[bosses/Hexaghost]] Inferno:** Play Wave before block cards on the Inferno turn. Each block card applies Weak, reducing Inferno's multi-hit damage significantly.
-**Draft priority:** Medium-high. The Watcher's primary Weak source. Weak is critical for surviving Inferno and large attacks, so Wave fills a mandatory slot in boss preparation.
+**Draft priority:** Medium-high. The [[characters/Watcher]]'s primary Weak source. Weak is critical for surviving Inferno and large attacks, so Wave fills a mandatory slot in boss preparation.
heuristics/cards/whirlwind.md
@@ -4,10 +4,10 @@ AOE that scales with energy. Uses ALL remaining energy.
**When to play:** Play LAST -- uses all remaining energy automatically. You CANNOT specify X manually. To control X, spend energy on other cards first, then Whirlwind last.
-**Synergies:** Extra energy (Lantern, Seeing Red+, Berserk), Strength (each hit gains bonus).
+**Synergies:** Extra energy ([[relics/Lantern]], [[cards/Seeing Red]], [[cards/Berserk]]), [[buffs/Strength]] (each hit gains bonus).
**Upgrade priority:** Significant. 5->8 per hit.
-**Matchups:** Slime Boss split -- hits both slimes. Sentries -- 15 damage to all 3. Gremlin Leader -- clears summoned gremlins.
+**Matchups:** [[bosses/Slime Boss]] split -- hits both slimes. Sentries -- 15 damage to all 3. [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] -- clears summoned gremlins.
-**Anti-synergy:** Snake Plant Malleable -- each hit triggers Malleable, granting increasing block. Do NOT use against enemies with Malleable. Use single large hits instead.
+**Anti-synergy:** [[enemies/Snake Plant]] [[buffs/Malleable]] -- each hit triggers Malleable, granting increasing block. Do NOT use against enemies with Malleable. Use single large hits instead.
heuristics/cards/wraith-form.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Wraith Form
-2-3 turns of Intangible (take only 1 damage) at the cost of permanent cumulative -1 Dex/turn.
+2-3 turns of [[buffs/Intangible]] (take only 1 damage) at the cost of permanent cumulative -1 Dex/turn.
**Short fights (hallway, 4-6 turns):** Play freely. 2-3 turns of Intangible trivializes most hallway fights. Dex penalty barely matters.
@@ -8,8 +8,8 @@
**Long fights (bosses, 15+ turns):** DO NOT PLAY EARLY. Playing Turn 2 means Dex -11 by Turn 13. At -7 Dex, ALL Skill-based block becomes 0. FUNDAMENTAL RULE: do NOT play until the enemy is within 5 turns of dying.
-**Two confirmed deaths from early WF:** Dex reached -11 (died 5 HP short vs Collector). Dex reached -7 (died 8 damage short of Mode Shift vs Guardian).
+**Two confirmed deaths from early WF:** Dex reached -11 (died 5 HP short vs Collector). Dex reached -7 (died 8 damage short of [[buffs/Mode Shift]] vs Guardian).
-**Interactions:** After Image -- becomes ONLY reliable block source at high negative Dex. Blur -- critical companion (block from Intangible turns carries to post-Intangible). Footwork+ -- partially offsets drain but insufficient in 19-turn fights.
+**Interactions:** [[cards/After Image]] -- becomes ONLY reliable block source at high negative Dex. [[cards/Blur]] -- critical companion (block from Intangible turns carries to post-Intangible). [[cards/Footwork]] -- partially offsets drain but insufficient in 19-turn fights.
**Collector boss warning:** Fight can last 15-20 turns. Hold Wraith Form until Collector is below 50 HP or within 5-6 turns of dying.
heuristics/cards/wreath-of-flame.md
@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@
Grants Vigor (flat bonus damage) to the next Attack played. Persists across turns until consumed.
-**When to play:** Before your biggest single-hit attack (Smite, Eruption) for maximum value. On free turns (enemy not attacking), play to bank Vigor for the next turn's attacks.
+**When to play:** Before your biggest single-hit attack (Smite, [[cards/Eruption]]) for maximum value. On free turns (enemy not attacking), play to bank Vigor for the next turn's attacks.
**Upgrade priority:** Medium. 5->8 additional damage. The difference is meaningful when doubled in Wrath (10->16 bonus).
heuristics/cards/zap.md
@@ -2,8 +2,8 @@
Free orb channel every time it is drawn. Lightning orb deals 3 passive damage at end of turn (or 8 on evoke).
-**When to play:** Always play when drawn -- it costs 0E and generates value. The only exception is Gremlin Nob, where Zap is a Skill and triggers Enrage (+2 Str).
+**When to play:** Always play when drawn -- it costs 0E and generates value. The only exception is [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], where Zap is a Skill and triggers [[buffs/Enrage]] (+2 Str).
-**Upgrade priority:** Low compared to other Defect cards. No functional change.
+**Upgrade priority:** Low compared to other [[characters/Defect]] cards. No functional change.
**Nob warning:** Zap is a Skill card. Defect's starting deck has 7 Skills vs 3 Attacks -- Zap being a Skill is a key reason Nob is the Defect's worst elite matchup.
heuristics/characters/defect.md
@@ -3,23 +3,23 @@
## Identity
The Defect scales through Focus and orb manipulation. One proven winning engine:
-1. **Echo Form+ orb engine:** Echo Form+ + Seek+ (Bottled Lightning) + Coolheaded+ + Barrage+ + Mummified Hand
+1. **[[cards/Echo Form]] orb engine:** Echo Form+ + [[cards/Seek]] ([[relics/Bottled Lightning]]) + [[cards/Coolheaded]] + [[cards/Barrage]] + [[relics/Mummified Hand]]
## Key Heuristics
-- **Focus is everything.** Defragment is the #1 priority card — equivalent of Inflame for Ironclad. Without Focus, orb passives are too weak for Act 2.
+- **Focus is everything.** [[cards/Defragment]] is the #1 priority card — equivalent of [[cards/Inflame]] for [[characters/Ironclad]]. Without Focus, orb passives are too weak for [[acts/Act 2]].
- **Frost orbs replace Defend cards.** With Focus 3+, each Frost orb gives 5 passive block/turn. Three Frost orbs = 15 free block/turn.
- **Channel order matters.** Leftmost orb evokes first. Channel damage orbs you want to evoke (Lightning) before defensive orbs you want to keep (Frost).
-- **Fission MUST be upgraded.** Unupgraded removes orbs without evoking. Upgraded evokes all orbs AND gives Energy + draws. Critical distinction.
+- **[[cards/Fission]] MUST be upgraded.** Unupgraded removes orbs without evoking. Upgraded evokes all orbs AND gives Energy + draws. Critical distinction.
## Weaknesses
-- **Worst Gremlin Nob matchup.** 6 Skills vs 4 Attacks in starting deck. Zap and Dualcast are both Skills. Do NOT path into Nob without picking up Attack cards.
-- **Cold Snap is the best Nob card** — Attack that also channels Frost (orb passives don't trigger Enrage).
-- **Echo Form copies count toward Time Eater's counter.** Each doubled card = 2 toward the 12-card limit. Plan accordingly.
+- **Worst [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] matchup.** 6 Skills vs 4 Attacks in starting deck. Zap and [[cards/Dualcast]] are both Skills. Do NOT path into Nob without picking up Attack cards.
+- **[[cards/Cold Snap]] is the best Nob card** — Attack that also channels Frost (orb passives don't trigger [[buffs/Enrage]]).
+- **Echo Form copies count toward [[bosses/Time Eater]]'s counter.** Each doubled card = 2 toward the 12-card limit. Plan accordingly.
## Deck Building
-- Prioritize Attack card pickups in first 3-4 floors (Compile Driver, Sweeping Beam, Cold Snap, Ball Lightning).
-- Mummified Hand offsets the cost of 3E Powers (Echo Form, Capacitor, Machine Learning).
+- Prioritize Attack card pickups in first 3-4 floors ([[cards/Compile Driver]], [[cards/Sweeping Beam]], Cold Snap, [[cards/Ball Lightning]]).
+- Mummified Hand offsets the cost of 3E Powers (Echo Form, [[cards/Capacitor]], [[cards/Machine Learning]]).
- Seek+ with Bottled Lightning guarantees finding your key card Turn 1 of every fight.
heuristics/characters/ironclad.md
@@ -2,23 +2,23 @@
## Identity
-The Ironclad's power comes from Strength scaling and exhaust synergies. Two proven winning engines:
-1. **Strength engine:** Snecko Eye + Limit Break + Heavy Blade + Immolate+ for AOE
-2. **Block engine:** Barricade + Corruption + Feel No Pain + Entrench + Body Slam+
+The Ironclad's power comes from [[buffs/Strength]] scaling and exhaust synergies. Two proven winning engines:
+1. **Strength engine:** [[relics/Snecko Eye]] + [[cards/Limit Break]] + [[cards/Heavy Blade]] + [[cards/Immolate]] for AOE
+2. **Block engine:** [[cards/Barricade]] + [[cards/Corruption]] + [[cards/Feel No Pain]] + [[cards/Entrench]] + [[cards/Body Slam]]
## Key Heuristics
-- **Burning Blood buffer:** At full HP, up to 6 damage is "free" in hallway fights. Below max HP, this buffer doesn't exist.
-- **Healing cards are mandatory:** Reaper is the most important card in the game for Ironclad. Take it over almost anything. Feed is #2.
-- **Strength resets between combats.** Only Vajra persists. Don't carry Strength expectations between fights.
-- **Act 1 tier list exists** — see heuristics/strategy.md for the full list.
+- **[[relics/Burning Blood]] buffer:** At full HP, up to 6 damage is "free" in hallway fights. Below max HP, this buffer doesn't exist.
+- **Healing cards are mandatory:** [[cards/Reaper]] is the most important card in the game for Ironclad. Take it over almost anything. Feed is #2.
+- **Strength resets between combats.** Only [[relics/Vajra]] persists. Don't carry Strength expectations between fights.
+- **[[acts/Act 1]] tier list exists** — see heuristics/strategy.md for the full list.
## Deck Building
-Pursue whichever engine the offerings support. If Barricade + Corruption + FNP appear by mid-Act 2, commit to block. If Snecko Eye is offered as Act 2 boss relic and Limit Break exists, commit to Strength.
+Pursue whichever engine the offerings support. If Barricade + Corruption + FNP appear by mid-[[acts/Act 2]], commit to block. If [[enemies/Snecko]] Eye is offered as Act 2 boss relic and Limit Break exists, commit to Strength.
## Weaknesses
- Skill-heavy starting deck is balanced (5 Attacks) but Nob is still dangerous without additional Attack pickups.
-- No innate Weak source (depends on Bash for Vulnerable, needs Shockwave/Clothesline for Weak).
-- Self-damage cards (Offering, Hemokinesis, Brutality) are powerful but compound with HP attrition if not managed.
+- No innate [[debuffs/Weak]] source (depends on [[cards/Bash]] for [[debuffs/Vulnerable]], needs [[cards/Shockwave]]/[[cards/Clothesline]] for Weak).
+- Self-damage cards ([[cards/Offering]], [[cards/Hemokinesis]], [[cards/Brutality]]) are powerful but compound with HP attrition if not managed.
heuristics/characters/silent.md
@@ -3,23 +3,23 @@
## Identity
The Silent wins through many small card plays with stacked multipliers, not single massive hits. Two proven winning engines:
-1. **Shiv burst:** Accuracy x2 + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer
-2. **Dead Branch exhaust:** Dead Branch + Terror + Storm of Steel + After Image + Footwork
+1. **[[cards/Shiv]] burst:** [[cards/Accuracy]] x2 + [[cards/After Image]] + [[cards/Wraith Form]] + [[cards/Phantasmal Killer]]
+2. **[[relics/Dead Branch]] exhaust:** Dead Branch + [[cards/Terror]] + [[cards/Storm of Steel]] + After Image + [[cards/Footwork]]
## Key Heuristics
-- **Ring of the Snake makes Turn 1 strong.** 7-card opening hand gives more options than other characters. Use Turn 1 aggressively.
-- **Neutralize is free Weak.** 0E Attack — always play it. Safe against Nob (Attack card).
-- **Poison scales passively.** Once applied, poison does its damage without further card plays. Good for fights where you need to block heavily.
-- **Shivs are card plays.** Each Shiv counts toward Time Eater's 12-card limit, Velvet Choker's 6-card limit, etc.
+- **[[relics/Ring of the Snake]] makes Turn 1 strong.** 7-card opening hand gives more options than other characters. Use Turn 1 aggressively.
+- **[[cards/Neutralize]] is free [[debuffs/Weak]].** 0E Attack — always play it. Safe against Nob (Attack card).
+- **[[debuffs/Poison]] scales passively.** Once applied, poison does its damage without further card plays. Good for fights where you need to block heavily.
+- **Shivs are card plays.** Each Shiv counts toward [[bosses/Time Eater]]'s 12-card limit, [[relics/Velvet Choker]]'s 6-card limit, etc.
## Weaknesses
-- Most Skill-heavy early deck (9+ Skills vs 5 Attacks). Gremlin Nob is extremely punishing.
-- No innate Strength scaling. Relies on Accuracy (for Shivs) or Poison for damage scaling.
+- Most Skill-heavy early deck (9+ Skills vs 5 Attacks). [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] is extremely punishing.
+- No innate [[buffs/Strength]] scaling. Relies on Accuracy (for Shivs) or Poison for damage scaling.
- Wraith Form is a finisher, not early defense. -1 Dex/turn is permanent and devastating in long fights.
## Boss Matchups
- **Time Eater:** Shivs with Accuracy deal massive damage per card play, making the 12-card counter manageable.
-- **Awakened One:** Dead Branch + exhaust engine beats it by dumping Powers in Phase 1 (Str resets) and playing zero Powers in Phase 2.
+- **[[bosses/Awakened One]]:** Dead Branch + exhaust engine beats it by dumping Powers in Phase 1 (Str resets) and playing zero Powers in Phase 2.
heuristics/characters/watcher.md
@@ -3,10 +3,10 @@
## The Energy Loop
The core engine is cycling between Calm and Wrath:
-1. Enter Calm (Vigilance, Inner Peace, Meditate).
-2. Next turn, exit Calm into Wrath (Eruption, Crescendo). Gain +2 Energy from leaving Calm.
+1. Enter Calm ([[cards/Vigilance]], [[cards/Inner Peace]], [[cards/Meditate]]).
+2. Next turn, exit Calm into Wrath ([[cards/Eruption]], [[cards/Crescendo]]). Gain +2 Energy from leaving Calm.
3. Play attacks in Wrath at doubled damage using the bonus energy.
-4. Exit Wrath before end of turn (Vigilance, Inner Peace, Empty Body, Fear No Evil).
+4. Exit Wrath before end of turn (Vigilance, Inner Peace, [[cards/Empty Body]], [[cards/Fear No Evil]]).
5. Repeat.
With Eruption+ (1E), entering Wrath from Calm costs net -1E. The Watcher effectively gets 2 free energy per cycle compared to no-stance play.
@@ -25,17 +25,17 @@ Consider:
## Stance Sequencing
-Play cards that trigger on stance change BEFORE changing stance. Flurry of Blows must be in the discard pile before the stance change for the trigger to work. Check individual card files for play-order rules.
+Play cards that trigger on stance change BEFORE changing stance. [[cards/Flurry of Blows]] must be in the discard pile before the stance change for the trigger to work. Check individual card files for play-order rules.
## Key Cards
- **Flurry of Blows** (0E, returns from discard on stance change) — best common card. Free damage on every cycle. Take multiples.
- **Eruption+** — highest priority upgrade. 2E→1E makes the Calm loop net positive.
-- **Battle Hymn** (Power, generates Smite each turn) — best sustained damage for boss fights.
-- **Rushdown** (Power, draw 2-3 on Wrath entry) — enables massive burst turns.
-- **Wave of the Hand** (block gain applies Weak) — primary Weak source.
-- **Talk to the Hand** (Block Return, Exhaust) — excellent against multi-hit enemies.
-- **Judgment** (instant kill at 30/40 HP) — powerful finisher.
+- **[[cards/Battle Hymn]]** (Power, generates Smite each turn) — best sustained damage for boss fights.
+- **[[cards/Rushdown]]** (Power, draw 2-3 on Wrath entry) — enables massive burst turns.
+- **[[cards/Wave of the Hand]]** (block gain applies [[debuffs/Weak]]) — primary Weak source.
+- **[[cards/Talk to the Hand]]** (Block Return, Exhaust) — excellent against multi-hit enemies.
+- **[[cards/Judgment]]** (instant kill at 30/40 HP) — powerful finisher.
## Boss Preparation
heuristics/combat.md
@@ -6,7 +6,7 @@
Every turn:
-1. Read total incoming damage (sum all attacking enemies, apply Weak if on them).
+1. Read total incoming damage (sum all attacking enemies, apply [[debuffs/Weak]] if on them).
2. Enumerate ALL paths to zero damage:
- **Kill attackers** + block remainder
- **Pure block** (block cards, Weak on attacker)
@@ -49,7 +49,7 @@ CHOSEN: [A/B] because [reason]
### Draw Effects — CRITICAL
-**NEVER include cards after a draw card in a `turn()` sequence.** If a card draws (Backflip, Shrug It Off, Pommel Strike, Battle Trance, Offering, etc.), it MUST be the last card before `"end"`. Play it via `send()` and re-read state before continuing.
+**NEVER include cards after a draw card in a `turn()` sequence.** If a card draws ([[cards/Backflip]], [[cards/Shrug It Off]], [[cards/Pommel Strike]], [[cards/Battle Trance]], [[cards/Offering]], etc.), it MUST be the last card before `"end"`. Play it via `send()` and re-read state before continuing.
---
@@ -68,35 +68,35 @@ Before every turn, verify each of these eight items.
Check the + suffix on every card. Common errors:
- Shrug It Off+ = 11 block (not 8)
-- Flame Barrier+ = 16 block AND 6 counter-damage
-- Armaments+ = 5 block AND upgrades ALL cards in hand
-- Bash+ = 10 damage, 3 Vulnerable (not 2)
-- Intimidate+ = 2 Weak (not 1)
-- Spot Weakness = +3 Strength unupgraded / +4 Strength upgraded
+- [[cards/Flame Barrier]] = 16 block AND 6 counter-damage
+- [[cards/Armaments]] = 5 block AND upgrades ALL cards in hand
+- [[cards/Bash]] = 10 damage, 3 [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (not 2)
+- [[cards/Intimidate]] = 2 Weak (not 1)
+- [[cards/Spot Weakness]] = +3 [[buffs/Strength]] unupgraded / +4 Strength upgraded
### 3. Does my Strength reset?
-- **Inflame, Spot Weakness, Demon Form:** per-combat only, resets between fights.
-- **Vajra (relic):** permanent, persists across combats.
-- **Strength Potion:** temporary, lasts one combat.
-- **Rampage counter:** also resets between fights.
+- **[[cards/Inflame]], Spot Weakness, [[cards/Demon Form]]:** per-combat only, resets between fights.
+- **[[relics/Vajra]] (relic):** permanent, persists across combats.
+- **[[potions/Strength Potion]]:** temporary, lasts one combat.
+- **[[cards/Rampage]] counter:** also resets between fights.
- NEVER carry Strength values from a previous fight into the current one.
### 4. VERIFY KILL MATH BEFORE ENDING TURN
On kill turns, NEVER include `end` in the same command as the final attack. Play the attack, verify the kill, THEN end the turn separately.
-**Example fatal error:** played Iron Wave (13 damage) against a 20 HP Hexaghost and submitted `end` in the same command, when Headbutt (17 damage) was still in hand with 2 energy remaining.
+**Example fatal error:** played [[cards/Iron Wave]] (13 damage) against a 20 HP [[bosses/Hexaghost]] and submitted `end` in the same command, when [[cards/Headbutt]] (17 damage) was still in hand with 2 energy remaining.
### 5. X-COST CARDS USE ALL REMAINING ENERGY
-X-cost cards (Malaise, Whirlwind, Skewer) consume ALL remaining energy automatically. Always PLAY THEM LAST. To control the value of X, spend energy on other cards first.
+X-cost cards ([[cards/Malaise]], [[cards/Whirlwind]], [[cards/Skewer]]) consume ALL remaining energy automatically. Always PLAY THEM LAST. To control the value of X, spend energy on other cards first.
**Example fatal error:** intended Malaise X=1 but played it first with 4 energy available. It consumed all energy, leaving zero block, and died.
### 6. FOSSILIZED HELIX / BUFFER: BLOCK IS CONSUMED FIRST
-Fossilized Helix and Buffer prevent HP loss, not damage. Block is consumed BEFORE the prevention check. Against multi-hit attacks, this means Block is depleted by hit 1 (Helix/Buffer catches overflow), then hits 2+ land with no Block remaining.
+[[relics/Fossilized Helix]] and [[cards/Buffer]] prevent HP loss, not damage. Block is consumed BEFORE the prevention check. Against multi-hit attacks, this means Block is depleted by hit 1 (Helix/Buffer catches overflow), then hits 2+ land with no Block remaining.
**Wrong:** "Helix absorbs hit 1 entirely, my 5 Block handles hit 2." (Overestimates survivability)
**Right:** "Hit 1 (6) eats my 5 Block, Helix catches the 1 overflow. Hit 2 (6) lands in full." (5 HP worse)
@@ -105,11 +105,11 @@ When calculating damage against multi-hit enemies with Helix/Buffer, assume Bloc
### 7. WRAITH FORM: DO NOT PLAY UNTIL ENEMY IS WITHIN 5 TURNS OF DYING
-Wraith Form gives Intangible but applies a permanent -1 Dexterity per turn. Three recorded deaths from playing it too early. Estimate the number of turns remaining before playing. Wraith Form is a FINISHER, not an opener.
+[[cards/Wraith Form]] gives [[buffs/Intangible]] but applies a permanent -1 [[buffs/Dexterity]] per turn. Three recorded deaths from playing it too early. Estimate the number of turns remaining before playing. Wraith Form is a FINISHER, not an opener.
### 8. VERIFY CARD IDENTITY BEFORE PLAYING
-Before entering a `play N` command, confirm the card at index N matches the card in your plan. Hand indices shift after each play. Especially dangerous for 2E cards that look similar in reasoning (Bash vs Shockwave are both 2E and both target enemies, but Bash is single-target Attack damage while Shockwave is mass Weak+Vulnerable). Playing Bash when you mean Shockwave wastes 2E on minimal damage instead of critical debuffs.
+Before entering a `play N` command, confirm the card at index N matches the card in your plan. Hand indices shift after each play. Especially dangerous for 2E cards that look similar in reasoning (Bash vs [[cards/Shockwave]] are both 2E and both target enemies, but Bash is single-target Attack damage while Shockwave is mass Weak+Vulnerable). Playing Bash when you mean Shockwave wastes 2E on minimal damage instead of critical debuffs.
**Process:** After each card play, re-read the hand state. The card you intend to play next may have shifted index.
@@ -122,4 +122,4 @@ Play Shockwave+ on the **FIRST ATTACK TURN** of multi-enemy fights.
- **Turn 1 is NOT always correct:** if all enemies are buffing (e.g., Cultists on turn 1), Weak is wasted on a non-attack turn.
- **Turn 2 is usually correct:** most multi-enemy fights begin attacking on turn 2.
- **NEVER save for turn 4+:** by then enemies have already accumulated Strength and you have taken unnecessary damage.
-- **Priority on the first attack turn:** Shockwave+ (mass Weak + Vulnerable) > Reaper (healing) > single-target damage.
+- **Priority on the first attack turn:** Shockwave+ (mass Weak + Vulnerable) > [[cards/Reaper]] (healing) > single-target damage.
heuristics/drafting.md
@@ -1,19 +1,19 @@
# Drafting & Deck Building
-Card evaluation and deck construction heuristics for Ironclad. Every card pick should solve a problem, not add raw power.
+Card evaluation and deck construction heuristics for [[characters/Ironclad]]. Every card pick should solve a problem, not add raw power.
---
## Healing Card Priority (THE CRITICAL GAP)
-The single biggest strategic failure across 50 runs: entering Act 2 without a healing card. Burning Blood (+6/fight) heals 6% of max HP per fight. Act 2 fights drain 25-50 HP. The math does not work.
+The single biggest strategic failure across 50 runs: entering [[acts/Act 2]] without a healing card. [[relics/Burning Blood]] (+6/fight) heals 6% of max HP per fight. Act 2 fights drain 25-50 HP. The math does not work.
**Card reward priority when offered a healing card:**
-- **Reaper: TAKE IT.** Even over good damage/block cards. Best card in the game for Ironclad.
-- **Feed: TAKE IT** in early Act 1. +3-4 Max HP per kill compounds over 15+ fights.
-- If neither offered by Floor 12, seek healing potions (Blood Potion, Regen Potion) and healing relics (Toy Ornithopter, Meal Ticket, Bloody Idol) at shops.
+- **[[cards/Reaper]]: TAKE IT.** Even over good damage/block cards. Best card in the game for Ironclad.
+- **Feed: TAKE IT** in early [[acts/Act 1]]. +3-4 Max HP per kill compounds over 15+ fights.
+- If neither offered by Floor 12, seek healing potions ([[potions/Blood Potion]], [[potions/Regen Potion]]) and healing relics ([[relics/Toy Ornithopter]], [[relics/Meal Ticket]], [[relics/Bloody Idol]]) at shops.
-**This is non-negotiable.** A deck with Reaper + adequate damage will reach Act 3. A deck with perfect damage/block but no healing will die in Act 2 floors 20-30.
+**This is non-negotiable.** A deck with Reaper + adequate damage will reach [[acts/Act 3]]. A deck with perfect damage/block but no healing will die in Act 2 floors 20-30.
**Reaper alone is NOT sufficient.** Reaper exhausts after a single use per fight. In consecutive combat rooms (common in Act 2), Reaper heals once per fight but cannot offset sustained drain across multiple fights. Multiple healing sources are required. **Minimum TWO healing sources by Act 2 Floor 5.**
@@ -23,10 +23,10 @@ The single biggest strategic failure across 50 runs: entering Act 2 without a he
Verify by Floor 15. ALL FOUR criteria or die in Act 2:
-1. **Front-loaded Strength** -- Inflame, Spot Weakness. NOT Demon Form alone (too slow for hallway fights, confirmed in 2+ deaths). Demon Form fills the boss scaling role but does NOT satisfy this criterion. Without front-loaded Str, Reaper healing is also ineffective (Reaper needs Str on the turn it is played, not 3 turns later).
-2. **AOE damage** -- Thunderclap, Cleave, Immolate, Whirlwind. Required for Gremlin Leader (gremlins re-summon), 3 Cultists (hard-scaler, kill speed matters), Slime Boss split.
+1. **Front-loaded [[buffs/Strength]]** -- [[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Spot Weakness]]. NOT [[cards/Demon Form]] alone (too slow for hallway fights, confirmed in 2+ deaths). Demon Form fills the boss scaling role but does NOT satisfy this criterion. Without front-loaded Str, Reaper healing is also ineffective (Reaper needs Str on the turn it is played, not 3 turns later).
+2. **AOE damage** -- [[cards/Thunderclap]], [[cards/Cleave]], [[cards/Immolate]], [[cards/Whirlwind]]. Required for [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] (gremlins re-summon), 3 Cultists (hard-scaler, kill speed matters), [[bosses/Slime Boss]] split.
3. **Healing beyond Burning Blood** -- Reaper, Feed, potions/relics. Burning Blood (+6/fight) cannot offset 30-50 HP Act 2 fights.
-4. **Block scaling beyond basic Defends** -- Shrug It Off, Flame Barrier, Metallicize, Impervious. Basic Defends provide only 20 block from a full hand of 4. Act 2 boss Hyper Beam deals 45-57 damage. Even late Act 2 hallway fights (Book of Stabbing turn 5: 6x6=36) outscale basic Defends.
+4. **Block scaling beyond basic Defends** -- [[cards/Shrug It Off]], [[cards/Flame Barrier]], [[cards/Metallicize]], [[cards/Impervious]]. Basic Defends provide only 20 block from a full hand of 4. Act 2 boss Hyper Beam deals 45-57 damage. Even late Act 2 hallway fights ([[enemies/Book of Stabbing]] turn 5: 6x6=36) outscale basic Defends.
**If missing 2+ entering Act 2, shift to conservative pathing** -- avoid elites, path through shops and rest sites, skip unknown rooms.
@@ -43,42 +43,42 @@ The starting deck is nearly identical every run. Use a tier list, not first-prin
- Immolate (massive AOE + single target, solves multiple problems)
- Impervious (30 block in one card, solves every block problem)
- Shrug It Off (block + draw, best defensive common)
-- Pommel Strike (damage + draw, best offensive common)
+- [[cards/Pommel Strike]] (damage + draw, best offensive common)
**HIGH PRIORITY** (take to fill gaps):
- Spot Weakness (+3/+4 Str when enemy attacks -- most enemies attack)
-- Thunderclap (AOE + Vulnerable, critical for Byrds in Act 2)
-- Headbutt (damage + deck manipulation)
-- Carnage (high damage Ethereal attack -- great for Nob-safe damage)
-- Iron Wave (damage + block in one card)
-- Clothesline (damage + Weak)
-- Shockwave (mass Weak + Vulnerable, exhausts -- incredible vs multi-enemy)
+- Thunderclap (AOE + [[debuffs/Vulnerable]], critical for Byrds in Act 2)
+- [[cards/Headbutt]] (damage + deck manipulation)
+- [[cards/Carnage]] (high damage Ethereal attack -- great for Nob-safe damage)
+- [[cards/Iron Wave]] (damage + block in one card)
+- [[cards/Clothesline]] (damage + [[debuffs/Weak]])
+- [[cards/Shockwave]] (mass Weak + Vulnerable, exhausts -- incredible vs multi-enemy)
- Flame Barrier (block + counter damage, excellent in multi-hit fights)
**SITUATIONAL** (take only if it fills a specific gap):
-- Evolve (MUST-TAKE if Slime Boss is the Act 1 boss -- trivializes Slimed cards)
-- Fire Breathing (MUST-TAKE if Slime Boss AND you have Evolve)
-- Uppercut (Weak + Vulnerable in one card, but 2E is expensive)
-- Perfected Strike (only if 3+ Strike cards remain in deck)
-- Anger (free attack, good for Nob, but adds copies to discard)
-- Rampage (scaling damage, needs multiple plays to ramp)
-- True Grit (ONLY if you can upgrade it soon -- unupgraded random exhaust is dangerous)
-- Sentinel (block + energy on exhaust)
+- [[cards/Evolve]] (MUST-TAKE if Slime Boss is the Act 1 boss -- trivializes [[cards/Slimed]] cards)
+- [[cards/Fire Breathing]] (MUST-TAKE if Slime Boss AND you have Evolve)
+- [[cards/Uppercut]] (Weak + Vulnerable in one card, but 2E is expensive)
+- [[cards/Perfected Strike]] (only if 3+ Strike cards remain in deck)
+- [[cards/Anger]] (free attack, good for Nob, but adds copies to discard)
+- [[cards/Rampage]] (scaling damage, needs multiple plays to ramp)
+- [[cards/True Grit]] (ONLY if you can upgrade it soon -- unupgraded random exhaust is dangerous)
+- [[cards/Sentinel]] (block + energy on exhaust)
**SKIP** (do not take in Act 1):
- Demon Form (too slow for hallway fights, does not satisfy front-loaded Str)
-- Limit Break (needs a Str source first)
-- Barricade (3E, too slow, no immediate value)
-- Brutality (self-damage in long fights)
-- Berserk (self-Vulnerable is extremely dangerous)
+- [[cards/Limit Break]] (needs a Str source first)
+- [[cards/Barricade]] (3E, too slow, no immediate value)
+- [[cards/Brutality]] (self-damage in long fights)
+- [[cards/Berserk]] (self-Vulnerable is extremely dangerous)
**Boss-specific overrides (APPLY BEFORE tier list):**
- Slime Boss visible: Evolve and Fire Breathing become MUST-TAKES
-- Hexaghost visible: Weak sources (Shockwave, Clothesline, Intimidate) become MUST-TAKES. A Weak source outranks ANY generic tier list pick -- take Clothesline over Pommel Strike, take Intimidate over Iron Wave, etc. Without Weak, Hexaghost's Inferno and Tackles deal full damage for 16+ turns. No amount of block cards compensates.
-- The Guardian visible: Burst damage for Mode Shift (Carnage, Bludgeon)
+- [[bosses/Hexaghost]] visible: Weak sources (Shockwave, Clothesline, [[cards/Intimidate]]) become MUST-TAKES. A Weak source outranks ANY generic tier list pick -- take Clothesline over Pommel Strike, take Intimidate over Iron Wave, etc. Without Weak, Hexaghost's Inferno and Tackles deal full damage for 16+ turns. No amount of block cards compensates.
+- [[bosses/The Guardian]] visible: Burst damage for [[buffs/Mode Shift]] (Carnage, [[cards/Bludgeon]])
**Elite-specific overrides:**
-- Gremlin Nob: Take attacks, not skills. Nob gains +2 Str per Skill you play.
+- [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]]: Take attacks, not skills. Nob gains +2 Str per Skill you play.
- Slavers in Act 2: AOE and mass debuff are critical for 3-enemy fight.
- Book of Stabbing in Act 2: Exhaust tools for Wound removal.
@@ -111,7 +111,7 @@ A mediocre card that fills a gap is better than a strong card that duplicates wh
## Strength Scaling Is Mandatory
-Spheric Guardian has Barricade -- its block never expires and grows 15-20 per defend cycle. At 0-1 Strength, Strikes deal 6-7 damage per play against 15-20 block gained per cycle. The fight is mathematically unwinnable without Strength scaling.
+[[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] has Barricade -- its block never expires and grows 15-20 per defend cycle. At 0-1 Strength, Strikes deal 6-7 damage per play against 15-20 block gained per cycle. The fight is mathematically unwinnable without Strength scaling.
**By Floor 15, the deck MUST have at least one Strength source.**
@@ -120,7 +120,7 @@ Priority when no Str source exists:
2. Spot Weakness (+3/+4 Str when enemy attacks, 1E) -- excellent but conditional
3. Limit Break (doubles current Str) -- only if another Str source exists to double
4. Demon Form (+2 Str/turn, 3E) -- too slow for hallway fights but works for bosses
-5. Vajra relic (+1 Str) -- passive, always on
+5. [[relics/Vajra]] relic (+1 Str) -- passive, always on
**Card reward priority when the deck has NO Strength scaling:**
Inflame > Spot Weakness > Limit Break (with source) > anything.
@@ -132,9 +132,9 @@ Inflame > Spot Weakness > Limit Break (with source) > anything.
Curses are removable at shops for 75g (except Parasite, which cannot be removed).
**When an event offers gold + a curse, check the map:**
-- **Shop within 2-3 floors:** Take the gold + curse, remove at shop. Net profit after 75g removal. Sssserpent (175g + Doubt curse) nets 100g profit.
+- **Shop within 2-3 floors:** Take the gold + curse, remove at shop. Net profit after 75g removal. [[events/Sssserpent]] (175g + Doubt curse) nets 100g profit.
- **No shop before boss/elite:** Refuse. A curse stuck in the deck for 5+ floors degrades draw quality and costs HP in hard fights.
-- **Parasite: Never take.** Cannot be removed at shops. No exceptions unless Omamori will negate it.
+- **Parasite: Never take.** Cannot be removed at shops. No exceptions unless [[relics/Omamori]] will negate it.
Evaluate the map context, not just the curse itself.
@@ -164,11 +164,11 @@ Single-purpose cards (Strike, Defend) are the weakest cards in the deck. When ch
## 3-Cost Power Setup Trap
-Corruption (3E), Barricade (3E), Demon Form (3E) consume ALL energy on a 3-energy turn. Playing them on Turn 1 against attacking enemies leaves zero energy for block. Two deaths confirmed from this pattern.
+[[cards/Corruption]] (3E), Barricade (3E), Demon Form (3E) consume ALL energy on a 3-energy turn. Playing them on Turn 1 against attacking enemies leaves zero energy for block. Two deaths confirmed from this pattern.
**Only play 3E Powers when:**
- Enemy is buffing/defending (free turn with no incoming damage)
-- You have 4+ energy (from Lantern, Sozu, Offering, etc.), leaving energy for block
