+- You have 4+ energy (from [[relics/Lantern]], [[relics/Sozu]], [[cards/Offering]], etc.), leaving energy for block
- You upgraded the Power to 2E (Corruption+ is 2E, leaving 1E for Defend)
- It is a boss fight where spending HP for setup speed is acceptable
@@ -194,7 +194,7 @@ Against fast-scaling enemies at low HP (3 Cultists, multi-enemy fights), setup t
Activate by Floor 17 if no healing card has been acquired. Do not wait until HP is critical.
1. **Shop healing:** Buy Blood Potion or Regen Potion at every shop. Buy Toy Ornithopter or Meal Ticket if offered.
-2. **Event healing:** The Cleric heals. Big Fish offers healing. Woman in Blue sells potions. Prioritize these events.
+2. **Event healing:** [[events/The Cleric]] heals. [[events/Big Fish]] offers healing. [[events/Woman in Blue]] sells potions. Prioritize these events.
3. **Path through more rest sites:** Without healing cards, rest sites become the primary healing source. Accept fewer upgrades. Degraded strategy but better than dying.
4. **Potion management:** Treat Blood Potions and Regen Potions as precious resources. Do not use them in trivial fights.
5. **Conservative pathing:** Take safer paths, skip elites after Floor 15, avoid Unknown rooms below 50% HP.
heuristics/enemies/3-cultists.md
@@ -2,27 +2,27 @@
**THIS IS THE MOST LETHAL HALLWAY FIGHT IN THE GAME.** Six deaths across all runs -- more than any other single encounter. Deaths at 5%, 30%, 37%, 39%, 52%, and 53% HP entry.
-**Core rule:** Enter with 50%+ HP MINIMUM -- this is a hard threshold, not a guideline. Kill one Cultist ASAP to reduce scaling by 33%.
+**Core rule:** Enter with 50%+ HP MINIMUM -- this is a hard threshold, not a guideline. Kill one [[enemies/Cultist]] ASAP to reduce scaling by 33%.
## Strategy
-- Turn 1 is free -- use it for Powers (Metallicize, Inflame) and initial damage on one target
-- Turn 2-3: Play Shockwave+ on the FIRST ATTACK TURN for mass Weak + Vulnerable
+- Turn 1 is free -- use it for Powers ([[cards/Metallicize]], [[cards/Inflame]]) and initial damage on one target
+- Turn 2-3: Play [[cards/Shockwave]] on the FIRST ATTACK TURN for mass [[debuffs/Weak]] + [[debuffs/Vulnerable]]
- Focus fire one Cultist at a time -- each dead Cultist permanently removes one scaling threat
- Reducing from 3 to 2 cuts incoming by 33%; reducing to 1 makes it trivial
## Why Decks Fail Here
-- Slow scaling engines (Demon Form, Limit Break) need 2-3 free setup turns that do not exist at low HP
-- Even strong block tools (Impervious+, Calipers) only delay the inevitable if kill speed is insufficient
+- Slow scaling engines ([[cards/Demon Form]], [[cards/Limit Break]]) need 2-3 free setup turns that do not exist at low HP
+- Even strong block tools ([[cards/Impervious]], [[relics/Calipers]]) only delay the inevitable if kill speed is insufficient
- The fight demands BOTH burst damage AND sustained block for 4-6 turns simultaneously
## What to Bring
- Shockwave+ (MUST play on first attack turn)
- Powers for free turn setup (Inflame, Metallicize)
-- Single-target burst for focus fire (Rampage, Fiend Fire, Iron Wave+)
-- Immediate block (Defend, Ghostly Armor)
+- Single-target burst for focus fire ([[cards/Rampage]], [[cards/Fiend Fire]], [[cards/Iron Wave]])
+- Immediate block (Defend, [[cards/Ghostly Armor]])
## What to Avoid
heuristics/enemies/3-shapes.md
@@ -3,8 +3,8 @@
## Kill Priority
1. **Exploders FIRST** -- Detonate on Turn 3 for 30 damage each. Must die before detonation. Non-negotiable.
-2. **Repulsors SECOND** -- Dazed clogging degrades hand quality. Low HP makes them easy kills.
-3. **Spikers LAST** -- Thorns are annoying but direct damage (7/turn) is low. Poison bypasses Thorns entirely.
+2. **Repulsors SECOND** -- [[cards/Dazed]] clogging degrades hand quality. Low HP makes them easy kills.
+3. **Spikers LAST** -- Thorns are annoying but direct damage (7/turn) is low. [[debuffs/Poison]] bypasses Thorns entirely.
## Strategy
@@ -15,6 +15,6 @@
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Poison is the best tool. Kills Spikers without triggering Thorns and provides passive damage on all targets. Blade Dance / Dagger Spray for fast Exploder kills. Noxious Fumes hits all 3 passively. Avoid using Shivs on Spikers with high Thorns.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** Poison is the best tool. Kills Spikers without triggering Thorns and provides passive damage on all targets. [[cards/Blade Dance]] / [[cards/Dagger Spray]] for fast Exploder kills. [[cards/Noxious Fumes]] hits all 3 passively. Avoid using Shivs on Spikers with high Thorns.
**Danger Level:** Medium. Exploders are the primary threat. Without them, low-risk. With 2 Exploders, the Turn 3 timer creates urgency.
heuristics/enemies/acid-slime-l.md
@@ -4,8 +4,8 @@
## Strategy
-- In the Slime Boss fight, going from 2 enemies to 3 unless both large slimes are killed in quick succession
-- Weak reduces YOUR damage output, making it harder to finish off remaining enemies
+- In the [[bosses/Slime Boss]] fight, going from 2 enemies to 3 unless both large slimes are killed in quick succession
+- [[debuffs/Weak]] reduces YOUR damage output, making it harder to finish off remaining enemies
- Burst to kill before Weak stacks become crippling
## What to Bring

+**Danger Level:** Low. Below-average threat for [[acts/Act 1]].
heuristics/enemies/book-of-stabbing.md
@@ -6,11 +6,11 @@
## Strategy
-- Apply Vulnerable immediately
-- Use exhaust tools for Wound removal (True Grit+, Burning Pact, Second Wind, Fiend Fire)
+- Apply [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] immediately
+- Use exhaust tools for Wound removal ([[cards/True Grit]], [[cards/Burning Pact]], [[cards/Second Wind]], [[cards/Fiend Fire]])
- Enter with HIGH HP (60%+ minimum)
- Deck size matters: a 15-card deck survives Wound clog better than a 21-card deck
-- Weak and Piercing Wail are extremely valuable but temporary
+- [[debuffs/Weak]] and [[cards/Piercing Wail]] are extremely valuable but temporary
## Deck Requirements
@@ -18,7 +18,7 @@ If your deck has no exhaust tools (True Grit+, Burning Pact, Second Wind, Fiend
## Brimstone Interaction (CRITICAL)
-If you have Brimstone, DO NOT fight Book of Stabbing. Brimstone gives Book +2 Str/turn on TOP of its native scaling. By turn 5, 6+ hits at 17+ damage each = 100+ total incoming. Hard avoid.
+If you have [[relics/Brimstone]], DO NOT fight Book of Stabbing. Brimstone gives Book +2 Str/turn on TOP of its native scaling. By turn 5, 6+ hits at 17+ damage each = 100+ total incoming. Hard avoid.
## Turn Efficiency
@@ -30,9 +30,9 @@ Do not waste turns on setup that does not deal damage. Prioritize damage cards o
- Burst damage front-loaded in turns 1-3
- Vulnerable application
- Weak sources (reduce each individual hit)
-- Piercing Wail (Silent -- temporarily reduces all enemy Str)
-- Malaise (Silent -- semi-permanent Str reduction)
-- Poison scaling (Silent -- kills through block without needing to out-damage the Str scaling)
+- Piercing Wail ([[characters/Silent]] -- temporarily reduces all enemy Str)
+- [[cards/Malaise]] (Silent -- semi-permanent Str reduction)
+- [[debuffs/Poison]] scaling (Silent -- kills through block without needing to out-damage the Str scaling)
## What to Avoid
@@ -43,4 +43,4 @@ Do not waste turns on setup that does not deal damage. Prioritize damage cards o
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Dex stacking alone cannot keep up with scaling. Needs either: (1) burst poison (Deadly Poison + Catalyst+), (2) Malaise to permanently reduce Str, or (3) avoid the fight entirely. Shiv decks provide decent damage (~24-30/turn) but cannot block 30-54 incoming on turns 5-6.
+**Silent:** Dex stacking alone cannot keep up with scaling. Needs either: (1) burst poison ([[cards/Deadly Poison]] + [[cards/Catalyst]]), (2) Malaise to permanently reduce Str, or (3) avoid the fight entirely. [[cards/Shiv]] decks provide decent damage (~24-30/turn) but cannot block 30-54 incoming on turns 5-6.
heuristics/enemies/bronze-automaton.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Bronze Automaton (Enemy Entry)

heuristics/enemies/byrd.md
@@ -1,42 +1,42 @@
# Byrd
-**Core rule:** Thunderclap is CRITICAL. Without it, Flight 3 on three enemies means 9 hits just to strip all Flight once -- at half damage each.
+**Core rule:** [[cards/Thunderclap]] is CRITICAL. Without it, Flight 3 on three enemies means 9 hits just to strip all Flight once -- at half damage each.
**Benchmark:** With Thunderclap + burst: 7-turn fight, ~20 HP lost. Without AOE: 8-10 turns, 36+ HP lost. Worst case observed: 73 HP lost (74 to 1).
## Strategy
-- Use Thunderclap to strip 1 Flight from ALL Byrds and apply Vulnerable simultaneously
+- Use Thunderclap to strip 1 Flight from ALL Byrds and apply [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] simultaneously
- Focus fire one Byrd at a time -- going from 3 to 2 removes 33% of incoming damage
- Once Flight is at 0, burst with Vulnerable attacks to kill
- Expect 7-12 turn fights and 20-73 HP lost
## Engine Setup Trap
-Do NOT spend 3 turns setting up powers (Evolve + Metallicize + Fire Breathing + Inflame). Each setup turn costs 12-23 unblocked HP because Byrds attack for 10-25 per turn and a single Defend + Metallicize only blocks 8. A 3-turn setup burns 40-60 HP before dealing any damage. Instead, play at most 1 power per turn while blocking, or skip setup entirely if HP is below 60%. Kill speed matters more than engine value when entering below 70% HP.
+Do NOT spend 3 turns setting up powers ([[cards/Evolve]] + [[cards/Metallicize]] + [[cards/Fire Breathing]] + [[cards/Inflame]]). Each setup turn costs 12-23 unblocked HP because Byrds attack for 10-25 per turn and a single Defend + Metallicize only blocks 8. A 3-turn setup burns 40-60 HP before dealing any damage. Instead, play at most 1 power per turn while blocking, or skip setup entirely if HP is below 60%. Kill speed matters more than engine value when entering below 70% HP.
## Turn 2 Trap
-If all 3 Byrds attack simultaneously (after gaining Str from buff turns), combined damage can reach 38+. With only 1 block card in hand, there is no survival path. The deck needs block density (multiple Shrug It Off, Ghostly Armor, Impervious).
+If all 3 Byrds attack simultaneously (after gaining Str from buff turns), combined damage can reach 38+. With only 1 block card in hand, there is no survival path. The deck needs block density (multiple [[cards/Shrug It Off]], [[cards/Ghostly Armor]], [[cards/Impervious]]).
## What to Bring
- AOE (Thunderclap for Flight stripping + Vulnerable -- THE key card)
-- Burst damage (Carnage+ nearly one-shots a Byrd)
-- Multi-hit attacks (Twin Strike+ strips 2 Flight per play)
+- Burst damage ([[cards/Carnage]] nearly one-shots a Byrd)
+- Multi-hit attacks ([[cards/Twin Strike]] strips 2 Flight per play)
- Metallicize (essential for long fight)
-- Reaper (sustain healing)
+- [[cards/Reaper]] (sustain healing)
## What to Avoid
- Small single-hit attacks (halved by Flight and inefficient)
- Entering below 50% HP
-- Havoc or unupgraded True Grit (random exhaust can destroy key cards)
+- Havoc or unupgraded [[cards/True Grit]] (random exhaust can destroy key cards)
## Route Planning Warning
-If the map forces a Monster room immediately after a Byrd fight with no rest/shop/event option, the run may be lost. When planning Act 2 routes, ensure the first 2-3 floors include at least one non-combat room as a safety valve.
+If the map forces a Monster room immediately after a Byrd fight with no rest/shop/event option, the run may be lost. When planning [[acts/Act 2]] routes, ensure the first 2-3 floors include at least one non-combat room as a safety valve.
## Chosen+Byrd Fight
-Combines Flight mechanics with Hex (Skills add Dazed). Extremely dangerous -- need Attacks for Byrd Flight stripping but also need block from Skills, which trigger Hex. Enter at 50%+ HP minimum.
+Combines Flight mechanics with [[debuffs/Hex]] (Skills add [[cards/Dazed]]). Extremely dangerous -- need Attacks for Byrd Flight stripping but also need block from Skills, which trigger Hex. Enter at 50%+ HP minimum.
heuristics/enemies/centurion-mystic.md
@@ -1,13 +1,13 @@
# Centurion + Mystic
-**Core rule:** KILL MYSTIC FIRST. Non-negotiable. If you attack Centurion while Mystic is alive, you're in an unwinnable attrition war (Mystic heals all enemies 16 HP). Never split damage.
+**Core rule:** KILL MYSTIC FIRST. Non-negotiable. If you attack [[enemies/Centurion]] while [[enemies/Mystic]] is alive, you're in an unwinnable attrition war (Mystic heals all enemies 16 HP). Never split damage.
**Benchmark:** Kill Mystic in 5-6 turns. Expect 25-61 HP lost even with correct play. Enter with 60%+ HP.
## Strategy
- Focus all damage on Mystic despite Centurion being the bigger per-turn threat
-- Apply Weak to Centurion to reduce incoming while you kill Mystic
+- Apply [[debuffs/Weak]] to Centurion to reduce incoming while you kill Mystic
- Expect 6-12 turns total
- Do NOT spend all potions on the Mystic kill -- save at least one defensive resource for Centurion solo phase
@@ -19,18 +19,18 @@ Once Mystic is dead, Centurion uses the 6x3 multi-hit attack (18-30 damage). If
- Burst damage for Mystic (need to break through 15 block/turn)
- Weak for Centurion
-- Double Metallicize is excellent
-- Headbutt+ (find specific cards)
-- Stone Calendar (52 damage nuke on turn 7 lands automatically after Mystic is dead)
+- Double [[cards/Metallicize]] is excellent
+- [[cards/Headbutt]] (find specific cards)
+- [[relics/Stone Calendar]] (52 damage nuke on turn 7 lands automatically after Mystic is dead)
## What to Avoid
- Attacking Centurion while Mystic lives
- Entering below 50% HP (confirmed deaths at 49% and 71% entry)
- Wasting Fairy here when elites are ahead
-- Splitting damage between Centurion and Mystic even with temporary Strength buffs
+- Splitting damage between Centurion and Mystic even with temporary [[buffs/Strength]] buffs
- Spending ALL potions on the Mystic kill phase
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Poison + Dex stacking works but expect 24+ HP drain. Malaise on Centurion early (-2 Str, 2 Weak) neuters early attacks. Catalyst doubles poison for sustained kill. Piercing Wail+ on Centurion's 8x3 multi-hit is critical. Fight lasts ~10 turns. At 55% HP entry, survivable but leaves only 21% HP.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** [[debuffs/Poison]] + Dex stacking works but expect 24+ HP drain. [[cards/Malaise]] on Centurion early (-2 Str, 2 Weak) neuters early attacks. [[cards/Catalyst]] doubles poison for sustained kill. [[cards/Piercing Wail]] on Centurion's 8x3 multi-hit is critical. Fight lasts ~10 turns. At 55% HP entry, survivable but leaves only 21% HP.
heuristics/enemies/chosen.md
@@ -1,29 +1,29 @@
# Chosen
-**Core rule:** Minimize Skill usage after Hex is applied. Front-load damage with Attacks. Kill before Strength buffs make attacks lethal.
+**Core rule:** Minimize Skill usage after [[debuffs/Hex]] is applied. Front-load damage with Attacks. Kill before [[buffs/Strength]] buffs make attacks lethal.
**Benchmark:** Kill in 4-5 turns. Must enter with 40+ HP minimum. This fight routinely drains 30-40 HP.
## Kill Priority (Multi-Enemy Fights)
-**Kill Chosen FIRST.** Hex's Dazed flooding is CATASTROPHIC -- every Skill played after Turn 2 adds a Dazed to the draw pile, and by Turn 4-5 the deck is so diluted that critical turns draw 2-3 Dazed. Killing Chosen first stops Hex immediately.
+**Kill Chosen FIRST.** Hex's [[cards/Dazed]] flooding is CATASTROPHIC -- every Skill played after Turn 2 adds a Dazed to the draw pile, and by Turn 4-5 the deck is so diluted that critical turns draw 2-3 Dazed. Killing Chosen first stops Hex immediately.
-**Exception:** If the Cultist can be killed in 1-2 turns (Fire Potion burst, or it has <15 HP), killing it first is acceptable because Hex only applies for 1-2 turns.
+**Exception:** If the [[enemies/Cultist]] can be killed in 1-2 turns ([[potions/Fire Potion]] burst, or it has <15 HP), killing it first is acceptable because Hex only applies for 1-2 turns.
## Strategy
- Attacks and Powers are safe (do not trigger Hex)
- Play Defend only when absolutely necessary (each one adds Dazed)
-- Iron Wave is excellent (Attack that blocks)
-- Metallicize is excellent (passive block without triggering Hex -- Power, not a Skill)
+- [[cards/Iron Wave]] is excellent (Attack that blocks)
+- [[cards/Metallicize]] is excellent (passive block without triggering Hex -- Power, not a Skill)
- Kill speed is critical -- Strength scaling is lethal
## What to Bring
- Attack-heavy deck
- Iron Wave (Attack that blocks)
-- Inflame (Strength overcomes Weak penalty)
-- Bash+ (Attack, applies Vulnerable)
+- [[cards/Inflame]] (Strength overcomes [[debuffs/Weak]] penalty)
+- [[cards/Bash]] (Attack, applies [[debuffs/Vulnerable]])
- Metallicize (passive block without triggering Hex)
## What to Avoid
@@ -31,8 +31,8 @@
- Playing Defend unless absolutely necessary
- Slow kill (Strength scaling is lethal)
- Entering below 30 HP
-- Fiend Fire unless the burst will kill (exhausts ALL other cards in hand including defensive cards)
+- [[cards/Fiend Fire]] unless the burst will kill (exhausts ALL other cards in hand including defensive cards)
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Hex is disproportionately devastating. Silent decks are naturally Skill-heavy (Backflip, Footwork, Survivor, Deflect, Blur, Piercing Wail). Powers (Footwork, After Image, A Thousand Cuts) do NOT trigger Hex. Front-load damage with Attacks and Shivs. Use Piercing Wail Turn 1 BEFORE Hex is applied. Avoid curse-heavy decks (Du-Vu Doll strategy) when Hex encounters are likely.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** Hex is disproportionately devastating. Silent decks are naturally Skill-heavy ([[cards/Backflip]], [[cards/Footwork]], [[cards/Survivor]], [[cards/Deflect]], [[cards/Blur]], [[cards/Piercing Wail]]). Powers (Footwork, [[cards/After Image]], [[cards/A Thousand Cuts]]) do NOT trigger Hex. Front-load damage with Attacks and Shivs. Use Piercing Wail Turn 1 BEFORE Hex is applied. Avoid curse-heavy decks ([[relics/Du-Vu Doll]] strategy) when Hex encounters are likely.
heuristics/enemies/cultist.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Cultist
-**Core rule:** Go all-in Turn 1 (Bash + Strikes for 17+ damage). Kill by Turn 3-4 before attacks become dangerous.
+**Core rule:** Go all-in Turn 1 ([[cards/Bash]] + Strikes for 17+ damage). Kill by Turn 3-4 before attacks become dangerous.
**Turn 1 is free.** Cultist always uses Incantation. No damage incoming.
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@
## Strategy
-- Apply Vulnerable with Bash on Turn 1
+- Apply [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] with Bash on Turn 1
- Front-load burst attacks
- Do not let the fight go past Turn 4
@@ -23,4 +23,4 @@
## Act 2 Pairing (Cultist + Chosen)
-Kill Cultist first if it can die in 1-2 turns (lower HP, fast kill stops scaling). If Cultist will take 3+ turns, kill Chosen first to stop Hex from flooding the deck with Dazed.
+Kill Cultist first if it can die in 1-2 turns (lower HP, fast kill stops scaling). If Cultist will take 3+ turns, kill [[enemies/Chosen]] first to stop [[debuffs/Hex]] from flooding the deck with [[cards/Dazed]].
heuristics/enemies/darklings.md
@@ -1,17 +1,17 @@
# Darklings
-**Core rule:** AOE is mandatory. Must kill all 3 simultaneously or they revive at half HP via Life Link. Soften all 3 to kill range first, then use a single AOE play to finish all at once.
+**Core rule:** AOE is mandatory. Must kill all 3 simultaneously or they revive at half HP via [[buffs/Life Link]]. Soften all 3 to kill range first, then use a single AOE play to finish all at once.
## Strategy
-1. Use single-target attacks to bring each Darkling to low HP
+1. Use single-target attacks to bring each [[enemies/Darkling]] to low HP
2. Save AOE for the kill turn -- do not waste AOE early when Darklings are at full HP
-3. Build Strength, whittle them down, then deliver the killing AOE blow
+3. Build [[buffs/Strength]], whittle them down, then deliver the killing AOE blow
4. Simultaneous AOE kills bypass Life Link entirely
## What You Need
-- AOE damage (Immolate+, Whirlwind+, Cleave)
+- AOE damage ([[cards/Immolate]], [[cards/Whirlwind]], [[cards/Cleave]])
- Strength scaling to ensure the AOE kill hits hard enough
- Block density for the 4-6 turns of softening before the kill turn
- 50%+ HP entry recommended
heuristics/enemies/exploder.md
@@ -6,11 +6,11 @@
- Front-load all damage on Exploders (even at the cost of taking Slam damage, preventing the 30 explosion is correct)
- 30 HP is very low -- most decks can kill in 1-2 turns
-- Poison can work if applied heavily on Turn 1 (need 30+ total ticks in 2 turns)
+- [[debuffs/Poison]] can work if applied heavily on Turn 1 (need 30+ total ticks in 2 turns)
- Block the Explosion if you cannot kill in time (it does NOT bypass block)
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Deadly Poison+ (12 poison) on Turn 1 needs supplemental damage (12+11=23 by end of Turn 2, need ~7 more). Noxious Fumes alone is too slow. Best approach: direct attacks + Backstab for burst.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** [[cards/Deadly Poison]] (12 poison) on Turn 1 needs supplemental damage (12+11=23 by end of Turn 2, need ~7 more). [[cards/Noxious Fumes]] alone is too slow. Best approach: direct attacks + [[cards/Backstab]] for burst.
**Danger Level:** High if not killed quickly. Low if you have burst damage or AOE.
heuristics/enemies/fungi-beast.md
@@ -24,4 +24,4 @@
## Act 2 Pairing (Fungi Beast + Shelled Parasite)
-Kill Fungi Beast first to avoid giving Shelled Parasite a damage amplifier from Spore Cloud Vulnerability, but be prepared to block heavily on the turn it dies (Vulnerable from Spore Cloud + Shelled Parasite attack).
+Kill Fungi Beast first to avoid giving [[enemies/Shelled Parasite]] a damage amplifier from Spore Cloud Vulnerability, but be prepared to block heavily on the turn it dies ([[debuffs/Vulnerable]] from Spore Cloud + Shelled Parasite attack).
heuristics/enemies/giant-head.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Giant Head
-**Core rule:** This is a DPS race against escalating damage. Must have either Demon Form (Strength scaling) or Barricade + Body Slam (block scaling). Without either engine, the fight is unwinnable.
+**Core rule:** This is a DPS race against escalating damage. Must have either [[cards/Demon Form]] ([[buffs/Strength]] scaling) or [[cards/Barricade]] + [[cards/Body Slam]] (block scaling). Without either engine, the fight is unwinnable.
**HP Threshold:** Enter above 60% HP. The fight lasts 10+ turns with increasing damage.
@@ -8,24 +8,24 @@
- **Demon Form is critical.** Play Turn 1-2. At 500 HP, exponential Strength scaling is needed
- **Slow HELPS you** -- play many cards per turn to maximize damage
-- Immolate+ with high Strength is the best damage card
-- Entrench+ is essential for surviving high-damage turns
-- Impervious: save for turns where attacks spike past normal block capacity
-- Reaper is critical for sustain in this long fight
+- [[cards/Immolate]] with high Strength is the best damage card
+- [[cards/Entrench]] is essential for surviving high-damage turns
+- [[cards/Impervious]]: save for turns where attacks spike past normal block capacity
+- [[cards/Reaper]] is critical for sustain in this long fight
## Barricade Engine Approach
-Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam defeats Giant Head without Demon Form. Body Slam+ with 66-83 accumulated block deals massive single-card damage. The engine completed the kill in 8 turns with zero HP lost.
+Barricade + [[cards/Corruption]] + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam defeats Giant Head without Demon Form. Body Slam+ with 66-83 accumulated block deals massive single-card damage. The engine completed the kill in 8 turns with zero HP lost.
## Strategy (Silent)
-Poison bypasses Metallicize 8 entirely (ticks at end of enemy turn, ignoring block). Apply poison early, then triple with Catalyst+ for lethal total. The key math: tripling 16+ poison produces 48+ poison = 1176+ total damage over time.
+[[debuffs/Poison]] bypasses [[cards/Metallicize]] 8 entirely (ticks at end of enemy turn, ignoring block). Apply poison early, then triple with [[cards/Catalyst]] for lethal total. The key math: tripling 16+ poison produces 48+ poison = 1176+ total damage over time.
**Critical timing window:** After Catalyst+ triples poison, survive 2-3 more turns for poison to finish. Block aggressively. Use ALL available potions on survival turns -- a potion can be the difference between enemy dying this turn vs surviving at 1 HP.
## What NOT to Do
- Delay Demon Form past Turn 2-3
-- Fail to use offensive potions before the kill window closes (confirmed death: Giant Head survived at 1 HP with Strength Potion and Explosive Potion unused)
+- Fail to use offensive potions before the kill window closes (confirmed death: Giant Head survived at 1 HP with [[potions/Strength Potion]] and [[potions/Explosive Potion]] unused)
- Play purely defensively without damage scaling
- Enter below 60% HP
heuristics/enemies/gremlin-gang.md
@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
# Gremlin Gang
-**Kill priority order:** Sneaky (9 dmg/turn, glass cannon) > Wizard (25 dmg nuke on timer) > Fat (4 dmg + Weak) > Mad (4 dmg, scales with Angry) > Shield (7 block to ally, lowest threat).
+**Kill priority order:** Sneaky (9 dmg/turn, glass cannon) > Wizard (25 dmg nuke on timer) > Fat (4 dmg + [[debuffs/Weak]]) > Mad (4 dmg, scales with Angry) > Shield (7 block to ally, lowest threat).
## Strategy
-- Kill Sneaky Gremlin ASAP (9-10 damage every turn, two turns ignored = 18-20 HP lost)
-- AOE is excellent (All-Out Attack, Dagger Spray, Thunderclap hit all 4)
-- Noxious Fumes is strong (3 poison/turn on 4 enemies = 12 total poison/turn)
+- Kill [[enemies/Sneaky Gremlin]] ASAP (9-10 damage every turn, two turns ignored = 18-20 HP lost)
+- AOE is excellent ([[cards/All-Out Attack]], [[cards/Dagger Spray]], [[cards/Thunderclap]] hit all 4)
+- [[cards/Noxious Fumes]] is strong (3 poison/turn on 4 enemies = 12 total poison/turn)
- Focus fire on highest-priority target first
- Kill Wizard before its Ultimate Blast fires (25 damage)
@@ -15,4 +15,4 @@
- Ignore Sneaky Gremlin while setting up
- Spread damage evenly without killing anything
- Spend all energy on block when a Sneaky Gremlin has 3 HP (killing it prevents more damage than blocking it)
-- Spread incidental damage to Mad Gremlin (Angry makes it stronger when hit)
+- Spread incidental damage to [[enemies/Mad Gremlin]] (Angry makes it stronger when hit)
heuristics/enemies/gremlin-leader.md
@@ -6,15 +6,15 @@
## Turn 1 Priority
-Deal damage, NOT setup. Playing Inflame+ (1E setup) + Impervious (2E block) on Turn 1 means zero damage dealt -- this wastes the most valuable turn. The correct Turn 1 is Inflame+ + the best available Attack. Take the unblocked hit. HP lost on Turn 1 is recoverable; a wasted damage turn against Rally scaling is not.
+Deal damage, NOT setup. Playing [[cards/Inflame]] (1E setup) + [[cards/Impervious]] (2E block) on Turn 1 means zero damage dealt -- this wastes the most valuable turn. The correct Turn 1 is Inflame+ + the best available Attack. Take the unblocked hit. HP lost on Turn 1 is recoverable; a wasted damage turn against Rally scaling is not.
## Strategy
-- AOE to clear gremlins efficiently (Thunderclap, Whirlwind, Immolate+)
-- Shockwave+ early for mass Weak + Vulnerable
-- Kill Gremlin Wizard before it fires (28 damage nuke)
+- AOE to clear gremlins efficiently ([[cards/Thunderclap]], [[cards/Whirlwind]], [[cards/Immolate]])
+- [[cards/Shockwave]] early for mass [[debuffs/Weak]] + [[debuffs/Vulnerable]]
+- Kill [[enemies/Gremlin Wizard]] before it fires (28 damage nuke)
- Prioritize Leader damage when possible (gremlins get re-summoned)
-- Strength scaling is mandatory for the DPS race
+- [[buffs/Strength]] scaling is mandatory for the DPS race
## Strength Scaling is Mandatory
@@ -24,7 +24,7 @@ Without permanent Strength, you cannot kill the Leader fast enough. Evaluate whe
- AOE for gremlin clearing
- Shockwave+ (mass debuff)
-- Disarm (reduce Leader Str)
+- [[cards/Disarm]] (reduce Leader Str)
- Non-exhaust damage cards for sustained fighting
- Rage (0E power, generates block per attack)
- Potions -- this fight demands aggressive potion use
@@ -33,11 +33,11 @@ Without permanent Strength, you cannot kill the Leader fast enough. Evaluate whe
- Entering below 60% HP
- Entering without potions
-- Entering without permanent Strength scaling (Ironclad) or poison engine (Silent)
+- Entering without permanent Strength scaling ([[characters/Ironclad]]) or poison engine ([[characters/Silent]])
- Exhaust-heavy decks (once key cards are gone, remaining deck is too weak)
- Wasting turns -- do NOT batch end with draw effects (drawn cards need to be played)
- Bloated decks (19+ cards with 4+ Strikes) cannot draw answers fast enough
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Poison engine (Noxious Fumes + Bouncing Flask + Catalyst+) is excellent -- poison ignores Metallicize block all enemies gain. A Thousand Cuts provides passive AOE per card played. Apparitions are critical survival tools (5 Intangible turns). Piercing Wail reduces all enemies' Str for 1 turn. Must kill Leader before Apparitions run out.
+**Silent:** [[debuffs/Poison]] engine ([[cards/Noxious Fumes]] + [[cards/Bouncing Flask]] + [[cards/Catalyst]]) is excellent -- poison ignores [[cards/Metallicize]] block all enemies gain. [[cards/A Thousand Cuts]] provides passive AOE per card played. Apparitions are critical survival tools (5 [[buffs/Intangible]] turns). [[cards/Piercing Wail]] reduces all enemies' Str for 1 turn. Must kill Leader before Apparitions run out.
heuristics/enemies/gremlin-nob.md
@@ -1,18 +1,18 @@
# Gremlin Nob
-**Core rule:** Play ZERO Skills. No Defend, no True Grit, no Shrug It Off, no Shockwave, no Impervious. Only Attacks and Powers. Use potions for block instead of Skill cards.
+**Core rule:** Play ZERO Skills. No Defend, no [[cards/True Grit]], no [[cards/Shrug It Off]], no [[cards/Shockwave]], no [[cards/Impervious]]. Only Attacks and Powers. Use potions for block instead of Skill cards.
-**Turn 1 is free.** Nob always Bellows. Go all-in on damage. Skills played Turn 1 (before Bellow) are safe because Enrage is not yet active.
+**Turn 1 is free.** Nob always Bellows. Go all-in on damage. Skills played Turn 1 (before Bellow) are safe because [[buffs/Enrage]] is not yet active.
-**Benchmark:** 3-4 turn kill with zero Skills. Strength Potion on free Turn 1 maximizes value. Playing 3 Skills gives Nob +6 Str, turning a manageable fight into near-death.
+**Benchmark:** 3-4 turn kill with zero Skills. [[potions/Strength Potion]] on free Turn 1 maximizes value. Playing 3 Skills gives Nob +6 Str, turning a manageable fight into near-death.
## Character Matchups
-**Ironclad:** Starter deck is balanced (5 Attacks including Bash vs 5 Defends). Manageable if you only play Attacks. Bash is safe (Attack card).
+**[[characters/Ironclad]]:** Starter deck is balanced (5 Attacks including [[cards/Bash]] vs 5 Defends). Manageable if you only play Attacks. Bash is safe (Attack card).
-**Silent:** Worst matchup among base characters. Starter deck has 9+ Skills vs 5 Attacks. Even "careful" play forces 5-7 Skill triggers across a 7-turn fight because pure-Attack hands are rare. Blade Dance generates Shiv Attacks but is itself a Skill that triggers Enrage. When Hexaghost is the boss, do NOT waste Piercing Wail on Nob — save it for Inferno.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** Worst matchup among base characters. Starter deck has 9+ Skills vs 5 Attacks. Even "careful" play forces 5-7 Skill triggers across a 7-turn fight because pure-Attack hands are rare. [[cards/Blade Dance]] generates [[cards/Shiv]] Attacks but is itself a Skill that triggers Enrage. When [[bosses/Hexaghost]] is the boss, do NOT waste [[cards/Piercing Wail]] on Nob — save it for Inferno.
-**Defect:** Worst elite matchup. 6 Skills (4 Defend + Zap + Dualcast) vs 4 Attacks. Both Zap and Dualcast are Skills that trigger Enrage. Do NOT path into Nob without picking up multiple Attack cards first (Compile Driver, Sweeping Beam, Ball Lightning, Cold Snap). Cold Snap is the best Nob card — Attack that also channels Frost for passive block (orb passives don't trigger Enrage). Turn 1 is the only safe turn for Skills.
+**[[characters/Defect]]:** Worst elite matchup. 6 Skills (4 Defend + Zap + [[cards/Dualcast]]) vs 4 Attacks. Both Zap and Dualcast are Skills that trigger Enrage. Do NOT path into Nob without picking up multiple Attack cards first ([[cards/Compile Driver]], [[cards/Sweeping Beam]], [[cards/Ball Lightning]], [[cards/Cold Snap]]). Cold Snap is the best Nob card — Attack that also channels Frost for passive block (orb passives don't trigger Enrage). Turn 1 is the only safe turn for Skills.
## Deck Composition Check
heuristics/enemies/jaw-worm-horde.md
@@ -1,17 +1,17 @@
# Jaw Worm Horde
-**Core rule:** This is a DPS race against triple Strength scaling. AOE is critical. Kill one quickly to reduce incoming by 33%.
+**Core rule:** This is a DPS race against triple [[buffs/Strength]] scaling. AOE is critical. Kill one quickly to reduce incoming by 33%.
-**Danger Level:** High. Triple Strength scaling makes this one of the most dangerous Act 3 hallway fights. Combined Turn 1 damage can be 30+ with no free setup turn.
+**Danger Level:** High. Triple Strength scaling makes this one of the most dangerous [[acts/Act 3]] hallway fights. Combined Turn 1 damage can be 30+ with no free setup turn.
## Strategy
-- AOE is mandatory (Die Die Die, Blade Dance + Accuracy, Dagger Spray, Corpse Explosion)
+- AOE is mandatory ([[cards/Die Die Die]], [[cards/Blade Dance]] + [[cards/Accuracy]], [[cards/Dagger Spray]], [[cards/Corpse Explosion]])
- Focus fire one Jaw Worm while AOE chips the others
-- Apply Weak or Piercing Wail to reduce combined incoming on big attack turns
+- Apply [[debuffs/Weak]] or [[cards/Piercing Wail]] to reduce combined incoming on big attack turns
- Kill order: whichever has lowest HP first, then next lowest
- The fight becomes unmanageable after 4-5 turns if all 3 are still alive and scaling
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Noxious Fumes applies poison to all 3 simultaneously. Piercing Wail reduces all 3 enemies' Strength. Die Die Die+ for burst AOE. Blade Dance provides multi-target damage. Wraith Form for emergency Intangible if scaling gets out of control.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** [[cards/Noxious Fumes]] applies poison to all 3 simultaneously. Piercing Wail reduces all 3 enemies' Strength. Die Die Die+ for burst AOE. Blade Dance provides multi-target damage. [[cards/Wraith Form]] for emergency [[buffs/Intangible]] if scaling gets out of control.
heuristics/enemies/jaw-worm.md
@@ -2,21 +2,21 @@
**Core rule:** Block Turn 1 (always Chomp for 11). Burst during Bellow turns (free turns).
-**Benchmark:** 4-turn kill typical. 10-17 damage per turn with Vulnerable.
+**Benchmark:** 4-turn kill typical. 10-17 damage per turn with [[debuffs/Vulnerable]].
## Strategy
- Turn 1: Block the Chomp (11 damage)
- Turn 2: Likely Bellow (free turn) -- apply Vulnerable, deal damage
-- Kill fast before Strength accumulates
+- Kill fast before [[buffs/Strength]] accumulates
## What to Bring
-- Bash for Vulnerable
+- [[cards/Bash]] for Vulnerable
- Strikes for follow-up
## What to Avoid
- Slow decks that let Strength accumulate
-**Danger Level:** Low. Standard Act 1 opener.
+**Danger Level:** Low. Standard [[acts/Act 1]] opener.

# Lagavulin
-**Core rule:** Wake it immediately with Bash (Vulnerable > chipping through 8 block). Front-load damage on debuff turns (free turns). Kill before Strength debuffs accumulate.
+**Core rule:** Wake it immediately with [[cards/Bash]] ([[debuffs/Vulnerable]] > chipping through 8 block). Front-load damage on debuff turns (free turns). Kill before [[buffs/Strength]] debuffs accumulate.
-**Benchmark:** 7-turn fight typical. Expect 20-36 HP damage. Use Weak Potion to reduce 18 to 13.
+**Benchmark:** 7-turn fight typical. Expect 20-36 HP damage. Use [[potions/Weak Potion]] to reduce 18 to 13.
-**Strategy:** Apply Vulnerable via Bash+ on Turn 1 to wake it, then burst during the stunned turn and subsequent Siphon Soul turns (free damage windows). Headbutt+ top-decks Bash+ for Vulnerable refresh.
+**Strategy:** Apply Vulnerable via Bash+ on Turn 1 to wake it, then burst during the stunned turn and subsequent Siphon Soul turns (free damage windows). [[cards/Headbutt]] top-decks Bash+ for Vulnerable refresh.
## What to Bring
- Bash+ for Vulnerable setup
- Headbutt+ (top-deck Bash+ for Vulnerable refresh)
- Burst damage
-- Weak source
+- [[debuffs/Weak]] source
## What to Avoid

-Combined damage is 20+ per turn with no free setup turns. AOE and fast kills are critical. Do not play expensive setup cards (Corruption at 3E, Barricade at 3E) because you cannot afford a turn without block against two enemies attacking simultaneously.

heuristics/enemies/nemesis.md
@@ -1,20 +1,20 @@
# Nemesis
-**Core rule:** The fight has a rhythm -- non-Intangible turns are your damage windows, Intangible turns are for setup/blocking. Do NOT waste damage cards on Intangible turns.
+**Core rule:** The fight has a rhythm -- non-[[buffs/Intangible]] turns are your damage windows, Intangible turns are for setup/blocking. Do NOT waste damage cards on Intangible turns.
## Strategy
- Track the Intangible pattern: Turn 1 (no), Turn 2 (yes), Turn 3 (no), Turn 4 (yes), etc.
- On non-Intangible turns: deal maximum damage. Front-load burst here.
- On Intangible turns: block, setup, play Powers, apply debuffs
-- Block for Scythe (45 damage). Impervious is ideal. Weak reduces it to 33.
+- Block for Scythe (45 damage). [[cards/Impervious]] is ideal. [[debuffs/Weak]] reduces it to 33.
- Apply Weak to survive Scythe turns -- without Weak, 45 damage is near-unsurvivable
- Burns management matters in long fights. Kill speed or exhaust tools to end before Burns overwhelm.
-- Poison is partially countered -- each tick only deals 1 on Intangible turns. Still works but takes twice as long.
-- High-burst poison (Catalyst+) on a non-Intangible turn is still strong.
+- [[debuffs/Poison]] is partially countered -- each tick only deals 1 on Intangible turns. Still works but takes twice as long.
+- High-burst poison ([[cards/Catalyst]]) on a non-Intangible turn is still strong.
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Wraith Form+ provides Intangible for YOU on dangerous Scythe turns. Piercing Wail reduces the 6x3 multi-hit. Malaise reduces Strength for multiple turns. Well-Laid Plans+ can save burst damage for non-Intangible turns.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** [[cards/Wraith Form]] provides Intangible for YOU on dangerous Scythe turns. [[cards/Piercing Wail]] reduces the 6x3 multi-hit. [[cards/Malaise]] reduces [[buffs/Strength]] for multiple turns. [[cards/Well-Laid Plans]] can save burst damage for non-Intangible turns.
**Danger Level:** High. Scythe (45 damage) can one-shot at low HP. Intangible doubles effective fight length. Burns clog draws.
heuristics/enemies/orb-walker.md
@@ -7,18 +7,18 @@
## Strategy
- Focus fire one OW to halve incoming damage ASAP
-- Poison (Deadly Poison+, Catalyst+) can handle one OW while you block against the other
+- [[debuffs/Poison]] ([[cards/Deadly Poison]], [[cards/Catalyst]]) can handle one OW while you block against the other
- Feed kills are valuable at ~93 HP (+3 max HP)
## Character Matchups
-**Ironclad:** Inflame+ + Immolate+ + Strike+ can kill a single OW in 2 turns. Headbutt + Immolate+ guarantees a Turn 2 kill.
+**[[characters/Ironclad]]:** [[cards/Inflame]] + [[cards/Immolate]] + Strike+ can kill a single OW in 2 turns. [[cards/Headbutt]] + Immolate+ guarantees a Turn 2 kill.
-**Silent:** Poison engine handles OWs well -- Deadly Poison+ plus Noxious Fumes+ ticks kill one in 3-4 turns. Piercing Wail+ is critical for reducing combined incoming. After Image+ provides passive block scaling. Do NOT use Catalyst+ on Orb Walkers if the Act 3 boss is Awakened One (save for Regenerate 10).
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** Poison engine handles OWs well -- Deadly Poison+ plus [[cards/Noxious Fumes]] ticks kill one in 3-4 turns. [[cards/Piercing Wail]] is critical for reducing combined incoming. [[cards/After Image]] provides passive block scaling. Do NOT use Catalyst+ on Orb Walkers if the [[acts/Act 3]] boss is [[bosses/Awakened One]] (save for Regenerate 10).
## What NOT to Do
- Set up slow Powers on Turn 1 (fight is too short for setup to pay off)
- Let the fight go past Turn 5
- Spend Catalyst+ here if the Act 3 boss is Awakened One
-- Enter at low HP when Mark of the Bloom is active (no healing = run-ending)
+- Enter at low HP when [[relics/Mark of the Bloom]] is active (no healing = run-ending)

heuristics/enemies/reptomancer.md
@@ -9,11 +9,11 @@
## Strategy
- Kill Daggers before they Explode (you have 1 turn per Dagger wave)
-- AOE is critical (Die Die Die, Blade Dance + Accuracy, Dagger Spray all hit multiple targets)
+- AOE is critical ([[cards/Die Die Die]], [[cards/Blade Dance]] + [[cards/Accuracy]], [[cards/Dagger Spray]] all hit multiple targets)
- After clearing initial Daggers, focus damage on Reptomancer (she resummons, but reducing her HP means fewer total Dagger waves)
- Big Bite (30 damage) requires serious block -- track her pattern
-- Weak from Snake Strike reduces your damage -- re-apply Vulnerable to compensate
+- [[debuffs/Weak]] from Snake Strike reduces your damage -- re-apply [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] to compensate
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** This is the Silent's best elite matchup in Act 3. Die Die Die+ (17x3 AOE) can kill all Daggers in one play. Blade Dance + Accuracy provides cheap multi-target damage. Piercing Wail reduces all enemy damage (critical for multi-Dagger turns). Corpse Explosion turns killed Daggers into AOE against Reptomancer. Poison on Reptomancer ticks while you manage Daggers.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** This is the Silent's best elite matchup in [[acts/Act 3]]. Die Die Die+ (17x3 AOE) can kill all Daggers in one play. Blade Dance + Accuracy provides cheap multi-target damage. [[cards/Piercing Wail]] reduces all enemy damage (critical for multi-Dagger turns). [[cards/Corpse Explosion]] turns killed Daggers into AOE against Reptomancer. [[debuffs/Poison]] on Reptomancer ticks while you manage Daggers.
heuristics/enemies/repulsor.md
@@ -5,11 +5,11 @@
## Strategy
- Low HP (29-35) means they die quickly to any focused damage
-- Evolve converts Dazed into card draws (if available)
+- [[cards/Evolve]] converts [[cards/Dazed]] into card draws (if available)
- Minor annoyance for strong decks; can cripple thin decks that rely on specific card draws
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Dazed clogging is extra painful since key combo pieces (Catalyst+, Wraith Form) need to be drawn at the right time. Tools of the Trade and Well-Laid Plans+ help. Poison ignores deck clogging once applied.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** Dazed clogging is extra painful since key combo pieces ([[cards/Catalyst]], [[cards/Wraith Form]]) need to be drawn at the right time. [[cards/Tools of the Trade]] and [[cards/Well-Laid Plans]] help. [[debuffs/Poison]] ignores deck clogging once applied.
**Danger Level:** Low. Direct threat is minimal. Danger is indirect -- Dazed clogging makes companion enemies harder to deal with.
heuristics/enemies/sentry.md
@@ -6,9 +6,9 @@
## Elite Readiness Gate
-Do NOT fight Sentries with a starter-quality deck (Strikes, Defends, Bash, 0-1 other cards). Without AOE or meaningful damage scaling, effective damage per Strike is only 2 (6 minus Metallicize 4 absorb). Confirmed: a deck with Strikes/Defends/Bash/Headbutt/SIO spent 20 turns and took 56 damage (76->20 HP).
+Do NOT fight Sentries with a starter-quality deck (Strikes, Defends, [[cards/Bash]], 0-1 other cards). Without AOE or meaningful damage scaling, effective damage per Strike is only 2 (6 minus [[cards/Metallicize]] 4 absorb). Confirmed: a deck with Strikes/Defends/Bash/[[cards/Headbutt]]/SIO spent 20 turns and took 56 damage (76->20 HP).
-The Sentries fight requires at least one of: AOE (Thunderclap, Whirlwind, Cleave), damage scaling (Inflame, Spot Weakness), or a strong attack card (Carnage, Pommel Strike+).
+The Sentries fight requires at least one of: AOE ([[cards/Thunderclap]], [[cards/Whirlwind]], [[cards/Cleave]]), damage scaling ([[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Spot Weakness]]), or a strong attack card ([[cards/Carnage]], [[cards/Pommel Strike]]).
## Path Timing Gate
@@ -16,9 +16,9 @@ Do NOT path into Sentries with only 0-2 monster rooms beforehand. A deck needs 3
## Strategy
-- Strip Artifact with Thunderclap before real debuffs
+- Strip [[buffs/Artifact]] with Thunderclap before real debuffs
- Focus fire one Sentry at a time
-- Kill before Dazed overwhelms draws
+- Kill before [[cards/Dazed]] overwhelms draws
## What to Bring
@@ -33,8 +33,8 @@ Do NOT path into Sentries with only 0-2 monster rooms beforehand. A deck needs 3
## Act 2 Pairing (Sentry + Spheric Guardian)
-Kill the Sentry first (no block to break through, lower HP) to reduce incoming damage count and stop Dazed generation. Gremlin Horn triggers on Sentry death, providing energy and draw to handle the Spheric Guardian.
+Kill the Sentry first (no block to break through, lower HP) to reduce incoming damage count and stop Dazed generation. [[relics/Gremlin Horn]] triggers on Sentry death, providing energy and draw to handle the [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]].
## Silent Act 2 Pairing
-Kill order: Sentry first (strip Artifact with Neutralize, apply Terror for Vuln, burst with Backstab + attacks). Use Piercing Wail to zero the Sentry's beam turn while setting up Footwork Dex. After Sentry dies, poison the Spheric Guardian (Deadly Poison, Noxious Fumes bypass Barricade block entirely). However, Dazed from Sentry Bolts combined with SG's Barricade block creates a lethal combination at low HP (<30%).
+Kill order: Sentry first (strip Artifact with [[cards/Neutralize]], apply [[cards/Terror]] for Vuln, burst with [[cards/Backstab]] + attacks). Use [[cards/Piercing Wail]] to zero the Sentry's beam turn while setting up [[cards/Footwork]] Dex. After Sentry dies, poison the Spheric Guardian ([[cards/Deadly Poison]], [[cards/Noxious Fumes]] bypass [[cards/Barricade]] block entirely). However, Dazed from Sentry Bolts combined with SG's Barricade block creates a lethal combination at low HP (<30%).
heuristics/enemies/shelled-parasite.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Shelled Parasite
-**Core rule:** Each hit must exceed 14 to make progress (Plated Armor absorbs 14 per attack). Vulnerable and Strength scaling are critical.
+**Core rule:** Each hit must exceed 14 to make progress ([[buffs/Plated Armor]] absorbs 14 per attack). [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] and [[buffs/Strength]] scaling are critical.
**Benchmark:** At 13 damage/turn over 8+ turns, expect 100+ total incoming damage. Do NOT enter below 40% HP.
@@ -8,7 +8,7 @@
- Apply Vulnerable first, then use large single hits (not multiple small hits)
- Strength scaling shortens the fight by 2-3 turns
-- Use potions immediately if forced into this fight at low HP -- a Strength Potion shortens the fight by 2-3 turns
+- Use potions immediately if forced into this fight at low HP -- a [[potions/Strength Potion]] shortens the fight by 2-3 turns
## Strength Scaling is Critical
@@ -16,18 +16,18 @@ Without Strength scaling, each attack must overcome 14 Plated Armor before deali
## What to Bring
-- Burst damage (Carnage, Heavy Blade)
-- Vulnerable application (Bash, Uppercut)
-- Strength scaling (Inflame, Strength Potion)
+- Burst damage ([[cards/Carnage]], [[cards/Heavy Blade]])
+- Vulnerable application ([[cards/Bash]], [[cards/Uppercut]])
+- Strength scaling ([[cards/Inflame]], Strength Potion)
## What to Avoid
-- Small multi-hit attacks (Whirlwind with low energy)
+- Small multi-hit attacks ([[cards/Whirlwind]] with low energy)
- Decks that rely on chip damage
- Entering below 40% HP
## Paired With Fungi Beast
-Kill Fungi Beast first to avoid Spore Cloud (Vulnerable 2) amplifying Shelled Parasite's attacks. But be prepared to block heavily on the kill turn.
+Kill [[enemies/Fungi Beast]] first to avoid Spore Cloud (Vulnerable 2) amplifying Shelled Parasite's attacks. But be prepared to block heavily on the kill turn.
**Danger Level:** Medium-High. The long fight duration amplifies any HP deficit.
heuristics/enemies/slavers.md
@@ -1,19 +1,19 @@
# Slavers
-**Core rule:** This fight is unwinnable at low HP. Block is mandatory Turn 1. Do NOT fight Slavers within 2 floors of the Act 2 boss.
+**Core rule:** This fight is unwinnable at low HP. Block is mandatory Turn 1. Do NOT fight Slavers within 2 floors of the [[acts/Act 2]] boss.
-**HP Threshold:** Do NOT enter below 50% HP. Two confirmed deaths at low HP entry (27% and 23%). At sub-25% HP, even mass Weak (Shockwave) cannot save you.
+**HP Threshold:** Do NOT enter below 50% HP. Two confirmed deaths at low HP entry (27% and 23%). At sub-25% HP, even mass [[debuffs/Weak]] ([[cards/Shockwave]]) cannot save you.
## Kill Priority
-Red Slaver first (highest damage at 13 per Stab, applies Vulnerable which amplifies ALL incoming, Entangle can lock you out of attacks). Then Blue Slaver (Weak is annoying but less dangerous). Taskmaster last (only 7 damage and Wounds are manageable).
+[[enemies/Red Slaver]] first (highest damage at 13 per Stab, applies [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] which amplifies ALL incoming, Entangle can lock you out of attacks). Then [[enemies/Blue Slaver]] (Weak is annoying but less dangerous). [[enemies/Taskmaster]] last (only 7 damage and Wounds are manageable).
## Strategy
-- **Turn 1:** Block is mandatory. Do not set up powers. Flame Barrier+ (16 block + 6 counter to all 3 attackers) is the ideal Turn 1 play
+- **Turn 1:** Block is mandatory. Do not set up powers. [[cards/Flame Barrier]] (16 block + 6 counter to all 3 attackers) is the ideal Turn 1 play
- Focus fire on the enemy with highest per-turn damage first
- Rage (0E, 3 block per Attack) is excellent for generating free block while dealing damage
-- Uppercut's Weak on the hardest-hitting enemy is critical
+- [[cards/Uppercut]]'s Weak on the hardest-hitting enemy is critical
## Timing Rule
@@ -21,7 +21,7 @@ Do NOT fight Slavers within 2 floors of the Act 2 boss. The HP drain (20-40+ HP)
## What to Bring
-- AOE (Thunderclap, Whirlwind, Immolate)
+- AOE ([[cards/Thunderclap]], [[cards/Whirlwind]], [[cards/Immolate]])
- Flame Barrier+ (block + punishes all 3)
- Weak sources
- High block density
@@ -33,4 +33,4 @@ Do NOT fight Slavers within 2 floors of the Act 2 boss. The HP drain (20-40+ HP)
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Shiv burst can eliminate one Slaver quickly (Ninja Scroll + STR = 21+ free damage Turn 1). Piercing Wail is excellent Turn 1 (reduces 27 combined incoming to ~9). Expect 30-40 HP drain even with correct play.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** [[cards/Shiv]] burst can eliminate one Slaver quickly ([[relics/Ninja Scroll]] + STR = 21+ free damage Turn 1). [[cards/Piercing Wail]] is excellent Turn 1 (reduces 27 combined incoming to ~9). Expect 30-40 HP drain even with correct play.

## Strategy
-- Kill the Acid Slime first to avoid Weak stacking
+- Kill the Acid Slime first to avoid [[debuffs/Weak]] stacking

+- Good fight for preserving HP early in [[acts/Act 1]]

heuristics/enemies/snake-plant.md
@@ -1,31 +1,31 @@
# Snake Plant
-**Core rule:** Use single large attacks rather than many small hits per turn (Malleable gains increasing block per hit). The debuff turn (Enfeebling Spores) is a free turn for offense.
+**Core rule:** Use single large attacks rather than many small hits per turn ([[buffs/Malleable]] gains increasing block per hit). The debuff turn (Enfeebling Spores) is a free turn for offense.
-**Benchmark:** With Strength scaling: 4-5 turn fight, 15-25 HP lost. Without Strength scaling: 7-turn fight, 40+ HP lost. This fight is a Strength scaling check.
+**Benchmark:** With [[buffs/Strength]] scaling: 4-5 turn fight, 15-25 HP lost. Without Strength scaling: 7-turn fight, 40+ HP lost. This fight is a Strength scaling check.
## Strategy
-- Apply Vulnerable early
-- Clothesline/Uppercut for Weak reduces 7x3=21 to ~5x3=15
+- Apply [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] early
+- [[cards/Clothesline]]/[[cards/Uppercut]] for [[debuffs/Weak]] reduces 7x3=21 to ~5x3=15
- Kill in 4-5 turns before debuffs stack
-- The debuff turn is a free turn for offense (enter Wrath for Watcher)
+- The debuff turn is a free turn for offense (enter Wrath for [[characters/Watcher]])
## What to Bring
-- Burst damage (Immolate+, Carnage+, Rampage)
+- Burst damage ([[cards/Immolate]], [[cards/Carnage]], [[cards/Rampage]])
- Clothesline/Uppercut for Weak
- Single large hits
- Strength scaling is critical (without it, the fight drains 40+ HP)
## What to Avoid
-- Multi-hit attacks (Whirlwind, Twin Strike, Sword Boomerang, especially Pummel which generates 3+4+5+6 = 18 block from Malleable alone)
-- Entering at low HP -- 21-27 damage/turn with Frail making block weaker is a death spiral
+- Multi-hit attacks ([[cards/Whirlwind]], [[cards/Twin Strike]], [[cards/Sword Boomerang]], especially [[cards/Pummel]] which generates 3+4+5+6 = 18 block from Malleable alone)
+- Entering at low HP -- 21-27 damage/turn with [[debuffs/Frail]] making block weaker is a death spiral
## Character Matchups
-**Watcher:** Talk to the Hand triggers on each of Snake Plant's 3 Chomp hits = 6-9 free block per attack turn. Use Wrath burst on Enfeebling Spores (free) turns. Exit Wrath via Fear No Evil on Chomp turns. Flying Sleeves (4x2) triggers Malleable twice -- prefer single large hits instead.
+**Watcher:** [[cards/Talk to the Hand]] triggers on each of Snake Plant's 3 Chomp hits = 6-9 free block per attack turn. Use Wrath burst on Enfeebling Spores (free) turns. Exit Wrath via [[cards/Fear No Evil]] on Chomp turns. [[cards/Flying Sleeves]] (4x2) triggers Malleable twice -- prefer single large hits instead.
## Unknown Room Warning
heuristics/enemies/snecko.md
@@ -1,15 +1,15 @@
# Snecko
-**Core rule:** Kill fast before cost Confusion ruins multiple turns. Apply Vulnerable early to maximize every successful attack turn.
+**Core rule:** Kill fast before cost Confusion ruins multiple turns. Apply [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] early to maximize every successful attack turn.
**HP is high (~114-120).** Plan for a 5-7 turn fight even with good damage.
## Strategy
-- With good RNG, Confused turns can be better than normal (0-cost Bash+, 0-cost Fiend Fire)
+- With good RNG, [[debuffs/Confused]] turns can be better than normal (0-cost [[cards/Bash]], 0-cost [[cards/Fiend Fire]])
- With bad RNG, entire turns are wasted on unplayable 3-cost cards
-- Metallicize is excellent: Powers are played once and provide guaranteed value every turn regardless of cost rolls
-- Demon Form is strong: if drawn at reduced cost under Confusion, play immediately for guaranteed scaling
+- [[cards/Metallicize]] is excellent: Powers are played once and provide guaranteed value every turn regardless of cost rolls
+- [[cards/Demon Form]] is strong: if drawn at reduced cost under Confusion, play immediately for guaranteed scaling
## What to Bring
@@ -17,8 +17,8 @@
- Vulnerable application
- Powers (guaranteed value regardless of cost rolls)
- Metallicize (passive block every turn)
-- Duplication Potion (duplicate a card Confusion set to 0-cost)
-- Gambler's Brew (redraws hand with new randomized costs -- re-rolls Confusion RNG)
+- [[potions/Duplication Potion]] (duplicate a card Confusion set to 0-cost)
+- [[potions/Gambler's Brew]] (redraws hand with new randomized costs -- re-rolls Confusion RNG)
## What to Avoid
@@ -27,4 +27,4 @@
## Escape Option
-Smoke Bomb works against Snecko. If Confusion has randomized costs badly and survival is impossible, escaping is correct. Losing rewards is better than dying.
+[[potions/Smoke Bomb]] works against Snecko. If Confusion has randomized costs badly and survival is impossible, escaping is correct. Losing rewards is better than dying.
heuristics/enemies/spheric-guardian.md
@@ -1,44 +1,44 @@
# Spheric Guardian
-**Core rule:** This is a DPS check disguised as a hallway fight. Must kill within 6-8 turns or block becomes insurmountable. Strip 3 Artifact charges first, then apply Vulnerable, then burst through remaining block + HP.
+**Core rule:** This is a DPS check disguised as a hallway fight. Must kill within 6-8 turns or block becomes insurmountable. Strip 3 [[buffs/Artifact]] charges first, then apply [[debuffs/Vulnerable]], then burst through remaining block + HP.
-**Benchmark:** Expect 30-50+ HP lost from Frail-reduced blocking vs 10x2 attacks. At low Strength without burst, this fight is a guaranteed loss through attrition.
+**Benchmark:** Expect 30-50+ HP lost from [[debuffs/Frail]]-reduced blocking vs 10x2 attacks. At low [[buffs/Strength]] without burst, this fight is a guaranteed loss through attrition.
## Strategy
-1. Strip 3 Artifact charges with cheap debuffs (Thunderclap ideal)
+1. Strip 3 Artifact charges with cheap debuffs ([[cards/Thunderclap]] ideal)
2. Apply Vulnerable
3. Burst through remaining block + HP
## What to Bring
-- Uppercut+ (strips 2 Artifact charges in one play)
+- [[cards/Uppercut]] (strips 2 Artifact charges in one play)
- Thunderclap (strip remaining Artifact)
-- Pen Nib (double damage to break block)
-- Burst damage (Fiend Fire, Immolate, Bludgeon)
-- FAST Strength scaling (Inflame, Spot Weakness, Limit Break -- NOT Demon Form)
-- Potions (Strength Potion, Fire Potion)
+- [[relics/Pen Nib]] (double damage to break block)
+- Burst damage ([[cards/Fiend Fire]], [[cards/Immolate]], [[cards/Bludgeon]])
+- FAST Strength scaling ([[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Spot Weakness]], [[cards/Limit Break]] -- NOT [[cards/Demon Form]])
+- Potions ([[potions/Strength Potion]], [[potions/Fire Potion]])
## What to Avoid
- Chip damage with Strikes (block regenerates faster than you can deplete it)
- Demon Form as sole Strength source (too slow -- 13 Str by turn 9 still lost to 65+ block)
-- Random exhaust effects (Havoc, unupgraded True Grit)
-- Exhausting Spot Weakness (permanent Strength gain is needed to break through Barricade)
-- Playing Brutality (1 HP/turn self-damage over an 8-13 turn fight)
+- Random exhaust effects (Havoc, unupgraded [[cards/True Grit]])
+- Exhausting Spot Weakness (permanent Strength gain is needed to break through [[cards/Barricade]])
+- Playing [[cards/Brutality]] (1 HP/turn self-damage over an 8-13 turn fight)
- Entering at low HP (<50%) -- routinely drains 30-50 HP
- Entering with zero potions
## Winning Formulas
-- Corruption + Feel No Pain engine (12 HP lost)
-- Immolate + Impervious+ + Calipers + Spot Weakness (20 HP lost)
+- [[cards/Corruption]] + [[cards/Feel No Pain]] engine (12 HP lost)
+- Immolate + [[cards/Impervious]] + [[relics/Calipers]] + Spot Weakness (20 HP lost)
- Front-loaded Str (Inflame/Spot Weakness) + burst (Fiend Fire, Bludgeon) + potions
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Dex stacking (Footwork+) + Shiv damage (Accuracy + Blade Dance) works but expect 26+ HP drain. Poison bypasses Barricade entirely (Deadly Poison, Noxious Fumes). Piercing Wail+ strips Artifact while negating damage. Block density under Frail 5 is precarious.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** Dex stacking ([[cards/Footwork]]) + [[cards/Shiv]] damage ([[cards/Accuracy]] + [[cards/Blade Dance]]) works but expect 26+ HP drain. [[debuffs/Poison]] bypasses Barricade entirely ([[cards/Deadly Poison]], [[cards/Noxious Fumes]]). [[cards/Piercing Wail]] strips Artifact while negating damage. Block density under Frail 5 is precarious.
## Danger Warning
-Single most dangerous Act 2 hallway fight. 5 deaths confirmed. Even with correct play and front-loaded Strength, expect 30-40 HP drain.
+Single most dangerous [[acts/Act 2]] hallway fight. 5 deaths confirmed. Even with correct play and front-loaded Strength, expect 30-40 HP drain.
heuristics/enemies/spike-slime-l.md
@@ -4,9 +4,9 @@
## Strategy
-- In the Slime Boss fight, killing this while Acid Slime (L) is at full HP creates a 3-enemy nightmare

+- Exhaust [[cards/Slimed]] cards to maintain hand quality

heuristics/enemies/spiker.md
@@ -1,16 +1,16 @@
# Spiker
-**Core rule:** Poison is the ideal damage source -- it bypasses Thorns entirely. If you must attack, use single large hits, not multi-hit attacks.
+**Core rule:** [[debuffs/Poison]] is the ideal damage source -- it bypasses Thorns entirely. If you must attack, use single large hits, not multi-hit attacks.
## Strategy
-- Poison (Noxious Fumes, Deadly Poison, Bouncing Flask) deals damage without triggering Thorns
-- If attacking, use single large hits (Dash+, Predator) not multi-hit attacks
+- Poison ([[cards/Noxious Fumes]], [[cards/Deadly Poison]], [[cards/Bouncing Flask]]) deals damage without triggering Thorns
+- If attacking, use single large hits ([[cards/Dash]], [[cards/Predator]]) not multi-hit attacks
- Kill Spikers early before Thorns compound (at Thorns 3-5 attacks are tolerable; at 9+ every attack is significant HP loss)
- In multi-enemy fights, kill Spiker first to stop Thorns from scaling
## Character Matchups
-**Silent:** Poison completely solves Spikers. Noxious Fumes + any poison source kills without ever taking Thorns damage. Shivs and Blade Dance are TRAPS -- each hit triggers Thorns. Piercing Wail does nothing useful here (Cut damage is low; Thorns is the real threat).
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** Poison completely solves Spikers. Noxious Fumes + any poison source kills without ever taking Thorns damage. Shivs and [[cards/Blade Dance]] are TRAPS -- each hit triggers Thorns. [[cards/Piercing Wail]] does nothing useful here (Cut damage is low; Thorns is the real threat).
**Danger Level:** Medium. Low direct damage but Thorns mechanic punishes attack-heavy decks severely. Poison decks trivialize this fight.
heuristics/enemies/the-maw.md
@@ -1,18 +1,18 @@
# The Maw
-**Core rule:** Setup on free turns. The Maw gives 3-4 free turns across the fight. Use every one for Power cards (Barricade, Metallicize, Inflame, Evolve). This is a defensive setup fight, not a burst fight.
+**Core rule:** Setup on free turns. The Maw gives 3-4 free turns across the fight. Use every one for Power cards ([[cards/Barricade]], [[cards/Metallicize]], [[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Evolve]]). This is a defensive setup fight, not a burst fight.
**Benchmark:** 8-9 turn fight. Must have block scaling online before the big hits arrive (33-56+ damage).
## Strategy
- **Barricade + Metallicize is the solution.** Block from early turns carries over, stacking higher each turn. By the time damage escalates to 33-56, the block wall exceeds incoming.
-- Mummified Hand + Power-heavy decks excel (multiple Power plays on free turns trigger Mummified Hand repeatedly)
-- Headbutt + Immolate+ loop for damage
+- [[relics/Mummified Hand]] + Power-heavy decks excel (multiple Power plays on free turns trigger Mummified Hand repeatedly)
+- [[cards/Headbutt]] + [[cards/Immolate]] loop for damage
- Feed is safe to exhaust early (Feed cannot kill the Maw at 300 HP)
## What NOT to Do
-- Waste Bloodletting+ on Turn 1 (the free turn -- Vulnerable increases future damage taken)
-- Ignore the Frail debuff (block values are reduced by 25% during Frail turns)
+- Waste [[cards/Bloodletting]] on Turn 1 (the free turn -- [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] increases future damage taken)
+- Ignore the [[debuffs/Frail]] debuff (block values are reduced by 25% during Frail turns)
- Rush the kill (300 HP means 8-9 turns minimum; defensive setup is more important than early burst)
heuristics/enemies/transient.md
@@ -10,9 +10,9 @@
## Strategy
-- Strength debuffs are excellent (Disarm, Dark Shackles, Malaise -- permanent or large temporary Str reduction compounds across turns)
-- Weak is excellent (reduces the already-Shifting-reduced damage by another 25%)
-- Impervious is best saved for Turns 3-5 when incoming is 50-70 base
+- [[buffs/Strength]] debuffs are excellent ([[cards/Disarm]], [[cards/Dark Shackles]], [[cards/Malaise]] -- permanent or large temporary Str reduction compounds across turns)
+- [[debuffs/Weak]] is excellent (reduces the already-Shifting-reduced damage by another 25%)
+- [[cards/Impervious]] is best saved for Turns 3-5 when incoming is 50-70 base
## What NOT to Do
@@ -21,8 +21,8 @@
## Character-Specific Notes
-**Ironclad:** Impervious covers one turn. Barricade lets block accumulate. Disarm is permanent Str reduction. Reaper heals chip damage.
+**[[characters/Ironclad]]:** Impervious covers one turn. [[cards/Barricade]] lets block accumulate. Disarm is permanent Str reduction. [[cards/Reaper]] heals chip damage.
-**Silent:** Wraith Form+ gives 3 turns Intangible (1 damage each). Piercing Wail reduces attack. Malaise+ is permanent Str reduction. After Image+ provides passive block per card. Poison does NOT help -- Transient fades on its own.
+**[[characters/Silent]]:** [[cards/Wraith Form]] gives 3 turns [[buffs/Intangible]] (1 damage each). [[cards/Piercing Wail]] reduces attack. Malaise+ is permanent Str reduction. [[cards/After Image]] provides passive block per card. [[debuffs/Poison]] does NOT help -- [[encounters/Transient]] fades on its own.
-**Defect:** Orb damage counts for Shifting. Lightning procs reduce Transient's Strength. Frost orbs provide block. Barrage with multiple orbs deals massive Shifting-reduced damage.
+**[[characters/Defect]]:** Orb damage counts for Shifting. Lightning procs reduce Transient's Strength. Frost orbs provide block. [[cards/Barrage]] with multiple orbs deals massive Shifting-reduced damage.
heuristics/enemies/writhing-mass.md
@@ -1,14 +1,14 @@
# Writhing Mass
-**Core rule:** Use single large hits, not multi-hit attacks. Malleable gains block per hit, punishing Whirlwind/Pummel severely.
+**Core rule:** Use single large hits, not multi-hit attacks. [[buffs/Malleable]] gains block per hit, punishing [[cards/Whirlwind]]/[[cards/Pummel]] severely.
**Benchmark:** Expect a long fight (6-8 turns) and 25-40 HP drain.
## Strategy
-- Hemokinesis (15 damage, 1 hit) is far better than Pummel (2x4, gives 2 stacks of Malleable block)
-- Apply Weak early (multi-hit attacks at 75% damage are much more survivable)
-- Disarm helps but does not solve the fight -- fight length is the real problem
+- [[cards/Hemokinesis]] (15 damage, 1 hit) is far better than Pummel (2x4, gives 2 stacks of Malleable block)
+- Apply [[debuffs/Weak]] early (multi-hit attacks at 75% damage are much more survivable)
+- [[cards/Disarm]] helps but does not solve the fight -- fight length is the real problem
## What NOT to Do
@@ -18,8 +18,8 @@
## Engine Approach
-Barricade + Corruption + FNP engine handles Writhing Mass effectively. Block accumulation makes the fight survivable, and Immolate+ (single large hit) bypasses Malleable efficiently. Spot Weakness works on Attack turns.
+[[cards/Barricade]] + [[cards/Corruption]] + FNP engine handles Writhing Mass effectively. Block accumulation makes the fight survivable, and [[cards/Immolate]] (single large hit) bypasses Malleable efficiently. [[cards/Spot Weakness]] works on Attack turns.
## Danger Warning
-This enemy is a major HP drain in Act 3. Entering the next fight at low HP after Writhing Mass is the real danger.
+This enemy is a major HP drain in [[acts/Act 3]]. Entering the next fight at low HP after Writhing Mass is the real danger.
heuristics/events/colosseum.md
@@ -1,14 +1,14 @@
# Colosseum
After Wave 1, check HP:
-1. Below 30 HP: ALWAYS escape (COWARDICE). The Wave 2 enemy pool includes Gremlin Nob, which can kill any deck at low HP.
-2. Check deck composition. Skill-heavy decks (Defect, early Silent) face worst-case matchups in Wave 2. Escape unless HP is above 60%.
+1. Below 30 HP: ALWAYS escape (COWARDICE). The Wave 2 enemy pool includes [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], which can kill any deck at low HP.
+2. Check deck composition. Skill-heavy decks ([[characters/Defect]], early [[characters/Silent]]) face worst-case matchups in Wave 2. Escape unless HP is above 60%.
3. Check potions. If all defensive potions were used in Wave 1, escape -- Wave 2 requires fresh resources.
4. Fight only if HP is above 50% after Wave 1 and the deck can handle another multi-enemy fight.
-The rewards from Wave 2 are NEVER worth a run-ending death. Colosseum appears mid-Act 2 where the remaining path has plenty of card/relic opportunities.
+The rewards from Wave 2 are NEVER worth a run-ending death. Colosseum appears mid-[[acts/Act 2]] where the remaining path has plenty of card/relic opportunities.
Warnings:
-- Pre-boss HP drain: If encountered 1-2 floors before the boss, the forced combat can drain 15-25 HP. Unknown rooms carry Colosseum risk even with Juzu Bracelet.
-- Entangle: Blue Slaver prevents all attacks for 1 turn. Plan for 1 turn of pure blocking.
-- Gremlin Nob in Wave 2: Nob's Enrage (+2 Str per Skill) is devastating for Skill-heavy decks.
+- Pre-boss HP drain: If encountered 1-2 floors before the boss, the forced combat can drain 15-25 HP. Unknown rooms carry Colosseum risk even with [[relics/Juzu Bracelet]].
+- Entangle: [[enemies/Blue Slaver]] prevents all attacks for 1 turn. Plan for 1 turn of pure blocking.
+- Gremlin Nob in Wave 2: Nob's [[buffs/Enrage]] (+2 Str per Skill) is devastating for Skill-heavy decks.
heuristics/events/council-of-ghosts.md
@@ -2,8 +2,8 @@
5 turns of invincibility is powerful, but the Max HP loss is devastating. At 100+ Max HP with a strong deck that ends fights quickly, Accept can work. At low Max HP (<80), Refuse -- being at 39 Max HP means one bad fight ends the run.
-Poison deck synergy: Apparitions are exceptionally powerful with a poison deck. Intangible buys time for poison to tick -- Noxious Fumes applies damage every turn regardless of Intangible status. Catalyst+ tripling a poison stack during Intangible turns is the optimal sequencing: build poison safely, then survive with block after Intangible expires. Tools of the Trade filters Apparitions to when needed; Hovering Kite generates energy from discarding unneeded ones.
+[[debuffs/Poison]] deck synergy: Apparitions are exceptionally powerful with a poison deck. [[buffs/Intangible]] buys time for poison to tick -- [[cards/Noxious Fumes]] applies damage every turn regardless of Intangible status. [[cards/Catalyst]] tripling a poison stack during Intangible turns is the optimal sequencing: build poison safely, then survive with block after Intangible expires. [[cards/Tools of the Trade]] filters Apparitions to when needed; [[relics/Hovering Kite]] generates energy from discarding unneeded ones.
-Against Hex (Chosen): Apparitions are Skills, so they trigger Hex (add Dazed). Playing 3-4 Apparitions adds 3-4 Dazed. Still worth it -- Intangible is more valuable than the Dazed penalty.
+Against [[debuffs/Hex]] ([[enemies/Chosen]]): Apparitions are Skills, so they trigger Hex (add [[cards/Dazed]]). Playing 3-4 Apparitions adds 3-4 Dazed. Still worth it -- Intangible is more valuable than the Dazed penalty.
Priority: Accept (poison deck with Noxious Fumes/Catalyst+, or 100+ Max HP) > Refuse (default, especially <80 Max HP without poison engine).
heuristics/events/cursed-tome.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Cursed Tome
-Book relics are powerful (Enchiridion gives a free Power every fight, Nilry's Codex generates cards every turn, Necronomicon doubles expensive Attacks). At 16 HP total cost, worth it when above 50% HP and not approaching a boss. Can bail early if HP gets too low -- you only lose the HP already spent.
+Book relics are powerful ([[relics/Enchiridion]] gives a free Power every fight, [[relics/Nilry's Codex]] generates cards every turn, [[relics/Necronomicon]] doubles expensive Attacks). At 16 HP total cost, worth it when above 50% HP and not approaching a boss. Can bail early if HP gets too low -- you only lose the HP already spent.
Priority: Read to completion (high HP, no boss imminent) > Leave early (moderate HP) > Leave immediately (low HP).
heuristics/events/forgotten-altar.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Forgotten Altar
- If HP > 40 and a rest site is upcoming: Sacrifice. 20 HP loss is recoverable; +5 Max HP is permanent value.
-- If HP <= 20: Desecrate (Sacrifice would kill you). Decay curse can be exhausted by Fiend Fire or removed at shops.
+- If HP <= 20: Desecrate (Sacrifice would kill you). Decay curse can be exhausted by [[cards/Fiend Fire]] or removed at shops.
- If HP 20-40 with no rest coming: Leave. Neither option is safe.
-- If you have exhaust tools (Fiend Fire, True Grit+, Second Wind): Desecrate is less punishing since you can exhaust the curse in combat.
+- If you have exhaust tools (Fiend Fire, [[cards/True Grit]], [[cards/Second Wind]]): Desecrate is less punishing since you can exhaust the curse in combat.
Priority (high HP): Sacrifice > Leave > Desecrate.
Priority (low HP, forced): Desecrate > Leave. Never Sacrifice below 21 HP.
heuristics/events/knowing-skull.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Knowing Skull
-Default: Leave immediately (pay the 6 HP exit cost and nothing more). The HP cost is rarely worth the reward, especially in Act 2 where HP is the scarcest resource. Even taking one ask (6 HP) and then leaving (6 HP) costs 12 HP total -- entering the dialogue commits you to at least 6 HP loss.
+Default: Leave immediately (pay the 6 HP exit cost and nothing more). The HP cost is rarely worth the reward, especially in [[acts/Act 2]] where HP is the scarcest resource. Even taking one ask (6 HP) and then leaving (6 HP) costs 12 HP total -- entering the dialogue commits you to at least 6 HP loss.
Do not engage near the boss. A 12 HP loss before elite territory or boss can be the difference between winning and dying.
-Engage only at high HP (70%+) with a specific, critical deck gap (e.g., need exhaust tools before Book of Stabbing, need AOE before Byrds) AND at least 5 floors before the boss. Limit to one ask maximum. Never take multiple asks -- the escalating cost compounds into a death spiral.
+Engage only at high HP (70%+) with a specific, critical deck gap (e.g., need exhaust tools before [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]], need AOE before Byrds) AND at least 5 floors before the boss. Limit to one ask maximum. Never take multiple asks -- the escalating cost compounds into a death spiral.
At low HP, always leave immediately (accept the 6 HP exit cost).
heuristics/events/living-wall.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Living Wall
-If a game-defining upgrade exists (True Grit -> True Grit+, Shockwave -> Shockwave+), ALWAYS choose Grow. Otherwise, Forget (Remove) is the default -- deck thinning is permanent value. Change (Transform) is high variance and a gamble.
+If a game-defining upgrade exists ([[cards/True Grit]] -> True Grit+, [[cards/Shockwave]] -> Shockwave+), ALWAYS choose Grow. Otherwise, Forget (Remove) is the default -- deck thinning is permanent value. Change (Transform) is high variance and a gamble.
Priority: Grow (if critical upgrade target exists) > Forget (default) > Change (gambling).
heuristics/events/masked-bandits.md
@@ -2,4 +2,4 @@
Pay the toll at low HP where combat risks death. Gold is recoverable; HP is not. If below 60% HP and the alternative is a fight that could cascade into a death spiral, pay without hesitation. Gold has no value if you die.
-Fight at high HP with a strong deck. The fight is a standard Act 2 hallway encounter. If the deck handles hallway fights cleanly, fight to preserve gold for shops.
+Fight at high HP with a strong deck. The fight is a standard [[acts/Act 2]] hallway encounter. If the deck handles hallway fights cleanly, fight to preserve gold for shops.
heuristics/events/match-and-keep.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Match and Keep
-Matched cards are added to your deck whether you want them or not -- unlike card rewards, you cannot refuse. This means the event can ADD UNWANTED CARDS (observed: Bash and Perfected Strike added to a deck that did not want them). Deck dilution from bad matches is a real downside.
+Matched cards are added to your deck whether you want them or not -- unlike card rewards, you cannot refuse. This means the event can ADD UNWANTED CARDS (observed: [[cards/Bash]] and [[cards/Perfected Strike]] added to a deck that did not want them). Deck dilution from bad matches is a real downside.
Play only when the deck is small enough that a few mediocre additions are tolerable, or when you are desperate for any card quality.
heuristics/events/mind-bloom.md
@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
**Ginger equipped:** Choose "I am Healthy" (free full heal, Doubt curse is harmless since Ginger prevents Weakened, remove at shop).
-**Strong deck:** Choose "I am War" -- an Act 1 boss (Guardian, Slime Boss, Hexaghost) is trivial with an Act 3 deck. The Rare Relic reward is guaranteed and can be game-changing.
+**Strong deck:** Choose "I am War" -- an [[acts/Act 1]] boss (Guardian, [[bosses/Slime Boss]], [[bosses/Hexaghost]]) is trivial with an [[acts/Act 3]] deck. The Rare Relic reward is guaranteed and can be game-changing.
-**I am Awake is a TRAP.** Mark of the Bloom prevents ALL healing for the rest of the run -- rest sites cannot heal, potions cannot heal, Burning Blood does nothing, Feed does nothing. Every point of damage from this moment forward is permanent. Only consider if ALL of the following are true: (1) multiple unupgraded cards with transformative upgrades, (2) ZERO remaining combat encounters on the path to the boss, AND (3) current HP is near max.
+**I am Awake is a TRAP.** [[relics/Mark of the Bloom]] prevents ALL healing for the rest of the run -- rest sites cannot heal, potions cannot heal, [[relics/Burning Blood]] does nothing, Feed does nothing. Every point of damage from this moment forward is permanent. Only consider if ALL of the following are true: (1) multiple unupgraded cards with transformative upgrades, (2) ZERO remaining combat encounters on the path to the boss, AND (3) current HP is near max.
-**I am Rich is a trap with Snecko Eye.** Normality limits the hand to 3 cards per turn. With Snecko Eye drawing 7 cards, 4 are immediately wasted. The 999 gold is useless in late Act 3 (few shops remaining).
+**I am Rich is a trap with [[relics/Snecko Eye]].** Normality limits the hand to 3 cards per turn. With Snecko Eye drawing 7 cards, 4 are immediately wasted. The 999 gold is useless in late Act 3 (few shops remaining).
Priority: Healthy (with Ginger) > War (strong deck) > Awake (almost never) > Rich (never).
heuristics/events/mushrooms.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Mushrooms
- **Eat (Heal 21 HP + Parasite)** when: HP is below 30% AND a shop is reachable within 2-3 floors for curse removal, OR HP is so critically low that dying before reaching a shop is more likely than the curse killing you. The heal is NOT free -- weigh the Parasite cost. Parasite is an Unplayable card that clogs draws permanently until removed.
-- **Stomp (Fight)** when: HP is above 70%, the deck does NOT rely on Vulnerable for damage, AND the boss is Guardian or Slime Boss (Odd Mushroom is less harmful against these bosses).
+- **Stomp (Fight)** when: HP is above 70%, the deck does NOT rely on [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] for damage, AND the boss is Guardian or [[bosses/Slime Boss]] ([[relics/Odd Mushroom]] is less harmful against these bosses).
- **Leave** when: HP is above 40% or no shop is available to remove Parasite within 3 floors. The Parasite curse is worse than 21 HP in most situations.
-HEXAGHOST ANTI-SYNERGY: Odd Mushroom reduces Vulnerable from 1.5x to 1.25x on enemies. Against Hexaghost, where kill speed determines whether Burns overwhelm you, this ~17% damage reduction extends the fight by 1-2 turns. Take Eat or Leave instead.
+HEXAGHOST ANTI-SYNERGY: Odd Mushroom reduces Vulnerable from 1.5x to 1.25x on enemies. Against [[bosses/Hexaghost]], where kill speed determines whether Burns overwhelm you, this ~17% damage reduction extends the fight by 1-2 turns. Take Eat or Leave instead.
Priority: Leave if HP above 40% or no shop nearby. Eat only at critical HP with shop access. Stomp only at 70%+ HP with non-Hexaghost boss.
heuristics/events/mysterious-sphere.md
@@ -1,8 +1,8 @@
# Mysterious Sphere
Open Sphere when:
-- HP is above 70% AND healing is available (rest sites ahead, no Mark of the Bloom)
