-- The deck can handle 2 Orb Walkers without using irreplaceable boss resources (Catalyst+)
+- HP is above 70% AND healing is available (rest sites ahead, no [[relics/Mark of the Bloom]])
+- The deck can handle 2 Orb Walkers without using irreplaceable boss resources ([[cards/Catalyst]])
- A Rare Relic could meaningfully improve the boss fight
Leave when:
heuristics/events/ominous-forge.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Ominous Forge
-ALWAYS choose Forge. Free upgrades are the best events in the game. No HP cost, no gold cost. Follow standard upgrade priority: Bash > key attack/skill cards > Thunderclap > Defend.
+ALWAYS choose Forge. Free upgrades are the best events in the game. No HP cost, no gold cost. Follow standard upgrade priority: [[cards/Bash]] > key attack/skill cards > [[cards/Thunderclap]] > Defend.
heuristics/events/purifier.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Purifier
-Free card removal -- always take it. Remove Strikes before Defends. Same logic as Bonfire Spirits: deck thinning is permanent value with zero cost.
+Free card removal -- always take it. Remove Strikes before Defends. Same logic as [[events/Bonfire Spirits]]: deck thinning is permanent value with zero cost.
heuristics/events/sssserpent.md
@@ -2,6 +2,6 @@
Take the gold if a shop is within 2-3 floors (remove curse for 75g, net 100g profit). Refuse if no shop is reachable before a boss or elite.
-Doubt is a manageable curse -- Weak on yourself reduces your attack damage by 25%, but does not affect block. If the curse gets stuck in hand for 1-2 fights before removal, the cost is reduced damage output, not death.
+Doubt is a manageable curse -- [[debuffs/Weak]] on yourself reduces your attack damage by 25%, but does not affect block. If the curse gets stuck in hand for 1-2 fights before removal, the cost is reduced damage output, not death.
Many events that offer gold + curse are designed with the expectation that you can route to a shop to remove the curse. The correct play is often: take the gold, route to a shop, remove the curse, net profit. Do not refuse the gold reflexively because "curses are bad" -- evaluate the map first.
heuristics/events/the-library.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# The Library
-Always choose Read. A pool of 20 cards is the largest selection in the game -- dramatically increases the odds of finding a specific card you need. This is the best opportunity to find Reaper, Feed, or other critical cards that may not have appeared in normal rewards.
+Always choose Read. A pool of 20 cards is the largest selection in the game -- dramatically increases the odds of finding a specific card you need. This is the best opportunity to find [[cards/Reaper]], Feed, or other critical cards that may not have appeared in normal rewards.
At any point where a healing card (Reaper, Feed) has not yet been found, The Library is potentially game-saving. The probability of at least one healing card appearing in 20 random cards is much higher than in the standard 3-card reward.
heuristics/events/the-nest.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# The Nest
-At low HP (<30), always take gold. 6 HP can be lethal when consecutive fights follow. Ritual Dagger is a long-term investment card -- only worth it if many fights remain AND current HP can absorb the 6 HP loss without entering the next fight below threshold. 99 Gold is reliable value (enables shop purchases, curse removal, potions).
+At low HP (<30), always take gold. 6 HP can be lethal when consecutive fights follow. [[buffs/Ritual]] Dagger is a long-term investment card -- only worth it if many fights remain AND current HP can absorb the 6 HP loss without entering the next fight below threshold. 99 Gold is reliable value (enables shop purchases, curse removal, potions).
If next room is combat and HP is marginal, gold is correct. The 6 HP loss cannot be recovered before the fight.
heuristics/events/vampires.md
@@ -4,8 +4,8 @@ The Max HP loss is SEVERE. Losing 24 Max HP (from 80 to 56) means every HP thres
The healing from Bites (2 HP per Bite played) is meaningful over long fights but cannot compensate for the reduced HP pool against burst damage. Removing all Strikes is good for deck quality (Bites are strictly better: same cost, similar damage, plus healing).
-AVOID if: you are below 70% HP, approaching a boss or elite, or your deck already has healing (Reaper, Feed). The Max HP loss compounds with Act 2's HP drain fights.
+AVOID if: you are below 70% HP, approaching a boss or elite, or your deck already has healing ([[cards/Reaper]], Feed). The Max HP loss compounds with [[acts/Act 2]]'s HP drain fights.
-CONSIDER if: you have no other healing sources and are at high HP with a safe path ahead. At 56 Max HP, the Gremlin Leader elite (27+ damage/turn with Str scaling) and 3 Cultists become nearly impossible to survive.
+CONSIDER if: you have no other healing sources and are at high HP with a safe path ahead. At 56 Max HP, the [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] elite (27+ damage/turn with Str scaling) and 3 Cultists become nearly impossible to survive.
Priority: Refuse (most situations) > Accept (only at high HP with no healing sources and a safe upcoming path).
heuristics/events/wheel-of-change.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Wheel of Change
-Below 60% HP: Risky. The 8 HP loss outcome is potentially lethal in Act 2.
+Below 60% HP: Risky. The 8 HP loss outcome is potentially lethal in [[acts/Act 2]].
Above 60% HP: Acceptable to spin. Even the worst known outcome (-8 HP) leaves a buffer, and the best known outcome (full heal) is extremely valuable.
heuristics/exhaust.md
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
# Exhaust Strategy
- **Before Exhausting, Ask**: (1) Can remaining deck survive the enemy's biggest attack? (2) Is the card truly expendable? (3) Am I in first half (thin=good) or second half (thin=fatal)?
-- **Random Exhaust Is Run-Ending**: Unupgraded True Grit and Havoc both cause random exhaust. Rule: Never play Havoc or unupgraded True Grit when deck contains irreplaceable cards. Upgrade True Grit IMMEDIATELY if taken.
-- **Safe Exhaust Targets**: Strikes (once better attacks exist), extra Defends, status cards (Slimed, Wound, Burn, Dazed).
-- **Never Exhaust**: Last copy of best damage card, last high-block card, core combo pieces (Bash+ when Vuln matters), synergy engines (Fire Breathing+ when Immolate is in deck).
+- **Random Exhaust Is Run-Ending**: Unupgraded [[cards/True Grit]] and Havoc both cause random exhaust. Rule: Never play Havoc or unupgraded True Grit when deck contains irreplaceable cards. Upgrade True Grit IMMEDIATELY if taken.
+- **Safe Exhaust Targets**: Strikes (once better attacks exist), extra Defends, status cards ([[cards/Slimed]], Wound, Burn, [[cards/Dazed]]).
+- **Never Exhaust**: Last copy of best damage card, last high-block card, core combo pieces ([[cards/Bash]] when Vuln matters), synergy engines ([[cards/Fire Breathing]] when [[cards/Immolate]] is in deck).
- **The Long Fight Problem**: Exhaust cards front-load power into first 5 turns. After that, deck plays like starter. Against scaling enemies, first 5 turns aren't enough. If 30%+ of deck exhausts/is Ethereal, must win in 5-6 turns.
-- **Fiend Fire + Unceasing Top Engine**: Exception to long fight problem. After Fiend Fire exhausts hand, Top draws a card. Cycles through draw pile in one mega-turn. With energy from Bloodletting/Offering, deals 50-100+ damage. Strongest combo observed for Ironclad.
-- **Boss Exhaust Trap**: Mass exhaust (Fiend Fire, Corruption) permanently destroys cards. This is dangerous in two scenarios:
- - **Phased bosses (The Champ, Awakened One):** Phase transitions cleanse debuffs. Debuffs exhausted by Fiend Fire in Phase 1 are gone forever — cannot be re-applied in Phase 2.
- - **Long fights (Time Eater, any 10+ turn fight):** Cards exhausted early are gone for the remaining 8-10 turns. Emergency tools (Dark Shackles -15 Str, Intimidate Weak, block cards) that seem redundant on Turn 2 become the difference between life and death when the enemy has +4-6 Str by Turn 10. Before mass-exhausting, separate every card in hand into "truly expendable" (Strikes, status, duplicates) and "needed later" (debuffs, emergency defense, block). Only mass-exhaust when hand contains NO category-2 cards.
+- **[[cards/Fiend Fire]] + [[relics/Unceasing Top]] Engine**: Exception to long fight problem. After Fiend Fire exhausts hand, Top draws a card. Cycles through draw pile in one mega-turn. With energy from [[cards/Bloodletting]]/[[cards/Offering]], deals 50-100+ damage. Strongest combo observed for [[characters/Ironclad]].
+- **Boss Exhaust Trap**: Mass exhaust (Fiend Fire, [[cards/Corruption]]) permanently destroys cards. This is dangerous in two scenarios:
+ - **Phased bosses ([[bosses/The Champ]], [[bosses/Awakened One]]):** Phase transitions cleanse debuffs. Debuffs exhausted by Fiend Fire in Phase 1 are gone forever — cannot be re-applied in Phase 2.
+ - **Long fights ([[bosses/Time Eater]], any 10+ turn fight):** Cards exhausted early are gone for the remaining 8-10 turns. Emergency tools ([[cards/Dark Shackles]] -15 Str, [[cards/Intimidate]] [[debuffs/Weak]], block cards) that seem redundant on Turn 2 become the difference between life and death when the enemy has +4-6 Str by Turn 10. Before mass-exhausting, separate every card in hand into "truly expendable" (Strikes, status, duplicates) and "needed later" (debuffs, emergency defense, block). Only mass-exhaust when hand contains NO category-2 cards.
heuristics/goals/play.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Goal: Play a Run
-Play Slay the Spire. Win by defeating the Act 3 boss. Make one decision at a time. Every action requires explicit reasoning.
+Play Slay the Spire. Win by defeating the [[acts/Act 3]] boss. Make one decision at a time. Every action requires explicit reasoning.
Start from `ontology/index.md` to understand the game.
heuristics/goals/review.md
@@ -52,5 +52,5 @@ Last 10 runs: floor reached, cause of death, deck quality.
## Principles
- **Be willing to delete.** Smaller and accurate beats comprehensive and noisy.
-- **Name the bottleneck.** Every plateau has a reason. Not "play better" but "we keep entering Act 2 without scaling because card reward evaluation doesn't weight it."
+- **Name the bottleneck.** Every plateau has a reason. Not "play better" but "we keep entering [[acts/Act 2]] without scaling because card reward evaluation doesn't weight it."
- **Measure progress.** Concrete numbers, not vibes.
heuristics/hp-management.md
@@ -1,10 +1,10 @@
# HP Management
- **HP is an act-level resource, not per-fight.** "I can afford 8 damage" thinking is wrong. The goal in EVERY hallway fight is zero damage taken. HP thresholds below are for MAP PATHING decisions.
-- **HP Thresholds for Map Pathing**: ANY fight: 20% minimum. Hallway easy: 25%. Hallway hard (3 Cultists, Centurion+Mystic, Snake Plant): 60%. Elite: 60%. Boss: 70% or Pantograph.
+- **HP Thresholds for Map Pathing**: ANY fight: 20% minimum. Hallway easy: 25%. Hallway hard (3 Cultists, [[enemies/Centurion]]+[[enemies/Mystic]], [[enemies/Snake Plant]]): 60%. Elite: 60%. Boss: 70% or [[relics/Pantograph]].
- **3 Cultists is the most lethal hallway fight** — 6 deaths at 5-53% HP. Triple +3 Str/turn = +9 combined. Hard-scaler exception. 60% entry NON-NEGOTIABLE.
- **What to Do at Low HP**: Take a different path (event/rest/shop). If forced into fight below threshold, use ALL potions turn 1. POTION CHECK ON EVERY LETHAL TURN. After fight drops below 30%, next room MUST be non-combat. Low HP does NOT change combat behavior — you should already be full blocking.
-- **The Act 2 Death Spiral**: Enter Act 2 at reasonable HP → take unnecessary damage (not full blocking) → enter next fight at critical HP → die. After ANY fight below 30%, next room must be non-combat. If map doesn't offer this, run was lost at map selection.
-- **What Causes HP Drain**: Byrd fights (36-73 HP), Centurion+Mystic (25-61 HP), Chosen (30-40 HP), Snake Plant (15-42 HP), Spheric Guardian (30-41 HP), no healing between fights, Fairy consumed in wrong fight, Decay curse compound damage, Vampires event Max HP loss.
-- **Act 2 Preparation**: Enter at full HP. Upgrades matter more (upgrade over rest when above 50%). Healing sustain is critical — Burning Blood cannot compensate for 30-50 HP fights. Minimum TWO healing sources (not counting rest sites) by Act 2 Floor 5.
-- **Act 3 HP Thresholds**: Hallway easy: 40%. Hallway hard: 60%. Transient: 50%. The Maw: 60%. Elite: 70%. Boss: 70% or Pantograph.
+- **The [[acts/Act 2]] Death Spiral**: Enter Act 2 at reasonable HP → take unnecessary damage (not full blocking) → enter next fight at critical HP → die. After ANY fight below 30%, next room must be non-combat. If map doesn't offer this, run was lost at map selection.
+- **What Causes HP Drain**: [[enemies/Byrd]] fights (36-73 HP), Centurion+Mystic (25-61 HP), [[enemies/Chosen]] (30-40 HP), Snake Plant (15-42 HP), [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] (30-41 HP), no healing between fights, Fairy consumed in wrong fight, Decay curse compound damage, Vampires event Max HP loss.
+- **Act 2 Preparation**: Enter at full HP. Upgrades matter more (upgrade over rest when above 50%). Healing sustain is critical — [[relics/Burning Blood]] cannot compensate for 30-50 HP fights. Minimum TWO healing sources (not counting rest sites) by Act 2 Floor 5.
+- **[[acts/Act 3]] HP Thresholds**: Hallway easy: 40%. Hallway hard: 60%. [[encounters/Transient]]: 50%. [[enemies/The Maw]]: 60%. Elite: 70%. Boss: 70% or Pantograph.
heuristics/map.md
@@ -2,12 +2,12 @@
## Relic Economy: Why Elites Matter
-Relics are the primary driver of run strength. Every winning formula was built on specific relics (Snecko Eye, Dead Branch, Mummified Hand, Bottled Lightning, After Image via Bottled Tornado). Elites are the primary source of relics — each elite drops a relic guaranteed. Skipping elites means fewer relics means weaker runs.
+Relics are the primary driver of run strength. Every winning formula was built on specific relics ([[relics/Snecko Eye]], [[relics/Dead Branch]], [[relics/Mummified Hand]], [[relics/Bottled Lightning]], [[cards/After Image]] via [[relics/Bottled Tornado]]). Elites are the primary source of relics — each elite drops a relic guaranteed. Skipping elites means fewer relics means weaker runs.
**Elite targets per act:**
-- **Act 1: Fight 2 elites.** The Act 1 elite pool (Gremlin Nob, Lagavulin, Sentries) is the easiest in the game. Two elite relics + boss relic = 3 relics by Act 2. If the map offers a 2-elite path with rest site coverage, take it. Only settle for 1 elite if the map forces bad rest site spacing or the deck is still starter-quality at Floor 5.
-- **Act 2: Fight 1-2 elites.** Act 2 elites are dangerous but the relic rewards are stronger (rare pool). If HP is above 60% and the deck has its scaling engine online, path through at least 1 elite. Skip only when the deck genuinely cannot handle the matchup (e.g., Brimstone vs Book of Stabbing).
-- **Act 3: Fight 0-1 elites.** Act 3 elites (Giant Head, Reptomancer, Nemesis) are extremely dangerous. Only take an elite if the path naturally includes one and the deck is strong. Don't go out of the way for Act 3 elites.
+- **[[acts/Act 1]]: Fight 2 elites.** The Act 1 elite pool ([[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], [[enemies/Lagavulin]], Sentries) is the easiest in the game. Two elite relics + boss relic = 3 relics by [[acts/Act 2]]. If the map offers a 2-elite path with rest site coverage, take it. Only settle for 1 elite if the map forces bad rest site spacing or the deck is still starter-quality at Floor 5.
+- **Act 2: Fight 1-2 elites.** Act 2 elites are dangerous but the relic rewards are stronger (rare pool). If HP is above 60% and the deck has its scaling engine online, path through at least 1 elite. Skip only when the deck genuinely cannot handle the matchup (e.g., [[relics/Brimstone]] vs [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]]).
+- **[[acts/Act 3]]: Fight 0-1 elites.** Act 3 elites ([[enemies/Giant Head]], [[enemies/Reptomancer]], [[enemies/Nemesis]]) are extremely dangerous. Only take an elite if the path naturally includes one and the deck is strong. Don't go out of the way for Act 3 elites.
**When comparing routes, elites are a POSITIVE, not just a risk.** A route with 1 elite + 1 rest site is often better than a route with 0 elites + 2 rest sites, because the relic from the elite compounds for the rest of the run while the extra rest only heals one fight's worth of damage.
@@ -19,14 +19,14 @@ Relics are the primary driver of run strength. Every winning formula was built o
- **Re-route only when forced.** Follow your act plan. Only deviate if HP drops below safe thresholds.
- **At each floor, verify the planned route is still viable.** Count remaining forced combats to the next rest site. If the path forces 2+ combats and HP is below 50%, re-route.
- Prefer routes with a rest site in the last 1-2 floors before the boss.
-- After a brutal fight (Byrds, Centurion+Mystic), next room MUST be healing, not another combat.
+- After a brutal fight (Byrds, [[enemies/Centurion]]+[[enemies/Mystic]]), next room MUST be healing, not another combat.
- If the path forces an elite at low HP, skip it entirely — take any alternative path.
## Act 2 Route Selection
-- **Act 2 route must include a non-combat room in the first 2-3 floors.** The first Act 2 hallway fight can be 3 Byrds (50-73 HP drain), Spheric Guardian (30-41 HP drain), or other high-damage encounters. If the first 2-3 floors are all Monster rooms with no rest/shop/event escape valve, a single brutal fight leaves no recovery option. Prefer routes where a rest site, shop, or event appears by floor 2-3.
+- **Act 2 route must include a non-combat room in the first 2-3 floors.** The first Act 2 hallway fight can be 3 Byrds (50-73 HP drain), [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] (30-41 HP drain), or other high-damage encounters. If the first 2-3 floors are all Monster rooms with no rest/shop/event escape valve, a single brutal fight leaves no recovery option. Prefer routes where a rest site, shop, or event appears by floor 2-3.
- **Evaluate rest site density across the entire path.** Count forced combats between rest sites on each viable path. A route of M-R-E-M-M-?-E forces 6 fights with only 1 rest site. Prefer paths where rest sites break up combat sequences (e.g., M-M-R-M-R-E gives recovery opportunities). At reduced Max HP (post-Apparitions, post-Vampires), rest site density is even more critical.
-- **Unknown rooms are NOT safe in Act 2.** They resolve as any hallway fight ~50% of the time, including Byrds, Snake Plant, and Spheric Guardian. FIVE deaths confirmed from Unknown rooms resolving as combat. Treat Unknown rooms as Monster rooms when routing in Act 2. **At below 30% HP, Unknown rooms are lethal.**
+- **Unknown rooms are NOT safe in Act 2.** They resolve as any hallway fight ~50% of the time, including Byrds, [[enemies/Snake Plant]], and Spheric Guardian. FIVE deaths confirmed from Unknown rooms resolving as combat. Treat Unknown rooms as Monster rooms when routing in Act 2. **At below 30% HP, Unknown rooms are lethal.**
## Elite Risk Assessment
@@ -38,7 +38,7 @@ Before fighting an elite, check:
**Act 2 Elite Threat Ranking:**
- **Book of Stabbing** (4 deaths, #1 elite killer): Double scaling — +1 hit/turn AND +3 Str/turn. Wounds clog draw pile. NEED: exhaust for Wounds, fast kill, 60%+ HP. **If you have Brimstone, DO NOT FIGHT. Brimstone + Book of Stabbing = unsurvivable.**
-- **Gremlin Leader** (3 deaths): Rally gives +3 Str to ALL enemies per cast. Fight is unwinnable after turn 7. NEED: AOE for gremlins, Shockwave for mass debuff, 60%+ HP.
+- **[[enemies/Gremlin Leader]]** (3 deaths): Rally gives +3 Str to ALL enemies per cast. Fight is unwinnable after turn 7. NEED: AOE for gremlins, [[cards/Shockwave]] for mass debuff, 60%+ HP.
- **Slavers** (2 deaths): 3 enemies, 27+ combined damage from Turn 1. NEED: AOE, mass debuff, block density, 60%+ HP.
## Brimstone Elite Avoidance
@@ -52,7 +52,7 @@ When holding Brimstone, avoid elite paths entirely if HP is below 70%.
## Pre-Boss Routing (last 5 floors)
-- **No HP-for-reward trades.** No Knowing Skull, Golden Idol, or HP-cost events.
+- **No HP-for-reward trades.** No [[events/Knowing Skull]], [[relics/Golden Idol]], or HP-cost events.
- **Skip elites in the last 3 floors.** Relic value < boss HP threshold.
- **Rest over upgrade at every rest site if below 70% HP.**
- **Path priority: Rest > Shop > Event > Monster > Elite.** Invert normal priority.
@@ -60,10 +60,10 @@ When holding Brimstone, avoid elite paths entirely if HP is below 70%.
## Fairy Management
-Fairy in a Bottle is often consumed in Centurion+Mystic fights. If Fairy is your only death insurance, plan paths so Fairy is available for the most dangerous fight (elite or boss).
+[[potions/Fairy in a Bottle]] is often consumed in Centurion+Mystic fights. If Fairy is your only death insurance, plan paths so Fairy is available for the most dangerous fight (elite or boss).
## Mark of the Bloom = No Optional Combat
-If Mark of the Bloom is active, every point of damage is permanent. Under this condition:
-- Do NOT fight Mysterious Sphere, Unknown rooms, or elites unless forced.
+If [[relics/Mark of the Bloom]] is active, every point of damage is permanent. Under this condition:
+- Do NOT fight [[events/Mysterious Sphere]], Unknown rooms, or elites unless forced.
- Take only shops, rest sites (upgrades only), events with known non-combat outcomes.
heuristics/potions/blessing-of-the-forge.md
@@ -2,4 +2,4 @@
Use before a fight, not during. Best used early in the run when most cards are unupgraded (higher chance of upgrading something useful). At rest sites, using this potion gives a free upgrade without spending the rest site action -- use it in addition to the normal Smith action for two upgrades at one rest site.
-Value is moderate due to random targeting. Could upgrade a Strike (low value) or a key card like Bash (high value). Most valuable when the deck has many unupgraded cards, least valuable when most cards are already upgraded.
+Value is moderate due to random targeting. Could upgrade a Strike (low value) or a key card like [[cards/Bash]] (high value). Most valuable when the deck has many unupgraded cards, least valuable when most cards are already upgraded.
heuristics/potions/cultist-potion.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Cultist Potion
-Use Turn 1 of long fights (bosses, elites). The earlier it is used, the more turns of Strength accumulation. In a 9-turn boss fight used Turn 1, provides +1, +2, +3... up to +8 Str by Turn 9. Comparable to Demon Form (+2/turn) over 4 turns.
+Use Turn 1 of long fights (bosses, elites). The earlier it is used, the more turns of [[buffs/Strength]] accumulation. In a 9-turn boss fight used Turn 1, provides +1, +2, +3... up to +8 Str by Turn 9. Comparable to [[cards/Demon Form]] (+2/turn) over 4 turns.
-Synergies: Rampage (Strength compounds with per-play scaling for multiplicative damage growth), Sword Boomerang (multi-hit multiplies Strength bonus), Shivs + Accuracy (Ritual Str stacks with Accuracy bonus on every Shiv -- by Turn 10, each 0-cost Shiv deals 23 damage).
+Synergies: [[cards/Rampage]] (Strength compounds with per-play scaling for multiplicative damage growth), [[cards/Sword Boomerang]] (multi-hit multiplies Strength bonus), Shivs + [[cards/Accuracy]] ([[buffs/Ritual]] Str stacks with Accuracy bonus on every [[cards/Shiv]] -- by Turn 10, each 0-cost Shiv deals 23 damage).
-Best targets: Hexaghost (13-turn fight, massive scaling value), Bronze Automaton (long fight, DPS race), Time Eater (10-turn fight). Do NOT waste on hallway fights -- the fight ends before meaningful scaling occurs.
+Best targets: [[bosses/Hexaghost]] (13-turn fight, massive scaling value), [[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] (long fight, DPS race), [[bosses/Time Eater]] (10-turn fight). Do NOT waste on hallway fights -- the fight ends before meaningful scaling occurs.
heuristics/potions/distilled-chaos.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Distilled Chaos
-Use when draw pile contains high-cost cards (Immolate+, Barricade, Demon Form) that would normally consume your entire turn's energy. The free play is incredible value. Also good when you need an extra action but have 0 energy.
+Use when draw pile contains high-cost cards ([[cards/Immolate]], [[cards/Barricade]], [[cards/Demon Form]]) that would normally consume your entire turn's energy. The free play is incredible value. Also good when you need an extra action but have 0 energy.
-The card played is random (top of draw pile), so outcomes vary. Best used when draw pile is stacked with good options. After Battle Trance or other draw effects thin the draw pile, remaining cards are more predictable.
+The card played is random (top of draw pile), so outcomes vary. Best used when draw pile is stacked with good options. After [[cards/Battle Trance]] or other draw effects thin the draw pile, remaining cards are more predictable.
Best for emergency big-block or big-damage on critical turns. Synergizes with decks that have expensive Power cards or heavy AOE in draw pile.
heuristics/potions/duplication-potion.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Duplication Potion
-Use before a high-value card play. Rampage at 40 damage = 80 total. Carnage+ at 28 = 56 total. Iron Wave+ = double block + double damage (14/14 total). Best with damage cards for burst or Iron Wave+ for combined offense/defense.
+Use before a high-value card play. [[cards/Rampage]] at 40 damage = 80 total. [[cards/Carnage]] at 28 = 56 total. [[cards/Iron Wave]] = double block + double damage (14/14 total). Best with damage cards for burst or Iron Wave+ for combined offense/defense.
-Under Confusion (Snecko Eye), wait for a high-value card to roll 0 cost, then duplicate for double value at zero energy cost. Best confirmed use: Duplication Potion on 0-cost Immolate+ dealt 60 AOE to both Donu and Deca. This is the highest-value play -- target cards whose cost was randomly set to 0.
+Under Confusion ([[relics/Snecko Eye]]), wait for a high-value card to roll 0 cost, then duplicate for double value at zero energy cost. Best confirmed use: Duplication Potion on 0-cost [[cards/Immolate]] dealt 60 AOE to both [[bosses/Donu and Deca]]. This is the highest-value play -- target cards whose cost was randomly set to 0.
heuristics/potions/elixir.md
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
# Elixir
-Use when hand is clogged with status cards (Burns, Wounds, Slimed, Dazed). Mass-exhaust garbage cards to improve future draws. Also useful for controlled deck thinning mid-combat (exhaust extra Strikes/Defends that are diluting key card draws).
+Use when hand is clogged with status cards (Burns, Wounds, [[cards/Slimed]], [[cards/Dazed]]). Mass-exhaust garbage cards to improve future draws. Also useful for controlled deck thinning mid-combat (exhaust extra Strikes/Defends that are diluting key card draws).
## Multi-Select Index Shifting (CRITICAL)
Elixir opens a HAND_SELECT screen where you choose cards one at a time. **After each card is selected, it is removed from the displayed hand and all indices shift down.** You must re-read the hand state after each selection.
-**Example fatal error:** Hand was [Carnage, Wound, Wound, Wound, Defend, Shockwave]. Player intended to exhaust 3 Wounds. After selecting indices 1 and 2 (two Wounds), the hand became [Carnage, Wound, Defend, Shockwave]. The player selected index 3 expecting the third Wound, but index 3 was now Shockwave. Shockwave was exhausted, removing the only Weak source, and the player died to unmitigated damage.
+**Example fatal error:** Hand was [Carnage, Wound, Wound, Wound, Defend, Shockwave]. Player intended to exhaust 3 Wounds. After selecting indices 1 and 2 (two Wounds), the hand became [Carnage, Wound, Defend, Shockwave]. The player selected index 3 expecting the third Wound, but index 3 was now [[cards/Shockwave]]. Shockwave was exhausted, removing the only [[debuffs/Weak]] source, and the player died to unmitigated damage.
**Rule:** During multi-select, ALWAYS re-read the hand after each selection. Never assume indices from the original hand still apply. Select by reading the current hand, not by counting from the original.
heuristics/potions/energy-potion.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Energy Potion
-Use on turns where you have more cards to play than energy allows. Enables playing expensive combos (Bash+ 2E + Shockwave 2E in one turn with only 3 base energy). Use on critical turns, not throwaway turns.
+Use on turns where you have more cards to play than energy allows. Enables playing expensive combos ([[cards/Bash]] 2E + [[cards/Shockwave]] 2E in one turn with only 3 base energy). Use on critical turns, not throwaway turns.
heuristics/potions/essence-of-steel.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Essence of Steel
-Best used Turn 1 of long fights (bosses, elites). Plated Armor 4 provides 4 free block every turn. Over 9 turns = 36 total block. Functionally similar to Metallicize. The earlier it's used, the more total block generated. Note that Plated Armor degrades on unblocked hits, so pair with strong block cards to preserve the stacks.
+Best used Turn 1 of long fights (bosses, elites). [[buffs/Plated Armor]] 4 provides 4 free block every turn. Over 9 turns = 36 total block. Functionally similar to [[cards/Metallicize]]. The earlier it's used, the more total block generated. Note that Plated Armor degrades on unblocked hits, so pair with strong block cards to preserve the stacks.
heuristics/potions/explosive-potion.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Explosive Potion
-Excellent against multi-enemy fights (Collector Torch Heads, Gremlin Leader gremlins, Darklings). Against enemies with Metallicize (Giant Head's 8 Metallicize), Explosive Potion deals damage that bypasses block entirely -- the 10 damage goes straight to HP.
+Excellent against multi-enemy fights (Collector Torch Heads, [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] gremlins, Darklings). Against enemies with [[cards/Metallicize]] ([[enemies/Giant Head]]'s 8 Metallicize), Explosive Potion deals damage that bypasses block entirely -- the 10 damage goes straight to HP.
In close fights where the enemy is 1-10 HP from dying to poison, this potion can be the difference between victory and death. Use before the turn the enemy would otherwise survive by a razor-thin margin. Do not hoard for a future fight if the current fight is lethal. An unused Explosive Potion on a death screen is a strategic failure.
heuristics/potions/fairy-in-a-bottle.md
@@ -1,9 +1,9 @@
# Fairy in a Bottle
-Keep as safety net for boss fights or dangerous elites. Do not waste in hallway fights -- Fairy is often consumed in Centurion+Mystic hallway fights. Plan paths so Fairy is available for the most dangerous fight, not consumed early.
+Keep as safety net for boss fights or dangerous elites. Do not waste in hallway fights -- Fairy is often consumed in [[enemies/Centurion]]+[[enemies/Mystic]] hallway fights. Plan paths so Fairy is available for the most dangerous fight, not consumed early.
-Can be intentionally sacrificed when the math works: if an enemy's attack will trigger Fairy and your Thorns/Bronze Scales will kill the enemy from the revival, this is a legitimate strategy. Calculate exactly before committing.
+Can be intentionally sacrificed when the math works: if an enemy's attack will trigger Fairy and your Thorns/[[relics/Bronze Scales]] will kill the enemy from the revival, this is a legitimate strategy. Calculate exactly before committing.
-In scaling fights (Gremlin Leader), Fairy buys one extra turn but does NOT solve the underlying problem of insufficient damage output vs scaling. Plan to win before Fairy is needed.
+In scaling fights ([[enemies/Gremlin Leader]]), Fairy buys one extra turn but does NOT solve the underlying problem of insufficient damage output vs scaling. Plan to win before Fairy is needed.
-**Fairy triggers on ANY lethal damage, including end-of-turn effects.** Burns in hand deal damage at end of turn. If your HP equals or is less than total Burn damage, Fairy triggers on the Burns, not on the boss's next attack. This wastes the safety net on chip damage instead of saving it for the actual lethal hit. Against Hexaghost, account for Burn EOT damage when calculating whether Fairy will survive to the Inferno turn.
+**Fairy triggers on ANY lethal damage, including end-of-turn effects.** Burns in hand deal damage at end of turn. If your HP equals or is less than total Burn damage, Fairy triggers on the Burns, not on the boss's next attack. This wastes the safety net on chip damage instead of saving it for the actual lethal hit. Against [[bosses/Hexaghost]], account for Burn EOT damage when calculating whether Fairy will survive to the Inferno turn.
heuristics/potions/fear-potion.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Fear Potion
-Use when Bash+ or other Vulnerable sources are not available or not in hand. Excellent at the start of boss fights to guarantee Vulnerable on Turn 1 without spending energy on Bash. Also valuable mid-fight when Vulnerable has expired and Bash is in the discard pile.
+Use when [[cards/Bash]] or other [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] sources are not available or not in hand. Excellent at the start of boss fights to guarantee Vulnerable on Turn 1 without spending energy on Bash. Also valuable mid-fight when Vulnerable has expired and Bash is in the discard pile.
-Vulnerable 3 means 3 turns of 50% increased damage. On a turn with Bludgeon (32 base), Vulnerable adds 16 damage. Over 3 turns of attacks, the total bonus can be 30-60+ damage. Synergizes with Bludgeon (48 vs 32), Immolate (31 vs 21), Reaper (more damage = more healing). Any high-damage turn benefits enormously.
+Vulnerable 3 means 3 turns of 50% increased damage. On a turn with [[cards/Bludgeon]] (32 base), Vulnerable adds 16 damage. Over 3 turns of attacks, the total bonus can be 30-60+ damage. Synergizes with Bludgeon (48 vs 32), [[cards/Immolate]] (31 vs 21), [[cards/Reaper]] (more damage = more healing). Any high-damage turn benefits enormously.
heuristics/potions/fire-potion.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Fire Potion
-Use to finish off an enemy or break through block. Excellent for killing Cultist in Cultist+Chosen fights (removes the scaling threat immediately). Good for killing Mystic when it has block up. At 20 damage, it one-shots many small enemies and chunks Act 2 hallway enemies significantly.
+Use to finish off an enemy or break through block. Excellent for killing [[enemies/Cultist]] in Cultist+[[enemies/Chosen]] fights (removes the scaling threat immediately). Good for killing [[enemies/Mystic]] when it has block up. At 20 damage, it one-shots many small enemies and chunks [[acts/Act 2]] hallway enemies significantly.
heuristics/potions/flex-potion.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Flex Potion
-Use on burst turns where you play multiple Attack cards. Each Attack benefits from +2 damage, but only this turn. Best with Bash+ or high-damage cards on the same turn. Do NOT play on a turn where you only play 1 Attack -- the value is wasted. Plan to play 2-3 Attacks on the Flex turn. Much weaker than Strength Potion due to 1-turn limitation.
+Use on burst turns where you play multiple Attack cards. Each Attack benefits from +2 damage, but only this turn. Best with [[cards/Bash]] or high-damage cards on the same turn. Do NOT play on a turn where you only play 1 Attack -- the value is wasted. Plan to play 2-3 Attacks on the Flex turn. Much weaker than [[potions/Strength Potion]] due to 1-turn limitation.
heuristics/potions/gamblers-brew.md
@@ -4,6 +4,6 @@ Use when the current hand lacks what you need to survive the turn. Discard attac
Best used when you can identify which cards in hand are useless for the current turn. Discard all damage cards when you need block to survive, or discard all block when you have a free turn for burst. The more cards you discard, the higher the variance -- but at 2 HP needing block, maximum redraw is correct.
-Never discard block cards (Shrug It Off, Defend, Ghostly Armor) when facing an attack intent, even if the current plan is to burst damage. Block cards are insurance -- discarding them gambles the entire run on the redraw.
+Never discard block cards ([[cards/Shrug It Off]], Defend, [[cards/Ghostly Armor]]) when facing an attack intent, even if the current plan is to burst damage. Block cards are insurance -- discarding them gambles the entire run on the redraw.
-Exceptional under Confusion (Snecko fight). Confusion randomizes card costs, and a bad roll can make the entire hand unplayable (all 3-cost cards with 3 energy). Gambler's Brew redraws the hand with NEW randomized costs, effectively re-rolling the Confusion RNG.
+Exceptional under Confusion ([[enemies/Snecko]] fight). Confusion randomizes card costs, and a bad roll can make the entire hand unplayable (all 3-cost cards with 3 energy). Gambler's Brew redraws the hand with NEW randomized costs, effectively re-rolling the Confusion RNG.
heuristics/potions/liquid-memories.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Liquid Memories
-Use when a key card is in the discard pile and you need it NOW. Retrieve Impervious for a big incoming attack, Bash+ to refresh Vulnerable, or a kill card for a lethal opportunity. The 0 cost makes it free value.
+Use when a key card is in the discard pile and you need it NOW. Retrieve [[cards/Impervious]] for a big incoming attack, [[cards/Bash]] to refresh [[debuffs/Vulnerable]], or a kill card for a lethal opportunity. The 0 cost makes it free value.
-Do not plan around using Liquid Memories to recover exhausted cards (Intimidate, Shockwave, Impervious, Reaper) -- it cannot retrieve them from the exhaust pile. It will retrieve a different card from discard instead, wasting the potion.
+Do not plan around using Liquid Memories to recover exhausted cards ([[cards/Intimidate]], [[cards/Shockwave]], Impervious, [[cards/Reaper]]) -- it cannot retrieve them from the exhaust pile. It will retrieve a different card from discard instead, wasting the potion.
heuristics/potions/power-potion.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Power Potion
-Best used Turn 1 of a boss fight, especially on a free turn. Powers are permanent, so early setup = maximum value. Can find Metallicize (passive block), Inflame (Strength), or other strong Powers. Always choose the permanent scaling option -- avoid Berserk (self-Vulnerable).
+Best used Turn 1 of a boss fight, especially on a free turn. Powers are permanent, so early setup = maximum value. Can find [[cards/Metallicize]] (passive block), [[cards/Inflame]] ([[buffs/Strength]]), or other strong Powers. Always choose the permanent scaling option -- avoid [[cards/Berserk]] (self-[[debuffs/Vulnerable]]).
heuristics/potions/skill-potion.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Skill Potion
-Best during boss fights when you need a specific answer (mass debuff, big block). Can find Impervious (30 block), Shockwave (mass debuff), or other clutch Skills. The 0 cost means it's free value on any turn.
+Best during boss fights when you need a specific answer (mass debuff, big block). Can find [[cards/Impervious]] (30 block), [[cards/Shockwave]] (mass debuff), or other clutch Skills. The 0 cost means it's free value on any turn.
heuristics/potions/smoke-bomb.md
@@ -2,6 +2,6 @@
Use when the fight is unwinnable and continuing will result in death. Use when estimated incoming damage on the next turn exceeds your current HP + maximum possible block. Do not save it for "a worse situation" -- dying to an avoidable fight is worse than losing rewards from one combat.
-Carry one into Act 2 whenever possible as insurance against dangerous hallway fights (3 Cultists, Byrds, Snake Plant) at low HP. Also valuable in Act 1 against Gremlin Gang fights at low HP -- 4 gremlins deal 22+ combined damage on Turn 1, which is lethal at sub-30% HP without strong AOE.
+Carry one into [[acts/Act 2]] whenever possible as insurance against dangerous hallway fights (3 Cultists, Byrds, [[enemies/Snake Plant]]) at low HP. Also valuable in [[acts/Act 1]] against [[encounters/Gremlin Gang]] fights at low HP -- 4 gremlins deal 22+ combined damage on Turn 1, which is lethal at sub-30% HP without strong AOE.
A single Smoke Bomb can extend a run by multiple floors. An unused Smoke Bomb on a death screen is a strategic failure.
heuristics/potions/snecko-oil.md
@@ -4,4 +4,4 @@ Very high variance -- use as a desperation play or burst turn. Can give incredib
Do NOT use when your survival depends on playing specific cards -- use it only when the default hand is already insufficient. At low HP, the variance can seal a loss by making block cards unplayable on critical survival turns.
-With Snecko Eye synergy: when already Confused, the cost randomization is redundant -- cards are already randomized each draw. The draw 5 effect becomes pure upside with zero additional downside. Use Turn 1 of a boss fight to expand the hand from 7 to approximately 10 cards, enabling massive burst openings.
+With [[relics/Snecko Eye]] synergy: when already [[debuffs/Confused]], the cost randomization is redundant -- cards are already randomized each draw. The draw 5 effect becomes pure upside with zero additional downside. Use Turn 1 of a boss fight to expand the hand from 7 to approximately 10 cards, enabling massive burst openings.
heuristics/potions/strength-potion.md
@@ -2,6 +2,6 @@
Best used Turn 1 of boss fights (compounds over all attacks all fight). In hallway fights, use for kill turns or when Full Block needs extra damage to kill an attacker.
-If HP is below 35% entering a fight, use this potion IMMEDIATELY on turn 1. Do not save it. A Strength Potion shortens fights by 2-3 turns against high-HP enemies (Shelled Parasite, Centurion+Mystic), which means 2-3 fewer turns of incoming damage. An unused Strength Potion on a death screen means 10-20 damage was left on the table.
+If HP is below 35% entering a fight, use this potion IMMEDIATELY on turn 1. Do not save it. A [[buffs/Strength]] Potion shortens fights by 2-3 turns against high-HP enemies ([[enemies/Shelled Parasite]], [[enemies/Centurion]]+[[enemies/Mystic]]), which means 2-3 fewer turns of incoming damage. An unused Strength Potion on a death screen means 10-20 damage was left on the table.
-Against Giant Head with Metallicize 8, even +2 Str on a Strike turns it from 6 damage (bounces off Metallicize) to 8 damage (exactly breaks through). In any fight approaching lethal, use ALL offensive potions before the window closes. Potions do not count as card plays (no Slow interaction).
+Against [[enemies/Giant Head]] with [[cards/Metallicize]] 8, even +2 Str on a Strike turns it from 6 damage (bounces off Metallicize) to 8 damage (exactly breaks through). In any fight approaching lethal, use ALL offensive potions before the window closes. Potions do not count as card plays (no Slow interaction).
heuristics/potions/weak-potion.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Weak Potion
-Use against enemies with big attacks when no other Weak source is available. Against Hexaghost Inferno: reduces 42 to ~28. Against Lagavulin: reduces 18 to ~13. Use on the turn BEFORE a big attack lands.
+Use against enemies with big attacks when no other [[debuffs/Weak]] source is available. Against [[bosses/Hexaghost]] Inferno: reduces 42 to ~28. Against [[enemies/Lagavulin]]: reduces 18 to ~13. Use on the turn BEFORE a big attack lands.
heuristics/relics/anchor.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Anchor
-Free block on turn 1 reduces opening damage. Equivalent to a free Defend + half a Defend. Reduces the need to play block cards on turn 1, freeing energy for Bash+ or other setup. Compounds over the run -- less HP lost to turn 1 attacks in every fight.
+Free block on turn 1 reduces opening damage. Equivalent to a free Defend + half a Defend. Reduces the need to play block cards on turn 1, freeing energy for [[cards/Bash]] or other setup. Compounds over the run -- less HP lost to turn 1 attacks in every fight.
heuristics/relics/ancient-tea-set.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Ancient Tea Set
-ALWAYS rest/smith at the rest site before a boss when you have this relic. The 2E bonus on turn 1 is worth more than most upgrades. With 5E turn 1: set up Metallicize + Dark Embrace + play damage, or Bash+ + 3 Attacks for massive burst. Don't waste the energy -- plan to spend ALL 5E on turn 1.
+ALWAYS rest/smith at the rest site before a boss when you have this relic. The 2E bonus on turn 1 is worth more than most upgrades. With 5E turn 1: set up [[cards/Metallicize]] + [[cards/Dark Embrace]] + play damage, or [[cards/Bash]] + 3 Attacks for massive burst. Don't waste the energy -- plan to spend ALL 5E on turn 1.
heuristics/relics/bag-of-marbles.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Bag of Marbles
-Free Vulnerable on Turn 1 without spending energy or a card play. Enables aggressive Turn 1 plays -- every attack card deals significantly more damage before Bash+ is even needed.
+Free [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] on Turn 1 without spending energy or a card play. Enables aggressive Turn 1 plays -- every attack card deals significantly more damage before [[cards/Bash]] is even needed.
-SYNERGIES: Paper Phrog (1.75x damage to all enemies Turn 1 for free), Immolate+ (28 * 1.75 = 49 AOE Turn 1), Heavy Blade (Str multiplied + Vuln), any front-loaded damage strategy.
+SYNERGIES: [[relics/Paper Phrog]] (1.75x damage to all enemies Turn 1 for free), [[cards/Immolate]] (28 * 1.75 = 49 AOE Turn 1), [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (Str multiplied + Vuln), any front-loaded damage strategy.
One of the best common relics. Free damage amplification on Turn 1 of every combat.
heuristics/relics/bird-faced-urn.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Bird-Faced Urn
-Each Power played becomes a small heal. In Power-heavy decks (3+ Powers), adds 6+ HP healing per combat, partially offsetting the Burning Blood gap. Incentivizes taking and playing Power cards.
+Each Power played becomes a small heal. In Power-heavy decks (3+ Powers), adds 6+ HP healing per combat, partially offsetting the [[relics/Burning Blood]] gap. Incentivizes taking and playing Power cards.
-SYNERGIES: Mummified Hand (free Power plays = free healing), Corruption (frees energy for Powers), multiple cheap Powers (Inflame 1E, Metallicize 1E) maximize triggers per fight.
+SYNERGIES: [[relics/Mummified Hand]] (free Power plays = free healing), [[cards/Corruption]] (frees energy for Powers), multiple cheap Powers ([[cards/Inflame]] 1E, [[cards/Metallicize]] 1E) maximize triggers per fight.
-Best in long fights (bosses, elites) where multiple Powers are played. Less impactful in short hallway fights where only 1 Power is played. Does not replace real healing cards (Reaper, Feed) but supplements them.
+Best in long fights (bosses, elites) where multiple Powers are played. Less impactful in short hallway fights where only 1 Power is played. Does not replace real healing cards ([[cards/Reaper]], Feed) but supplements them.
heuristics/relics/black-star.md
@@ -2,6 +2,6 @@
Doubles relic acquisition from elite fights. In a run fighting 3-4 elites, provides 3-4 extra relics -- each potentially game-changing.
-DECISION FRAMEWORK: Best taken at end of Act 1 (before Act 2/3 elites). Weaker after Act 2 (only Act 3 elites remain). Requires actively routing through elites to realize value -- taking Black Star and avoiding elites wastes the relic slot.
+DECISION FRAMEWORK: Best taken at end of [[acts/Act 1]] (before [[acts/Act 2]]/3 elites). Weaker after Act 2 (only [[acts/Act 3]] elites remain). Requires actively routing through elites to realize value -- taking Black Star and avoiding elites wastes the relic slot.
-RISK: Boss relics replace the starter relic. Losing Burning Blood's sustain must be weighed against long-term relic advantage. For the Defect, losing Cracked Core is relatively low-cost compared to losing Burning Blood.
+RISK: Boss relics replace the starter relic. Losing [[relics/Burning Blood]]'s sustain must be weighed against long-term relic advantage. For the [[characters/Defect]], losing [[relics/Cracked Core]] is relatively low-cost compared to losing Burning Blood.
heuristics/relics/bloody-idol.md
@@ -2,6 +2,6 @@
Provides both economy and sustain. Every combat victory that awards gold also heals 5 HP and gives 5 bonus gold. Over a full act (8-12 gold-awarding events): 40-60 bonus gold and 40-60 HP healing.
-Makes combat more sustainable (5 HP heal per fight stacks with Burning Blood's +6 = 11 HP recovered per fight). Incentivizes taking fights over avoiding them since each fight provides healing.
+Makes combat more sustainable (5 HP heal per fight stacks with [[relics/Burning Blood]]'s +6 = 11 HP recovered per fight). Incentivizes taking fights over avoiding them since each fight provides healing.
-SYNERGIES: Maw Bank (gold gain triggers heal), Golden Idol (+25% gold = more frequent triggers), any relic or event that awards gold.
+SYNERGIES: [[relics/Maw Bank]] (gold gain triggers heal), [[relics/Golden Idol]] (+25% gold = more frequent triggers), any relic or event that awards gold.
heuristics/relics/bottled-flame.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Bottled Flame
-Guarantees a specific Attack on turn 1 every fight. Best target: Bash+ (guaranteed Vulnerable turn 1 in every fight). Other good targets: Carnage+ (guaranteed burst), Feed (guaranteed kill setup). The consistency of always having your best Attack turn 1 is enormous.
+Guarantees a specific Attack on turn 1 every fight. Best target: [[cards/Bash]] (guaranteed [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] turn 1 in every fight). Other good targets: [[cards/Carnage]] (guaranteed burst), Feed (guaranteed kill setup). The consistency of always having your best Attack turn 1 is enormous.
heuristics/relics/bottled-lightning.md
@@ -3,8 +3,8 @@
Guarantees a specific Skill on Turn 1 of every fight. Provides perfect consistency for engine-defining cards.
BEST TARGETS:
-- **Seek/Seek+** (Defect): Guaranteed Turn 1 tutor that finds Echo Form, Barrage+, or any needed card. Strongest known target for Defect.
-- **Offering** (Ironclad): Guaranteed Turn 1 energy burst + draw.
-- **Coolheaded+** (Defect): Guaranteed Turn 1 Frost orb + 3 draws.
+- **[[cards/Seek]]/Seek+** ([[characters/Defect]]): Guaranteed Turn 1 tutor that finds [[cards/Echo Form]], [[cards/Barrage]], or any needed card. Strongest known target for Defect.
+- **[[cards/Offering]]** ([[characters/Ironclad]]): Guaranteed Turn 1 energy burst + draw.
+- **[[cards/Coolheaded]]** (Defect): Guaranteed Turn 1 Frost orb + 3 draws.
Confirmed dominant: Bottled Lightning on Seek+ guaranteed Echo Form+ in hand Turn 1 of every boss fight in the first Defect victory. This single relic-card combination was the foundation of the winning engine.
heuristics/relics/bottled-tornado.md
@@ -2,14 +2,14 @@
Guarantees a specific Power from Turn 1 of every fight. The value depends entirely on which Power is bottled.
-BEST TARGETS (Silent):
-1. **After Image** (victory-proven) -- 1 block per card played from Turn 1.
-2. **Noxious Fumes+** -- Passive poison from Turn 1.
-3. **Footwork+** -- +3 Dex from Turn 1.
-
-BEST TARGETS (Ironclad):
-1. **Barricade** -- Block persists from Turn 1. Excellent with Corruption engine.
-2. **Metallicize** -- Passive block every turn from Turn 1.
-3. **Inflame** -- +2 Str from Turn 1.
+BEST TARGETS ([[characters/Silent]]):
+1. **[[cards/After Image]]** (victory-proven) -- 1 block per card played from Turn 1.
+2. **[[cards/Noxious Fumes]]** -- Passive poison from Turn 1.
+3. **[[cards/Footwork]]** -- +3 Dex from Turn 1.
+
+BEST TARGETS ([[characters/Ironclad]]):
+1. **[[cards/Barricade]]** -- Block persists from Turn 1. Excellent with [[cards/Corruption]] engine.
+2. **[[cards/Metallicize]]** -- Passive block every turn from Turn 1.
+3. **[[cards/Inflame]]** -- +2 Str from Turn 1.
High draft priority when you have a Power that you always want Turn 1. After Image innate is game-defining; a situational Power is less impactful.
heuristics/relics/brimstone.md
@@ -2,16 +2,16 @@
Double-edged scaling relic. Your damage scales fast but enemies become devastating in long fights. Turns every fight into a race.
-WHEN TO TAKE: Deck has burst damage and can end fights in 4-5 turns. Pairs well with Weak application (Bash+, Thunderclap, Shockwave) to offset enemy Strength gains.
+WHEN TO TAKE: Deck has burst damage and can end fights in 4-5 turns. Pairs well with [[debuffs/Weak]] application ([[cards/Bash]], [[cards/Thunderclap]], [[cards/Shockwave]]) to offset enemy [[buffs/Strength]] gains.
**PURCHASE CHECKLIST:**
-1. Is Book of Stabbing a possible Act 2 elite? If yes, DO NOT BUY. Brimstone + Book of Stabbing is unsurvivable.
+1. Is [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]] a possible [[acts/Act 2]] elite? If yes, DO NOT BUY. Brimstone + Book of Stabbing is unsurvivable.
2. Can your deck end fights in 4-5 turns? If no, enemy Str gain will overwhelm you.
3. Do you have Weak application? Weak offsets enemy Str gains.
4. In Act 2 already? Evaluate remaining enemy pool before buying.
-SYNERGIES: Demon Form (+4 Str/turn combined), Reaper (heals more as Strength grows), Heavy Blade (3x/5x multiplied), Pummel/multi-hit (Strength added per hit), Limit Break (doubles accumulated Strength).
+SYNERGIES: [[cards/Demon Form]] (+4 Str/turn combined), [[cards/Reaper]] (heals more as Strength grows), [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (3x/5x multiplied), [[cards/Pummel]]/multi-hit (Strength added per hit), [[cards/Limit Break]] (doubles accumulated Strength).
-ANTI-SYNERGIES: Slow decks, long fights, enemies with multi-hit attacks (Book of Stabbing, Snake Plant).
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Slow decks, long fights, enemies with multi-hit attacks (Book of Stabbing, [[enemies/Snake Plant]]).
CRITICAL DANGER -- BOOK OF STABBING: By turn 5, incoming is 102 damage vs 42 without Brimstone (143% increase). If you have Brimstone, AVOID Book of Stabbing entirely or kill it in 3 turns.
heuristics/relics/bronze-scales.md
@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@
Passive damage every fight requiring no energy, no card plays, no setup. Particularly strong in multi-hit attack fights where the attacker hits many times (5-hit multi-attacks = 15 free damage per turn).
KEY INTERACTIONS:
-- **Donu and Deca**: Can deliver killing blows without requiring a card play (confirmed final 6 damage to Deca at 4 HP).
-- **Multi-hit enemies**: Centurion (10x3), Book of Stabbing (6xN), Gremlin Leader minions -- each hit triggers Bronze Scales.
+- **[[bosses/Donu and Deca]]**: Can deliver killing blows without requiring a card play (confirmed final 6 damage to Deca at 4 HP).
+- **Multi-hit enemies**: [[enemies/Centurion]] (10x3), [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]] (6xN), [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]] minions -- each hit triggers Bronze Scales.
Excellent as a finishing tool when enemies are at low HP. Scales with fight length. No downside.
heuristics/relics/burning-blood.md
@@ -2,4 +2,4 @@
At full HP, up to 6 damage is "free" in hallway fights because Burning Blood refunds it post-combat. This means you can trade HP for tempo (e.g., skip blocking to kill faster) when at max HP. **This buffer does NOT exist if below max HP** -- taking 6 damage at 70/80 HP means you end the fight at 70/80, not 76/80.
-At +6/fight, it cannot compensate for fights that drain 30-50 HP (Byrds, Centurion+Mystic). Do not rely on Burning Blood alone for Act 2 sustain -- you need Reaper, Feed, or rest sites.
+At +6/fight, it cannot compensate for fights that drain 30-50 HP (Byrds, [[enemies/Centurion]]+[[enemies/Mystic]]). Do not rely on Burning Blood alone for [[acts/Act 2]] sustain -- you need [[cards/Reaper]], Feed, or rest sites.
heuristics/relics/busted-crown.md
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
# Busted Crown
-Going from 3E to 4E is transformative. Every turn plays an extra card. On Turn 1, enables Bash+ (2E) + Defend (1E) + another card (1E), impossible at 3E.
+Going from 3E to 4E is transformative. Every turn plays an extra card. On Turn 1, enables [[cards/Bash]] (2E) + Defend (1E) + another card (1E), impossible at 3E.
-DOWNSIDE: Card rewards reduced from 3 to 1. Makes finding specific cards (Reaper, Impervious) much harder.
+DOWNSIDE: Card rewards reduced from 3 to 1. Makes finding specific cards ([[cards/Reaper]], [[cards/Impervious]]) much harder.
-WHEN TO TAKE: When the deck's core cards are already acquired (by Act 1 boss reward). The extra energy helps play those cards more effectively.
+WHEN TO TAKE: When the deck's core cards are already acquired (by [[acts/Act 1]] boss reward). The extra energy helps play those cards more effectively.
-WHEN TO AVOID: If the deck is still missing critical cards (healing, block scaling) and needs card rewards to find them. If Snecko Eye is also being considered, Busted Crown loses value since Snecko provides its own draw advantage.
+WHEN TO AVOID: If the deck is still missing critical cards (healing, block scaling) and needs card rewards to find them. If [[relics/Snecko Eye]] is also being considered, Busted Crown loses value since Snecko provides its own draw advantage.
-The +1E is reliable every turn and immediately impactful. The card reward reduction is real but can be mitigated by shops and Dream Catcher.
+The +1E is reliable every turn and immediately impactful. The card reward reduction is real but can be mitigated by shops and [[relics/Dream Catcher]].
heuristics/relics/calipers.md
@@ -3,12 +3,12 @@
Turns any large block turn into sustained defense. Eliminates the "wasted block" problem where overblocking a small attack loses the excess.
MATH:
-- Impervious+ (40 block): 25 retained next turn.
+- [[cards/Impervious]] (40 block): 25 retained next turn.
- Impervious (30 block): 15 retained next turn.
-- Shrug It Off + 2 Defends (18 block): 3 retained next turn.
+- [[cards/Shrug It Off]] + 2 Defends (18 block): 3 retained next turn.
-SYNERGIES: Impervious/Impervious+ (retains massive amounts), Metallicize (passive block stacks with retention). Strongest in fights with alternating high/low damage turns where you can stack block on low turns and retain for high turns.
+SYNERGIES: Impervious/Impervious+ (retains massive amounts), [[cards/Metallicize]] (passive block stacks with retention). Strongest in fights with alternating high/low damage turns where you can stack block on low turns and retain for high turns.
-ANTI-SYNERGIES: Barricade (already retains all block -- Calipers redundant). Decks generating exactly 5-15 block per turn see no benefit (all decays away).
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: [[cards/Barricade]] (already retains all block -- Calipers redundant). Decks generating exactly 5-15 block per turn see no benefit (all decays away).
Best in decks with Impervious or other large block sources. Does NOT replace the need for healing.
heuristics/relics/centennial-puzzle.md
@@ -4,6 +4,6 @@ Effectively gives an 8-card hand on the turn you first take damage. Extremely st
STRATEGY: On turn 1, if enemies are attacking, you can intentionally under-block to take a small hit and trigger the draw. In multi-turn fights, the 3 extra cards often let you play a devastating setup turn (Power + burst damage + block all in one turn). Against enemies that buff turn 1 (no damage), the trigger happens on turn 2 instead.
-SYNERGIES: Fiend Fire (larger hand = more damage), Metallicize (small chip damage still triggers draw), multi-enemy first attacks.
+SYNERGIES: [[cards/Fiend Fire]] (larger hand = more damage), [[cards/Metallicize]] (small chip damage still triggers draw), multi-enemy first attacks.
-ANTI-SYNERGY: Full block strategies that prevent all damage delay the trigger. Torii reduces damage to 1 but still triggers the draw since HP is still lost.
+ANTI-SYNERGY: Full block strategies that prevent all damage delay the trigger. [[relics/Torii]] reduces damage to 1 but still triggers the draw since HP is still lost.
heuristics/relics/champion-belt.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Champion Belt
-Turns Bash+ into a dual-debuff card (3 Vulnerable + 1 Weak) for free. Every Bash+ play both increases your damage AND reduces incoming damage simultaneously.
+Turns [[cards/Bash]] into a dual-debuff card (3 [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] + 1 [[debuffs/Weak]]) for free. Every Bash+ play both increases your damage AND reduces incoming damage simultaneously.
-SYNERGIES: Bash+ (main trigger), Thunderclap (mass Vuln = mass Weak to all enemies), Paper Phrog (75% Vuln + Weak = massive amplification with damage reduction).
+SYNERGIES: Bash+ (main trigger), [[cards/Thunderclap]] (mass Vuln = mass Weak to all enemies), [[relics/Paper Phrog]] (75% Vuln + Weak = massive amplification with damage reduction).
-Excellent defensive relic requiring no deck changes. Since Bash+ is played in nearly every combat, provides passive Weak application for free. Reduces the need for dedicated Weak sources (Intimidate, Shockwave, Clothesline).
+Excellent defensive relic requiring no deck changes. Since Bash+ is played in nearly every combat, provides passive Weak application for free. Reduces the need for dedicated Weak sources ([[cards/Intimidate]], [[cards/Shockwave]], [[cards/Clothesline]]).
heuristics/relics/charon-s-ashes.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Charon's Ashes
-Creates an exhaust damage engine. With True Grit (exhaust per turn) and Dark Embrace (draw on exhaust), every exhaust = 3 AOE damage + draw. Over 14 turns: ~30+ total damage.
+Creates an exhaust damage engine. With [[cards/True Grit]] (exhaust per turn) and [[cards/Dark Embrace]] (draw on exhaust), every exhaust = 3 AOE damage + draw. Over 14 turns: ~30+ total damage.
DANGER: encourages over-exhausting, which can thin the deck below survival threshold. Don't exhaust cards just for 3 damage -- consider whether you need the cards for later turns.
heuristics/relics/coffee-dripper.md
@@ -6,11 +6,11 @@ WHEN TO TAKE:
- Deck nearly complete and rest sites would be used for upgrades anyway.
- Multiple healing sources exist (healing cards, potions, relics).
- Energy-hungry deck benefits greatly from extra energy.
-- Ironclad with Burning Blood providing baseline healing per fight.
+- [[characters/Ironclad]] with [[relics/Burning Blood]] providing baseline healing per fight.
WHEN TO AVOID:
-- Early Act 1 when HP management is uncertain.
+- Early [[acts/Act 1]] when HP management is uncertain.
- No healing sources beyond rest sites.
-- Silent without healing cards or potions (no passive heal equivalent to Burning Blood).
+- [[characters/Silent]] without healing cards or potions (no passive heal equivalent to Burning Blood).
CRITICAL ANTI-SYNERGY -- COFFEE DRIPPER + ECTOPLASM: No healing from rest sites AND no gold to buy potions/healing at shops. Eliminates two of three primary recovery paths. Avoid this combination unless the deck reliably takes zero damage.
heuristics/relics/cracked-core.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Cracked Core
-Provides a guaranteed Lightning orb from Turn 1 of every fight without spending a card play or energy. The Lightning orb deals 3 passive damage at end of turn. Combined with Zap (0E, channel another), Defect can have 2 Lightning orbs dealing 6 passive damage per turn from Turn 1.
+Provides a guaranteed Lightning orb from Turn 1 of every fight without spending a card play or energy. The Lightning orb deals 3 passive damage at end of turn. Combined with Zap (0E, channel another), [[characters/Defect]] can have 2 Lightning orbs dealing 6 passive damage per turn from Turn 1.
-Also means Dualcast always has a target on Turn 1 (16 burst damage if used immediately). If swapped for a boss relic, Dualcast is dead Turn 1 unless another orb is channeled first.
+Also means [[cards/Dualcast]] always has a target on Turn 1 (16 burst damage if used immediately). If swapped for a boss relic, Dualcast is dead Turn 1 unless another orb is channeled first.
heuristics/relics/cursed-key.md
@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
# Cursed Key
-Strong when the deck can handle curse dilution. +1 Energy is transformative (4E to 5E enables Barricade/Corruption + block in one turn). The curse downside is manageable: only triggers on chests (optional), curses can be removed at shops or via exhaust cards.
+Strong when the deck can handle curse dilution. +1 Energy is transformative (4E to 5E enables [[cards/Barricade]]/[[cards/Corruption]] + block in one turn). The curse downside is manageable: only triggers on chests (optional), curses can be removed at shops or via exhaust cards.
WHEN TO TAKE:
-- Deck has exhaust tools (Second Wind+, Corruption).
-- Deck needs energy for 3E Powers (Barricade, Corruption, Demon Form).
+- Deck has exhaust tools ([[cards/Second Wind]], Corruption).
+- Deck needs energy for 3E Powers (Barricade, Corruption, [[cards/Demon Form]]).
- Chest rooms are avoidable on the current map path.
NORMALITY CURSE WARNING: The Normality curse (limit 3 card plays/turn) is the most dangerous curse from Cursed Key. Cripples Barricade + Corruption engine by limiting skill exhaust chains. Remove at first shop opportunity.
-SYNERGY: Omamori (negates 2 curses from chests), exhaust engines (Corruption makes curse removal trivial).
+SYNERGY: [[relics/Omamori]] (negates 2 curses from chests), exhaust engines (Corruption makes curse removal trivial).
heuristics/relics/data-disk.md
@@ -1,10 +1,10 @@
# Data Disk
-Focus is the Defect's primary scaling stat. Each point adds 1 to every orb passive (Frost block, Lightning damage, Dark scaling). With 3 Frost orbs: +3 block per turn passively. Over a 10-turn fight: 30+ block from a single relic.
+Focus is the [[characters/Defect]]'s primary scaling stat. Each point adds 1 to every orb passive (Frost block, Lightning damage, Dark scaling). With 3 Frost orbs: +3 block per turn passively. Over a 10-turn fight: 30+ block from a single relic.
KEY SYNERGIES:
- **Frost orbs**: +1 passive block per orb per turn. With 5 Frost: +5 passive block/turn.
-- **Defragment**: Stacks for total Focus 2+ making orb passives immediately impactful.
-- **Loop/Emotion Chip**: Focus bonus applies to all passive triggers.
+- **[[cards/Defragment]]**: Stacks for total Focus 2+ making orb passives immediately impactful.
+- **Loop/[[relics/Emotion Chip]]**: Focus bonus applies to all passive triggers.
Provides baseline Focus that makes Frost orb passives meaningful from Turn 1, before additional Focus scaling is established.
heuristics/relics/dead-branch.md
@@ -2,14 +2,14 @@
Transforms exhaust from a resource-spending mechanic into a resource-generating one. The more cards exhausted per fight, the more value generated.
-**CORRUPTION SYNERGY (strongest combo in the game):** With Corruption, every Skill costs 0E and exhausts, and Dead Branch replaces it with a random card. Hand never empties, cycling enormous numbers of cards per turn. Any generated block/damage cards are effectively free. This engine wins any fight it survives to activate.
+**CORRUPTION SYNERGY (strongest combo in the game):** With [[cards/Corruption]], every Skill costs 0E and exhausts, and Dead Branch replaces it with a random card. Hand never empties, cycling enormous numbers of cards per turn. Any generated block/damage cards are effectively free. This engine wins any fight it survives to activate.
SETUP RISK: Corruption (3E unupgraded) + Dead Branch requires surviving the setup turn with no immediate block.
-**FEEL NO PAIN SYNERGY:** Every exhaust triggers both Dead Branch (new card) AND Feel No Pain (3 block). Provides defensive sustain during engine turns.
+**FEEL NO PAIN SYNERGY:** Every exhaust triggers both Dead Branch (new card) AND [[cards/Feel No Pain]] (3 block). Provides defensive sustain during engine turns.
-**SILENT EXHAUST SYNERGY:** Shivs exhaust after play -- each triggers Dead Branch. Storm of Steel -> 5 Shivs -> 5 Dead Branch procs = massive card generation. Terror, Endless Agony, Adrenaline all provide repeatable triggers.
+**SILENT EXHAUST SYNERGY:** Shivs exhaust after play -- each triggers Dead Branch. [[cards/Storm of Steel]] -> 5 Shivs -> 5 Dead Branch procs = massive card generation. [[cards/Terror]], [[cards/Endless Agony]], [[cards/Adrenaline]] all provide repeatable triggers.
-When you have Dead Branch, prioritize cards that exhaust (Corruption, Second Wind, True Grit+, Fiend Fire). Corruption becomes the highest-priority card to acquire.
+When you have Dead Branch, prioritize cards that exhaust (Corruption, [[cards/Second Wind]], [[cards/True Grit]], [[cards/Fiend Fire]]). Corruption becomes the highest-priority card to acquire.
Always a strong relic pick. Universally strong with no significant anti-synergies.
heuristics/relics/ectoplasm.md
@@ -7,12 +7,12 @@ WHEN TO TAKE:
- No other boss relic is better for the deck.
WHEN TO AVOID:
-- Early in Act 1 when shops are still critical for deck building.
+- Early in [[acts/Act 1]] when shops are still critical for deck building.
- Card removal needed (Strikes/Defends polluting deck).
- Deck is energy-efficient (3E is enough).
-ANTI-SYNERGIES: Maw Bank, Ceramic Fish, Golden Idol, Bloody Idol (all negated). Shops in general.
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: [[relics/Maw Bank]], [[relics/Ceramic Fish]], [[relics/Golden Idol]], [[relics/Bloody Idol]] (all negated). Shops in general.
-**CRITICAL -- Coffee Dripper + Ectoplasm**: No healing from rest AND no gold for potions/healing at shops. Eliminates two of three primary recovery paths. Confirmed lethal. Avoid unless deck reliably takes zero damage.
+**CRITICAL -- [[relics/Coffee Dripper]] + Ectoplasm**: No healing from rest AND no gold for potions/healing at shops. Eliminates two of three primary recovery paths. Confirmed lethal. Avoid unless deck reliably takes zero damage.
Spend all gold BEFORE taking Ectoplasm if possible. Boss relics are offered after the boss fight, so a prior shop is the last chance.
heuristics/relics/enchiridion.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Enchiridion
-Free Power every fight. Can generate Metallicize, Inflame, Corruption, Dark Embrace, or other Powers. The 0 cost means it never competes with other turn 1 plays. High variance -- sometimes generates a duplicate of a Power you already have. Overall excellent value for free.
+Free Power every fight. Can generate [[cards/Metallicize]], [[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Corruption]], [[cards/Dark Embrace]], or other Powers. The 0 cost means it never competes with other turn 1 plays. High variance -- sometimes generates a duplicate of a Power you already have. Overall excellent value for free.
-DANGER -- UNCONTROLLABLE POWER SELECTION: The Power is random. Enchiridion can generate Brutality (lose 1 HP/turn), which is actively harmful in long fights or at low HP. Before playing an Enchiridion-generated Power, evaluate whether it helps or hurts in THIS specific fight. Do not autopilot "free Power = always play."
+DANGER -- UNCONTROLLABLE POWER SELECTION: The Power is random. Enchiridion can generate [[cards/Brutality]] (lose 1 HP/turn), which is actively harmful in long fights or at low HP. Before playing an Enchiridion-generated Power, evaluate whether it helps or hurts in THIS specific fight. Do not autopilot "free Power = always play."
-DANGEROUS POWERS: Brutality (HP drain in long fights), Berserk (Vulnerable to self), Combust (HP drain per turn). Safe powers: Metallicize, Inflame, Dark Embrace, Feel No Pain, Rage.
+DANGEROUS POWERS: Brutality (HP drain in long fights), [[cards/Berserk]] ([[debuffs/Vulnerable]] to self), [[cards/Combust]] (HP drain per turn). Safe powers: Metallicize, Inflame, Dark Embrace, [[cards/Feel No Pain]], Rage.
heuristics/relics/eternal-feather.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Eternal Feather
-Supplements Burning Blood's flat 6 HP/fight. At 15 cards: +9 HP/fight (total 15 with Burning Blood). At 20 cards: +12 HP/fight. At 25 cards: +15 HP/fight. Rewards larger decks, which conflicts with typical deck-thinning strategy.
+Supplements [[relics/Burning Blood]]'s flat 6 HP/fight. At 15 cards: +9 HP/fight (total 15 with Burning Blood). At 20 cards: +12 HP/fight. At 25 cards: +15 HP/fight. Rewards larger decks, which conflicts with typical deck-thinning strategy.
Do not take extra cards just to increase Eternal Feather healing -- deck quality matters more than marginal HP gains. In a typical 15-20 card deck, the healing is modest (9-12 HP). Only valuable when the run already has a larger deck and no better relic options. Mediocre overall.
heuristics/relics/fossilized-helix.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Fossilized Helix
-Prevents the first instance of HP loss each combat. Extremely strong in Act 1 where most enemies attack once per turn.
+Prevents the first instance of HP loss each combat. Extremely strong in [[acts/Act 1]] where most enemies attack once per turn.
## Multi-Hit Interaction (CRITICAL)
@@ -21,5 +21,5 @@ Fossilized Helix prevents **HP loss**, not **damage**. Block is consumed BEFORE
## Valuation
- Against single-hit attacks: prevents the full hit. Excellent.
-- Against multi-hit attacks (Book of Stabbing, Byrds, Slimes): prevents only the overflow from hit 1. Much weaker. Plan block as if Helix does not exist for hits 2+.
+- Against multi-hit attacks ([[enemies/Book of Stabbing]], Byrds, Slimes): prevents only the overflow from hit 1. Much weaker. Plan block as if Helix does not exist for hits 2+.
- Against enemies with both single and multi-hit patterns: value depends on which pattern they use turn 1. Do not rely on Helix for survival against multi-hit enemies.
heuristics/relics/frozen-egg.md
@@ -1,6 +1,6 @@
# Frozen Egg
-Strong in decks that plan to take more Powers. Saves rest site upgrades for non-Power cards. Ironclad Powers include Metallicize, Inflame, Evolve, Barricade, Demon Form, Corruption, Dark Embrace, Fire Breathing -- many are high-priority upgrades.
+Strong in decks that plan to take more Powers. Saves rest site upgrades for non-Power cards. [[characters/Ironclad]] Powers include [[cards/Metallicize]], [[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Evolve]], [[cards/Barricade]], [[cards/Demon Form]], [[cards/Corruption]], [[cards/Dark Embrace]], [[cards/Fire Breathing]] -- many are high-priority upgrades.
BEST SYNERGIES:
- **Mid-run Power pickup**: Getting Frozen Egg before taking Metallicize means Metallicize+ (4 block/turn) for free.
heuristics/relics/fusion-hammer.md
@@ -1,19 +1,19 @@
# Fusion Hammer
-Excellent when the deck is already well-upgraded. +1 Energy/turn is transformative. Rest sites become heal-only (and Ancient Tea Set always triggers).
+Excellent when the deck is already well-upgraded. +1 Energy/turn is transformative. Rest sites become heal-only (and [[relics/Ancient Tea Set]] always triggers).
WHEN TO TAKE:
-- Most key cards already upgraded (Bash+, core block/damage cards).
+- Most key cards already upgraded ([[cards/Bash]], core block/damage cards).
- Deck needs energy more than upgrades.
-- Blessing of the Forge potion available (one-time mass upgrade substitute).
+- [[potions/Blessing of the Forge]] potion available (one-time mass upgrade substitute).
- Ancient Tea Set equipped (always triggers since you must rest).
WHEN TO AVOID:
-- Critical cards still unupgraded (Bash, Corruption, True Grit, Limit Break).
+- Critical cards still unupgraded (Bash, [[cards/Corruption]], [[cards/True Grit]], [[cards/Limit Break]]).
- Deck recently assembled and needs 3+ upgrades.
SYNERGIES:
- Ancient Tea Set (always triggers since you can only rest, not smith).
-- Armaments+ (upgrade ALL cards in hand, substitutes for smith in-combat).
-- Regal Pillow (extra healing since you always rest).
-- **Apotheosis** (becomes the single most valuable card -- provides the ONLY way to upgrade cards). Actively seek Apotheosis from colorless events when Fusion Hammer is equipped.
+- [[cards/Armaments]] (upgrade ALL cards in hand, substitutes for smith in-combat).
+- [[relics/Regal Pillow]] (extra healing since you always rest).
+- **[[cards/Apotheosis]]** (becomes the single most valuable card -- provides the ONLY way to upgrade cards). Actively seek Apotheosis from colorless events when [[cards/Fusion]] Hammer is equipped.
heuristics/relics/gambling-chip.md
@@ -1,14 +1,14 @@
# Gambling Chip
-The mulligan effect lets you find key cards (Bash+, Inflame+, Shockwave, Whirlwind) on Turn 1. Consistently useful in every combat.
+The mulligan effect lets you find key cards ([[cards/Bash]], [[cards/Inflame]], [[cards/Shockwave]], [[cards/Whirlwind]]) on Turn 1. Consistently useful in every combat.
FIGHT-SPECIFIC MULLIGANS:
-- **Gremlin Leader**: Discard block, keep attacks. Turn economy demands damage from Turn 1.
-- **Spheric Guardian**: Discard block, keep Artifact strippers and attacks. DPS check.
+- **[[enemies/Gremlin Leader]]**: Discard block, keep attacks. Turn economy demands damage from Turn 1.
+- **[[enemies/Spheric Guardian]]**: Discard block, keep [[buffs/Artifact]] strippers and attacks. DPS check.
- **Hallway fights**: Discard expensive Powers, keep Bash+ and cheap attacks.
-- **Chosen/Hex**: Discard Skills (trigger Hex adding Dazed). Keep Attacks and Powers.
+- **[[enemies/Chosen]]/[[debuffs/Hex]]**: Discard Skills (trigger Hex adding [[cards/Dazed]]). Keep Attacks and Powers.
- **Boss fights**: Discard based on boss pattern.
SYNERGIES: Lean decks (15-17 cards, mulligan more likely to find specific answers). Cards with fight-specific value can be safely included because they can be discarded when not needed.
-**SNECKO EYE SYNERGY**: When Snecko assigns expensive costs (3E) to key cards, Gambling Chip lets you discard and redraw with new cost rolls. Dramatically improves Turn 1 consistency.
+**SNECKO EYE SYNERGY**: When [[enemies/Snecko]] assigns expensive costs (3E) to key cards, Gambling Chip lets you discard and redraw with new cost rolls. Dramatically improves Turn 1 consistency.
heuristics/relics/ginger.md
@@ -3,12 +3,12 @@
Immunity to an entire debuff type is always valuable.
KEY INTERACTIONS:
-1. **Mind Bloom "I am Healthy" becomes free.** The Doubt curse (1 Weak/turn) is completely harmless with Ginger. Choose it for a free full heal, then remove Doubt at the next shop.
-2. **Enemy Weak applications ignored.** Chosen's Hex, Collector's debuff, Time Eater's Ripple -- no effect on damage output.
+1. **[[events/Mind Bloom]] "I am Healthy" becomes free.** The Doubt curse (1 [[debuffs/Weak]]/turn) is completely harmless with Ginger. Choose it for a free full heal, then remove Doubt at the next shop.
+2. **Enemy Weak applications ignored.** [[enemies/Chosen]]'s [[debuffs/Hex]], Collector's debuff, [[bosses/Time Eater]]'s Ripple -- no effect on damage output.
3. **Block calculations simplified.** Damage output is always at full value.
SYNERGIES:
-- Shiv decks benefit most since Weak would reduce every individual Shiv hit.
+- [[cards/Shiv]] decks benefit most since Weak would reduce every individual Shiv hit.
- Boss fights with Weak application (Collector, Time Eater) become safer offensively.
High draft priority. The Mind Bloom interaction alone can provide 15-30 HP of free healing.
heuristics/relics/gremlin-horn.md
@@ -3,8 +3,8 @@
Transforms multi-enemy fights from resource-draining attrition into snowball opportunities. Against 4 Gremlins, killing 3 provides +3E and +3 cards -- enough for an entire extra turn's worth of actions.
SYNERGIES:
-- **AOE damage** (Thunderclap, Cleave, Whirlwind) -- weakens multiple enemies, triggering chain kills.
-- **Slime Boss split** -- kills during the split phase refund energy for handling new slimes.
-- **Any multi-enemy fight** -- 3 Cultists, Gremlins, Slavers, Centurion+Mystic.
+- **AOE damage** ([[cards/Thunderclap]], [[cards/Cleave]], [[cards/Whirlwind]]) -- weakens multiple enemies, triggering chain kills.
+- **[[bosses/Slime Boss]] split** -- kills during the split phase refund energy for handling new slimes.
+- **Any multi-enemy fight** -- 3 Cultists, Gremlins, Slavers, [[enemies/Centurion]]+[[enemies/Mystic]].
-Always take. No downside. In single-enemy fights it does nothing harmful. In multi-enemy fights (the most common dangerous Act 2 encounters), it is one of the strongest relics available.
+Always take. No downside. In single-enemy fights it does nothing harmful. In multi-enemy fights (the most common dangerous [[acts/Act 2]] encounters), it is one of the strongest relics available.
heuristics/relics/happy-flower.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Happy Flower
-Passive energy gain in long fights. In a 9-turn fight: 3 extra energy total. Less impactful than Lantern but provides sustained value. Plan big plays on the turns when the extra energy arrives.
