+Passive energy gain in long fights. In a 9-turn fight: 3 extra energy total. Less impactful than [[relics/Lantern]] but provides sustained value. Plan big plays on the turns when the extra energy arrives.
heuristics/relics/horn-cleat.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Horn Cleat
-Provides free 14 block on turn 2, which is often the first big attack turn from enemies. Effectively a free Defend + Shrug It Off on the turn you need it most. No action required -- automatic.
+Provides free 14 block on turn 2, which is often the first big attack turn from enemies. Effectively a free Defend + [[cards/Shrug It Off]] on the turn you need it most. No action required -- automatic.
-14 block covers most single-enemy attacks in Act 1 and partially mitigates Act 2 attacks. Frees up energy on turn 2 to play offensive cards instead of blocking. Helpful against Cultists (buff turn 1, attack turn 2 -- Horn Cleat covers the attack).
+14 block covers most single-enemy attacks in [[acts/Act 1]] and partially mitigates [[acts/Act 2]] attacks. Frees up energy on turn 2 to play offensive cards instead of blocking. Helpful against Cultists (buff turn 1, attack turn 2 -- Horn Cleat covers the attack).
heuristics/relics/hovering-kite.md
@@ -3,11 +3,11 @@
Effectively raises base energy from 3 to 4 (or 4 to 5) when combined with any discard source.
KEY SYNERGIES:
-- **Tools of the Trade**: Discards at start of turn, triggering Kite automatically. +1E every turn with zero effort.
-- **Survivor/Survivor+**: Manual discard trigger. Block + energy in one play.
-- **Tactician+**: When discarded, returns 2E. Combined with Kite's +1E, a single TotT discard of Tactician+ generates +3E total.
-- **Calculated Gamble**: Discard entire hand, Kite triggers on first discard.
+- **[[cards/Tools of the Trade]]**: Discards at start of turn, triggering Kite automatically. +1E every turn with zero effort.
+- **[[cards/Survivor]]/Survivor+**: Manual discard trigger. Block + energy in one play.
+- **[[cards/Tactician]]**: When discarded, returns 2E. Combined with Kite's +1E, a single TotT discard of Tactician+ generates +3E total.
+- **[[cards/Calculated Gamble]]**: Discard entire hand, Kite triggers on first discard.
Excellent when the deck has discard synergy (Tools of the Trade, Survivor+, Tactician+). Also useful for discarding Burns and curses -- turning dead cards into energy.
-ANTI-SYNERGIES: Decks without discard sources get inconsistent value. Snecko Eye randomizes costs, making +1E less impactful when cards already cost 0.
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Decks without discard sources get inconsistent value. [[relics/Snecko Eye]] randomizes costs, making +1E less impactful when cards already cost 0.
heuristics/relics/incense-burner.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Incense Burner
-The Intangible turn is a free turn for maximum offense -- play all attacks, use no block. Track the counter to plan around it. Against bosses with predictable big-hit turns (Hexaghost Inferno, Bronze Automaton Hyper Beam), Intangible can negate the most dangerous attack entirely.
+The [[buffs/Intangible]] turn is a free turn for maximum offense -- play all attacks, use no block. Track the counter to plan around it. Against bosses with predictable big-hit turns ([[bosses/Hexaghost]] Inferno, [[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] Hyper Beam), Intangible can negate the most dangerous attack entirely.
-Best in long fights (bosses, elites) where the counter triggers 2+ times. A 12-turn fight gets 2 Intangible turns. Combined with scaling (Rampage, Demon Form), Intangible turns enable all-offense plays that accelerate the kill.
+Best in long fights (bosses, elites) where the counter triggers 2+ times. A 12-turn fight gets 2 Intangible turns. Combined with scaling ([[cards/Rampage]], [[cards/Demon Form]]), Intangible turns enable all-offense plays that accelerate the kill.
MATCHUPS: Excellent vs Hexaghost (Intangible on Inferno = 42 damage reduced to 1). Excellent vs Bronze Automaton (Hyper Beam = 45-57 reduced to 1).
heuristics/relics/juzu-bracelet.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Juzu Bracelet
-Extremely high value. Unknown rooms become free value (events) instead of potential death traps. Fundamentally changes Act 2 routing -- Unknown rooms become preferable to Monster rooms.
+Extremely high value. Unknown rooms become free value (events) instead of potential death traps. Fundamentally changes [[acts/Act 2]] routing -- Unknown rooms become preferable to Monster rooms.
-In Act 2, Unknown rooms can spawn Spheric Guardian (30-41 HP drain), Byrds (36-73 HP drain), or Snake Plant (15-42 HP drain). Juzu Bracelet eliminates this risk entirely.
+In Act 2, Unknown rooms can spawn [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] (30-41 HP drain), Byrds (36-73 HP drain), or [[enemies/Snake Plant]] (15-42 HP drain). Juzu Bracelet eliminates this risk entirely.
Worth buying at ~147 gold if gold allows. Route through Unknown rooms for guaranteed safe rooms.
heuristics/relics/kunai.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Kunai
-Passive Dexterity scaling from playing Attacks. In long fights (bosses, elites), compounds: by turn 5-6, block cards generate 3-5 more block each, drastically improving survivability.
+Passive [[buffs/Dexterity]] scaling from playing Attacks. In long fights (bosses, elites), compounds: by turn 5-6, block cards generate 3-5 more block each, drastically improving survivability.
-Best in Attack-heavy decks with cheap Attacks (Anger at 0E, Pommel Strike, Twin Strike, Headbutt). Pairs naturally with Ironclad's Attack-heavy card pool. Purely upside with no drawback -- always take when offered.
+Best in Attack-heavy decks with cheap Attacks ([[cards/Anger]] at 0E, [[cards/Pommel Strike]], [[cards/Twin Strike]], [[cards/Headbutt]]). Pairs naturally with [[characters/Ironclad]]'s Attack-heavy card pool. Purely upside with no drawback -- always take when offered.
-SYNERGY: Shuriken (same trigger -- playing 3 Attacks gives +1 Dex AND +1 Str if both relics held).
+SYNERGY: [[relics/Shuriken]] (same trigger -- playing 3 Attacks gives +1 Dex AND +1 Str if both relics held).
heuristics/relics/lantern.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Lantern
-Enables stronger openers. Play a setup Power AND still have energy for damage/block on turn 1. Weaker than Ancient Tea Set (+2E but only after rest sites) but more consistent. Use the extra energy on turn 1 -- don't waste it.
+Enables stronger openers. Play a setup Power AND still have energy for damage/block on turn 1. Weaker than [[relics/Ancient Tea Set]] (+2E but only after rest sites) but more consistent. Use the extra energy on turn 1 -- don't waste it.
heuristics/relics/letter-opener.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Letter Opener
-Turns every Defend into Defend + 5 damage. In fights where you play 2-3 Skills per turn, adds 10-15 free damage. Over a 12-turn fight: ~40-120 free damage. Conflicts with Gremlin Nob (can't play Skills) and Chosen (Skills trigger Hex). Synergizes with Intimidate (0E Skill = free 5 damage + Weak).
+Turns every Defend into Defend + 5 damage. In fights where you play 2-3 Skills per turn, adds 10-15 free damage. Over a 12-turn fight: ~40-120 free damage. Conflicts with [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]] (can't play Skills) and [[enemies/Chosen]] (Skills trigger [[debuffs/Hex]]). Synergizes with [[cards/Intimidate]] (0E Skill = free 5 damage + [[debuffs/Weak]]).
heuristics/relics/liquid-bronze.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Liquid Bronze
-Passive retaliation. Against multi-hit attacks (Hexaghost 7x6): 7*3 = 21 thorns damage from one Inferno. Against multiple attackers: 3 damage to each. However, damage reduction (Weak) is more important than damage reflection. Don't rely on thorns as a primary damage source.
+Passive retaliation. Against multi-hit attacks ([[bosses/Hexaghost]] 7x6): 7*3 = 21 thorns damage from one Inferno. Against multiple attackers: 3 damage to each. However, damage reduction ([[debuffs/Weak]]) is more important than damage reflection. Don't rely on thorns as a primary damage source.
heuristics/relics/lizard-tail.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Lizard Tail
-A safety net that heals significantly more than Fairy in a Bottle (50% Max HP vs ~30%). Does not take a potion slot. Once triggered, gone for the rest of the run.
+A safety net that heals significantly more than [[potions/Fairy in a Bottle]] (50% Max HP vs ~30%). Does not take a potion slot. Once triggered, gone for the rest of the run.
Can be deliberately "spent" by accepting lethal damage on a turn where you maximize damage output instead of blocking, knowing the revival will sustain you for killing turns. Not just a safety net -- it can be a strategic resource in DPS-race fights.
-Confirmed victory-enabling: revived from 0 HP to 35 HP during Time Eater fight, enabling 2 more turns of burst that killed the boss from 139 HP.
+Confirmed victory-enabling: revived from 0 HP to 35 HP during [[bosses/Time Eater]] fight, enabling 2 more turns of burst that killed the boss from 139 HP.
heuristics/relics/mark-of-pain.md
@@ -3,15 +3,15 @@
Strong boss relic. 4E per turn is a massive upgrade. The Wound downside is manageable and can become an advantage with the right cards.
SYNERGIES:
-- **Evolve+ (BEST)**: Each Wound drawn triggers 2 additional draws. Playing attacks generates future card advantage. If Evolve+ is already in deck, Mark of Pain is the clear best boss relic choice.
+- **[[cards/Evolve]] (BEST)**: Each Wound drawn triggers 2 additional draws. Playing attacks generates future card advantage. If Evolve+ is already in deck, Mark of Pain is the clear best boss relic choice.
- **Evolve (unupgraded)**: Each Wound triggers 1 draw. Upgrade Evolve ASAP after taking Mark of Pain.
-- **Fire Breathing**: Wounds trigger Fire Breathing damage to all enemies.
-- **Second Wind**: Exhausts Wounds for 5 block each.
-- **True Grit+**: Chosen exhaust can target Wounds specifically.
+- **[[cards/Fire Breathing]]**: Wounds trigger Fire Breathing damage to all enemies.
+- **[[cards/Second Wind]]**: Exhausts Wounds for 5 block each.
+- **[[cards/True Grit]]**: [[enemies/Chosen]] exhaust can target Wounds specifically.
ANTI-SYNERGIES:
- **No Evolve/Status management**: Wounds clog draw pile. By turn 4-5, drawing 2-3 Wounds/hand reduces effective hand to 2-3 real cards.
-- **Battle Trance**: "Cannot draw additional cards" prevents Evolve triggering on Wound draws that turn.
+- **[[cards/Battle Trance]]**: "Cannot draw additional cards" prevents Evolve triggering on Wound draws that turn.
WHEN TO TAKE: When Evolve is already in deck. A 15+ card deck dilutes Wounds better than a 10-card deck.
heuristics/relics/meal-ticket.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Meal Ticket
-Makes shop visits double as healing stops. At low HP, pathing through shops provides 15 HP recovery in addition to purchasing options. More healing than Burning Blood (6 HP) and stacks with it. Prioritize shop paths on the map when carrying Meal Ticket and low on HP.
+Makes shop visits double as healing stops. At low HP, pathing through shops provides 15 HP recovery in addition to purchasing options. More healing than [[relics/Burning Blood]] (6 HP) and stacks with it. Prioritize shop paths on the map when carrying Meal Ticket and low on HP.
-15 HP per shop is significant -- roughly one fight's worth of damage absorption. In Act 2 where HP drain is the primary cause of death, Meal Ticket can extend a run by several floors if the map has shop nodes available.
+15 HP per shop is significant -- roughly one fight's worth of damage absorption. In [[acts/Act 2]] where HP drain is the primary cause of death, Meal Ticket can extend a run by several floors if the map has shop nodes available.
LIMITATION: Only triggers at shops (1-2 per act). Cannot be relied upon as a primary healing source, but excellent supplemental healing alongside Burning Blood.
heuristics/relics/meat-on-the-bone.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Meat on the Bone
-In Act 2, fights routinely drain 30-50 HP and leave the player below 50%. Meat on the Bone provides double the healing of Burning Blood (12 vs 6) when it triggers. Combined with Burning Blood, a fight ending below 50% heals 18 HP total.
+In [[acts/Act 2]], fights routinely drain 30-50 HP and leave the player below 50%. Meat on the Bone provides double the healing of [[relics/Burning Blood]] (12 vs 6) when it triggers. Combined with Burning Blood, a fight ending below 50% heals 18 HP total.
Does not trigger when HP is above 50% at fight end. Efficient fights where block prevents most damage get zero value. Does not help DURING combat -- only after.
-Partially addresses the Act 2 HP sustain problem. Not a replacement for in-combat healing (Reaper, Feed) but a solid supplementary source. Always take when offered -- unconditional upside with no drawback.
+Partially addresses the Act 2 HP sustain problem. Not a replacement for in-combat healing ([[cards/Reaper]], Feed) but a solid supplementary source. Always take when offered -- unconditional upside with no drawback.
heuristics/relics/mercury-hourglass.md
@@ -2,4 +2,4 @@
Free passive AOE damage every turn. Over a 10-turn boss fight: 30 free damage to every enemy. Against multi-enemy fights (Collector + 2 Torch Heads): 3 to each = 9 total per turn.
-Best in long fights and multi-enemy encounters. Vulnerable on enemies amplifies it (3 * 1.5 = 4 per enemy). Can finish low-HP enemies at start of turn without triggering additional effects (killed Snake Plant without triggering Malleable block).
+Best in long fights and multi-enemy encounters. [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] on enemies amplifies it (3 * 1.5 = 4 per enemy). Can finish low-HP enemies at start of turn without triggering additional effects (killed [[enemies/Snake Plant]] without triggering [[buffs/Malleable]] block).
heuristics/relics/mummified-hand.md
@@ -4,11 +4,11 @@ Excellent in Power-heavy decks. Each Power play enables a free card -- effective
SYNERGIES:
- **Multiple Powers**: More Powers = more free plays.
-- **Barricade + Metallicize setup turns**: Playing Barricade triggers a free Defend+ or Strike+ on the setup turn. Partially offsets Barricade's cost.
-- **Immolate+**: A free Immolate+ is 28-31 damage for zero energy.
+- **[[cards/Barricade]] + [[cards/Metallicize]] setup turns**: Playing Barricade triggers a free Defend+ or Strike+ on the setup turn. Partially offsets Barricade's cost.
+- **[[cards/Immolate]]**: A free Immolate+ is 28-31 damage for zero energy.
-DEFECT SYNERGY: Excellent Power density (Echo Form, Loop, Capacitor, Defragment, Creative AI). Mummified Hand + Echo Form + Loop generates multiple free plays during setup, offsetting Echo Form's 3E cost.
+DEFECT SYNERGY: Excellent Power density ([[cards/Echo Form]], Loop, [[cards/Capacitor]], [[cards/Defragment]], [[cards/Creative AI]]). Mummified Hand + Echo Form + Loop generates multiple free plays during setup, offsetting Echo Form's 3E cost.
-ANTI-SYNERGIES: Decks with 0-1 Powers (trigger too rare). Corruption (Skills already cost 0, so free card is less impactful if it hits a Skill).
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Decks with 0-1 Powers (trigger too rare). [[cards/Corruption]] (Skills already cost 0, so free card is less impactful if it hits a Skill).
-Scales in value as more Powers are added. Confirmed dominant across both Ironclad and Defect Power-heavy archetypes.
+Scales in value as more Powers are added. Confirmed dominant across both [[characters/Ironclad]] and [[characters/Defect]] Power-heavy archetypes.
heuristics/relics/mutagenic-strength.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Mutagenic Strength
-Provides a powerful turn-1 burst window. With +3 Strength, the combined effect across multiple cards on turn 1 can add 10-20 extra damage. After turn 1, the Strength disappears and has no further effect.
+Provides a powerful turn-1 burst window. With +3 [[buffs/Strength]], the combined effect across multiple cards on turn 1 can add 10-20 extra damage. After turn 1, the Strength disappears and has no further effect.
-Front-load damage on turn 1. Play as many Attack cards as possible before the Strength expires. Pairs especially well with AOE attacks (Immolate, Thunderclap) where the bonus applies to each enemy hit. The free relic is always worth taking from the event.
+Front-load damage on turn 1. Play as many Attack cards as possible before the Strength expires. Pairs especially well with AOE attacks ([[cards/Immolate]], [[cards/Thunderclap]]) where the bonus applies to each enemy hit. The free relic is always worth taking from the event.
heuristics/relics/necronomicon.md
@@ -3,12 +3,12 @@
Doubles the first expensive Attack each turn for free. Effectively doubles damage AND secondary effects (orb channels, debuffs, draws).
KEY INTERACTIONS:
-- **Barrage (2E base)**: Always triggers. With 5 orbs: 40 damage total (50 upgraded). Highest single-card damage output.
-- **Cold Snap (when 2+ cost)**: Plays twice = 12 damage + channels 2 Frost orbs.
+- **[[cards/Barrage]] (2E base)**: Always triggers. With 5 orbs: 40 damage total (50 upgraded). Highest single-card damage output.
+- **[[cards/Cold Snap]] (when 2+ cost)**: Plays twice = 12 damage + channels 2 Frost orbs.
- **Any Attack with secondary effects**: Full replay including draw/channel/debuff.
SNECKO EYE SYNERGY: Cards normally costing 0-1 can roll 2+, triggering Necronomicon. With 7 cards drawn, at least one Attack costing 2+ is very likely. Dramatically increases trigger frequency.
-NECRONOMICURSE: Unplayable hand clog, cannot be removed at shops. In Snecko Eye deck (7 draws), minor nuisance. In standard 5-draw, more impactful. Keep deck lean.
+NECRONOMICURSE: Unplayable hand clog, cannot be removed at shops. In [[relics/Snecko Eye]] deck (7 draws), minor nuisance. In standard 5-draw, more impactful. Keep deck lean.
-DECISION: Take when deck has multiple 2+ cost Attacks, or Snecko Eye is active. Plan turns so highest-value Attack is played first to receive the doubling.
+DECISION: Take when deck has multiple 2+ cost Attacks, or [[enemies/Snecko]] Eye is active. Plan turns so highest-value Attack is played first to receive the doubling.
heuristics/relics/nilrys-codex.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Nilry's Codex
-Provides a free card every turn, excellent in long fights where hand quality matters. Generated cards include powerful options like Impervious and Immolate from any class pool. In a boss fight lasting 8+ turns: 8+ free cards, some game-changing.
+Provides a free card every turn, excellent in long fights where hand quality matters. Generated cards include powerful options like [[cards/Impervious]] and [[cards/Immolate]] from any class pool. In a boss fight lasting 8+ turns: 8+ free cards, some game-changing.
High value in long fights (bosses, elites). The card generation compensates for thin decks or decks lacking specific answers. Lower value in short hallway fights (2-3 turns, only 2-3 extra cards).
heuristics/relics/nunchaku.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Nunchaku
-In Attack-heavy decks, provides a reliable energy boost every few turns. With Snecko Eye drawing 7 cards/turn and 4-5 being Attacks, triggers every 2-3 turns. The bonus energy enables playing one more card on the trigger turn.
+In Attack-heavy decks, provides a reliable energy boost every few turns. With [[relics/Snecko Eye]] drawing 7 cards/turn and 4-5 being Attacks, triggers every 2-3 turns. The bonus energy enables playing one more card on the trigger turn.
-SYNERGIES: Attack-heavy decks (Anger adds copies, increasing density), Snecko Eye (more cards drawn = more Attacks played). Multi-hit attacks like Pummel count as one play toward the counter, not per hit.
+SYNERGIES: Attack-heavy decks ([[cards/Anger]] adds copies, increasing density), [[enemies/Snecko]] Eye (more cards drawn = more Attacks played). Multi-hit attacks like [[cards/Pummel]] count as one play toward the counter, not per hit.
Solid common relic. Not flashy but reliable. Counter persists across combats.
heuristics/relics/odd-mushroom.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Odd Mushroom
-DOUBLE-EDGED relic. Defensively, reduces damage taken when you are Vulnerable. Offensively, REDUCES your damage output by ~17% against Vulnerable targets.
+DOUBLE-EDGED relic. Defensively, reduces damage taken when you are [[debuffs/Vulnerable]]. Offensively, REDUCES your damage output by ~17% against Vulnerable targets.
-WHEN TO TAKE: Good if your deck relies on block/defense and rarely applies Vulnerable offensively. BAD if your deck relies on Bash+/Thunderclap+ Vulnerable for burst damage (most Ironclad decks). Consider taking the 22 HP heal (Eat option) from the Mushroom event instead if your deck is Vulnerable-dependent.
+WHEN TO TAKE: Good if your deck relies on block/defense and rarely applies Vulnerable offensively. BAD if your deck relies on [[cards/Bash]]/[[cards/Thunderclap]] Vulnerable for burst damage (most [[characters/Ironclad]] decks). Consider taking the 22 HP heal (Eat option) from the Mushroom event instead if your deck is Vulnerable-dependent.
-ANTI-SYNERGIES: Bash+ (Vuln 3 is your primary damage amp), Thunderclap+ (mass Vuln for burst), any DPS-race fight (Bronze Automaton, Book of Stabbing), Hexaghost (the ~17% damage reduction extends the fight by 1-2 turns, adding more Burns -- confirmed lethal).
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Bash+ (Vuln 3 is your primary damage amp), Thunderclap+ (mass Vuln for burst), any DPS-race fight ([[bosses/Bronze Automaton]], [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]]), [[bosses/Hexaghost]] (the ~17% damage reduction extends the fight by 1-2 turns, adding more Burns -- confirmed lethal).
heuristics/relics/oddly-smooth-stone.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Oddly Smooth Stone
-Permanent +1 block on every block card played. Defend generates 6 block instead of 5. Shrug It Off generates 9 instead of 8. Compounds with multiple block cards per turn. Over a long fight: 10-20 extra block total.
+Permanent +1 block on every block card played. Defend generates 6 block instead of 5. [[cards/Shrug It Off]] generates 9 instead of 8. Compounds with multiple block cards per turn. Over a long fight: 10-20 extra block total.
-Solid common relic with no downside. Less impactful than Vajra (+1 Strength) for Ironclad because Ironclad's win condition is typically killing fast, not blocking more. But in Act 2 where chip damage accumulates, the extra block helps reduce HP drain between rest sites.
+Solid common relic with no downside. Less impactful than [[relics/Vajra]] (+1 [[buffs/Strength]]) for [[characters/Ironclad]] because Ironclad's win condition is typically killing fast, not blocking more. But in [[acts/Act 2]] where chip damage accumulates, the extra block helps reduce HP drain between rest sites.
heuristics/relics/omamori.md
@@ -7,4 +7,4 @@ WHEN TO USE:
- General insurance against random curse events in Unknown rooms.
- Note: Bites (Vampires event) are NOT Curses -- Omamori does not help there.
-Confirmed: Used to safely open a Coffin event for a free relic (Ceramic Fish). The curse was negated, gaining a relic at zero cost.
+Confirmed: Used to safely open a Coffin event for a free relic ([[relics/Ceramic Fish]]). The curse was negated, gaining a relic at zero cost.
heuristics/relics/ornamental-fan.md
@@ -2,6 +2,6 @@
Moderate value. Provides passive block in attack-heavy turns without costing energy. Works well in decks that naturally play 3+ attacks per turn.
-Less useful in Corruption engine decks where most plays are Skills (which exhaust for block via Feel No Pain instead). The 4 block per 3 attacks is small relative to 30-40 block from Impervious or FNP chains.
+Less useful in [[cards/Corruption]] engine decks where most plays are Skills (which exhaust for block via [[cards/Feel No Pain]] instead). The 4 block per 3 attacks is small relative to 30-40 block from [[cards/Impervious]] or FNP chains.
-SYNERGY: High-attack decks, Snecko Eye (many cheap attacks), Anger (0E attack helps reach 3 plays).
+SYNERGY: High-attack decks, [[relics/Snecko Eye]] (many cheap attacks), [[cards/Anger]] (0E attack helps reach 3 plays).
heuristics/relics/pandoras-box.md
@@ -2,7 +2,7 @@
Extremely high variance. Removes 10 starter cards and replaces them with random class cards. Can produce game-winning decks or unplayable ones.
-CRITICAL RISK -- BLOCK LOSS: Removes ALL 4 Defend cards. If replacements contain zero block cards, the deck has NO defensive capability for Act 1. Confirmed death: offense-heavy result with zero block cards died on Floor 16 to Guardian.
+CRITICAL RISK -- BLOCK LOSS: Removes ALL 4 Defend cards. If replacements contain zero block cards, the deck has NO defensive capability for [[acts/Act 1]]. Confirmed death: offense-heavy result with zero block cards died on Floor 16 to Guardian.
WHEN TO TAKE:
- From Neow: HIGH RISK. Cannot undo. Only if comfortable gambling the run.
@@ -11,8 +11,8 @@ WHEN TO TAKE:
AFTER TAKING:
- Immediately assess: does the new deck have ANY block source?
- If zero block cards: EMERGENCY. Prioritize acquiring block at very next shop or card reward.
-- Iron Wave (1E, 5/5) is minimum viable. Flame Barrier (2E, 12 block) is better.
+- [[cards/Iron Wave]] (1E, 5/5) is minimum viable. [[cards/Flame Barrier]] (2E, 12 block) is better.
-DEFECT NOTE: Slightly safer than other classes because Defect starters (Zap, Dualcast) are weak, and random Defect pool includes many strong orb-channeling cards.
+DEFECT NOTE: Slightly safer than other classes because [[characters/Defect]] starters (Zap, [[cards/Dualcast]]) are weak, and random Defect pool includes many strong orb-channeling cards.
A run-defining gamble. Prefer safer Neow options (100 Gold, Remove 2 Cards) unless no other path to a strong deck exists.
heuristics/relics/pantograph.md
@@ -4,4 +4,4 @@ Strong but not game-changing. 25 HP is significant but does not remove the need
CRITICAL CORRECTION: Pantograph heals exactly 25 HP, NOT to full. Plan boss HP thresholds accordingly -- you need to enter bosses at no more than 25 HP below max to benefit fully. Entering a boss at 30% HP with 87 Max HP means healing to ~54%, not full.
-With Sozu (no potions), Pantograph is still valuable as reliable healing, but it is NOT a full heal replacement.
+With [[relics/Sozu]] (no potions), Pantograph is still valuable as reliable healing, but it is NOT a full heal replacement.
heuristics/relics/paper-phrog.md
@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
# Paper Phrog
-Massively amplifies all damage against Vulnerable targets. Bash+ becomes a much stronger setup card. Over a multi-turn Vulnerable window (Bash+ = 3 turns), adds up to massive extra damage.
+Massively amplifies all damage against [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] targets. [[cards/Bash]] becomes a much stronger setup card. Over a multi-turn Vulnerable window (Bash+ = 3 turns), adds up to massive extra damage.
MATH (with Paper Phrog):
- Strike (6) vs Vulnerable: 10 (vs 9 without)
-- Immolate+ (28) vs Vulnerable: 49 (vs 42 without)
-- Twin Strike (5+5) vs Vulnerable: 17 (vs 14 without)
+- [[cards/Immolate]] (28) vs Vulnerable: 49 (vs 42 without)
+- [[cards/Twin Strike]] (5+5) vs Vulnerable: 17 (vs 14 without)
-SYNERGIES: Bash+ (3 turns amplified Vulnerable), Thunderclap (mass Vulnerable), Bag of Marbles (free Turn 1 Vulnerable), any high-damage attack card.
+SYNERGIES: Bash+ (3 turns amplified Vulnerable), [[cards/Thunderclap]] (mass Vulnerable), [[relics/Bag of Marbles]] (free Turn 1 Vulnerable), any high-damage attack card.
-One of the best common relics for Ironclad. Since Bash+ applies Vulnerable in every fight, provides permanent damage amplification. Stronger than most uncommon relics.
+One of the best common relics for [[characters/Ironclad]]. Since Bash+ applies Vulnerable in every fight, provides permanent damage amplification. Stronger than most uncommon relics.
heuristics/relics/pen-nib.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Pen Nib
-Track the counter. Time the 10th Attack to coincide with high-damage cards (Fiend Fire, Carnage+, Headbutt+) or Vulnerable targets. Pen Nib + Strike = 12 damage. Pen Nib + Fiend Fire (4 cards) = 56 damage. Plan your Attacks across fights to line up the 10th hit on the right card.
+Track the counter. Time the 10th Attack to coincide with high-damage cards ([[cards/Fiend Fire]], [[cards/Carnage]], [[cards/Headbutt]]) or [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] targets. Pen Nib + Strike = 12 damage. Pen Nib + Fiend Fire (4 cards) = 56 damage. Plan your Attacks across fights to line up the 10th hit on the right card.
heuristics/relics/potion-belt.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Potion Belt
-More potions means more options for boss fights and dangerous elites. Pairs with White Beast Statue (guaranteed potion drops fill more slots) and Toy Ornithopter (more potions = more healing). Stock up for bosses.
+More potions means more options for boss fights and dangerous elites. Pairs with [[relics/White Beast Statue]] (guaranteed potion drops fill more slots) and [[relics/Toy Ornithopter]] (more potions = more healing). Stock up for bosses.
heuristics/relics/preserved-insect.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Preserved Insect
-Makes every elite fight significantly easier. 25% HP reduction means fewer turns needed to kill, which means fewer turns of incoming damage. Particularly strong against high-HP elites like Book of Stabbing and Gremlin Leader.
+Makes every elite fight significantly easier. 25% HP reduction means fewer turns needed to kill, which means fewer turns of incoming damage. Particularly strong against high-HP elites like [[enemies/Book of Stabbing]] and [[enemies/Gremlin Leader]].
-Passive benefit with no downside. Always take when offered. The 25% reduction compounds with Vulnerable (50% more damage taken) -- an elite at 75% HP taking 50% more damage effectively has ~50% of its original effective HP.
+Passive benefit with no downside. Always take when offered. The 25% reduction compounds with [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (50% more damage taken) -- an elite at 75% HP taking 50% more damage effectively has ~50% of its original effective HP.
heuristics/relics/pure-water.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Pure Water
-Effectively +1 energy on Turn 1 of every combat. The Miracle Retains, so it can be saved for a turn where the energy matters most (e.g., a burst turn exiting Calm into Wrath).
+Effectively +1 energy on Turn 1 of every combat. The [[cards/Miracle]] Retains, so it can be saved for a turn where the energy matters most (e.g., a burst turn exiting Calm into Wrath).
INTERACTION WITH DEUS EX MACHINA: Generates 2-3 additional Miracles when drawn. Combined with Pure Water's Turn 1 Miracle, accumulate 3-4 Miracles. Managing these (not wasting on turns with no useful plays) is critical.
-BOSS RELIC SWAP: Losing guaranteed +1E per combat is a real cost, especially early when few energy-generating cards exist. Keep Pure Water unless the boss relic is clearly superior (Snecko Eye, Pandora's Box, etc.).
+BOSS RELIC SWAP: Losing guaranteed +1E per combat is a real cost, especially early when few energy-generating cards exist. Keep Pure Water unless the boss relic is clearly superior ([[relics/Snecko Eye]], [[relics/Pandora's Box]], etc.).
heuristics/relics/red-mask.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Red Mask
-Free damage reduction every fight with zero player input. 1 Weak = 25% less enemy damage on turn 1. Against multi-enemy fights (3 Cultists, Byrds), applies to every enemy simultaneously.
+Free damage reduction every fight with zero player input. 1 [[debuffs/Weak]] = 25% less enemy damage on turn 1. Against multi-enemy fights (3 Cultists, Byrds), applies to every enemy simultaneously.
-**Artifact stripping**: Against enemies with Artifact (Bronze Automaton Artifact 3, Spheric Guardian Artifact 3), Red Mask strips 1 Artifact charge automatically before the player's first turn, reducing the stripping requirement by 1.
+**[[buffs/Artifact]] stripping**: Against enemies with Artifact ([[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] Artifact 3, [[enemies/Spheric Guardian]] Artifact 3), Red Mask strips 1 Artifact charge automatically before the player's first turn, reducing the stripping requirement by 1.
Against enemies that buff turn 1 (Cultists, some Fungi Beasts), the Weak is wasted since they don't attack. Provides consistent incremental value across every single fight for the rest of the run.
heuristics/relics/regal-pillow.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Regal Pillow
-Turns rest sites from 24 HP healing to 39 HP healing. In Act 3 where HP management is critical, can restore nearly half of max HP at each rest site. Makes it safer to choose Rest over Smith when HP is below threshold.
+Turns rest sites from 24 HP healing to 39 HP healing. In [[acts/Act 3]] where HP management is critical, can restore nearly half of max HP at each rest site. Makes it safer to choose Rest over Smith when HP is below threshold.
Strong utility relic that patches healing gaps. Enabled healing from 53 HP to 80/80 (full HP) at the pre-boss rest site in the victory run, ensuring 100% HP entry that contributed to the win.
heuristics/relics/runic-capacitor.md
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
# Runic Capacitor
-Doubles starting orb capacity. More slots means more simultaneous passive effects and dramatically higher Barrage damage.
+Doubles starting orb capacity. More slots means more simultaneous passive effects and dramatically higher [[cards/Barrage]] damage.
KEY SYNERGIES:
- **Barrage+**: Each additional filled slot adds 7 damage. 3 extra slots = +21 Barrage+ damage.
-- **Capacitor (card)**: Stacks for even more slots. Runic Capacitor + Capacitor+ = 9 orb slots.
-- **Coolheaded+**: More slots means Frost orbs persist longer before being pushed out. Frost wall is more stable.
-- **Blizzard**: More Frost orbs channeled = higher Blizzard damage.
+- **[[cards/Capacitor]] (card)**: Stacks for even more slots. Runic Capacitor + Capacitor+ = 9 orb slots.
+- **[[cards/Coolheaded]]**: More slots means Frost orbs persist longer before being pushed out. Frost wall is more stable.
+- **[[cards/Blizzard]]**: More Frost orbs channeled = higher Blizzard damage.
With 6 slots filled with Frost orbs at Focus 2: 24 passive block/turn. Enables truly massive orb boards.
heuristics/relics/runic-dome.md
@@ -2,8 +2,8 @@
Almost never take. The +1 Energy is powerful, but losing intent information is devastating. Intent drives every decision: when to block, when to attack, when to play Powers, when to use potions.
-THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: Some boss/elite attacks are near-fatal if unblocked (Bronze Automaton Hyper Beam, Guardian 32-damage, Hexaghost Inferno 42 damage). Without intent, you cannot prepare. Two deaths resulted from taking Runic Dome without enemy pattern knowledge.
+THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: Some boss/elite attacks are near-fatal if unblocked ([[bosses/Bronze Automaton]] Hyper Beam, Guardian 32-damage, [[bosses/Hexaghost]] Inferno 42 damage). Without intent, you cannot prepare. Two deaths resulted from taking Runic Dome without enemy pattern knowledge.
PREREQUISITE: Requires memorized enemy attack patterns to use safely. Until all enemy patterns are known, skip it in favor of other boss relics.
-WHEN IT COULD WORK: A deck with heavy passive block (Metallicize, Barricade + block engine) that doesn't need to react to intents. Or once all enemy patterns are memorized and predictable from cycle position.
+WHEN IT COULD WORK: A deck with heavy passive block ([[cards/Metallicize]], [[cards/Barricade]] + block engine) that doesn't need to react to intents. Or once all enemy patterns are memorized and predictable from cycle position.
heuristics/relics/runic-pyramid.md
@@ -2,10 +2,10 @@
Retains entire hand between turns. No discard at end of turn. Cards accumulate across turns.
-**Strengths:** Guarantees access to key cards every turn. Eliminates draw RNG for critical plays. Allows holding combo pieces (Fiend Fire + Strength buffs, Shockwave + block cards) until the right turn.
+**Strengths:** Guarantees access to key cards every turn. Eliminates draw RNG for critical plays. Allows holding combo pieces ([[cards/Fiend Fire]] + [[buffs/Strength]] buffs, [[cards/Shockwave]] + block cards) until the right turn.
-**Danger with exhaust cards:** Mass exhaust effects (Fiend Fire, Second Wind, Corruption) become more powerful but also more destructive. With a growing hand, Fiend Fire exhausts MORE cards for more damage -- but also exhausts cards being held for later turns. In phased boss fights (The Champ, Awakened One), this can permanently destroy Phase 2 cards.
+**Danger with exhaust cards:** Mass exhaust effects (Fiend Fire, [[cards/Second Wind]], [[cards/Corruption]]) become more powerful but also more destructive. With a growing hand, Fiend Fire exhausts MORE cards for more damage -- but also exhausts cards being held for later turns. In phased boss fights ([[bosses/The Champ]], [[bosses/Awakened One]]), this can permanently destroy Phase 2 cards.
-**The Champ trap:** Runic Pyramid retains debuff cards (Disarm, Shockwave, Exhume) in hand across turns while waiting for Phase 2. If Fiend Fire fires during Phase 1, ALL retained cards are exhausted -- including the debuffs needed for Phase 2. Before any mass exhaust effect, identify which cards in hand are being saved and play or protect them first.
+**The Champ trap:** Runic Pyramid retains debuff cards ([[cards/Disarm]], Shockwave, [[cards/Exhume]]) in hand across turns while waiting for Phase 2. If Fiend Fire fires during Phase 1, ALL retained cards are exhausted -- including the debuffs needed for Phase 2. Before any mass exhaust effect, identify which cards in hand are being saved and play or protect them first.
**Hand management rule:** With Runic Pyramid, every end-of-turn hand is a deliberate inventory. Before playing mass-exhaust cards, mentally separate the hand into "expendable" and "must keep." Play or protect "must keep" cards before triggering mass exhaust.
heuristics/relics/self-forming-clay.md
@@ -4,4 +4,4 @@ Moderate defensive relic. Provides 3 free block the turn after taking damage. Wo
Good in multi-turn fights where occasional chip damage is unavoidable. Provides incremental block that helps reach full block thresholds on subsequent turns (3 block from Clay + 2 Defends = 13 block instead of 10).
-Weak when you take zero damage consistently (never triggers) or when a single massive hit kills you (3 block next turn is meaningless if dead this turn).
+[[debuffs/Weak]] when you take zero damage consistently (never triggers) or when a single massive hit kills you (3 block next turn is meaningless if dead this turn).
heuristics/relics/shuriken.md
@@ -1,11 +1,11 @@
# Shuriken
-Incentivizes playing 3+ Attacks per turn. Over a long fight, provides 3-5+ Strength passively -- comparable to Inflame but requires no card slot or energy. High pick priority in Attack-heavy decks.
+Incentivizes playing 3+ Attacks per turn. Over a long fight, provides 3-5+ [[buffs/Strength]] passively -- comparable to [[cards/Inflame]] but requires no card slot or energy. High pick priority in Attack-heavy decks.
SYNERGIES:
-- **Anger**: 0-cost Attack that copies itself. Playing 3x Anger triggers Shuriken for free.
-- **Demon Form**: +2 Str/turn stacks with Shuriken's passive gains. By turn 5, total Strength can be +12-15 combined.
+- **[[cards/Anger]]**: 0-cost Attack that copies itself. Playing 3x Anger triggers Shuriken for free.
+- **[[cards/Demon Form]]**: +2 Str/turn stacks with Shuriken's passive gains. By turn 5, total Strength can be +12-15 combined.
- **Rage**: 0-cost Power that gives block per Attack. Every Attack-heavy turn generates both block and Strength.
-- **Multi-attack turns**: Thunderclap + Strike + Anger = 3 attacks = +1 Str. Very easy to trigger naturally.
+- **Multi-attack turns**: [[cards/Thunderclap]] + Strike + Anger = 3 attacks = +1 Str. Very easy to trigger naturally.
Excellent in long fights (bosses, elites) where 5-8 triggers accumulate to significant Strength.
heuristics/relics/slavers-collar.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Slaver's Collar
-The extra energy in elite and boss fights is significant -- it allows playing an additional card per turn in the hardest fights. A 4E turn means you can play Bash+ (2E) + Rampage (1E) + Defend (1E) instead of choosing between offense and defense.
+The extra energy in elite and boss fights is significant -- it allows playing an additional card per turn in the hardest fights. A 4E turn means you can play [[cards/Bash]] (2E) + [[cards/Rampage]] (1E) + Defend (1E) instead of choosing between offense and defense.
High value for elite-heavy paths. If your route includes 2-3 elites and the Act boss, the relic provides value in 3-4 of the hardest fights. Less valuable if avoiding elites.
-Pairs well with expensive cards (Bludgeon at 3E, Reaper at 2E) that normally crowd out other plays.
+Pairs well with expensive cards ([[cards/Bludgeon]] at 3E, [[cards/Reaper]] at 2E) that normally crowd out other plays.
heuristics/relics/sling-of-courage.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Sling of Courage
-Free permanent Strength in the two most important fight types. With Heavy Blade (3x multiplier), +2 Str becomes +6 damage per play. With Heavy Blade+ (5x), it becomes +10. Compounds with Spot Weakness+ and Limit Break (doubles all Str including this bonus).
+Free permanent [[buffs/Strength]] in the two most important fight types. With [[cards/Heavy Blade]] (3x multiplier), +2 Str becomes +6 damage per play. With Heavy Blade+ (5x), it becomes +10. Compounds with [[cards/Spot Weakness]] and [[cards/Limit Break]] (doubles all Str including this bonus).
Excellent shop purchase for Strength-scaling decks. Every point of base Strength has outsized value due to multiplicative scaling with Heavy Blade and Limit Break.
heuristics/relics/snecko-eye.md
@@ -1,17 +1,17 @@
# Snecko Eye
-Drawing 7 cards per turn means reliably finding key combo pieces every turn. Cost randomization averages 1.5E/card, but high-cost cards (2E+) benefit enormously -- 25% chance of costing 0E each turn. Present in both consecutive Ironclad victories.
+Drawing 7 cards per turn means reliably finding key combo pieces every turn. Cost randomization averages 1.5E/card, but high-cost cards (2E+) benefit enormously -- 25% chance of costing 0E each turn. Present in both consecutive [[characters/Ironclad]] victories.
-WHEN TO TAKE: Deck has multiple 2E+ cards. The more expensive the average card cost, the better. A deck with Immolate+, Heavy Blade, Impervious, Flame Barrier, Bash+ has 5 cards that can become free.
+WHEN TO TAKE: Deck has multiple 2E+ cards. The more expensive the average card cost, the better. A deck with [[cards/Immolate]], [[cards/Heavy Blade]], [[cards/Impervious]], [[cards/Flame Barrier]], [[cards/Bash]] has 5 cards that can become free.
-WHEN TO AVOID: Decks built around 0-cost cards (Anger, Rage) or X-cost cards (Whirlwind) get worse. 0-cost cards randomized to 1-3 makes them worse.
+WHEN TO AVOID: Decks built around 0-cost cards ([[cards/Anger]], Rage) or X-cost cards ([[cards/Whirlwind]]) get worse. 0-cost cards randomized to 1-3 makes them worse.
KEY SYNERGIES:
-- **Gambling Chip**: Mulligan bad cost rolls for new ones. Dramatically improves Turn 1.
-- **Necronomicon**: Cards randomized to 2+ trigger doubling. Dramatically increases trigger frequency.
-- **Headbutt**: Top-decked cards get new cost rolls.
-- **Nunchaku**: 7 cards drawn = more Attacks played per turn.
+- **[[relics/Gambling Chip]]**: Mulligan bad cost rolls for new ones. Dramatically improves Turn 1.
+- **[[relics/Necronomicon]]**: Cards randomized to 2+ trigger doubling. Dramatically increases trigger frequency.
+- **[[cards/Headbutt]]**: Top-decked cards get new cost rolls.
+- **[[relics/Nunchaku]]**: 7 cards drawn = more Attacks played per turn.
-S-tier for Strength-scaling decks with expensive power cards. The extra draws solve consistency problems and cost randomization provides massive energy efficiency. Take as Act 2 boss relic whenever deck has high-cost cards.
+S-tier for [[buffs/Strength]]-scaling decks with expensive power cards. The extra draws solve consistency problems and cost randomization provides massive energy efficiency. Take as [[acts/Act 2]] boss relic whenever deck has high-cost cards.
-DEFECT VALUE: Strong because Defect's orb-based defense partially compensates for bad cost rolls on block cards (orbs generate block automatically without playing cards).
+DEFECT VALUE: Strong because [[characters/Defect]]'s orb-based defense partially compensates for bad cost rolls on block cards (orbs generate block automatically without playing cards).
heuristics/relics/sozu.md
@@ -2,9 +2,9 @@
The extra energy (3 -> 4 per turn) is extremely powerful, but the potion lockout is a severe tradeoff. Losing ALL potion access means no safety net for bad draws and no supplemental healing.
-ANTI-SYNERGIES: Toy Ornithopter (completely dead), Potion Belt (worthless), White Beast Statue (worthless). Healing potions as sustain plan is unavailable -- healing cards (Reaper, Feed) become even MORE critical.
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: [[relics/Toy Ornithopter]] (completely dead), [[relics/Potion Belt]] (worthless), [[relics/White Beast Statue]] (worthless). Healing potions as sustain plan is unavailable -- healing cards ([[cards/Reaper]], Feed) become even MORE critical.
-SILENT-SPECIFIC DANGER (3 deaths confirmed): The Silent lacks innate healing and relies on potions as emergency saves. Pattern: Sozu removes the safety net that would have saved the run in a single critical turn. For the Silent, **strongly prefer other boss relics** unless the deck has Wraith Form+ and multiple block sources independent of potions.
+SILENT-SPECIFIC DANGER (3 deaths confirmed): The [[characters/Silent]] lacks innate healing and relies on potions as emergency saves. Pattern: Sozu removes the safety net that would have saved the run in a single critical turn. For the Silent, **strongly prefer other boss relics** unless the deck has [[cards/Wraith Form]] and multiple block sources independent of potions.
WHEN TO TAKE:
- Skip all potion-related relics and events.
heuristics/relics/stone-calendar.md
@@ -3,10 +3,10 @@
Free 52 AOE damage with no energy or card investment in long fights. In fights that end in 4-5 turns, the relic does nothing.
KEY MATCHUPS:
-- **Centurion+Mystic**: Ideal -- fight naturally lasts 7+ turns while killing Mystic through block. 52 damage lands on Centurion.
+- **[[enemies/Centurion]]+[[enemies/Mystic]]**: Ideal -- fight naturally lasts 7+ turns while killing Mystic through block. 52 damage lands on Centurion.
- **Boss fights**: Most last 10+ turns, so the nuke always fires. 52 free damage is significant.
-- **Shelled Parasite**: 52 damage overkills through remaining Plated Armor block.
-- **Spheric Guardian**: 52 damage into 65 block only removes block -- does NOT kill. Insufficient against high-block enemies.
+- **[[enemies/Shelled Parasite]]**: 52 damage overkills through remaining [[buffs/Plated Armor]] block.
+- **[[enemies/Spheric Guardian]]**: 52 damage into 65 block only removes block -- does NOT kill. Insufficient against high-block enemies.
- **Short fights**: Useless against enemies that die in 3-5 turns.
-Strong in decks that expect long fights (defensive/block-heavy builds). Weak in burst-focused decks that aim to kill quickly.
+Strong in decks that expect long fights (defensive/block-heavy builds). [[debuffs/Weak]] in burst-focused decks that aim to kill quickly.
heuristics/relics/torii.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# Torii
-Exceptional against chip damage. Burns (2 damage each) become 1 damage. Multi-hit attacks where each hit is small (5 or less after block) are drastically reduced. Against Hexaghost, Torii halves Burns attrition. Against Sentries, small hits are reduced to 1. Does NOT help against large single hits (Guardian 32, Hexaghost Inferno hits at 6 each are already above 5 per hit without Weak).
+Exceptional against chip damage. Burns (2 damage each) become 1 damage. Multi-hit attacks where each hit is small (5 or less after block) are drastically reduced. Against [[bosses/Hexaghost]], Torii halves Burns attrition. Against Sentries, small hits are reduced to 1. Does NOT help against large single hits (Guardian 32, Hexaghost Inferno hits at 6 each are already above 5 per hit without [[debuffs/Weak]]).
heuristics/relics/toy-ornithopter.md
@@ -1,7 +1,7 @@
# Toy Ornithopter
-Turns potion usage into healing. Each potion heals an extra 5 HP. With Potion Belt and aggressive potion acquisition, can heal 25-35 HP across a run.
+Turns potion usage into healing. Each potion heals an extra 5 HP. With [[relics/Potion Belt]] and aggressive potion acquisition, can heal 25-35 HP across a run.
-HEALING GAP SOLUTION: When Reaper and Feed are not offered, Toy Ornithopter + aggressive potion usage becomes the backup healing plan. Buy healing potions at shops, use liberally in tough fights. Three potion uses in a single boss fight = 15 HP healed, equivalent to a medium Reaper hit.
+HEALING GAP SOLUTION: When [[cards/Reaper]] and Feed are not offered, Toy Ornithopter + aggressive potion usage becomes the backup healing plan. Buy healing potions at shops, use liberally in tough fights. Three potion uses in a single boss fight = 15 HP healed, equivalent to a medium Reaper hit.
-ANTI-SYNERGY: Completely dead with Sozu (prevents obtaining potions). Do NOT take Toy Ornithopter if you have Sozu.
+ANTI-SYNERGY: Completely dead with [[relics/Sozu]] (prevents obtaining potions). Do NOT take Toy Ornithopter if you have Sozu.
heuristics/relics/unceasing-top.md
@@ -3,6 +3,6 @@
Creates a draw engine with any card that empties your hand. One of the strongest relics for exhaust-focused decks. Less useful in decks with large hands that never empty.
KEY SYNERGIES:
-- **Fiend Fire** (CRITICAL): Exhausts hand -> empty -> draw -> play -> empty -> draw. Infinite draw engine limited only by energy and draw pile size.
-- **0-cost cards**: Bloodletting (gain 2 energy, hand empties), Rage (play for free, hand empties), Inflame/powers (play, hand empties) all trigger additional draws.
-- **Offering**: Lose 6 HP but gain 2 energy + draw 3. Combined with Top, massive turns.
+- **[[cards/Fiend Fire]]** (CRITICAL): Exhausts hand -> empty -> draw -> play -> empty -> draw. Infinite draw engine limited only by energy and draw pile size.
+- **0-cost cards**: [[cards/Bloodletting]] (gain 2 energy, hand empties), Rage (play for free, hand empties), [[cards/Inflame]]/powers (play, hand empties) all trigger additional draws.
+- **[[cards/Offering]]**: Lose 6 HP but gain 2 energy + draw 3. Combined with Top, massive turns.
heuristics/relics/vajra.md
@@ -1,10 +1,10 @@
# Vajra
-Permanent, unconditional +1 damage to every Attack play for every fight. Modest on its own, but compounds with Strength-scaling cards and effects. Over a 5-turn fight playing 2 Attacks/turn: +10 total damage. Multi-hit cards benefit especially: Twin Strike gains +2 total, Pummel gains +4-5 total.
+Permanent, unconditional +1 damage to every Attack play for every fight. Modest on its own, but compounds with [[buffs/Strength]]-scaling cards and effects. Over a 5-turn fight playing 2 Attacks/turn: +10 total damage. Multi-hit cards benefit especially: [[cards/Twin Strike]] gains +2 total, [[cards/Pummel]] gains +4-5 total.
SYNERGIES:
-- **Heavy Blade** -- +1 Str becomes +3 damage (or +5 upgraded) due to the multiplier.
-- **Limit Break** -- doubling Strength that starts at 1 instead of 0 is the difference between the card doing nothing and doing something.
-- **Reaper** -- more Strength = more damage to all enemies = more healing.
+- **[[cards/Heavy Blade]]** -- +1 Str becomes +3 damage (or +5 upgraded) due to the multiplier.
+- **[[cards/Limit Break]]** -- doubling Strength that starts at 1 instead of 0 is the difference between the card doing nothing and doing something.
+- **[[cards/Reaper]]** -- more Strength = more damage to all enemies = more healing.
-Safe value from Neow or early chests. No downside. Particularly strong when building toward Strength scaling (Inflame, Limit Break, Heavy Blade).
+Safe value from Neow or early chests. No downside. Particularly strong when building toward Strength scaling ([[cards/Inflame]], Limit Break, Heavy Blade).
heuristics/relics/whetstone.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Whetstone
-Equivalent to two rest site upgrades for Attacks specifically, but with no control over which cards are chosen. Best when the deck has multiple high-priority Attack upgrades (Heavy Blade 3x->5x, Carnage 20->28, Twin Strike, Pommel Strike). Worst when key Attacks are already upgraded or only Strikes remain.
+Equivalent to two rest site upgrades for Attacks specifically, but with no control over which cards are chosen. Best when the deck has multiple high-priority Attack upgrades ([[cards/Heavy Blade]] 3x->5x, [[cards/Carnage]] 20->28, [[cards/Twin Strike]], [[cards/Pommel Strike]]). Worst when key Attacks are already upgraded or only Strikes remain.
Strong early (more Attacks in deck = higher chance of hitting good targets, plus upgrades compound over more fights). Weaker late. Always acceptable as a free relic with no downside.
heuristics/relics/white-beast-statue.md
@@ -1,3 +1,3 @@
# White Beast Statue
-Guarantees a steady potion supply. With full potion slots, every fight generates a potion. Extremely valuable for boss preparation -- enter with a full potion belt. Pairs well with Potion Belt (+2 slots) and Toy Ornithopter (+5 HP per potion used).
+Guarantees a steady potion supply. With full potion slots, every fight generates a potion. Extremely valuable for boss preparation -- enter with a full potion belt. Pairs well with [[relics/Potion Belt]] (+2 slots) and [[relics/Toy Ornithopter]] (+5 HP per potion used).
heuristics/relics/wing-boots.md
@@ -2,4 +2,4 @@
Useful for avoiding dangerous fights or reaching specific paths (shops, rest sites, elites) at low HP.
-Use sparingly -- only 3 charges for the entire run. Best saved for Act 2-3 where hallway fights are deadlier and pathing is more critical. Do not waste charges in Act 1 unless absolutely necessary for survival.
+Use sparingly -- only 3 charges for the entire run. Best saved for [[acts/Act 2]]-3 where hallway fights are deadlier and pathing is more critical. Do not waste charges in [[acts/Act 1]] unless absolutely necessary for survival.
heuristics/rest-sites.md
@@ -1,5 +1,5 @@
# Rest Site Decisions
-- **Upgrade vs Rest Framework**: Upgrade when HP > 50% Act 2 (> 35% Act 1) and critical upgrade target exists. Rest when HP < 50% Act 2 (< 35% Act 1), or next room is elite/boss and below 60%, or path has 2+ combats before next rest and below 50%.
-- **The Upgrade Death Spiral**: Zero upgrades = death sentence. Unupgraded cards → longer fights → more HP loss → forced resting → no upgrades. MANDATORY RULE: Upgrade at EVERY rest site above threshold. Bash should be upgraded by Floor 8.
-- **Upgrade Priority (Ironclad)**: 1. Bash (3 Vuln vs 2). 2. Corruption (3E→2E). 3. True Grit (chosen vs random exhaust). 4. Armaments (1 card → ALL cards). 5. Carnage (28 vs 20). 6. Iron Wave (7/7 vs 5/5). 7. Pommel Strike (10+draw 2 vs 9+draw 1). 8. Thunderclap (7 vs 4 AOE). 9. SIO (11 vs 8). 10. Headbutt (12 vs 9). 11. Defend (8 vs 5). Context overrides: Corruption above Bash if it's the engine piece. Limit Break is #1 for Str-scaling decks approaching Act 3 boss.
+- **Upgrade vs Rest Framework**: Upgrade when HP > 50% [[acts/Act 2]] (> 35% [[acts/Act 1]]) and critical upgrade target exists. Rest when HP < 50% Act 2 (< 35% Act 1), or next room is elite/boss and below 60%, or path has 2+ combats before next rest and below 50%.
+- **The Upgrade Death Spiral**: Zero upgrades = death sentence. Unupgraded cards → longer fights → more HP loss → forced resting → no upgrades. MANDATORY RULE: Upgrade at EVERY rest site above threshold. [[cards/Bash]] should be upgraded by Floor 8.
+- **Upgrade Priority ([[characters/Ironclad]])**: 1. Bash (3 Vuln vs 2). 2. [[cards/Corruption]] (3E→2E). 3. [[cards/True Grit]] (chosen vs random exhaust). 4. [[cards/Armaments]] (1 card → ALL cards). 5. [[cards/Carnage]] (28 vs 20). 6. [[cards/Iron Wave]] (7/7 vs 5/5). 7. [[cards/Pommel Strike]] (10+draw 2 vs 9+draw 1). 8. [[cards/Thunderclap]] (7 vs 4 AOE). 9. SIO (11 vs 8). 10. [[cards/Headbutt]] (12 vs 9). 11. Defend (8 vs 5). Context overrides: Corruption above Bash if it's the engine piece. [[cards/Limit Break]] is #1 for Str-scaling decks approaching [[acts/Act 3]] boss.
Extract analysis ontology: methodology navigable via [[links]]
a401f00c
2026-05-19
heuristics/goals/analyze.md
@@ -2,83 +2,7 @@
Review a completed run and update the knowledge base. Extract what was learned. Write it down so the next play session is better.
-## Inputs
+Start from `ontology/analysis/index.md` to understand the methodology.
+Start from `ontology/index.md` to understand the game.
-- Run log at `analyst/runs/run_NNN.json` — decision log with game state after each action
-- Ontology files in `ontology/` — facts about the game
-- Heuristic files in `heuristics/` — strategic guidance
-- Working notes in `analyst/observations.md`
-
-**Read the run JSON first.** It has everything.
-
-## Priority 1: Prediction Errors
-
-Before analyzing strategy, analyze understanding. The player fills out combat plans with predictions. The run log has both the decision (with reasoning) and the result (actual state). Compare them.
-
-Prediction errors reveal broken mental models. More important than bad strategy because they're upstream.
-
-Look for:
-- **Damage miscalculations**: Predicted taking 5 but took 15
-- **Kill miscalculations**: Predicted killing enemy but it survived
-- **Mechanic misunderstandings**: Didn't know an enemy does X, didn't know a card exhausts
-- **Missing information**: Player said "I don't know what this does"
-
-For each prediction error: fix ontology if facts are wrong, update heuristic if strategy was wrong.
-
-## Priority 2: Strategic Patterns
-
-Decisions that were technically correct (math right) but strategically wrong (plan bad).
-
-## What You Write
-
-### Heuristics (`heuristics/`) — Strategic guidance
-
-Each entity can have a heuristic file. Write like an expert — clear, direct, actionable.
-
-Rules:
-1. **No run numbers.** State conclusions, not history.
-2. **No confidence tags.** If confident, state it. If uncertain, put it in `analyst/observations.md`.
-3. **Surgical updates.** One file per entry. Add a note, refine a threshold, adjust language.
-4. **Wrong info gets fixed.** Don't flag it — correct it.
-5. **Expert voice.** Reader should get exactly what they need.
-
-### Ontology (`ontology/`) — Corrections only
-
-Fix only factual errors: wrong damage numbers, missing attacks, incorrect mechanics. Do NOT add strategic commentary to ontology. Ontology is formally closed — entities describe what they ARE and DO, with `[[category/Name]]` links. No opinions.
-
-### Observations (`analyst/observations.md`)
-
-Things noticed but not confirmed:
-```
-## Unconfirmed
-- [item]: [what we think, what needs verification]
-
-## Open Questions
-- [question]: [context]
-```
-
-Promote confirmed observations to heuristics and remove from observations.
-
-## Process
-
-1. Read the run JSON. First pass: prediction errors. Second pass: strategic mistakes.
-2. Read existing heuristic files for entities encountered.
-3. Make surgical updates to `heuristics/`. Fix wrong info, add new entries, refine existing.
-4. Fix ontology only if facts are wrong.
-5. Add uncertain items to `analyst/observations.md`.
-6. Promote confirmed observations.
-
-## Where to Put Lessons
-
-**Attribute lessons to the mechanic that caused the mistake, not the fight where it happened.** If Fiend Fire exhausted critical debuff cards during a Champ fight, the lesson belongs on Fiend Fire's heuristic (or a Fiend Fire + Runic Pyramid interaction), not on The Champ's. The Champ didn't cause the problem — the card sequencing did. Ask: "would this lesson apply in a different fight?" If yes, it doesn't belong on the fight.
-
-Boss/enemy heuristics should describe how to fight that enemy. Card/relic heuristics should describe how that card/relic behaves across contexts. Don't overcorrect on the fatal fight.
-
-## Don't
-
-- Don't rewrite heuristic files from scratch
-- Don't speculate beyond evidence — uncertain goes in observations
-- Don't update for things that didn't come up in the run
-- Don't add strategic commentary to ontology files
-- Don't create or modify files in `analyst/runs/`
-- Don't put card/relic interaction lessons on boss files — put them on the card or relic
+Run log at `analyst/runs/run_NNN.json` — read it first.
ontology/analysis/attribution.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Attribution
+
+**Attribute lessons to the mechanic that caused the mistake, not the fight where it happened.**
+
+If Fiend Fire exhausted critical debuff cards during a Champ fight, the lesson belongs on Fiend Fire's heuristic (or a Fiend Fire + Runic Pyramid interaction), not on The Champ's. The Champ didn't cause the problem — the card sequencing did.
+
+## Test
+
+Ask: "Would this lesson apply in a different fight?" If yes, it doesn't belong on the fight.
+
+## Where Things Go
+
+- **Boss/enemy heuristics** describe how to fight that enemy
+- **Card/relic heuristics** describe how that card/relic behaves across contexts
+- **Topic heuristics** (combat.md, drafting.md) describe cross-cutting patterns
+
+Don't overcorrect on the fatal fight. Don't put card/relic interaction lessons on boss files — put them on the card or relic.
ontology/analysis/heuristic-updates.md
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+# Heuristic Updates
+
+Heuristics live in `heuristics/`. Each entity can have a heuristic file mirroring the ontology structure (`heuristics/cards/`, `heuristics/enemies/`, `heuristics/bosses/`, etc.). Topic-level heuristics cover cross-cutting concerns (`heuristics/combat.md`, `heuristics/map.md`, etc.).
+
+Write like an expert — clear, direct, actionable.
+
+## Rules
+
+1. **No run numbers.** State conclusions, not history.
+2. **No confidence tags.** If confident, state it. If uncertain, put it in [[analysis/observations]].
+3. **Surgical updates.** One file per entry. Add a note, refine a threshold, adjust language.
+4. **Wrong info gets fixed.** Don't flag it — correct it.
+5. **Expert voice.** Reader should get exactly what they need.
+
+## Don't
+
+- Don't rewrite heuristic files from scratch
+- Don't speculate beyond evidence — uncertain goes in observations
+- Don't update for things that didn't come up in the run
ontology/analysis/index.md
@@ -0,0 +1,39 @@
+# Run Analysis
+
+Review a completed run to extract what was learned and improve the knowledge base for future runs.
+
+## Inputs
+
+- **Run log** at `analyst/runs/run_NNN.json` — decision log with game state after each action
+- **Ontology** at `ontology/` — facts about the game
+- **Heuristics** at `heuristics/` — strategic guidance
+- **Working notes** at `analyst/observations.md`
+
+Read the run JSON first. It has everything.
+
+## Priorities
+
+1. **[[analysis/prediction-errors]]** — Before analyzing strategy, analyze understanding. Prediction errors reveal broken mental models. More important than bad strategy because they're upstream.
+
+2. **[[analysis/strategic-patterns]]** — Decisions that were technically correct (math right) but strategically wrong (plan bad).
+
+## Outputs
+
+The analyst writes to three locations, each with different rules:
+
+- **[[analysis/heuristic-updates]]** — Strategic guidance in `heuristics/`
+- **[[analysis/ontology-corrections]]** — Factual fixes in `ontology/`
+- **[[analysis/observations]]** — Uncertain findings in `analyst/observations.md`
+
+## Core Principle
+
+**[[analysis/attribution]]** — Attribute lessons to the mechanic that caused the mistake, not the fight where it happened.
+
+## Process
+
+1. Read the run JSON. First pass: prediction errors. Second pass: strategic mistakes.
+2. Read existing heuristic files for entities encountered.
+3. Make surgical updates to `heuristics/`. Fix wrong info, add new entries, refine existing.
+4. Fix ontology only if facts are wrong.
+5. Add uncertain items to `analyst/observations.md`.
+6. Promote confirmed observations.
ontology/analysis/observations.md
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+# Observations
+
+Working notes live in `analyst/observations.md`. Things noticed but not confirmed.
+
+## Format
+
+```
+## Unconfirmed
+- [item]: [what we think, what needs verification]
+
+## Open Questions
+- [question]: [context]
+```
+
+## Lifecycle
+
+- New uncertain findings go in as Unconfirmed
+- When a subsequent run confirms an observation, promote it to the appropriate heuristic file and remove it from observations
+- Observations that are disproven get removed with a note about why
ontology/analysis/ontology-corrections.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Ontology Corrections
+
+Ontology lives in `ontology/`. It is formally closed — entries describe what entities ARE and DO, using `[[category/Name]]` links. No opinions, no strategy.
+
+## When to Change Ontology
+
+Fix only factual errors:
+- Wrong damage numbers
+- Missing attacks or abilities
+- Incorrect mechanics
+- Wrong interactions
+
+## When NOT to Change Ontology
+
+Do NOT add strategic commentary. "Jaw Worm is dangerous" is a heuristic. "Jaw Worm attacks for 11" is ontology.
ontology/analysis/prediction-errors.md
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# Prediction Errors
+
+The player fills out combat plans with predictions. The run log has both the decision (with reasoning) and the result (actual state). Compare them.
+
+Prediction errors reveal broken mental models. They are Priority 1 because they're upstream of strategy — if the model of the game is wrong, strategy built on it will be wrong too.
+
+## What to Look For
+
+- **Damage miscalculations**: Predicted taking 5 but took 15
+- **Kill miscalculations**: Predicted killing enemy but it survived
+- **Mechanic misunderstandings**: Didn't know an enemy does X, didn't know a card exhausts
+- **Missing information**: Player said "I don't know what this does"
+
+## What to Do
+
+For each prediction error: fix [[analysis/ontology-corrections|ontology]] if facts are wrong, update [[analysis/heuristic-updates|heuristics]] if strategy was wrong.
ontology/analysis/strategic-patterns.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Strategic Patterns
+
+Decisions that were technically correct (math right) but strategically wrong (plan bad). Priority 2 — after [[analysis/prediction-errors]].
+
+Examples:
+- Correct damage calculation, but attacked when blocking was better
+- Correctly identified card values, but picked the wrong one for the deck's needs
+- Correct map reading, but chose a dangerous path when a safe one existed
+- Correct boss mechanics, but used resources too aggressively early in the fight
ontology/index.md
@@ -37,6 +37,10 @@ Each run plays through [[acts/Act 1]], [[acts/Act 2]], [[acts/Act 3]], then an o
- [[characters/Defect]] — Orb-based. Starter relic channels 1 Lightning at combat start.
- [[characters/Watcher]] — Stance-based. Starter relic adds Miracle to hand at combat start.
+## Analysis Methodology
+
+For run analysis, see [[analysis/index]] — prediction errors, attribution, file conventions, observation lifecycle.
+
## Strategic Guidance
Heuristics — cached reasoning about how to play — live in `heuristics/`. The directory mirrors the ontology structure: `heuristics/cards/`, `heuristics/enemies/`, `heuristics/bosses/`, etc. For any entity, check whether a heuristic file exists alongside the ontology entry.
Run 136 (F19): card identity verification, Byrd engine trap, Flight math
eeb5b631
2026-05-19
heuristics/cards/immolate.md
@@ -11,3 +11,5 @@ High-damage AOE attack. Hits ALL enemies. The Burn downside is minor.
**Snecko Eye synergy:** Frequently costs 0-1E instead of 2E, enabling multiple plays per turn.
**Matchups:** Excellent vs Byrds, Slime Boss split, 3 Cultists, Guardian, The Collector. Primary damage source in both consecutive victories.
+
+**Flight damage math:** Against Byrds (Flight 3), damage is halved and rounded down. Include Strength in the base BEFORE halving: (21 + Str) / 2 floored. With +2 Str from Inflame: floor(23/2) = 11 per Byrd, not 10. Forgetting Strength underestimates damage by 1 per Byrd.
heuristics/combat.md
@@ -55,7 +55,7 @@ CHOSEN: [A/B] because [reason]
## Combat Arithmetic Checklist
-Before every turn, verify each of these seven items.
+Before every turn, verify each of these eight items.
### 1. WHO has Weak?
@@ -107,6 +107,12 @@ When calculating damage against multi-hit enemies with Helix/Buffer, assume Bloc
Wraith Form gives Intangible but applies a permanent -1 Dexterity per turn. Three recorded deaths from playing it too early. Estimate the number of turns remaining before playing. Wraith Form is a FINISHER, not an opener.
+### 8. VERIFY CARD IDENTITY BEFORE PLAYING
+
+Before entering a `play N` command, confirm the card at index N matches the card in your plan. Hand indices shift after each play. Especially dangerous for 2E cards that look similar in reasoning (Bash vs Shockwave are both 2E and both target enemies, but Bash is single-target Attack damage while Shockwave is mass Weak+Vulnerable). Playing Bash when you mean Shockwave wastes 2E on minimal damage instead of critical debuffs.
+
+**Process:** After each card play, re-read the hand state. The card you intend to play next may have shifted index.
+
---
## Shockwave+ Timing Rule
heuristics/enemies/byrd.md
@@ -11,6 +11,10 @@
- Once Flight is at 0, burst with Vulnerable attacks to kill
- Expect 7-12 turn fights and 20-73 HP lost
+## Engine Setup Trap
+
+Do NOT spend 3 turns setting up powers (Evolve + Metallicize + Fire Breathing + Inflame). Each setup turn costs 12-23 unblocked HP because Byrds attack for 10-25 per turn and a single Defend + Metallicize only blocks 8. A 3-turn setup burns 40-60 HP before dealing any damage. Instead, play at most 1 power per turn while blocking, or skip setup entirely if HP is below 60%. Kill speed matters more than engine value when entering below 70% HP.
+
## Turn 2 Trap
If all 3 Byrds attack simultaneously (after gaining Str from buff turns), combined damage can reach 38+. With only 1 block card in hand, there is no survival path. The deck needs block density (multiple Shrug It Off, Ghostly Armor, Impervious).
de3b9927ec33d335e4a7fab592ea0a5b4558bd98|2026-05-19 21:31:39 -0400|Run 135 (F16, Hexaghost): Dark Embrace Burns anti-synergy, drafting overrides

## Preparation Checklist (must have at least 3 of 4)
-1. **Weak source** (Shockwave, Clothesline, Intimidate, Weak Potion) -- reduces Inferno from 42 to 28. MANDATORY.
+1. **Weak source** (Shockwave, Clothesline, Intimidate, Weak Potion) -- reduces all damage by 25%. Inferno scales with accumulated Strength from Inflame turns (first Inferno: 24 at A0, 30 at A4; second Inferno: 36 at A0, 42 at A4). Without Weak, this damage is unmitigable except by block. MANDATORY.
2. **HP above 70%** OR Pantograph relic
3. **Passive block** (Metallicize, Flame Barrier+) OR Impervious for the Inferno turn
4. **Burns management** OR damage scaling to end the fight before Burns overwhelm
@@ -19,10 +19,12 @@ Priority order:
5. Second Wind (mass Burns removal)
6. Damage scaling (Inflame, Spot Weakness, Demon Form, Rampage) -- shortens fight from 13 to 8-9 turns
+**Dark Embrace is NOT Burns management.** It draws a card when you exhaust a Burn, but Burns replenish every Sear turn. Once the deck has 3+ Burns, exhausting one with True Grit+ draws another Burn from the saturated draw pile. The "engine" becomes a wash. Dark Embrace is strong with finite status cards (Wounds, Dazed) but counterproductive against Hexaghost's continuously replenishing Burns.
+
## Strategy
- **Turn 1 (free):** Setup. Demon Form is ideal. Otherwise: Thunderclap + Metallicize + Evolve.
-- **Turn 2 (MUST BLOCK):** ~30 damage multi-hit. Budget at LEAST 1E for block. Do not spend all 3E on setup.
+- **Turn 2 (MUST BLOCK):** Divider = (floor(HP/12)+1) x 6 multi-hit. At 80 HP = 42, at 67 HP = 36, at 50 HP = 30. Budget at LEAST 1E for block. Do not spend all 3E on setup. Use Headbutt on Turn 1 to place Impervious on top of draw pile if Impervious is in the discard.
- **Save Weak for Inferno:** Apply Shockwave/Clothesline on the first Inferno turn.
- **Kill speed matters:** Shorter fight = fewer Burns = less attrition.

- Demon Form (+2 Str/turn, best scaling card for Hexaghost)
- Spot Weakness+ (+4 Str on attack turns)
-- Flame Barrier+ (16 block + 42 counter damage from Inferno's 7 hits)
+- Flame Barrier+ (16 block + counter damage from Inferno's 6 hits = 36 counter at Flame Barrier+ value of 6/hit)
- Incense Burner (Intangible on Inferno turn negates it entirely)
## What NOT to Do

- Waste Shockwave on a non-Inferno turn

## Character Matchups
heuristics/cards/dark-embrace.md
@@ -6,4 +6,6 @@ Draw engine for exhaust-heavy decks.
**Synergies:** True Grit+ (exhaust + draw every turn), Charon's Ashes (exhaust = draw + 3 AOE damage), Fiend Fire (exhaust entire hand = draw many cards enabling a second wave of plays), status cards (exhausting Slimed/Dazed/Wounds draws a real card), Sentinel (exhaust = draw + 2-3E refund).
-**Matchups:** Strong vs Book of Stabbing -- Wounds are exhaust fodder that convert into real draws. Dark Embrace + Burning Pact/True Grit+ turns every Wound into card advantage.
+**Anti-synergy with Burns saturation:** Dark Embrace draws from whatever is in the draw pile. Against Hexaghost, Burns accumulate faster than True Grit+ can exhaust them. Once the deck has 3+ Burns, exhausting a Burn draws another Burn -- the "engine" becomes a wash. Dark Embrace is strong with finite status cards (Wounds, Slimed, Dazed) that don't replenish, but weak against Hexaghost where Burns are continuously added. Do NOT buy Dark Embrace specifically to handle Hexaghost Burns.
+
+**Matchups:** Strong vs Book of Stabbing -- Wounds are exhaust fodder that convert into real draws. Dark Embrace + Burning Pact/True Grit+ turns every Wound into card advantage. Weak vs Hexaghost -- see anti-synergy note above.
heuristics/cards/impervious.md
@@ -8,6 +8,6 @@ Best single-card block in the game. One-time use (Exhaust).
**Upgrade priority:** High. 30->40 block. The extra 10 block is the difference between absorbing a big hit cleanly and taking chip.
-**Matchups:** Bronze Automaton -- THE most important card. Hyper Beam deals 45-57 damage. Save for Hyper Beam turns. Guardian -- absorbs the 32-damage attack completely (upgraded takes 0). Gremlin Leader -- save for Turn 4-5 when Rally-scaled damage reaches 34+. Collector -- critical for post-STRONG_DEBUFF turns (37-50+ incoming). Transient -- save for Turn 3+ (50+ damage), NOT Turn 1. Snake Plant -- absorbs 9x3=27 multi-hit.
+**Matchups:** Bronze Automaton -- THE most important card. Hyper Beam deals 45-57 damage. Save for Hyper Beam turns. Guardian -- absorbs the 32-damage attack completely (upgraded takes 0). Gremlin Leader -- save for Turn 4-5 when Rally-scaled damage reaches 34+. Collector -- critical for post-STRONG_DEBUFF turns (37-50+ incoming). Transient -- save for Turn 3+ (50+ damage), NOT Turn 1. Snake Plant -- absorbs 9x3=27 multi-hit. Hexaghost -- save for Divider (Turn 2) or Inferno. In a 16-card deck, there is a ~31% chance Impervious is NOT in your Turn 2 hand. Headbutt can place Impervious on top of draw pile the turn before a predicted Divider/Inferno. If Impervious is the only plan for a specific high-damage turn, the plan is unreliable -- need backup block (Weak, Flame Barrier, multiple Defends).
**Synergies:** Calipers (retains 25 block next turn upgraded), Barricade (retained permanently), Spot Weakness (use the safe turn to set up Strength).

- Brutality (self-damage in long fights)
- Berserk (self-Vulnerable is extremely dangerous)
-**Boss-specific overrides:**
+**Boss-specific overrides (APPLY BEFORE tier list):**
- Slime Boss visible: Evolve and Fire Breathing become MUST-TAKES
-- Hexaghost visible: Weak sources (Shockwave, Clothesline, Intimidate) become MUST-TAKES
+- Hexaghost visible: Weak sources (Shockwave, Clothesline, Intimidate) become MUST-TAKES. A Weak source outranks ANY generic tier list pick -- take Clothesline over Pommel Strike, take Intimidate over Iron Wave, etc. Without Weak, Hexaghost's Inferno and Tackles deal full damage for 16+ turns. No amount of block cards compensates.
