-## Dagger Pattern
-
-Fixed: Stab (9 damage + 1 Wound to discard) -> Explode (25 damage, Dagger dies). Each Dagger is a 2-turn time bomb.
-
-| Dagger Move | Damage | Effect |
-|-------------|--------|--------|
-| Stab | 9 | Adds 1 Wound to discard |
-| Explode | 25 | Dagger dies |
-
## Mechanics
-**Dagger Time Bombs:** Starting 2 Daggers will Stab Turn 1 (9 each + Wounds) then Explode Turn 2 (25 each = 50 total). Meanwhile Reptomancer herself deals 26-30. Total incoming Turn 2 can exceed 75 damage.
-
-**Wound Generation:** Daggers add Wounds (Unplayable status) to discard via Stab.
+Starts with 2 [[enemies/Dagger]] minions. Maximum 4 [[enemies/Dagger]] active at any time.
ontology/enemies/repulsor.md
@@ -6,19 +6,12 @@
## Pattern
-Each turn, 80% chance to use Bash (adds 2 Dazed to draw pile), 20% chance to use Repulse (attack). Cannot use Repulse twice in a row.
+Each turn, 80% chance to use Bash (adds 2 [[cards/Dazed]] to draw pile), 20% chance to use Repulse (attack). Cannot use Repulse twice in a row.
## Attacks
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
| Repulse | 11 (13 at A2) | -- |
-| Bash | -- | Adds 2 Dazed cards to draw pile |
+| Bash | -- | Adds 2 [[cards/Dazed]] to draw pile |
-## Mechanics
-
-**Dazed Generation:** Dazed cards are Unplayable + Ethereal status cards that clog the draw pile. Each Bash adds 2 Dazed, rapidly degrading hand quality. Over 4 turns, 6-8 Dazed cards can be added.
-
-## Appearances
-
-Appears with other Shapes (Spikers, Exploders) in the 3 Shapes encounter.
ontology/enemies/sentry.md
@@ -12,17 +12,9 @@ Outer two Sentries start with Bolt, middle Sentry starts with Beam. They alterna
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
-| Bolt | -- | Adds 2 Dazed to discard pile |
+| Bolt | -- | Adds 2 [[cards/Dazed]] to discard pile |
| Beam | 9 (10 at A2) | -- |
-Two Sentries on Beam = 18-20 total damage per turn.
-
## Mechanics
-**Artifact 1:** Each Sentry starts with 1 Artifact charge, blocking the first debuff application.
-
-**Dazed Generation:** Dazed cards are Unplayable + Ethereal status cards that clog draws. Each Bolt adds 2 Dazed to the discard pile.
-
-## Act 2 Appearance
-
-Sentries also appear in Act 2 hallway fights paired with other enemies (e.g., Sentry + Spheric Guardian). In these pairings, the Sentry still generates Dazed and attacks for ~9 damage per turn.
+Each Sentry starts with [[buffs/Artifact]] 1.
ontology/enemies/shelled-parasite.md
@@ -14,12 +14,8 @@ Cannot use Fell on first turn. 40% Double Strike, 40% Life Suck, 20% Fell. Canno
|------|--------|--------|
| Double Strike | 6x2 = 12 (7x2 = 14 at A2) | -- |
| Life Suck | 10 (12 at A2) | Heals equal to unblocked damage |
-| Fell | 18 (21 at A2) | Applies 2 Frail |
+| Fell | 18 (21 at A2) | Applies 2 [[debuffs/Frail]] |
## Mechanics
-**Plated Armor 14:** Reduces incoming damage by 14 per turn. Each attack that deals damage reduces Plated Armor by 1. Multi-hit attacks strip Plated Armor faster (Double Strike removes 2 stacks).
-
-**Stun on Break:** If all Plated Armor is broken, Shelled Parasite is Stunned for one turn (happens once per combat).
-
-**Life Suck Healing:** Heals for unblocked damage dealt, creating an attrition problem in long fights.
+Has [[buffs/Plated Armor]] 14. Stunned for one turn when all [[buffs/Plated Armor]] is removed (once per combat).
ontology/enemies/shield-gremlin.md
@@ -6,12 +6,12 @@
## Pattern
-While allies alive: Protect (give random ally 7 [[buffs/Block]]) every turn.
+While allies alive: Protect (give random ally 7 Block) every turn.
When alone: Shield Bash every turn.
## Attacks
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
-| Protect | -- | Give random ally 7 [[buffs/Block]] |
+| Protect | -- | Give random ally 7 Block |
| Shield Bash | 6 | Only when alone |
ontology/enemies/snake-plant.md
@@ -13,14 +13,8 @@
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
| Chomp | 7x3 = 21 (8x3 = 24 at A2) | -- |
-| Enfeebling Spores | -- | Applies 2 Frail + 2 Weak |
+| Enfeebling Spores | -- | Applies 2 [[debuffs/Frail]] + 2 [[debuffs/Weak]] |
## Mechanics
-**Malleable 3:** Upon receiving attack damage, gains Block. First hit: gains 3 block. Second hit: 4 block. Third hit: 5 block. Increases by 1 each time triggered in the same turn. Resets at start of player turn.
-
-**Frail + Weak Combination:** Frail cripples block (25% reduction) while Chomp (21-24) overwhelms weakened defenses.
-
-## Appearances
-
-Can appear in Unknown rooms (not just hallway combat).
+Starts with [[buffs/Malleable]] 3.
ontology/enemies/sneaky-gremlin.md
@@ -14,6 +14,3 @@ Uses Puncture every turn.
|------|--------|--------|
| Puncture | 9 | -- |
-## Mechanics
-
-- Highest damage-per-HP ratio in Act 1. Kill priority target.
ontology/enemies/snecko.md
@@ -12,16 +12,7 @@ Always starts with Perplexing Glare (Confused debuff). Then each turn: 60% Bite,
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
-| Perplexing Glare | -- | Applies Confused |
+| Perplexing Glare | -- | Applies [[debuffs/Confused]] |
| Bite | 15 (18 at A2) | -- |
-| Tail Whip | 8 (10 at A2) | Applies 2 Vulnerable (+ 2 Weak at A17) |
+| Tail Whip | 8 (10 at A2) | Applies 2 [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] (+ 2 [[debuffs/Weak]] at A17) |
-## Mechanics
-
-**Confusion:** When applied, all cards in hand are randomly assigned costs of 0-3 each turn for the rest of combat. Persists until combat ends.
-
-**X-Cost Interaction:** Whirlwind (X-cost) under Confusion uses ALL remaining energy regardless of randomized cost. X-cost cards are reliable under Confusion because they always consume whatever energy is left.
-
-**Tail Whip + Bite Combo:** Vulnerable from Tail Whip makes subsequent Bite attacks hit for 22+ damage.
-
-**Red Mask Relic:** Applies 1 Weak to Snecko at combat start, slightly reducing first attack.
ontology/enemies/spheric-guardian.md
@@ -2,13 +2,13 @@
- **Type:** Hallway
- **Act:** 2
-- **HP:** 20 (effectively 60+ with starting block)
+- **HP:** 20
## Starting State
-- Artifact 3
-- Block 40
-- Barricade (block never expires)
+- [[buffs/Artifact]] 3
+- 40 Block
+- [[buffs/Barricade]]
## Pattern
@@ -19,20 +19,6 @@ Fixed sequence: Activate (25 Block) -> Slam (10 damage + 5 Frail) -> then altern
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
| Activate | -- | Gains 25 Block |
-| Slam (first) | 10 | Applies 5 Frail |
+| Slam (first) | 10 | Applies 5 [[debuffs/Frail]] |
| Slam (subsequent) | 10x2 = 20 | -- |
-| Harden | 10 | Gains 15 Block |
-
-## Mechanics
-
-**Barricade:** Block never expires. The Spheric Guardian gains block on defend turns (15-20 per action) and since block persists, it stacks indefinitely.
-
-**Artifact 3:** First 3 debuff applications are negated.
-
-**Frail 5:** Reduces block from ALL sources by 25% for 5 turns. Defend gives 3 instead of 5. Shrug It Off gives 6 instead of 8.
-
-**Malleable Block Growth:** Combined with alternating attack/defend, block grows faster than chip damage can remove it at low Strength.
-
-## Appearances
-
-Paired with Sentry in Act 2 hallway fights. Paired with Spikers in Act 3.
+| Harden | 10 | Gains 15 Block |
\ No newline at end of file
ontology/enemies/spike-slime-l.md
@@ -12,12 +12,6 @@
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
-| Flame Tackle | 16 (18 at A2) | Adds 2 Slimed cards to discard pile |
-| Lick | -- | Applies 2 Frail |
+| Flame Tackle | 16 (18 at A2) | Adds 2 [[cards/Slimed]] to discard pile |
+| Lick | -- | Applies 2 [[debuffs/Frail]] |
| Split | -- | At 50% HP or below, spawns 2 Spike Slime (M) with current HP each |
-
-## Mechanics
-
-**Split at 50% HP:** Creates 2 medium slimes with current HP, extending the fight significantly.
-
-**Frail 2:** Reduces block gained from cards by 25%.

ontology/enemies/spiker.md
@@ -13,16 +13,8 @@ Each turn, 50/50 chance of Cut (attack) or Spike (buff Thorns). Cannot use Cut t
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
| Cut | 7 (9 at A2) | -- |
-| Spike | -- | Gains +2 Thorns |
+| Spike | -- | Gains +2 [[buffs/Thorns]] |
## Mechanics
-**Thorns 3 (innate):** Starts with Thorns 3. Each Spike adds +2 Thorns. Every time the player deals attack damage to a Spiker, the player takes damage equal to current Thorns.
-
-**Thorns Scaling:** 3 -> 5 -> 7 -> 9 -> 11 -> 13 -> 15 (after 6 Spikes, maximum reached). Multi-hit attacks trigger Thorns per hit (4-hit Blade Dance against Thorns 9 costs 36 HP).
-
-**Maximum Spikes:** After 6 Spike uses, Spiker can only use Cut.
-
-## Appearances
-
-Appears with other Shapes (Exploders, Repulsors) in the 3 Shapes encounter. Can appear in mixed Act 3 hallway fights.
+Starts with [[buffs/Thorns]] 3.

+| Constrict | -- | Apply 10 [[debuffs/Constricted]] |
| Smash | 22 | -- |
-## Mechanics
-
-- High HP pool makes it a long fight
-- Constricted stacks: each application adds more end-of-turn damage

ontology/enemies/the-maw.md
@@ -6,24 +6,15 @@
## Pattern
-- **Turn 1:** Roar (applies 3 Weak + 3 Frail). No damage.
+- **Turn 1:** Roar (applies 3 [[debuffs/Weak]] + 3 [[debuffs/Frail]]). No damage.
- **Turns 2+:** After Drool -> 50/50 Slam or Nom Nom. After Slam -> 50/50 Nom Nom or Drool. After Nom Nom -> always Drool.
## Attacks
| Move | Damage | Effect |
|------|--------|--------|
-| Roar | -- | Applies 3 Weak + 3 Frail |
+| Roar | -- | Applies 3 [[debuffs/Weak]] + 3 [[debuffs/Frail]] |
| Slam | 25 (30 at A2) | -- |
| Nom Nom | 5xN (N increases -- roughly current turn / 2 rounded up) | -- |
-| Drool | -- | Gains 3 Strength (5 at A5) |
+| Drool | -- | Gains 3 [[buffs/Strength]] (5 at A5) |
-## Mechanics
-
-**Turn 1 Debuff:** Applies Weak, Frail, and Vulnerable simultaneously. Lasts 2-3 turns.
-
-**Escalating Damage:** Early attacks are trivial (5-16 damage), but later attacks reach 33-56+.
-
-**Multiple Free Turns:** The Maw frequently buffs (Drool) instead of attacking, giving free turns for Power setup.
-
-**Fight Length:** 8-9 turns minimum due to 300 HP.
ontology/enemies/transient.md
@@ -3,7 +3,7 @@
- **Type:** Hallway
- **Act:** 3
- **HP:** 999
-- **Fading:** 5 (dies after 5 turns)
+- **[[buffs/Fading]]:** 5
## Pattern
@@ -14,12 +14,10 @@ Attacks every turn with escalating damage:
- Turn 4: 60 (70 at A2)
- Turn 5: 70 (80 at A2)
-Total damage over 5 turns: 250 at A0. Damage increases by 10 per turn.
+Damage increases by 10 per turn.
## Mechanics
-**Fading 5:** Loses 1 per turn, dies at 0 (Fading 6 at A17). The Transient attacks BEFORE fading -- you must survive every hit including the last.
+Has [[buffs/Fading]] 5 ([[buffs/Fading]] 6 at A17). The Transient attacks BEFORE fading.
-**Shifting:** When the Transient takes damage, it LOSES that much Strength for the rest of the turn. If the Transient would deal 50 and you deal 20 damage to it, it only hits for 30. This means attacking REDUCES its damage this turn.
-
-**Damage Efficiency:** 1 energy on a Strike (6 damage) = 6 less incoming via Shifting = effectively 6 block. 1 energy on a Defend (5 block) = 5 block. Attacking is strictly better than blocking because damage dealt via Shifting reduces the attack AND you still play block cards with remaining energy.
+Has [[buffs/Shifting]].
ontology/enemies/writhing-mass.md
@@ -16,12 +16,10 @@
|------|--------|--------|
| Multi Hit | 7x3 = 21 (9x3 = 27 at A2) | -- |
| Big Hit | 32 (38 at A2) | -- |
-| Debuff Attack | 10 (12 at A2) | Applies 2 Weak + 2 Vulnerable |
+| Debuff Attack | 10 (12 at A2) | Applies 2 [[debuffs/Weak]] + 2 [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] |
| Block Attack | 15 (16 at A2) | Gains 16 Block |
| Parasite | -- | Permanently adds Parasite curse to deck (once per combat) |
## Mechanics
-**Reactive:** Changes the Writhing Mass's weakness/intent when the player plays certain card types. Unpredictable.
-
-**Malleable:** Gains block each time it is hit. Multi-hit attacks (Whirlwind, Pummel) are punished -- each hit gives it more block.
+Has [[buffs/Reactive]] and [[buffs/Malleable]].

ontology/events/colosseum.md
@@ -2,8 +2,7 @@
- **Act:** 2
- **Options:**
- - Continue (forced): Enter a 2-wave combat. No alternative to avoid the first wave.
- - Wave 1: Fight 2 Slavers ([[enemies/Red Slaver]] + [[enemies/Blue Slaver]]). Combined ~20+ damage/turn. [[enemies/Blue Slaver]]'s [[debuffs/Entangled]] prevents attacks for 1 turn.
- - After Wave 1: Choose Fight (Wave 2 for rewards) or COWARDICE (escape with no additional reward).
- - Wave 2: Fight unpredictable enemies (can include [[enemies/Gremlin Nob]], [[enemies/Taskmaster]], or other elites).
-- **Note:** [[relics/Juzu Bracelet]] does NOT prevent this event (it is an event with forced combat, not a monster room).
+ - Enter (forced): 2-wave combat.
+ - Wave 1: Fight [[enemies/Red Slaver]] + [[enemies/Blue Slaver]]
+ - After Wave 1: Choose to Fight (Wave 2) or Cowardice (leave with no reward)
+ - Wave 2: Fight an elite-tier enemy. Reward: Relic + card reward.

- Leave: Nothing happens

-- **Known book relics:**
- - [[relics/Enchiridion]]: Free random 0-cost [[types/Power]] card at start of each combat
- - [[relics/Nilry's Codex]]: At end of each turn, choose 1 of 3 randomly generated cards to add to hand
- - [[relics/Necronomicon]]: First [[types/Attack]] costing 2+ energy each turn plays twice. Adds Necronomicurse (unplayable, unremovable) to the deck.

- - I am Rich: Gain 999 gold. Obtain 2 Normality [[types/Curse]]s (limit hand to 3 cards per turn).

ontology/interface/stream.md
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# Stream
+
+The agent plays for a live stream audience via an OBS overlay.
+
+## Overlay
+
+The stream overlay displays:
+- Current game state
+- The agent's most recent action and reasoning (`reason=` parameter)
+- The agent's strategic analysis (`think()` output)
+
+## Visibility
+
+- The `reason=` parameter on `send()` and `turn()` is displayed to viewers as the rationale for each action
+- The `think()` function posts longer strategic analysis visible in the overlay's thinking panel
+- Viewers see WHAT the agent does and WHY — both must be present for every action
ontology/interface/tools.md
@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+# Tools
+
+The agent interacts with the game through Python functions imported from `cmd.py`.
+
+## Setup
+
+```python
+import sys
+sys.path.insert(0, r"C:\Users\tkond\projects\autoplay\games\sts1")
+from cmd import state, send, turn, play, end, choose, proceed, skip, potion_use, potion_discard, plan, reason, think, deck, start
+```
+
+## Observation
+
+| Function | Description |
+|----------|-------------|
+| `state()` | Returns formatted text of the current game state (HP, enemies, hand, map, etc.) |
+| `deck()` | Returns full deck contents sorted by type with costs and upgrade status |
+
+## Knowledge Loading
+
+| Function | Description |
+|----------|-------------|
+| `plan()` | Auto-detects context and loads relevant ontology + heuristics. In combat: enemy entries, hand card entries, relic entries, resolved [[links]]. Outside combat: strategy, character heuristics, boss entry, deck/relic/potion notes. |
+| `reason(topic)` | Searches ontology and heuristics for the named entity. Returns both layers with resolved [[links]]. |
+
+## Action
+
+| Function | Description |
+|----------|-------------|
+| `send(command, reason="...")` | Execute a single game action. `reason` is displayed on stream overlay. |
+| `turn([actions], reason="...")` | Execute a full combat turn as a batch of actions. Each action is a string like `"play Bash 0"` or `"end"`. |
+| `play(card, target)` | Play a card at a target. Card is 1-indexed or name. Target is 0-indexed enemy. |
+| `end()` | End the current turn. |
+| `choose(option)` | Choose an option by index or name. |
+| `proceed()` | Confirm / proceed / continue. |
+| `skip()` | Skip / cancel / leave. |
+| `potion_use(slot, target)` | Use a potion from a slot at a target. |
+| `potion_discard(slot)` | Discard a potion from a slot. |
+| `start(character, ascension)` | Start a new run with the given character and ascension level. |
+
+## Communication
+
+| Function | Description |
+|----------|-------------|
+| `think(reasoning, label)` | Post strategic analysis to the stream overlay. `reasoning` is the text viewers see. `label` is a short heading (default: "Strategy"). |
+
+## Card and Enemy References
+
+- Card names resolve against the current hand: `"play Bash 0"` finds Bash in hand
+- Card indices are 1-indexed: `"play 1 0"` plays the first card
+- Enemy indices are 0-indexed and absolute: they don't shift when enemies die
+- Enemy names work when enemies have distinct names: `"play Bash Jaw Worm"`
+- When enemies share names (e.g., multiple Byrds), use numeric indices
ontology/potions/attack-potion.md
@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@
# Attack Potion
-- **Effect:** Choose 1 of 3 random [[types/Attack]] cards and add it to your hand. It costs 0 energy this turn.

@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@

ontology/potions/skill-potion.md

@@ -1,5 +1,4 @@
# Smoke Bomb
-- **Effect:** Escape from a non-boss combat. Immediately ends the fight. No rewards are given.
- **Rarity:** Rare
-- **Interaction:** Cannot be used against bosses. Cards can still be played on the same turn before using Smoke Bomb. Does not count as a card play (no [[debuffs/Time Warp]] counter).
+- **Effect:** Escape from a non-boss combat. No rewards are given.
ontology/potions/snecko-oil.md
@@ -1,5 +1,4 @@
# Snecko Oil
-- **Effect:** Draw 5 cards. Randomize the cost of ALL cards in hand (0-3 energy each), including cards already in hand before the draw.
- **Rarity:** Rare
-- **Interaction:** Cards already in hand also get costs randomized. Similar to [[relics/Snecko Eye]]. When multiple copies exist with different randomized costs, the game selects the cheapest copy when played by name.
+- **Effect:** Draw 5 cards. Randomize the cost of ALL cards in your hand (0-3 energy each).

@@ -1,4 +1,4 @@

# Necronomicon
-- **Rarity:** Rare (Book relic)
-- **Effect:** The first [[types/Attack]] card costing 2 or more energy played each turn is played a second time automatically. Adds a Necronomicurse (unplayable, cannot be removed except by special means) to the deck.
-- **Obtained:** Cursed Tome event (costs 16 HP total)
-- **Note:** Triggers only on the FIRST 2+ cost [[types/Attack]] each turn. The entire card effect replays (damage, draw effects, channel effects, debuffs).
+- **Rarity:** Rare
+- **Effect:** The first [[types/Attack]] card costing 2 or more energy played each turn is played a second time automatically. Adds a [[types/Curse]] (Necronomicurse) to your deck.

ontology/rules/block.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Block
+
+Absorbs incoming damage before HP. Damage reduces Block first; excess hits HP.
+
+## Player Block
+
+- Resets to 0 at start of player's turn
+- Modified by [[buffs/Dexterity]]: +1 per point to block gained from cards
+- Modified by [[debuffs/Frail]]: x0.75 block from cards
+- Persistence exceptions: [[buffs/Barricade]] (no reset), [[relics/Calipers]] (lose only 15), [[cards/Blur]] (no reset this turn)
+
+## Enemy Block
+
+- Resets to 0 at start of enemy's turn (same as player)
+- Persistence exceptions: enemies with [[buffs/Barricade]] retain block between turns
ontology/rules/buffs-and-debuffs.md
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+# Buffs and Debuffs
+
+## Buffs
+
+A buff is a positive status effect on an entity (player or enemy). Displayed as green icons.
+
+- Buffs persist until their stacks reach 0, at which point they disappear
+- Most buffs have fixed stacks that don't change on their own (e.g., [[buffs/Strength]] 3 stays at 3)
+- Some buffs have stacks that decrement each turn (e.g., [[buffs/Intangible]] loses 1 stack per turn)
+- Some buffs have stacks consumed by events (e.g., [[buffs/Buffer]] loses 1 stack when HP loss is prevented)
+- Some buffs have stacks that increase via other effects (e.g., [[buffs/Ritual]] adds [[buffs/Strength]] each turn)
+- All buffs are removed between combats unless otherwise noted
+
+## Debuffs
+
+A debuff is a negative status effect on an entity. Displayed as red icons.
+
+- Most debuffs have stacks that decrement at the start of the affected entity's turn
+- When stacks reach 0, the debuff disappears
+- [[buffs/Artifact]] prevents debuff application (consumes 1 Artifact stack per prevented debuff)
+- All debuffs are removed between combats
+
+## Stacking
+
+When a buff/debuff is applied and the entity already has it:
+- Stack values are added (e.g., applying 2 [[debuffs/Weak]] to an entity with 1 [[debuffs/Weak]] = 3 stacks)
ontology/rules/card-draw.md
@@ -3,11 +3,11 @@
- Standard draw: 5 cards per turn
- Draw happens at start of turn from the draw pile
- When the draw pile is empty and a draw triggers, the discard pile shuffles into the draw pile
-- Cards that draw (Pommel Strike, Shrug It Off, Battle Trance) add cards to hand mid-turn
- Hand size limit: 10 cards. Draws past 10 are discarded.
-- At end of turn, all cards in hand (except [[Retain]]ed cards) go to the discard pile
+- At end of turn, all cards in hand (except [[rules/Retain]]ed cards) go to the discard pile
-## Draw modifiers
-- Relics: Ring of the Snake (+2 Turn 1), Bag of Preparation (+2 Turn 1)
-- Powers: Machine Learning (+1/turn), Brutality (+1/turn)
-- Cards: Offering (draw 3), Battle Trance (draw 3), Backflip (draw 2)
+## Draw Modifiers
+
+- Relics: [[relics/Ring of the Snake]] (+2 Turn 1), [[relics/Bag of Preparation]] (+2 Turn 1)
+- Powers: [[cards/Machine Learning]] (+1/turn), [[cards/Brutality]] (+1/turn)
+- Cards: [[cards/Offering]] (draw 3), [[cards/Battle Trance]] (draw 3), [[cards/Backflip]] (draw 2)
ontology/rules/damage.md
@@ -5,8 +5,8 @@
`floor((card_base_damage + strength) * multipliers)`
Order of operations:
-1. Add [[Strength]] to base damage
-2. Apply multipliers ([[Weak]] on attacker: x0.75, [[Vulnerable]] on target: x1.5)
+1. Add [[buffs/Strength]] to base damage
+2. Apply multipliers ([[debuffs/Weak]] on attacker: x0.75, [[debuffs/Vulnerable]] on target: x1.5)
3. Floor the result (round down)
Multipliers stack multiplicatively: `floor(base * 0.75 * 1.5)`
@@ -23,4 +23,4 @@ Multipliers stack multiplicatively: `floor(base * 0.75 * 1.5)`
## Enemy Intent Display
-Enemy intent numbers show the FINAL damage including all modifiers (Vulnerable on player, Weak on enemy, enemy Strength). Use the displayed number directly for block calculations — do not recalculate modifiers on top of it.
+Enemy intent numbers show the FINAL damage including all modifiers ([[debuffs/Vulnerable]] on player, [[debuffs/Weak]] on enemy, enemy [[buffs/Strength]]). Use the displayed number directly for block calculations.
ontology/rules/energy.md
@@ -1,12 +1,12 @@
# Energy
-- Base energy: 3 per turn (at Ascension 0, no modifiers)
+- Base energy: 3 per turn (Ascension 0, no modifiers)
- Energy resets each turn — unspent energy is lost
-- Some relics modify base energy: Lantern (+1 Turn 1 only), Coffee Dripper (+1 permanent, can't rest), Fusion Hammer (+1 permanent, can't upgrade), Ectoplasm (+1 permanent, can't gain gold), Sozu (+1 permanent, can't gain potions)
-- Some cards grant energy: Offering (+2), Bloodletting (+2), Seeing Red (+2), Aggregate (variable), [[Miracle]] (+1)
+- Some relics modify base energy: [[relics/Lantern]] (+1 Turn 1 only), [[relics/Coffee Dripper]] (+1, can't rest), [[relics/Fusion Hammer]] (+1, can't upgrade), [[relics/Ectoplasm]] (+1, can't gain gold), [[relics/Sozu]] (+1, can't gain potions)
+- Some cards grant energy: [[cards/Offering]] (+2), [[cards/Bloodletting]] (+2), [[cards/Seeing Red]] (+2), [[cards/Aggregate]] (variable), [[cards/Miracle]] (+1)
- X-cost cards consume ALL remaining energy when played
-## Energy from Stances (Watcher)
+## Stances ([[characters/Watcher]])
-- Leaving [[Calm]] grants +2 Energy
-- Entering [[Divinity]] grants +3 Energy
+- Leaving [[buffs/Calm]] grants +2 Energy
+- Entering [[buffs/Divinity]] grants +3 Energy
ontology/rules/retain.md
@@ -2,7 +2,6 @@
- **Effect:** Card stays in hand at end of turn instead of being discarded
- **Types:**
- - Innate Retain: card always retains (keyword on the card itself, e.g., Miracle)
- - Granted Retain: given by other effects (Well-Laid Plans, Establishment)
-- **Interaction:** Retained cards still count toward hand size. They are not discarded and not drawn again.
-- **Watcher:** Heavy use of Retain (Miracle, Perseverance, Establishment Power)
+ - Innate Retain: keyword on the card itself (e.g., [[cards/Miracle]])
+ - Granted Retain: given by other effects ([[cards/Well-Laid Plans]], [[cards/Establishment]])
+- **Interaction:** Retained cards still count toward hand size limit (10). They are not discarded and not drawn again.

ontology/types/attack.md
@@ -1,15 +1,7 @@
# Attack
-A card type. Attacks deal damage to enemies.

A card type. Curses are permanent additions to the deck (persist between combats) until removed.
## Properties
-
-- Unplayable — cannot be played, just clogs hand
-- Persist in the deck between combats (unlike Status cards)

# Power
-A card type. Powers are played once and remain in effect for the rest of combat. They do not go to the discard pile — they leave the hand and activate permanently.
-
-## Mechanical interactions
+A card type. Powers are played once and remain in effect for the rest of combat. They do not go to the discard pile.
+## Properties
- Cannot be retrieved once played (no discard/draw pile interaction)
-- Triggers Awakened One's Curiosity: +2 Strength per Power played (Phase 2 only)
-- Does NOT trigger Gremlin Nob's Enrage
-- Does NOT trigger Hex (Chosen)
-- Mummified Hand: playing a Power makes a random card in hand cost 0
-- Bird-Faced Urn: heal 2 HP per Power played
-- Multiple copies of the same Power stack (e.g., two Metallicize = double the block)
+- Multiple copies of the same Power stack additively
+
+## Modifiers
+- Affected by [[cards/Amplify]], [[relics/Mummified Hand]], [[relics/Bird-Faced Urn]]

ontology/types/status.md
@@ -1,21 +1,11 @@
# Status
-A card type. Status cards are added to the player's deck/hand by enemies or events. They are generally harmful.
+A card type. Status cards are added to the player's deck or hand by enemies during combat. They are generally harmful and are removed after combat ends.
## Properties
+- Exist only within combat (not persistent between combats)
+- Some are Unplayable, some cost energy to play
-- Cannot be permanently removed from the deck (they're added during combat and leave after combat)
-- Some are Unplayable (Burn, Dazed, Wound) — they clog the hand
-- Some cost energy to play (Slimed costs 1E, does nothing except Exhaust itself)
-- Some have Ethereal (Dazed) — self-exhaust at end of turn if not played
-- Some deal damage (Burn: 2 damage at end of turn if in hand)
+## Status Cards
-## Common Status cards
-
-| Card | Playable | Effect | Source |
-|------|----------|--------|--------|
-| Slimed | Yes (1E) | Exhaust | Slime enemies |
-| Burn | No | 2 damage at end of turn if in hand | Hexaghost |
-| Dazed | No | Ethereal (self-exhausts end of turn) | Chosen (Hex), Sentries |
-| Wound | No | Nothing (pure hand clog) | Book of Stabbing |
-| Void | No | Ethereal, lose 1 Energy when drawn | Nemesis, some events |
+[[cards/Wound]], [[cards/Burn]], [[cards/Dazed]], [[cards/Void]], [[cards/Slimed]]
Restructure knowledge base: playbook → ontology + heuristics

heuristics/bosses/awakened-one.md
@@ -0,0 +1,54 @@
+# Awakened One
+
+**Core rule:** Phase 1: play ALL Powers (dump strategy -- Str resets at phase transition). Phase 2: play ZERO Powers (every Power gives the boss +2 permanent Str that compounds across all remaining turns).
+
+## Phase 1 -- Power Dump Strategy
+
+- Play ALL Powers early (the Curiosity Str gained resets when Phase 1 ends)
+- Apply Vulnerable and burst down Phase 1 quickly
+- Use Piercing Wail and Weak to counteract Curiosity Str during Phase 1
+- Kill Cultist minions quickly (they scale with Ritual)
+- Dex and After Image effects persist into Phase 2 -- Phase 1 Power plays are doubly valuable
+
+## Phase 2 -- Zero Powers, Maximum Discipline
+
+- **0 Powers is the rule.** Confirmed victory with ZERO Powers played in Phase 2.
+- **1 Power is the absolute maximum** in desperate situations.
+- Every Power beyond the first: +2 Str = ~+6 extra damage per multi-attack turn. Compounds across every remaining turn.
+- Must deal >10 net damage per turn to overcome Regenerate 10.
+
+## How to Win Without Powers
+
+- **Poison (Silent):** Catalyst+ triples poison, defeating Regenerate 10. Prioritize poison from Skills and Attacks.
+- **Strength scaling (Ironclad):** Inflame, Spot Weakness (both non-Power). Limit Break doubles Str.
+- **Front-loaded damage:** Immolate+, Carnage+, Glass Knife
+- **Block without Powers:** Impervious, Defend, Backflip, Leg Sweep
+- **Weak:** Neutralize, Leg Sweep, Sucker Punch
+
+## Dead Branch Strategy (confirmed victory)
+
+Dead Branch + exhaust-heavy deck is the best approach:
+1. Phase 1: dump all Powers. Apply Terror for Vuln 99.
+2. Phase 2: ZERO Powers. Use exhaust cards (Terror, Piercing Wail, Adrenaline, Storm of Steel) for Dead Branch procs generating random cards.
+3. Shivs from Cloak and Dagger exhaust for more procs, creating chain reactions.
+4. After Image + Dex + Blur from Phase 1 provides blocking without new Powers.
+
+## Preparation Checklist
+
+1. Damage source that works without Powers
+2. Block density (Impervious, Leg Sweep, Backflip)
+3. Weak application (Neutralize, Leg Sweep)
+4. Sustain vs Regenerate 10 (Catalyst+ on poison, or high Str + multi-hit)
+5. Enter at 70%+ HP
+
+## Deck Building
+
+- **Without Dead Branch:** Deprioritize Power cards during drafting. Every Power in deck is a wasted draw in Phase 2.
+- **With Dead Branch:** Powers are welcome (play all in Phase 1, ignore in Phase 2).
+- **Terror is the MVP card** (0-1E for Vuln 99 + exhaust + Dead Branch trigger)
+- If deck is Power-heavy without Dead Branch, this is the hardest Act 3 boss.
+
+## Potion Timing
+
+- Save at least 1 potion for Phase 2 (the harder phase)
+- Poison-applying potions bypass the Power restriction entirely
heuristics/bosses/bronze-automaton.md
@@ -0,0 +1,41 @@
+# Bronze Automaton
+
+**Core rule:** Enter at 70%+ HP. Use ONLY non-exhausting debuffs (Bash+, Thunderclap) for Artifact stripping. SAVE exhausting Weak sources (Intimidate, Shockwave) for Hyper Beam survival. Basic Defends cannot survive Hyper Beam.
+
+## Preparation Checklist
+
+1. Enter at 70%+ HP
+2. Artifact strippers (non-exhausting): Thunderclap, Bash+. Need 3+ applications.
+3. Weak source saved for Hyper Beam: Intimidate, Shockwave+, or Weak Potion
+4. Block scaling: Shrug It Off, Flame Barrier, Metallicize, Impervious, Ghostly Armor (basic Defends are NOT enough)
+5. Burst damage for post-Hyper Beam stun window
+6. Redundant key cards (Stasis steals cards -- single copies are fragile)
+
+## Fight Phases
+
+1. **Turns 1-3 (Artifact Strip):** Strip 3 Artifact with Bash+ and Thunderclap. Do NOT use Intimidate or Shockwave.
+2. **Turn 3-4 (Debuff Window):** Once Artifact is 0, apply Shockwave+ (Weak 3 + Vuln 3).
+3. **Turns 4-7 (Burst Phase):** With Vuln active, unload burst damage. Goal: 150+ damage in 3-4 turns.
+4. **Turn 6-8 (Hyper Beam #1):** MUST have 30+ block AND Weak active. Impervious+ covers cleanly.
+5. **Stun Window #1:** Free damage turn. Maximize output.
+6. **Turn 12-13 (Hyper Beam #2):** +12 Str means 57+ damage. Need second massive block source or Weak + multiple block cards.
+
+## What NOT to Do
+
+- Use Intimidate or Shockwave for Artifact stripping (they exhaust -- save for Hyper Beam)
+- Play Impervious on non-Hyper-Beam turns when Hyper Beam is approaching
+- Enter below 60% HP
+- Enter without block scaling beyond basic Defends
+- Rely on a single irreplaceable card (Stasis will steal something)
+- Take Runic Dome (cannot predict Hyper Beam timing without intent visibility)
+- Plan to recover exhausted cards with Liquid Memories (retrieves from DISCARD only, not EXHAUST)
+
+## Dark Shackles Interaction
+
+Reduces Hyper Beam by ~9 damage but NOT sufficient alone. Still need 30+ block on top. Dark Shackles + Metallicize + Ghostly Armor + 2 Defends = ~31 block vs reduced Hyper Beam = survivable at 70%+ HP.
+
+## Key Lessons from Deaths
+
+- Insufficient block scaling (basic Defends = 20 max vs 38+ Hyper Beam) is the #1 killer
+- Intimidate wasted on Artifact stripping caused death when no Weak was available for Hyper Beam
+- Long fights (12+ turns) face a second Hyper Beam at 57+ damage -- need kill speed or second Impervious
heuristics/bosses/donu-and-deca.md
@@ -0,0 +1,35 @@
+# Donu and Deca
+
+**Core rule:** AOE is king (every point does double duty). Focus fire Donu first (stops Strength scaling). Strength scaling + Heavy Blade for the finisher.
+
+## Winning Strategy (confirmed in 2 victories)
+
+1. **AOE is king.** Immolate+ hits BOTH bosses every cast. Three Immolate+ in one turn dealt 102 AOE.
+2. **Focus fire Donu.** All single-target attacks go to Donu. AOE handles Deca passively.
+3. **Strength scaling is the finisher.** Spot Weakness+ -> Limit Break -> Heavy Blade (50+ damage) as lethal finisher.
+4. **Snecko Eye is exceptional.** 7 cards/turn with random costs. Multiple 0-cost Immolate+ plays win the fight.
+5. **Dual Wield+ on Immolate+** creates three copies in hand for 100+ AOE.
+
+## Preparation Checklist
+
+1. AOE damage (Immolate+ is the best card)
+2. Strength scaling (Spot Weakness+, Inflame, Limit Break, Heavy Blade)
+3. Enter at 70%+ HP (ideally full). Combined incoming is 20-32/turn.
+4. Block density for 6-10 turns
+5. Potions: Snecko Oil Turn 1, Duplication Potion on 0-cost Immolate+, Strength Potion compounds with Limit Break
+
+## Key Cards
+
+- **Immolate+:** Best card in the fight (28-34 AOE per play)
+- **Heavy Blade+:** 5x Str multiplier finisher
+- **Dual Wield+:** Copies Immolate+ for triple AOE turns
+- **Limit Break+ (no exhaust):** Most impactful upgrade for this fight -- enables repeated Str doubling
+- **Flex+ with Limit Break+:** 4 temporary Str doubled by Limit Break+ = effectively 10 permanent Str
+- **Evolve:** Converts Burns from Immolate into card draws
+
+## Potion Timing
+
+- Snecko Oil Turn 1 (expands hand to 10 cards)
+- Strength Potion Turn 2 (compounds with every attack)
+- Duplication Potion on 0-cost Immolate+ (wait for favorable Snecko roll)
+- Liquid Memories retrieves Immolate+ from discard at new Snecko cost
heuristics/bosses/hexaghost.md
@@ -0,0 +1,51 @@
+# Hexaghost
+
+**Core rule:** Damage output is the #1 priority. Weak and block keep you alive temporarily, but insufficient damage is the actual killer. In 13+ turn fights, Burns accumulate and create a death spiral.
+
+## Preparation Checklist (must have at least 3 of 4)
+
+1. **Weak source** (Shockwave, Clothesline, Intimidate, Weak Potion) -- reduces Inferno from 42 to 28. MANDATORY.
+2. **HP above 70%** OR Pantograph relic
+3. **Passive block** (Metallicize, Flame Barrier+) OR Impervious for the Inferno turn
+4. **Burns management** OR damage scaling to end the fight before Burns overwhelm
+
+## Burns Management (CRITICAL)
+
+Priority order:
+1. Evolve + Fire Breathing (the best anti-Burns engine -- Burns become free damage with no downside)
+2. Evolve standalone (neutralizes hand clog)
+3. True Grit+ (exhaust Burns for 9 block)
+4. Fire Breathing standalone (Burns drawn deal 6-10 damage to Hexaghost)
+5. Second Wind (mass Burns removal)
+6. Damage scaling (Inflame, Spot Weakness, Demon Form, Rampage) -- shortens fight from 13 to 8-9 turns
+
+## Strategy
+
+- **Turn 1 (free):** Setup. Demon Form is ideal. Otherwise: Thunderclap + Metallicize + Evolve.
+- **Turn 2 (MUST BLOCK):** ~30 damage multi-hit. Budget at LEAST 1E for block. Do not spend all 3E on setup.
+- **Save Weak for Inferno:** Apply Shockwave/Clothesline on the first Inferno turn.
+- **Kill speed matters:** Shorter fight = fewer Burns = less attrition.
+
+## What Works
+
+- Evolve + Fire Breathing engine (confirmed clean victories)
+- Cultist Potion + Rampage (devastating scaling, can kill in 9 turns without Weak)
+- Demon Form (+2 Str/turn, best scaling card for Hexaghost)
+- Spot Weakness+ (+4 Str on attack turns)
+- Flame Barrier+ (16 block + 42 counter damage from Inferno's 7 hits)
+- Incense Burner (Intangible on Inferno turn negates it entirely)
+
+## What NOT to Do
+
+- Enter below 70% HP without Pantograph
+- Fight without Burns management OR damage scaling (six deaths had Weak but no way to handle Burns)
+- Play Brutality (Burns + Brutality self-damage = death spiral)
+- Play Berserk (self-Vulnerable + Inferno = death)
+- Waste Shockwave on a non-Inferno turn
+- Take Odd Mushroom when Hexaghost is boss (17% less Vuln damage extends fight)
+
+## Character Matchups
+
+**Silent:** No native Burns management. Only solution is kill speed: poison scaling (Deadly Poison + Catalyst+, Noxious Fumes) or Shiv burst with amplifiers (Accuracy, Terror). Save Piercing Wail for Inferno (do NOT spend on Gremlin Nob). Save Crippling Cloud for Inferno turn (Weak is worth more than 4 poison). Masterful Stab is a trap (cost increases with Burns chip damage).
+
+**Watcher:** Enter Wrath ONLY on free/debuff turns (Inflame intent). Do NOT enter Wrath on Turn 2 (Divider would be doubled = instant death). Wave of the Hand for Weak on Inferno. Kill speed target: 9-11 turns via Wrath burst. Judgment finishes at 30-40 HP remaining. Entry at 50%+ HP minimum.
heuristics/bosses/slime-boss.md
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+# Slime Boss
+
+**Core rule:** AOE is MANDATORY for the split phase. Without AOE, you must single-target each of 4+ enemies. Enter at 60%+ HP.
+
+## Preparation Checklist
+
+1. AOE for the split (Thunderclap, Whirlwind, Cleave) -- MANDATORY
+2. Burst single-target to kill one slime fast (Fiend Fire, Carnage+)
+3. Exhaust tools for Slimed cards (Fiend Fire converts them to 7 damage each, True Grit+, Feel No Pain)
+4. Feed+ for massive Max HP gain (best Feed fight in Act 1, confirmed +22 Max HP)
+5. At least 1 upgrade before the fight
+6. Enough block/HP to survive 2-3 turns of 25-36 combined damage
+
+## Strategy
+
+- **Pre-split:** Use debuff turns (free turns) for damage. Apply Vulnerable. Manage damage near the 70 HP threshold to avoid massive overkill.
+- **Splitting a slime about to attack IS your block:** If a slime is close to its split threshold and about to deal damage, prioritize damage to split it (removing the attacking enemy from the board is better than blocking).
+- **At split:** Thunderclap for mass Vulnerable on both slimes. AOE with Whirlwind or Cleave.
+- **Post-split:** SPREAD DAMAGE to both large slimes before killing either one. Killing one creates a 3-enemy nightmare. Reduce both to low HP, then kill in quick succession.
+- **Slimed management:** Exhaust as priority. Each one exhausted is one fewer dead draw for the rest of combat.
+
+## What NOT to Do
+
+- Enter without AOE (single-target cannot handle the split)
+- Enter with 0 upgrades (makes the fight dramatically harder)
+- Enter below 60% HP
+- Play Berserk (self-Vulnerable against 2 enemies doing 25-36 combined is suicidal)
+- Focus all damage on one large slime while ignoring the other
+- Use Hemokinesis at low HP during split phase
+
+## Confirmed Winning Approach
+
+Evolve + Thunderclap+ + Spot Weakness + Shuriken: Evolve neutralizes Slimed clog. Thunderclap+ applies AOE Vulnerable on split slimes. Spot Weakness + Shuriken provide Strength scaling across multi-enemy phase.
heuristics/bosses/the-champ.md
@@ -0,0 +1,26 @@
+# The Champ
+
+**Core rule:** Save debuffs for AFTER the Anger transition (below 50% HP). All debuffs applied before Anger are completely wasted. Burst through Phase 2 quickly.
+
+## Strategy
+
+- **Demon Form and slow scaling:** Need enough turns to ramp BEFORE Execute triggers. If Demon Form played Turn 1 and Execute happens Turn 5, only +10 Strength -- may not be enough.
+- **Burst through Phase 2:** Have enough burst to kill quickly after Anger. Slow attrition decks struggle because Metallicize 5 + cleansed debuffs mean he takes less damage and deals more.
+- **Re-apply debuffs after Anger:** Shockwave, Disarm, and other debuffs are wasted if applied before Anger. Save them (or have second copies) for Phase 2.
+- **HP management:** Enter at high HP. Post-Anger attacks with +8-12 Strength hit for 27x2 or more.
+- **Vulnerability window:** After Execute, The Champ spends one turn on the transition. Use this for block and preparation.
+
+## Character Matchups
+
+**Ironclad:**
+- Demon Form: good but needs early play (need accumulated Str to outscale Champ's +8 Str and Metallicize 5)
+- Shockwave+: save for AFTER Anger if possible
+- Reaper: single-use -- time for post-Anger when attacks are strongest
+- Dark Shackles: excellent emergency post-Anger
+- Heavy Blade+: strong finisher if Str is high from Demon Form
+
+**Defect:**
+- Barrage+ is the primary burst card (52+ damage with 5+ orbs and Vulnerable)
+- Echo Form doubling Barrage+ can deal 100+ in a single turn
+- Frost wall for Phase 1 survival
+- Re-apply Vulnerable after Anger (Beam Cell or Sweeping Beam)
heuristics/bosses/the-collector.md
@@ -0,0 +1,33 @@
+# The Collector
+
+**Core rule:** Enter at 70%+ HP or have Pantograph. The Mega Debuff on Turn 4 creates a 3-turn crisis where incoming spikes by 50% and block is reduced by 25%. AOE is the highest priority card type.
+
+## Preparation Checklist
+
+1. Enter at 70%+ HP or Pantograph
+2. AOE damage (Immolate+ is the MVP -- hits Collector + both Torch Heads)
+3. Mass debuff (Shockwave+ reduces combined incoming from 3 enemies by 25% for 3 turns)
+4. Impervious (30 block absorbs an entire post-debuff turn)
+5. Block density (Frail reduces all block by 25% -- need multiple sources)
+6. Disarm (permanently reduces Collector Strength)
+
+## Strategy
+
+- **Turn 1 (spawn turn):** Thunderclap+ for Vulnerable on all 3 enemies. Disarm on Collector.
+- **Turns 2-3:** Maximum damage to Collector while blocking Torch Heads. Shockwave+ for mass Weak.
+- **Turn 4 (Mega Debuff):** No damage incoming -- use for burst offense (free turn).
+- **Turns 5-7 (post-debuff crisis):** Impervious is the best card. Block is reduced by Frail, you take 50% more from Vulnerable.
+- **Turns 8-10 (cleanup):** Re-apply Vulnerable, AOE to clear re-summoned Torch Heads.
+
+## What NOT to Do
+
+- Enter below 70% HP without Pantograph (Mega Debuff creates mathematically unwinnable turns at low HP)
+- Ignore Torch Heads entirely (14 combined damage/turn adds up)
+- Save potions for "later" (post-debuff turns 5-7 are the crisis -- use potions here)
+- Play Shockwave+ on Turn 1 before Torch Heads spawn
+- Enter Unknown rooms before the boss without accounting for Colosseum event (forces combat, drains HP)
+- Enter without Impervious or Shockwave+ (both deaths missing Impervious confirmed)
+
+## Character Matchups
+
+**Silent:** Play defensive powers (After Image, Footwork+) IMMEDIATELY before Turn 4 debuff. Noxious Fumes+ is the primary win condition (3 poison/turn to all, ignores player debuffs). Piercing Wail+ reduces all enemy Str by 8 for 1 turn. Prioritize killing Torch Heads with Shivs (After Image + Shivs = block + damage). Wraith Form: do NOT play early (fight lasts 15-20 turns, Dex penalty compounds). Catalyst+ for tripling poison. WLP+ to retain key cards.
heuristics/bosses/the-guardian.md
@@ -0,0 +1,36 @@
+# The Guardian
+
+**Core rule:** Plan burst damage to trigger Mode Shift on lethal attack turns (32-damage and 5x4=20 attacks), canceling them. Do NOT over-exhaust -- this is a 12-14 turn fight.
+
+## Preparation Checklist
+
+1. One big block source for the 32-damage turn (Impervious, double Metallicize + Weak, or mass block cards)
+2. Burst damage for fast Mode Shift triggering (Bludgeon, Carnage+, Headbutt+ loops)
+3. Passive block for the long fight (Metallicize is the best card; double Metallicize is exceptional)
+4. Deck manipulation (Headbutt+ to guarantee key cards when needed)
+5. Enough cards in deck to last 12+ turns -- do NOT over-exhaust
+
+## Strategy
+
+- **Turn 1 setup:** Play Metallicize, Inflame, or Powers on the first free turn
+- **Attack Mode:** Block on attack turns (especially 32-damage). Damage on free turns. Refresh Vulnerable with Bash+ on free turns.
+- **Defensive Mode:** Block first to absorb Sharp Hide retaliation, then play Attacks. Powers are safe.
+- **Headbutt loops:** Top-deck Carnage+ for burst turns, Bash+ for Vulnerable refresh, Impervious for the 32-damage turn
+- **Know your block ceiling:** If maximum block is less than 32, stop exhausting immediately
+
+## What NOT to Do
+
+- Over-exhaust (a 3-card deck cannot block 32 damage)
+- Play unupgraded True Grit (random exhaust destroys key cards)
+- Ignore Sharp Hide (3 Attack cards in Sharp Hide mode = 9 HP)
+- Play Corruption without Dead Branch or Feel No Pain (burns through all Skills in 5-6 turns, leaving defenseless for second half)
+- Waste Impervious on small attacks (save for 32-damage or 5x4=20)
+- Play Fiend Fire as primary exhaust engine (thins deck catastrophically)
+- Play Sever Soul (exhausts ALL non-Attack cards)
+- Enter with fewer than 2 reliable block cards
+
+## Character Matchups
+
+**Silent:** Mode Shift + Shivs: multiple Shivs accumulate toward Mode Shift quickly. Cloak and Dagger+ (2 Shivs) is critical. Noxious Fumes played Turn 1 deals 78 total poison over 12 turns. Wraith Form: do NOT play early (Dex penalty compounds over 12+ turns). Poison Potions bypass Sharp Hide.
+
+**Defect:** Frost wall sustains through the long fight. Barrage with full orbs (5+) triggers Mode Shift in 1-2 plays. Echo Form early on a free Charging Up turn. Coolheaded+ cycling finds cards when needed.
heuristics/bosses/time-eater.md
@@ -0,0 +1,37 @@
+# Time Eater
+
+**Core rule:** Deliberate card counting is mandatory. Before playing any card, know the exact count (e.g., "9/12"). Plan your turn around the count. Play high-impact cards, minimize card count per turn.
+
+**HP Threshold:** Enter at 50%+ HP. The fight is long (10-15 turns) with escalating damage from Time Warp Str stacks.
+
+## Strategy
+
+- **Track the 12-card count explicitly every turn.** State count before and after each play.
+- **Play high-impact cards, minimize count per turn.** Body Slam+ (0E, damage = block) is ideal. Immolate+ (one card, 28+ AOE) is excellent.
+- **Barricade is the best card in this fight.** Block persists through Time Warp turn resets.
+- **Entrench+ doubles block for Body Slam+ scaling.** 2 cards for massive damage.
+- **Intentional phasing:** Choose WHEN Time Warp triggers. Plan to trigger it when your last 1-2 plays push past 12, so forced turn-end costs nothing.
+- **Corruption + Feel No Pain:** Excellent but be card-count conscious. Every free Skill counts toward the 12-card limit.
+
+## Confirmed Winning Approaches
+
+**Ironclad:** Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam engine. Accumulate 69 block, Entrench to 138, Body Slam+ for 146 damage.
+
+**Silent:** Accuracy x2 (+8 Shiv damage) + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer. Shivs deal 36 each doubled. Cultist Potion Turn 1 for Ritual scaling. Malaise X=5 for late-game Str/Weak reduction. Finisher scales with attacks played.
+
+**Defect:** Echo Form+ + Seek+ + Coolheaded+ + Barrage+. Barrage doubled by Echo Form for lethal. Track that Echo Form doubles count toward Time Warp (1 card = 2 counted plays). Frost wall persists through Time Warp.
+
+## What NOT to Do
+
+- Play many small cards per turn without purpose (accelerates Time Warp with low damage output)
+- Exhaust Impervious early (save for high-damage turns after Str stacks)
+- Enter below 40% HP
+- Waste Malaise on Artifact stripping (use Neutralize for that)
+- Lose track of the 12-card count
+
+## Potion Timing
+
+- Cultist Potion Turn 1 (maximum Str scaling over the long fight)
+- Fear Potion on Phantasmal Killer burst turns
+- Snecko Oil as emergency draw
+- Save Lizard Tail / Fairy as death insurance
heuristics/cards/accuracy.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Accuracy
+
+Permanent Power adding flat damage to every Shiv. The most important card in Shiv-based Silent decks.
+
+**When to play:** Turn 1 setup in every fight. The second copy is also excellent -- going from +4 to +8 per Shiv doubles offensive value of every Shiv-generating card.
+
+**Damage math:** With 2x Accuracy (+8) and Str 11: Shiv = 4 + 8 + 11 = 23 per 0-cost Shiv. Blade Dance (1E) = 3 Shivs = 69 damage. With Phantasmal Killer: 46 per Shiv, 138 from 3 Shivs.
+
+**Synergies:** Blade Dance (3 Shivs for 1E), Cloak and Dagger+ (2 Shivs + 6 block), Infinite Blades (free Shiv/turn), Phantasmal Killer (doubles Shiv damage), Cultist Potion (Ritual Str compounds on every Shiv), Finisher (Shivs build attack count), After Image (each Shiv = +1 block).
+
+**Time Eater interaction:** Shivs count toward 12-card counter, but with Accuracy stacking each is worth 20+ damage, making the counter manageable.
+
+**Draft priority:** High in any Shiv deck. Take early.
heuristics/cards/after-image.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# After Image
+
+Flat block per card played. Not affected by Dexterity or Frail.
+
+**When to play:** Turn 1 or Turn 2 of every fight. Every turn it sits unplayed is block lost. Before the Collector's Turn 4 debuff -- must be active before triple debuff lands. Priority over other Powers except Noxious Fumes+.
+
+**Key mechanic:** Uniquely valuable when Frail is active (only block source at full value) and when Dexterity is negative from Wraith Form (only block source that still works at -5 Dex or worse).
+
+**Combat value:** 5 cards/turn = 5 free block/turn. 8 cards/turn = 8. Over 10 turns: 50-80 total block for 1E investment. Shivs from Cloak and Dagger+ trigger After Image (block + damage simultaneously).
+
+**Bottled Tornado synergy:** Making After Image innate guarantees Turn 1 activation every combat. Top pick for Bottled Tornado in Silent decks.
+
+**Setup priority in boss fights:** 1. Noxious Fumes+ (win condition), 2. After Image (survival), 3. Footwork+ (block multiplier).
heuristics/cards/aggregate.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Aggregate
+
+Energy burst card. Generates energy based on cards remaining in draw pile.
+
+**When to play:** Play at the START of your turn when the draw pile is largest. Every 4 cards drawn or exhausted before playing reduces gain by 1. Do NOT play Coolheaded or Compile Driver before Aggregate.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Significant. Divisor from 4 to 3. With 12 cards: 3E vs 4E.
+
+**Snecko Eye synergy:** Extra energy fuels the extra cards drawn. When Snecko randomizes costs high, Aggregate provides energy to play them anyway.
+
+**Timing:** Play first each turn (before other draw effects reduce the draw pile).
heuristics/cards/anger.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Anger
+
+Free damage card that copies itself into discard when played. 0-cost means it never competes with other plays.
+
+**When to play:** Every turn you draw it. There is no reason to hold Anger since it costs 0 energy. In multi-enemy fights, target the enemy closest to death for the free kill.
+
+**Synergies:** Shuriken (each Anger counts toward the 3-Attack threshold), Rage (3 block per Attack for free), Demon Form (free attacks benefit greatly from Strength scaling -- at +10 Str, each Anger deals 16 for 0E), Vulnerable (50% bonus on a free card).
+
+**Matchups:** Great vs Hexaghost (long fight, copies accumulate, Strength scaling applies). Great vs Gremlin Nob (Attack card, avoids Enrage trigger).
+
+**Caution:** Anger adds copies to the discard pile, inflating deck size. In decks relying on drawing specific cards, extra copies can dilute draws. Rarely a problem since Anger replaces itself with damage.
heuristics/cards/apotheosis.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Apotheosis
+
+Mass upgrade for the entire deck. The single most impactful card when upgrades are scarce.
+
+**When to play:** Play on a free turn (enemy buffing/debuffing) early in a fight. The earlier it is played, the more turns benefit. In boss fights, Turn 1-2 is ideal. Under Corruption, costs 0E and exhausts -- best possible outcome.
+
+**Decision points:** With Fusion Hammer (cannot Smith at rest sites), Apotheosis is the ONLY way to upgrade cards. It upgrades everything: Barricade 3E->2E, Corruption 3E->2E, Impervious 30->40, Feel No Pain 3->4, Immolate 21->28, all simultaneously.
+
+**Synergies:** Fusion Hammer (the #1 synergy), Corruption (becomes 0E), Feel No Pain (exhaust under Corruption triggers block).
+
+**Source:** Colorless card from events (Sensory Stone "Recall" option). Cannot be obtained from normal card rewards.
heuristics/cards/armaments.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Armaments
+
+Block + card upgrade in one play. Upgraded version is transformative.
+
+**When to play:** Armaments+ is one of the best cards in the game -- every turn it upgrades your entire hand permanently for the combat. Play every turn in long fights. Prioritize upgrading Armaments itself first.
+
+**Block value reminder:** Armaments provides 5 block in addition to its upgrade effect. ALWAYS include this 5 block in calculations. This has been forgotten in survival-critical calculations.
+
+**When NOT to play:** In DPS-race fights where every turn of damage matters (Book of Stabbing, Gremlin Leader). Spending 1E on Armaments+ for 5 block + upgrades can cost you the fight if the enemy's damage scaling outpaces your block growth.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** One of the highest-priority upgrades in the game. Single card -> ALL cards in hand.
+
+**Matchups:** Against Gremlin Nob, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage).
heuristics/cards/bane.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Bane
+
+Deals damage TWICE if the target is Poisoned. Effectively 14/20 damage for 1E against any Poisoned enemy.
+
+**When to play:** Against any Poisoned enemy. In Silent decks with poison sources (Noxious Fumes, Deadly Poison, Envenom, Bouncing Flask), Bane is almost always dealing double damage.
+
+**Synergies:** Terror (Vulnerable 99 active: 7*2*1.5 = 21 unupgraded, 10*2*1.5 = 30 upgraded for 1E), all poison sources, Dead Branch (can generate Bane randomly -- excellent against Poisoned Vulnerable boss).
+
+**Limitation:** Against enemies without Poison, only 7/10 damage for 1E -- below average. Do not take unless the deck has reliable poison application.
+
+**Double-hit note:** Counts as two separate attacks for Kunai and Shuriken per-attack counters.
heuristics/cards/barrage.md
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+# Barrage
+
+Defect's primary scaling Attack. Damage scales directly with channeled orbs.
+
+**When to play:** After filling orb slots. With 1 orb: 4 damage for 2E (terrible). With 5 orbs: 20/30 damage (excellent). Always channel orbs before playing Barrage.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 4->6 per orb. At 5 orbs: 20->30 damage (50% increase).
+
+**The Boot interaction:** Each per-orb hit is separate. The Boot bumps unupgraded hits from 4 to 5. With 5 orbs and The Boot: 25 instead of 20.
+
+**Echo Form synergy:** Echo Form plays Barrage twice. With 3 orbs: 36 total damage for one card play.
+
+**Snecko Eye synergy:** Can randomize to 0E -- free 20+ damage. Even at 1E, well above rate.
+
+**Necronomicon synergy:** At 2+ energy, Necronomicon doubles it. With 5 Frost orbs: 40 damage from one card play.
+
+**Vulnerability scaling:** With Vulnerable on target and 5 orbs, Barrage+ deals 45 damage. Primary burst source in first Defect victory.
+
+**Prediction error warning:** Actual values are 4/6 per orb (not 5/7). The Boot bumping may cause confusion.
+
+**Early priority:** Moderate. Weak early with empty slots. Strong once Capacitor/Coolheaded fill the board.
heuristics/cards/barricade.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Barricade
+
+Block persists between turns instead of resetting to 0. Transforms block cards from temporary defense into permanent HP shields.
+
+**When to play:** At 3E (2E upgraded), playing Barricade costs an entire turn. Only acceptable if: (1) enemy is buffing/defending, (2) HP is high enough to absorb one unblocked attack, (3) you have 4+ energy, or (4) at 5E budget, Barricade+ leaves 3E for block. NEVER play when incoming will kill or leave critical HP.
+
+**Synergies:** Body Slam (damage = block, scales every turn), Shrug It Off, True Grit+, Iron Wave (all become permanent), Metallicize (passive block stacks permanently -- 2x Metallicize = 21 block by Turn 3, 35 by Turn 5), Entrench+ (doubles accumulated block).
+
+**Anti-synergies:** Low energy decks (3E cost prohibitive), short fights, fights at low HP, Brutality.
+
+**Victory engine:** Barricade + Corruption + Feel No Pain + Entrench + Body Slam defeated Time Eater (480 HP). Block snowball makes hallway fights trivial.
+
+**Matchups:** Guardian -- deploy on free turn (DEFEND/STRONG_DEBUFF intent), then build permanent block wall. Multi-enemy fights with constant damage -- 3E setup often too expensive.
heuristics/cards/bash.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Bash
+
+Primary Vulnerable applicator. Opens every fight.
+
+**When to play:** Always valuable. Vulnerable for 2-3 turns means all subsequent attacks deal 50% more damage. On free turns (enemy buffing/debuffing), Bash is ideal. On high-damage incoming turns, 2E on Bash means less block. Safe against Gremlin Nob (Attack card).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Top priority in any deck. 3 turns Vulnerable vs 2 is enormous — the extra turn means one more full hand of boosted attacks.
+
+**Synergies:** Headbutt (put Bash+ back on top to refresh Vulnerable), Dropkick (free cycling when target is Vulnerable), all Attack cards (50% more damage).
+
+**Matchups:** Lagavulin — refresh Vulnerable after it wears off via Headbutt. Guardian — apply on free turns (DEFEND/STRONG_DEBUFF intent). Gremlin Nob — safe to play (Attack card).
heuristics/cards/battle-hymn.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Battle Hymn
+
+Sustained damage engine. Generates a free Smite (12/16 damage, Retain, Exhaust) every turn for the rest of combat.
+
+**When to play:** As early as possible on a safe turn. Like all Powers, every unplayed turn is lost value. The free turn against Hexaghost (Turn 1 Activate) is ideal.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. Upgraded Smites deal 16 instead of 12 -- over a 10-turn boss fight, that is 160 vs 120 total free damage.
+
+**Smite Retain synergy:** Generated Smites can be held for Wrath burst turns. In Wrath: 24/32 doubled damage from a free card.
+
+**Hexaghost relevance:** Free 12-damage card each turn helps end the fight before Burns overwhelm. The Watcher's equivalent of Demon Form for long fights.
+
+**Draft priority:** High. One of the Watcher's best rare cards for boss fights. Take when offered.
heuristics/cards/battle-trance.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Battle Trance
+
+Free card draw. Finds key cards faster.
+
+**When to play:** Always. Play at start of turn before making other decisions, since the drawn cards may change the optimal plan. The only exception is if you already have a perfect hand.
+
+**Critical sequencing:** Do NOT batch `end` in the same turn() call as Battle Trance. The drawn cards need to be evaluated and played before ending the turn. Play Battle Trance first, read the new game state, THEN plan the rest of the turn.
+
+**Decision points:** Against Gremlin Nob, Battle Trance is a Skill and triggers Enrage -- still worth playing on free turns but avoid during attack turns.
+
+**Downside:** You cannot draw additional cards the turn you play Battle Trance (locks out further draw effects like Pommel Strike draw, Shrug It Off draw).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. Draw 4 instead of 3.
+
+**Synergies:** Demon Form (finding it turns 1-2 instead of turn 5 is game-changing), any combo deck (more draw = more likely to find combo pieces).
heuristics/cards/beam-cell.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Beam Cell
+
+A 0-cost Vulnerable applicator. The damage is negligible -- the Vulnerable application is the entire value.
+
+**When to play:** Play before Barrage or other damage cards to maximize Vulnerable amplification. In short fights, even 1 turn of Vulnerable on Barrage+ with 5 orbs adds 17 bonus damage.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** The extra Vulnerable turn (1->2) is significant, keeping enemies Vulnerable longer.
heuristics/cards/berserk.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Berserk
+
+DANGEROUS. Self-Vulnerable (take 50% more damage) for 2 turns. Permanent +1 energy.
+
+**When to play:** ONLY when enemies are not attacking for the next 2 turns AND you can survive the Vulnerable penalty afterward. In practice, almost never safe.
+
+**Decision points:** Can you survive 2 turns of taking 50% more damage from ALL enemies? Against multiple enemies, the combined boosted damage is often lethal. Against bosses at low HP, the self-Vulnerable is suicidal. The energy gain does not matter if you die.
+
+**Matchups:** Hexaghost -- NEVER play. Burns + self-damage + Vulnerable is a death spiral. Multi-enemy fights -- NEVER play. Combined 50% boosted damage from all enemies is overwhelming.
heuristics/cards/bite.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Bite
+
+Colorless card obtained from the Vampires event. Replaces Strikes (all Strikes removed, 5 Bites added).
+
+**When to play:** Always play when drawn. Better than Strike in every way (similar damage, plus healing). 2 HP per play adds up: in a 5-turn fight playing 1-2 Bites per turn, that is 10-20 HP healed.
+
+**Decision points:** The card itself is fine -- the problem is the cost of obtaining it. The Vampires event removes ~30% of your Max HP. At a reduced Max HP pool, the 2 HP healing per Bite cannot compensate for Act 2 elite burst damage.
+
+**Synergies:** Strength scaling (Inflame, Demon Form) boosts both damage and efficiency. Reaper still outclasses Bite for healing, but Bites provide consistent small heals every turn.
+
+**Anti-synergy:** The Max HP loss from obtaining Bites makes Reaper less effective (less HP to heal back to).
+
+**Note:** Bite is NOT an Ironclad card -- it is colorless. Molten Egg will NOT auto-upgrade Bites.
heuristics/cards/blizzard.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Blizzard
+
+AOE attack that scales with total Frost orbs channeled this combat.
+
+**When to play:** Play after channeling several Frost orbs for maximum damage. Weak in early turns (2-6 damage) but devastating in turns 5+. Best in decks that naturally channel many Frost orbs for defense.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 2x->3x multiplier is 50% more damage from the same Frost count.
+
+**Decision points:** Excellent in multi-enemy fights where Frost defense and AOE damage are both needed. The card is a natural pairing with a Frost-heavy defensive strategy -- the defense generates offense automatically.
heuristics/cards/blood-for-blood.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Blood for Blood
+
+Starts at 4 Energy but becomes cheaper as you take damage. After losing 4+ HP instances, costs 0 Energy -- free 18-22 damage.
+
+**When to play:** At 0 cost, always play. At 1-2 cost, evaluate energy vs damage. At 3-4 cost (start of fight before taking damage), hold it and play other cards first. The cost reduction is permanent for the combat.
+
+**Decision points:** In decks that prevent all damage (Barricade + heavy block), the cost may never reduce. At 3-4E, it is dead weight and a prime candidate for True Grit+ exhaust.
+
+**Synergies:** Double Tap (double a 0-cost Attack = 36+ free damage), Inflame/Strength, Brutality's 1 HP/turn self-damage counts as a damage instance (reaches 0E faster).
+
+**Weaknesses:** Terrible on turn 1 of fights where you block all damage. Not reliable as the only damage source.
heuristics/cards/bloodletting.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Bloodletting
+
+Energy generation at an HP cost. Enables big turns.
+
+**When to play:** When the extra energy enables something critical this turn. Do NOT play if you have nothing impactful to spend the energy on -- 3 HP for wasted energy is a net loss. At low HP (<15), the 3 HP cost becomes dangerous.
+
+**Critical sequencing:** Do NOT batch Bloodletting with `end` in the same action sequence. The generated energy is wasted if the turn ends immediately. Play Bloodletting, evaluate hand, spend the energy, THEN end the turn.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Significant. 2->3 energy makes Bloodletting+ one of the best energy cards in the game.
+
+**Synergies:** Rupture (lose HP triggers +1 Str permanently -- best Rupture enabler since it costs 0E), Corruption (extra energy helps survive the 3E setup turn), any 2-3E card needing energy flexibility.
heuristics/cards/bludgeon.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Bludgeon
+
+The hardest-hitting single Attack in Ironclad's card pool.
+
+**When to play:** Boss fights and elite fights where a single massive hit matters. Play on free turns (enemy defending, buffing, or debuffing) where you would not benefit from blocking.
+
+**Decision points:** At 3E, Bludgeon consumes your entire turn. Against The Guardian, save for DEFEND or STRONG_DEBUFF intent turns. Against The Collector, play on the STRONG_DEBUFF turn (Turn 4).
+
+**Synergies:** Inflame (Strength amplified by Vulnerable's 1.5x), Bash+ (3 turns Vulnerable for repeated value), Double Tap (2x Bludgeon = 96 damage with Vulnerable), Liquid Memories (retrieve for 0 cost).
+
+**Weaknesses:** 3E cost means no block on the turn it is played. Bad against multi-enemy fights where damage needs splitting.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. 32->42 damage is significant but the base card is already strong.
heuristics/cards/blur.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Blur
+
+Block that carries over to the next turn. Critical companion to Wraith Form.
+
+**When to play:** On free turns (enemy buffing/debuffing) where block would normally be wasted. On the last Intangible turn to maximize carried block for the first dangerous turn. When After Image generates excess block.
+
+**Wraith Form synergy:** During Intangible turns, generate block freely (enemy damage reduced to 1). Blur carries this to post-Intangible turns when Dex penalty makes new block impossible. Without Blur, the transition to post-Intangible is a cliff.
+
+**Draft priority:** Mid-priority for Silent. Becomes high-priority when deck includes Wraith Form. Without Wraith Form, decent but not essential (5/8 block for 1E is below average, but retention adds conditional value).
heuristics/cards/body-slam.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Body Slam
+
+Converts block into damage. Scales with every block source in the deck.
+
+**When to play:** With Barricade. Body Slam+ costs 0E -- a completely free attack every cycle. With Barricade accumulating 100+ block, deals 100+ damage for 0E. Without Barricade, block resets each turn, limiting damage to 10-20.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** CRITICAL. Body Slam+ costs 0E (from 1E). One of the most impactful upgrades because it costs nothing and leaves full energy for block generation.
+
+**Synergies:** Barricade (block persists = damage grows every turn), Entrench+ (doubling block doubles Body Slam damage), Impervious (30-40 block = 30-40 free damage), Corruption + Feel No Pain (exhausted Skills add block that Body Slam converts), Metallicize (passive block accumulation converted to damage).
+
+**Anti-synergies:** Decks without Barricade (block resets = low damage), Frail-heavy fights.
+
+**Act 1 tier:** LOW. Do not take in Act 1 unless Barricade is already in the deck.
heuristics/cards/brutality.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Brutality
+
+DANGEROUS in long fights. Extra draw at the cost of permanent HP drain.
+
+**When to play:** Short fights (3-4 turns) where the extra draw finds lethal faster. With healing (Reaper, Feed) to offset self-damage. With Rupture, each Brutality provides +1 Strength/turn permanently.
+
+**Decision points:** How long will this fight last? In a 9-turn fight, Brutality costs 9 HP minimum. NEVER play against bosses with status card damage (Hexaghost Burns) or fights expected to last 7+ turns. With Rupture in play, Brutality becomes a scaling engine -- but NEVER play 2 copies at low HP.
+
+**The Brutality Trap:** Feels free at 0 cost and the extra draw seems universally good. But in any fight that might go 7+ turns, the cumulative self-damage becomes lethal. Before playing, ask: "Am I confident this fight ends in 4-5 turns?" If no -- do NOT play.
+
+**Synergies:** Rupture (+1 Str/turn permanently per Brutality). With 2 Brutalities + Rupture = +2 Str/turn but lose 2 HP/turn. Only play multiple copies at 40+ HP.
+
+**Matchups:** Hexaghost -- NEVER play. Book of Stabbing -- NEVER play. Spheric Guardian -- NEVER play (fight extends to 8-13 turns, self-damage seals death).
heuristics/cards/burning-pact.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Burning Pact
+
+Controlled exhaust + card draw. Net +1 card in hand.
+
+**When to play:** Removing status cards (Slimed, Dazed, Wound). Cycling to find key cards. Thinning in-combat deck safely. Critical vs Book of Stabbing -- exhaust Wounds to keep draws functional. Essential in Slime Boss fight -- exhaust Slimed cards to prevent hand clog. With Feel No Pain, each Slimed exhausted generates 3 block AND draws 2 cards.
+
+**Decision points:** What is expendable in hand? Exhaust status cards or extra Strikes. Against Gremlin Nob and Chosen, do NOT play (Skill card triggers Enrage/Hex).
heuristics/cards/capacitor.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Capacitor
+
+More orb slots = more passive effects and higher Barrage damage.
+
+**When to play:** Play early in long fights to maximize turns with extra slots. In short hallway fights, the Power setup matters less.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. +2->+3 orb slots. The extra slot is worth 2+Focus block/turn (Frost) or 7 damage per Barrage+ play.
+
+**Runic Capacitor interaction:** With the relic (+3 slots at start) plus Capacitor+, Defect reaches 9 orb slots. Enables massive Barrage damage (63 per play with Barrage+) and enormous Frost wall.
+
+**Barrage scaling:** Each filled slot adds 4/7 damage to Barrage/Barrage+. Capacitor+ adding 3 slots = +21 Barrage+ damage when all filled.
heuristics/cards/carnage.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Carnage
+
+Massive single-target burst. Must be played or it exhausts permanently (Ethereal).
+
+**When to play:** Boss fights for big burst turns. Against Guardian Mode Shift (fast phase transitions). Any fight where 2E for 28+ damage is game-changing.
+
+**Decision points:** Can you afford 2E on damage this turn, or do you need block? If drawn on a must-block turn, you either spend 2E on damage or lose Carnage permanently. This tension is manageable with enough block cards.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Top priority. 20->28 damage. With Vulnerable: 30->42. One of the highest single-card damage outputs.
+
+**Synergies:** Headbutt (guarantee Carnage+ on top for next turn -- used repeatedly for consistent 28-42 damage turns), Vulnerable (42 damage).
+
+**Matchups:** Guardian -- 42 Vuln damage triggers Mode Shift fast. Byrds -- 28 damage halved to ~14 nearly one-shots a Byrd.
heuristics/cards/carve-reality.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Carve Reality
+
+Generates a Smite for sustained damage. Effectively 18 total damage (6 + 12 Smite) for 2E total across two plays.
+
+**When to play:** Play on any turn where 1E can be spent on damage. The generated Smite Retains, so it can be held for a burst turn.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium. 6->10 damage improves immediate output but does not change the Smite generation.
+
+**Wrath synergy:** Both Carve Reality and the generated Smite benefit from Wrath doubling. Playing Carve in Wrath = 12 damage + Smite for later.
+
+**Smite Retain:** Generate Smite on a defensive turn, hold it for the next offensive turn in Wrath (24 doubled damage).
+
+**Draft priority:** Medium. Good sustained damage for boss fights but does not directly interact with the stance engine.
heuristics/cards/chill.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Chill
+
+In multi-enemy fights (3 Shapes, Darklings, Gremlin Gang), channels 3 Frost orbs for 0E -- enormous defensive burst.
+
+**When to play:** Play immediately on Turn 1 of multi-enemy fights for maximum Frost generation. In single-enemy fights, play whenever a Frost orb is needed. Exhausts after use.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Gains Innate. Useful if already in the deck -- guarantees Turn 1 availability.
heuristics/cards/claw.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Claw
+
+Defect's 0-cost scaling Attack. Deals increasing damage each time any Claw is played this combat.
+
+**When to play:** Play freely whenever 0E is available. The damage accumulates without cost. First Claw deals 3/5, second 5/7, third 7/9, etc. (+2 per Claw play across all copies).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. 3->5 base is a meaningful bump on each play, especially early when scaling is low.
+
+**Akabeko synergy:** Claw at 0E is an ideal first attack for Akabeko's +8 Vigor. Turn 1: 3 + 8 = 11 damage for free.
+
+**Gremlin Nob:** One of the best Turn 1 plays against Nob -- 0E Attack, no Enrage trigger.
+
+**Scaling reality check:** Slow accumulation (+2 per play across all Claws). In a 10-turn fight with 1 Claw played 3-4 times: 3+5+7+9 = 24 total. Decent complementary damage, not a primary engine.
+
+**Decision points:** Never hold Claw. At 0E with no downside, play it every time it appears in hand regardless of other priorities.
heuristics/cards/cleave.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Cleave
+
+Cheap AOE damage to all enemies. Does NOT apply Vulnerable.
+
+**When to play:** Multi-enemy fights for raw AOE damage. Good on free turns (turn 1 of Cultist fights) to soften all targets.
+
+**Decision points:** Cleave vs Thunderclap -- Thunderclap applies Vulnerable but deals less damage. Usually Thunderclap is better for the Vulnerable.
heuristics/cards/cloak-and-dagger.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Cloak and Dagger
+
+Dual-purpose: block + damage via Shiv generation. Shivs benefit from Accuracy, Strength, and other modifiers.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** UPGRADE WHEN GUARDIAN IS THE BOSS. The upgrade from 1 Shiv to 2 Shivs adds 9+ damage per play (with Accuracy 4 + Vajra 1). A confirmed death occurred because unupgraded CaD produced 1 Shiv instead of 2, leaving damage 8 short of Mode Shift threshold.
+
+**Interactions:** Accuracy (each Shiv gets full bonus), After Image (each Shiv = +1 block, making upgraded CaD effectively 8+ block), Dead Branch (Shivs exhaust, triggering random card generation), Wraith Form Dex penalty (at negative Dex, block drops to 0 but Shiv damage unaffected).
+
+**Sharp Hide warning:** Shivs are Attack cards and trigger Guardian Sharp Hide (3 damage per Shiv). During Defensive Mode, each Shiv costs 3 HP.
+
+**Draft priority:** High in Shiv decks. Both offense and defense in a single 1E play.
heuristics/cards/clothesline.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Clothesline
+
+Expensive but applies Weak. Reduces enemy attack damage by 25%.
+
+**When to play:** Against single big attackers where Weak saves more HP than alternative plays. Against Hexaghost when no other Weak source is available.
+
+**Decision points:** Is 2E for 12 damage + Weak worth more than two 1E cards? Against multiple enemies, you can only Weak one. Consider energy economy.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium. 12->14 damage, 2->3 Weak. The extra Weak turn is significant for boss fights.
+
+**Matchups:** Hexaghost -- Weak reduces Inferno from 42 to 28. Bronze Automaton -- BEST Weak source because it does NOT exhaust. Can apply Weak on BOTH Hyper Beam turns. Reduces Hyper Beam from 57 to 42. Always save enough energy to play on Hyper Beam turns. Slime Boss -- supplementary defensive tool post-split.
heuristics/cards/cold-snap.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Cold Snap
+
+Dual-purpose Attack: damage + Frost orb. Safe against Gremlin Nob.
+
+**When to play:** Play on the highest-priority damage target. The Frost orb channels regardless of whether the damage kills. In Frost-heavy builds, fills orb slots for passive block.
+
+**Early Defect priority:** High. One of the best early pickups because it is an Attack (Nob protection) that also builds Frost defense. Take when offered in Act 1.
+
+**Necronomicon interaction:** When Cold Snap costs 2+ energy (via Snecko randomization) and Necronomicon triggers, it plays twice -- double damage AND two Frost orbs.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. 6->9 damage. Frost channel is the primary value.
heuristics/cards/compile-driver.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Compile Driver
+
+Multi-purpose Attack providing both damage and card draw. Draw scales with orb count.
+
+**When to play:** Best when orbs are channeled. With 3 orbs, draws 3 cards for 1E while dealing 7 damage -- excellent value. With 0 orbs, just a 7-damage Strike equivalent. Channel orbs before playing to maximize draw.
+
+**Early Defect priority:** High. Addresses both the Attack deficit and deck cycling needs. Take when offered in Act 1. Safe against Gremlin Nob as an Attack card.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** 7->10 damage. Useful but the draw is the primary value.
heuristics/cards/coolheaded.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Coolheaded
+
+Defensive cycling card. Frost orb + draw.
+
+**When to play:** Play early in the turn to cycle into better cards. The Frost orb provides ongoing block value for the rest of combat.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Extremely high. Draw 1->3. The +2 draw dramatically improves hand quality. Coolheaded+ becomes an engine piece rather than utility.
+
+**Echo Form synergy:** Echo Form plays Coolheaded+ twice: 2 Frost orbs and 6 draws. Enormous hand size boost -- primary defensive engine of first Defect victory.
+
+**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers Enrage. Against Nob, do NOT play unless you need the draw to find a lethal Attack.
heuristics/cards/corruption.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Corruption
+
+All Skills become free but one-time-use. Extremely powerful but fundamentally changes deck economy.
+
+**Setup cost trap:** At 3E unupgraded, Turn 1 leaves ZERO energy for blocking. Against multi-enemy fights, unblocked damage can be 20+ and fatal. Safe to play when: (1) enemy is buffing/defending, (2) you have 4+ energy, (3) HP absorbs the hit, (4) upgraded to 2E.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** HIGH. 3E->2E makes the difference between blocking and not on the setup turn.
+
+**Decision points:** After Corruption, every Skill exhausts -- plan for a finite number of block plays. Before playing, count remaining Skills and estimate remaining fight turns. If (Skills) < (turns needing block), do NOT play.
+
+**Synergies:** Dead Branch (every exhausted Skill generates a random card -- strongest combo in the game), Feel No Pain (3 block per exhaust makes Corruption safe), Dark Embrace (draw per exhaust), Second Wind (mass exhaust for block), Barricade (accumulated block persists).
+
+**Guardian warning:** Guardian fight lasts 12-14+ turns. Corruption exhausts ALL block Skills. Without Dead Branch or Feel No Pain, do NOT play against Guardian.
+
+**Victory engine:** Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam defeated Time Eater. Every Skill under Corruption generates FNP block AND exhausts free. Second Wind+ under Corruption can exhaust Normality curse permanently.
heuristics/cards/crescendo.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Crescendo
+
+Cheap one-time Wrath entry. Exhausts after use -- not a permanent engine card.
+
+**When to play:** On burst turns where you have multiple attacks ready and need Wrath for doubled damage. Also on free turns (enemy not attacking) where entering Wrath is safe. Do NOT play if you have no way to exit Wrath and the enemy attacks next turn.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium. 0E is better than 1E, but since it exhausts, the upgrade only matters once per combat.
+
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** Play Flurry of Blows BEFORE Crescendo. Crescendo enters Wrath, triggering stance-change effects. Flurry must be in the discard pile to return to hand.
+
+**Exhausts:** Only usable once per combat. Plan Wrath entries around having either Crescendo OR Eruption available -- do not rely solely on Crescendo for stance cycling.
+
+**Calm energy combo:** Combined with Calm exit energy (+2E), Crescendo enables explosive burst turns at minimal net energy cost.
heuristics/cards/cut-through-fate.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Cut Through Fate
+
+Scry + draw + damage in one card. Effectively replaces itself in hand while filtering bad draws.
+
+**When to play:** Play early in the turn when you need to find specific cards. The Scry + draw can reveal and deliver key cards for the rest of the turn.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium. 7->9 damage and Scry 2->3. More Scry is meaningful for filtering Burns/Curses.
+
