+**Hexaghost synergy:** Scrying away Burns is critical for maintaining hand quality against Hexaghost.
+
+**Wrath value:** 7/9 damage for 1E becomes 14/18 doubled in Wrath -- reasonable burst.
+
+**Decision points:** Since it replaces itself via draw, playing Cut Through Fate costs only 1E in hand size. Use it for deck filtering even when the damage is not needed.
heuristics/cards/dark-embrace.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Dark Embrace
+
+Draw engine for exhaust-heavy decks.
+
+**When to play:** With multiple exhaust sources. Every exhaust replaces itself in hand. Play on free turns. Powers do NOT trigger Gremlin Nob Enrage. WARNING: encourages over-exhausting, which can thin the deck dangerously.
+
+**Synergies:** True Grit+ (exhaust + draw every turn), Charon's Ashes (exhaust = draw + 3 AOE damage), Fiend Fire (exhaust entire hand = draw many cards enabling a second wave of plays), status cards (exhausting Slimed/Dazed/Wounds draws a real card), Sentinel (exhaust = draw + 2-3E refund).
+
+**Matchups:** Strong vs Book of Stabbing -- Wounds are exhaust fodder that convert into real draws. Dark Embrace + Burning Pact/True Grit+ turns every Wound into card advantage.
heuristics/cards/dark-shackles.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Dark Shackles
+
+Emergency defensive tool. Reduces enemy Strength for 1 turn only, then it returns. Costs 0 energy.
+
+**When to play:** Time it for the enemy's highest-damage turn. Using it on a low-damage turn wastes the exhaust. Against bosses with phase transitions (The Champ Execute), save for the first big hit after transition.
+
+**Decision points:** Because the Strength reduction is temporary (1 turn only), do not waste it on routine damage turns. Save for lethal-damage turns.
+
+**Matchups:** The Champ -- strong post-Execute when Champ has +8 Strength. Boss fights generally -- 0E emergency button for any turn where incoming would be lethal.
+
+**Synergies:** Works in any deck since it costs 0E. Pairs with other Strength-reduction effects (Disarm) for layered defense.
heuristics/cards/defend.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Defend
+
+Basic block. Frail reduces this to 3, which is terrible.
+
+**When to play:** When you need block and have nothing better. Defend+ at 8 block is actually decent.
+
+**Decision points:** Against Chosen with Hex active, every Defend adds a Dazed to your draw pile. Against Gremlin Nob, Defend triggers Enrage (+2 Str). Consider whether block is worth the cost.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Low individual priority but Ancient Writing mass upgrade is excellent. 5->8 block is a 60% increase.
heuristics/cards/demon-form.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Demon Form
+
+Long-fight scaling engine. Provides compounding damage over many turns.
+
+**When to play:** Turn 1 of boss fights. Turn 1 of any fight where the enemy buffs (free turn). NEVER play when at low HP and facing immediate lethal damage next turn -- spending 3E on future scaling when you need block NOW is how decks die.
+
+**Decision points:** 3E cost means the setup turn produces ZERO block and ZERO damage. Needs 2-3 turns after playing to meaningfully impact outcome. Useless when entering a fight at low HP vs fast-scaling enemies.
+
+**Reaper synergy warning:** Demon Form as the ONLY Strength source makes Reaper healing too slow in early turns. If Reaper is in the deck, pair Demon Form with a front-loaded Strength source (Inflame, Spot Weakness) so Reaper provides meaningful healing from the first play.
+
+**Synergies:** Heavy Blade+ (5x Strength bonus -- at +12 Str, Heavy Blade+ deals 74 damage), Limit Break (exponential Strength growth), Shuriken + Anger (combined Str from both sources), Evolve (maintains hand quality while Demon Form shortens fights).
+
+**Matchups:** The Champ -- needs to be played Turn 1 to accumulate enough before Execute. Dominates 10-15 turn boss fights.
+
+**Deck building note:** If Demon Form is primary scaling, the deck still needs fast burst for emergency hallway fights at low HP.
heuristics/cards/deus-ex-machina.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Deus Ex Machina
+
+Unplayable card that generates 2/3 Miracles on draw and exhausts itself. Free energy generation + deck thinning.
+
+**When to play:** Cannot be played. Triggers automatically when drawn. No decision needed on the trigger itself.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 3 Miracles instead of 2 is significant extra energy over a fight.
+
+**Miracle management:** The generated Miracles follow all Miracle rules -- hold them for turns where the energy matters. Do not waste them. See miracle.md.
+
+**Deck thinning:** Exhausts on draw, permanently removing one card from the combat deck. Improves future draw quality.
+
+**Draft priority:** Medium-high. Free energy generation and deck thinning works well in any Watcher deck. No setup required.
heuristics/cards/disarm.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Disarm
+
+Permanent Strength reduction. Extremely powerful against scaling enemies.
+
+**When to play:** Save for the enemy with the most dangerous Strength scaling. Against multi-hit enemies, the value compounds (5-hit attack loses 10 total damage).
+
+**Decision points:** Against Gremlin Nob, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage). Against Chosen, playing Disarm adds a Dazed via Hex -- weigh permanent -2 Str against deck clog. If fight lasts 4+ more turns, the Str reduction is usually worth one Dazed.
+
+**Matchups:** Gremlin Leader -- reduces Rally effectiveness. Hexaghost -- permanently reduces all attacks including each Inferno hit. Slavers -- play on the hardest-hitting Slaver. Collector -- play Turn 1 for maximum cumulative value. Transient -- essential for survival; two Disarms makes the fight trivial.
+
+**Slime Boss:** Disarm applied pre-split does NOT carry over to split slimes. Play post-split on the higher-damage slime.
+
+**Artifact interaction:** Against enemies with Artifact, Disarm strips 1 Artifact instead of applying -2 Str.
heuristics/cards/doom-and-gloom.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Doom and Gloom
+
+Damage card that also channels a Dark orb for scaling.
+
+**When to play:** When orb slots are not full of more valuable orbs (Frost for defense). If all slots are Frost and the deck relies on block, channeling Dark pushes a Frost rightward toward evocation. In damage-focused turns or when slots are available, excellent.
+
+**Dark orb mechanics:** Dark orbs gain 6+Focus damage every turn passively. They fire accumulated damage at the lowest-HP enemy when evoked. A Dark orb channeled Turn 1 with Focus 2 gains 8/turn; by Turn 5 it has 40 damage.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. 10->14 damage is useful but the Dark orb is the main value regardless.
+
+**Synergies:** Barrage (Dark orbs count toward orb count = +4/7 damage per Barrage play).
heuristics/cards/double-tap.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Double Tap
+
+Doubles the next Attack. The duplicated play counts as a separate play for all purposes.
+
+**When to play:** Play BEFORE your strongest Attack card. At 1E cost, only worth it when the doubled Attack deals significant damage.
+
+**Synergies:** Rampage (double play = double scaling acceleration + massive damage), Pommel Strike+ (20 damage + draw 4), Heavy Blade (at Str 6: 32 x 2 = 64 damage for 3E total), Bash+ (first hit 10 + 3 Vuln, second hit 15 with Vuln active = 25 total).
+
+**Matchups:** Bronze Automaton -- Double Tap is a high-value Stasis target. Orbs can steal it from your hand. Consider playing early before Orbs get a chance to steal it.
heuristics/cards/dropkick.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Dropkick
+
+If target is Vulnerable: gain 1 energy + draw 1 card. Effectively FREE when target is Vulnerable.
+
+**When to play:** Is the target Vulnerable? If yes, always play -- it is free damage + cycling. If no, it is a terrible card (5/8 damage, no benefit). Do NOT play against non-Vulnerable targets.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** 5->8 damage. With Vulnerable: 7->12 damage. The free damage is significantly higher upgraded.
+
+**Synergies:** Bash+ (3 turns Vulnerable = 3 free Dropkick plays), Thunderclap (mass Vulnerable = Dropkick any target).
+
+**Matchups:** Book of Stabbing -- Dropkick+ dealt 12 Vuln damage three times as free actions = 36 total for 0 net energy. Slime Boss pre-split -- excellent with Bash+ providing 3 turns of Vulnerable. Post-split -- requires re-applying Vulnerable to each slime individually.
heuristics/cards/dual-wield.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Dual Wield
+
+Creates copies of an Attack or Power card in your hand. Copies go to hand and can be played immediately.
+
+**When to play:** In boss fights and tough hallway fights on a high-value Attack. Timing: play mid-fight when the target card is in hand and you have energy to play the copies.
+
+**Best targets:** Immolate+ (84-102 AOE across 3 copies -- won Donu and Deca), Heavy Blade+ (triple at high Strength), Impervious (90-120 block in one turn), Flame Barrier (stacking block and return damage).
+
+**Snecko Eye synergy:** Under Confusion, each copy gets a new random cost. ~44% chance at least one costs 0E with 2 copies.
+
+**Decision points:** A Skill card -- triggers Gremlin Nob's Enrage and Chosen's Hex. In those fights, do not play.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Very high. 1 copy vs 2 copies is the difference between "good" and "game-winning."
heuristics/cards/dualcast.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Dualcast
+
+Burst damage (Lightning: 8x2 = 16) or burst block (Frost: 5x2 = 10) for 1E. Excellent finisher.
+
+**When to play:** Only valuable when an orb is channeled. With an empty orb slot, Dualcast does nothing. Plan orb channeling before using -- channel Lightning with Zap (0E) then Dualcast for 16 damage. With Focus scaling, evoke values increase multiplicatively.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 1E->0E is massive -- free double evoke.
+
+**Nob warning:** Dualcast is a Skill card. Against Nob, only play if the evoke damage justifies the +2 Str cost (e.g., killing Nob that turn).
heuristics/cards/echo-form.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Echo Form
+
+The single most powerful card in the Defect's pool. Doubles the first card played each turn.
+
+**When to play:** As early as possible in long fights. The doubling compounds -- Turn 1 doubles 9+ cards across 10 turns. Plan for a low-pressure turn (after building Frost wall) to deploy.
+
+**Critical mechanic -- Ethereal:** Unupgraded exhausts at end of turn if not played. If drawn on a turn where 3E cannot be spent, permanently lost.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Arguably the most important in the Defect pool. Removes Ethereal -- avoids permanent loss from bad draw timing.
+
+**Seek synergy:** Seek guarantees Echo Form in hand Turn 1 (or whenever needed). This Seek + Echo Form combination is the core of the Defect's most consistent winning engine.
+
+**Mummified Hand synergy:** Playing Echo Form triggers Mummified Hand, making a random card cost 0. Partially offsets 3E setup cost.
+
+**Time Eater warning:** Echo Form copies count toward 12-card counter. Each doubled card = 2 plays counted. Effective budget is ~6 real card plays per cycle.
+
+**Bottled Lightning interaction:** Can make Echo Form innate, but Bottled Lightning on Seek is often better (Seek adapts to specific fights).
heuristics/cards/empty-body.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Empty Body
+
+Wrath exit that leaves you in no stance (neutral). Provides block without entering Calm.
+
+**When to play:** When you need to exit Wrath safely but do not want the Calm energy cycle. Also useful as simple 0-cost-equivalent block (7/10 for 1E).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Low-medium. 7->10 block is a solid improvement but does not change the card's role.
+
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** Play Flurry of Blows BEFORE Empty Body. Leaving any stance triggers Flurry's return from discard.
+
+**Vigilance comparison:** Vigilance is generally better (enters Calm for +2E next turn). Empty Body provides flexibility when you do not want to be locked into the Calm->Wrath cycle.
+
+**Draft priority:** Low-medium. Take only if Vigilance copies are insufficient for Wrath exits.
heuristics/cards/entrench.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Entrench
+
+Doubles all accumulated block. The single most powerful block-scaling card with Barricade.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** VERY HIGH. Entrench+ costs 1E (from 2E). Under Corruption, Entrench+ costs 0E and exhausts -- game-breaking.
+
+**When to play:** With Barricade. Block persists, so Entrench doubles the accumulated total. Typical sequence: Turn 1 generate 20, Turn 2 add 15 (35 total), Turn 3 Entrench doubles to 70. Without Barricade, Entrench doubles only this turn's block (typically 10-20) -- mediocre for 2E.
+
+**Synergies:** Barricade (mandatory pairing), Corruption (becomes 0E + FNP trigger on exhaust), Feel No Pain (exhaust under Corruption = +3-4 block on top), Body Slam+ (doubled block = doubled damage for free), Impervious (30-40 base doubled to 60-80).
+
+**Anti-synergies:** Decks without Barricade (poor value), fights with Frail.
+
+**Do NOT take** without Barricade or a clear path to obtaining Barricade.
heuristics/cards/eruption.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Eruption
+
+Watcher's primary Wrath entry. The core of the Calm energy loop.
+
+**When to play:** On burst turns when you have attacks ready to benefit from doubled damage. In Calm, Eruption+ is effectively free (spend 1E, gain 2E from Calm exit = net +1E).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Very high. 2E->1E doubles the efficiency of the Calm energy loop and allows more cards to be played on burst turns. One of the most important Watcher upgrades.
+
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** If Flurry of Blows is in hand, play it BEFORE Eruption. Flurry needs to be in the discard pile to trigger its return when Eruption changes stance.
+
+**When NOT to play:** Do NOT play Eruption if you cannot exit Wrath this turn and the enemy is attacking. Doubled incoming damage at low HP is lethal. Hexaghost Turn 1 (free turn): Do NOT enter Wrath -- Turn 2 Divider would be doubled = instant death.
+
+**Calm energy loop:** In Calm: play Eruption+ (1E) -> exit Calm (+2E) -> enter Wrath (double damage). Net gain: +1E and doubled attacks. This is the Watcher's fundamental engine.
heuristics/cards/evolve.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Evolve
+
+Neutralizes hand-clogging from Status cards. Each Status drawn is immediately replaced by a real card.
+
+**When to play:** Play on free turns. At 1E, it competes with block/damage on attack turns. Best set up early in fights where Status cards are expected. Without Mark of Pain, zero value in fights without Status card generation -- do NOT play in non-Status fights as it wastes 1E.
+
+**Synergies:** Mark of Pain + Evolve+ (Wounds from attacks become massive card advantage -- 4E + bonus draws = 5-7 cards/turn), Hexaghost Burns / Immolate Burns (Burns become cycle-neutral), Sentries Dazed (draw replacement, Dazed exhausts itself), Fire Breathing (drawing Status = damage + replacement).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** HIGH with Mark of Pain (2 draws per Wound = dominant card advantage). Moderate without Mark of Pain.
+
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Hexaghost (top-priority Turn 1 setup). Excellent vs Sentries, Slime Boss, Book of Stabbing. With Mark of Pain, has value in EVERY fight. Chosen adds Dazed via Hex, so Evolve has value after Hex activation.
heuristics/cards/fear-no-evil.md
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+# Fear No Evil
+
+Conditional Calm entry that doubles as an attack. Only enters Calm when the enemy has attack intent.
+
+**When to play:** On attack turns (enemy intending to attack), Fear No Evil in Wrath deals doubled damage AND exits to Calm safely. On non-attack turns, it is a simple 8/11 damage attack with no stance change.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium. +3 damage (8->11) is solid but not game-changing.
+
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** On attack turns:
+1. Play Flurry of Blows first (goes to discard)
+2. Play Fear No Evil (deals damage, enters Calm, Flurry returns to hand)
+3. Play returned Flurry of Blows again
+
+On non-attack turns: Flurry order does not matter since no stance change occurs.
+
+**Conditional mechanic:** Only enters Calm when the target enemy has ATTACK intent. On BUFF, DEBUFF, DEFEND turns, it deals damage but does NOT change stance. Do not rely on it as your only Wrath exit.
+
+**Decision points:** Ideal for Wrath -> Calm transitions on attack turns. Provides both burst damage (doubled in Wrath) and automatic safety (enters Calm).
heuristics/cards/feed.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Feed
+
+One of the strongest Ironclad cards. Permanent Max HP scaling.
+
+**When to play:** Whenever you can land the killing blow on an enemy. Each kill adds 3-4 Max HP permanently. Plan other attacks to soften the target, then Feed for the kill.
+
+**Critical:** Feed exhausts on play regardless of whether it kills. If the enemy survives, you lose the card permanently with zero Max HP gained. Calculate exact damage before committing.
+
+**Common mistake:** Feed in the discard pile when an enemy is at killable HP. Feed only works if YOU play it as the killing blow. Plan Feed kills in advance -- hold Feed in hand when an enemy approaches kill range, or use Headbutt to place Feed on top.
+
+**Take early, use every fight:** Feed's value compounds over the run. In every multi-enemy fight, plan to Feed-kill at least one enemy. Do NOT save Feed for "the perfect moment."
+
+**Synergies:** Headbutt (place Feed on top when enemy is low), Vulnerable (15/18 damage kill threshold), Strength (+2 Str makes Feed+ deal 14 base / 21 with Vuln).
+
+**Matchups:** Best targets are low-HP enemies: Byrds (~25 HP), Mystic (40 HP), Gremlins, Cultists. Slime Boss split is the best Feed+ fight in Act 1.
heuristics/cards/feel-no-pain.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Feel No Pain
+
+Passive block engine that triggers on every exhaust. Permanent for the rest of combat.
+
+**When to play:** On a free turn for zero opportunity cost. At 1E, worth playing even when only 2-3 exhaust triggers are expected. Priority increases with more exhaust sources.
+
+**Synergies:** True Grit+ (exhaust + block + 3 FNP), Burning Pact (exhaust + draw + 3 block), Fiend Fire (exhaust entire hand = 3 per card), Corruption (all Skills exhaust = constant triggers -- FNP is critical for surviving Corruption), Corruption + Dead Branch (triple combo: free exhaust + 3 block + random card), Second Wind (mass exhaust = massive FNP burst).
+
+**Slime Boss synergy:** Each Slimed exhausted generates 3 block, partially offsetting 1E tempo cost. 5+ Slimed = 15+ block across the fight.
+
+**Spheric Guardian:** Corruption + FNP successfully defeated Spheric Guardian with only 12 HP lost. Recommended configuration for the hardest Act 2 hallway fight.
+
+**Victory engine:** Mandatory component of Barricade + Corruption engine. FNP+ (4 block/exhaust) generated 40-60+ block per fight from exhaust triggers alone.
+
+**Anti-synergy:** Weak in decks with few exhaust sources.
heuristics/cards/fiend-fire.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Fiend Fire
+
+Exhausts ALL other cards in hand. Deals 7 damage per card exhausted to a single target.
+
+**When to play:** When burst will kill the target, when remaining cards are expendable, or on free turns. Turns Slimed/Decay/Dazed/Burn cards into 7 damage each. After playing, your hand is EMPTY. Only play when: (1) the burst kills, (2) it is a free turn, or (3) remaining cards are expendable.
+
+**Synergies:** Status/Curse cards (become 7+ damage instead of dead draws), Strength (each card's damage scales), Vulnerable (each hit gets 50% bonus), Unceasing Top (empties hand, triggers Top draw engine), Snecko Oil (randomize costs before Fiend Fire).
+
+**Matchups:** Slime Boss -- burst + Slimed exhaust. Chosen -- only use when burst will kill or exhausted cards are genuinely expendable. If Chosen survives, remaining turns have zero block sources.
+
+**Caution:** Exhausting key cards (Barricade, Clothesline) is permanent. At critically low HP, exhausting everything for survival is correct -- survival > future value.
+
+**Guardian warning:** Fiend Fire as a primary engine drains the deck unsustainably in 12-14 turn fights. Use on free turns for burst, do not plan the deck around it as sole damage engine.
+
+**Index shifting danger:** Always reference Fiend Fire by name rather than index number. Earlier card plays can shift indices causing wrong cards to be played.
heuristics/cards/finisher.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Finisher
+
+Deals damage multiplied by number of Attacks played this turn. Shivs count as Attacks.
+
+**When to play:** Play as the LAST Attack card of the turn. After 4 Shivs: 4 * (6 + Str) damage for 1E. With Blade Dance (3 Shivs) + 1 extra Shiv: excellent burst.
+
+**Synergies:** Blade Dance (creates 3 Shivs that build the Attack count), Phantasmal Killer (Finisher is an Attack -- Double Damage doubles its total output), Accuracy (enables Shiv plays that build the count).
+
+**Draft priority:** Mid-to-high in Shiv decks. Without Shiv support, deals only 6-12 damage (1-2 attacks before it) -- mediocre.
heuristics/cards/fire-breathing.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Fire Breathing
+
+Passive AOE damage triggered by drawing Status or Curse cards.
+
+**When to play:** Play on free turns (enemy buff turns). At 1E, it competes with block and damage on attack turns. Best set up early when Status cards are expected to accumulate. In fights with no Status cards, Fire Breathing does nothing.
+
+**Key synergy -- Evolve + Fire Breathing:** The strongest anti-Status engine. Evolve draws replacement on Status draw. Fire Breathing deals damage on Status draw. Together, Status cards become pure upside: free damage with no hand clog. Confirmed Hexaghost counter.
+
+**Do NOT exhaust Fire Breathing+ when Immolate is in the deck.** Burns from Immolate trigger 10 damage on draw -- bypasses Plated Armor entirely. Exhausting Fire Breathing+ throws away 30-50+ free damage.
+
+**Synergies:** Power Through (Wounds trigger on draw), Evolve (Status = damage + replacement draw), Immolate Burns.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Significant. 6->10 damage per trigger is 67% more damage.
+
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Hexaghost (neutralizes Burns), Sentries (Dazed triggers), Slime Boss (Slimed triggers), Book of Stabbing (Wounds trigger). Irrelevant in fights with no Status cards.
heuristics/cards/fission.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Fission
+
+Critical upgrade difference between removing orbs (unupgraded) and evoking them (upgraded).
+
+**Unupgraded (BAD):** REMOVES orbs WITHOUT evoking. Orbs vanish -- no Frost block, no Lightning damage, no Dark burst. Destroys orb-based defense for Energy and draws that may not compensate. Do NOT play when Frost orbs are providing critical block.
+
+**Upgraded (EXCELLENT):** EVOKES all orbs, triggering effects. With 5 orbs: 5 Frost evokes (25 block), 5 Energy, 5 draws. Massive defensive burst AND enables playing drawn cards with gained energy.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Extremely high -- top-3 Defect upgrade target. The difference between removing and evoking is the difference between destroying your own defense and generating a massive burst turn.
+
+**Decision points:** At 0 orbs, does nothing. At 1 orb, minimal. Shines at 3+ orbs. Fission+ is a burst finisher when evoke effects + Energy/draws enable killing or massive block.
heuristics/cards/flame-barrier.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Flame Barrier
+
+Block + thorns effect. Punishes multi-hit attacks.
+
+**When to play:** Excellent against multi-hit enemies. Flame Barrier+ against Hexaghost Inferno (7 hits) = 16 block + 42 counter damage. Against multi-enemy fights (Slavers, Gremlins), each attacker triggers counter damage.
+
+**Decision points:** At 2E, competes with other defensive plays. Worth it when facing multi-hit attacks or multiple attackers. On single-hit attack turns, a Defend may be more energy-efficient.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High when facing Hexaghost. 12->16 block, 4->6 counter per hit. Single best defensive card for Inferno turns.
+
+**Synergies:** Hexaghost Inferno (7 hits x 6 counter = 42 free damage), multi-enemy fights (each attacker triggers counter), Book of Stabbing (multi-hit attacks trigger counter on every hit, and hit count increases each turn).
+
+**Matchups:** Best-in-class vs Hexaghost. Strong vs Book of Stabbing. Moderate vs single large attackers.
heuristics/cards/flex.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Flex
+
+Temporary Strength that disappears at end of turn. Standalone, almost never good.
+
+**When to play:** Do not take unless Limit Break+ is already in the deck or planned. If already in deck without Limit Break+, remove at next shop.
+
+**Exception -- Limit Break+ combo:** Flex+ becomes a legitimate combo piece with Limit Break+. Play Flex+ for +4 temporary Str, then Limit Break+ to double total. The doubled amount is permanent -- only the 4 temporary Str is lost at end of turn. This turned Flex+ from a dead card into a key boss-kill contributor.
+
+**Decision points:** Without Limit Break+, Inflame (1E, permanent +2 Str) is strictly better in any fight lasting 2+ turns.
heuristics/cards/flurry-of-blows.md
@@ -0,0 +1,21 @@
+# Flurry of Blows
+
+The Watcher's core free-damage engine. Returns to hand from discard on every stance change for another free 0-cost play.
+
+**Play order (CRITICAL -- SINGLE MOST IMPORTANT WATCHER RULE):** ALWAYS play Flurry of Blows BEFORE any stance-change card. Flurry must be in the DISCARD PILE when a stance change occurs for the return trigger to work.
+
+**WRONG:** Eruption -> Flurry. Flurry was in hand during the stance change. One play only (4 damage).
+
+**CORRECT:** Flurry (4 damage, goes to discard) -> Eruption (Flurry returns to hand) -> Flurry again (4 more damage). Two plays for 8 total.
+
+With 2 stance changes per turn: correct order yields 3 plays (12 damage) vs wrong order yields 1 play (4 damage). That is 8 free damage per turn wasted by incorrect sequencing.
+
+**In Wrath:** Damage doubles (8 base, 12 upgraded). Always play returned Flurry in Wrath for maximum value.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 4->6 per play. With 3 plays per turn: 12->18 damage difference.
+
+**Shuriken/Kunai synergy:** Each Flurry play counts as an attack. With 2-3 plays per turn, relic thresholds trigger frequently.
+
+**Multiple copies:** Two Flurry in the deck means two returns per stance change. With 2 copies and 2 stance changes: 6 free plays for 24 damage.
+
+**Draft priority:** High. Take in Act 1 whenever offered. Best common card for the stance-cycling damage engine. A second copy doubles output.
heuristics/cards/flying-sleeves.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Flying Sleeves
+
+Multi-hit Retain attack. Hold for Wrath turns to deal massive doubled damage.
+
+**When to play:** In Wrath for doubled damage: 8x2 = 16 (12x2 = 24 upgraded). Since it Retains, hold it from a safe turn for the next burst turn.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 4x2 -> 6x2 is significant, especially doubled in Wrath (16 -> 24).
+
+**Retain strategy:** Draw Flying Sleeves on a defensive turn, hold it for the next Wrath turn. No wasted draws.
+
+**Shuriken/Kunai synergy:** 2 hits = 2 counts toward the 3-attack threshold. Pairs well with Flurry of Blows for quick relic triggers.
+
+**Snake Plant warning:** Multi-hit attacks trigger Malleable block multiple times. Against Snake Plant, Flying Sleeves gives the enemy 3+4 = 7 block from Malleable (two triggers). Use single large hits against Snake Plant when possible.
heuristics/cards/follow-up.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Follow-Up
+
+Conditional 0-cost attack. Free when played immediately after another Attack card.
+
+**When to play:** Immediately after any Attack card to get the free cost. Do not play Skills between attacks if Follow-Up is in hand -- that breaks the chain and costs 1E.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 7->11 damage makes the free attack significantly more impactful, especially doubled in Wrath (22 damage for 0E).
+
+**Attack chaining:** Strike -> Follow-Up (free) -> Flurry (free) = 3 attacks for 1E total. Enables long burst sequences.
+
+**Kunai/Shuriken synergy:** Free attacks count toward the 3-attack threshold. Follow-Up + Flurry after any attack = 3 attacks for minimal energy, triggering both relics.
+
+**In Wrath:** A free 14/22 damage attack in Wrath is exceptional. Chain attacks to maximize doubled damage output.
heuristics/cards/ftl.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# FTL
+
+0-cost attack with conditional draw. When played first each turn, replaces itself (or generates +1 card upgraded).
+
+**When to play:** Always play FTL first if possible to get the draw bonus. If another card is more important first (especially with Echo Form), the draw bonus is lost and FTL becomes simple 5 damage for 0E.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. Draw 1->2 makes FTL+ a strong opening play each turn.
+
+**Echo Form note:** Interaction with "first card played" mechanic and Echo Form copies requires careful sequencing.
+
+**Safe against Gremlin Nob** (Attack card).
heuristics/cards/ghostly-armor.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Ghostly Armor
+
+Strong block for 1 energy (double Defend). Ethereal means must play or lose permanently.
+
+**When to play:** Whenever it is in hand. Always play -- losing it permanently is worse than wasting block.
+
+**Decision points:** Do you have 1E to spare? If yes, always play. If forced to choose between Ghostly Armor and a critical play, remember: losing Ghostly Armor permanently is a real cost.
heuristics/cards/halt.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Halt
+
+Free block card. Excellent in Wrath (12/14 block for 0E).
+
+**When to play:** After other cards when you have 0E remaining, or in Wrath when you need defensive padding for free. At 0E, there is never a reason not to play it.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Low. 3->4 base (12->14 in Wrath) is a small improvement.
+
+**In Wrath:** 12/14 block for 0E is extremely strong. If you need to stay in Wrath for one more attack but also need block, Halt provides both without spending energy.
+
+**Decision points:** Always play Halt before ending your turn if it is in hand. Free block is never wasted.
heuristics/cards/havoc.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Havoc
+
+Plays the top card of the draw pile for free, then exhausts it. The played card's energy cost is ignored.
+
+**When to play:** When the draw pile contains expendable cards (Strikes, status, Wounds, Burns, Dazed) and you want a free action. Best when you know what is on top (e.g., after Headbutt).
+
+**Critical danger:** Havoc exhausts the played card permanently. If the top card is a key scaling card (Rampage+, Fiend Fire+, Bash+), it is gone forever.
+
+**Decision points:** Before playing, consider what is in the draw pile. Havoc is safe when: (1) draw pile is mostly Strikes/Defends, (2) you tracked what remains, (3) the fight is trivial. Havoc is dangerous when: (1) deck contains irreplaceable cards, (2) you have not tracked draw pile contents.
+
+**Synergies:** Headbutt (place a specific card on top, then Havoc plays it for free -- controlled combo).
+
+**Anti-synergy:** Any deck with irreplaceable scaling cards (Rampage, Fiend Fire, Feed). DO NOT take if the deck depends on specific cards.
+
+**Matchups:** Gremlin Nob -- Havoc is a Skill, triggers Enrage. Do NOT play.
heuristics/cards/headbutt.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Headbutt
+
+Put a card from your discard pile on top of your draw pile. Strongest with predictable enemy patterns.
+
+**When to play:** When you have a high-value card in discard you need next turn. Check your discard pile -- what do you need next turn given enemy intent?
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High if Headbutt is a regular play. 9->12 damage. With Vulnerable: 13->18. In one fight, unupgraded left Mystic at 1 HP, extending the fight 2+ turns.
+
+**Synergies:** Rampage (top-deck to keep scaling chain unbroken), Carnage+ (guarantee 28-42 next turn), Feed (guarantee kill shot), Bash+ (refresh Vulnerable on demand), Immolate+ (top-deck for guaranteed 28+ AOE), Heavy Blade (top-deck for guaranteed finisher).
+
+**Snecko Eye interaction:** Top-decked cards receive a new random cost when drawn. Top-deck a 2E card and it has 25% chance of being free next turn.
+
+**Matchups:** Lagavulin -- top Bash+ to refresh Vulnerable. Guardian -- guarantee burst for Mode Shift phases. Bronze Automaton -- top-deck Heavy Blade for post-Hyper Beam stun turn. Donu and Deca -- top-deck Immolate+ repeatedly.
heuristics/cards/heavy-blade.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Heavy Blade
+
+High-cost Strength multiplier. Worthless without Strength, game-winning with it.
+
+**When to play:** Only take when the deck has Strength sources (Vajra, Inflame, Spot Weakness, Limit Break, Demon Form). At 0 Strength, just 14 damage for 2E (terrible). At Str 6+, highest damage-per-play available.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High in Strength decks. 3x to 5x Strength multiplier is transformative. At Str 6: 32 -> 44 damage.
+
+**Synergies:** Demon Form+ (by turn 4 at +12 Str, deals 74 damage; by turn 6 at +18 Str, deals 104), Limit Break+ (doubling Str before Heavy Blade = exponential), Double Tap (plays twice for 64+ damage), Spot Weakness (+3 Str becomes +9/+15 damage), Vulnerable (applies to full total).
+
+**Snecko Eye synergy:** 25% chance of costing 0E under Confusion. Free 30-94 damage play.
+
+**Matchups:** Excellent in boss fights where Strength has time to build. Less effective in quick hallway fights where 2E without setup is too slow.
heuristics/cards/hemokinesis.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Hemokinesis
+
+High burst for 1 energy. The 2 HP self-damage is minor when healthy.
+
+**When to play:** Opening turns, boss burst damage, finishing blows where HP cost is irrelevant. With Vulnerable on target: floor(15*1.5) = 22 damage for 1E -- exceptional burst.
+
+**Decision points:** Check current HP. At low HP in a long fight, the 2 HP per play adds up. Against Slime Boss split phase, the 2 HP cost is especially dangerous -- combined with split damage, even one Hemokinesis can be the difference between survival and death.
+
+**Synergies:** Vulnerable (22 damage for 1E), Rupture (+1 Str per play from self-damage), kill shots where HP cost is irrelevant.
heuristics/cards/immolate.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Immolate
+
+High-damage AOE attack. Hits ALL enemies. The Burn downside is minor.
+
+**When to play:** Multi-enemy fights (Byrds, Gremlin groups, Slime Boss split, Collector + Torch Heads). Play freely -- 21+ AOE is worth 2-4 HP of Burn damage across the fight.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 21->28 AOE. With Vulnerable: 42 AOE. With Paper Phrog (75%): 49 AOE. Most impactful card in multi-enemy boss fights.
+
+**Synergies:** Thunderclap (applies Vulnerable to all, then Immolate for 31+ AOE), Fire Breathing+ (Burns trigger 10 free damage on draw -- bypasses Plated Armor; do NOT exhaust Fire Breathing when Immolate is in deck), Evolve (Burns draw replacement cards -- makes Burn downside pure upside), Dual Wield+ (3 copies = 84-102 AOE damage in one turn -- won Donu and Deca), Headbutt (top-deck for guaranteed next turn).
+
+**Snecko Eye synergy:** Frequently costs 0-1E instead of 2E, enabling multiple plays per turn.
+
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Byrds, Slime Boss split, 3 Cultists, Guardian, The Collector. Primary damage source in both consecutive victories.
heuristics/cards/impervious.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Impervious
+
+Best single-card block in the game. One-time use (Exhaust).
+
+**When to play:** Save for the biggest incoming hit of the fight. Using 30-40 block on a 16-damage turn is wasteful -- Defends handle that. Calculate: "If I don't play Impervious now, will I die? If yes, play it. If no, save it."
+
+**Timing discipline:** NEVER play on Guardian's Sharp Hide attacks (9-16 damage) unless you would die. Save for the 32-damage single hit. Against Gremlin Nob, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 30->40 block. The extra 10 block is the difference between absorbing a big hit cleanly and taking chip.
+
+**Matchups:** Bronze Automaton -- THE most important card. Hyper Beam deals 45-57 damage. Save for Hyper Beam turns. Guardian -- absorbs the 32-damage attack completely (upgraded takes 0). Gremlin Leader -- save for Turn 4-5 when Rally-scaled damage reaches 34+. Collector -- critical for post-STRONG_DEBUFF turns (37-50+ incoming). Transient -- save for Turn 3+ (50+ damage), NOT Turn 1. Snake Plant -- absorbs 9x3=27 multi-hit.
+
+**Synergies:** Calipers (retains 25 block next turn upgraded), Barricade (retained permanently), Spot Weakness (use the safe turn to set up Strength).
heuristics/cards/inflame.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Inflame
+
+Cheap, permanent damage scaling. Every Attack deals 2 more damage for the entire fight.
+
+**When to play:** Play as early as possible in long fights (bosses, elites). On free turns, always play. On attack turns with high incoming, play Inflame (1E) and use remaining 2E on block. Do not stack multiple setup cards in one turn. Powers do NOT trigger Gremlin Nob Enrage.
+
+**Synergies:** Reaper (Strength increases damage to ALL enemies = more healing), Heavy Blade (+2 Str becomes +6 or +10 damage due to multiplier), Whirlwind (each hit gains +2), Twin Strike (+2 per hit = +4 total), every Attack card.
+
+**Matchups:** Guardian Defensive Mode -- safe (Power, not Attack, no Sharp Hide retaliation). Bronze Automaton -- play immediately to avoid Stasis theft. Spheric Guardian -- CRITICAL. Without Strength scaling, this fight is mathematically unwinnable. Inflame is the single best card for breaking through Spheric Guardian's defenses.
heuristics/cards/inner-peace.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Inner Peace
+
+Dual-purpose card: draws 3/4 cards when in Calm, enters Calm when not.
+
+**When to play:** Depends on current stance.
+
+**In Calm (draw mode):** Draw 3/4 cards. Use when already in Calm and need to find specific cards (Eruption for burst, Defend for blocking). Play early in the turn to maximize use of drawn cards.
+
+**In Wrath or no stance (Calm entry mode):** Enters Calm. Provides safe exit from Wrath and sets up +2E energy loop for next turn. Use when stuck in Wrath with no Vigilance available.
+
+**Play order (CRITICAL):** If Flurry of Blows is in hand and you are NOT in Calm, play Flurry BEFORE Inner Peace. Inner Peace entering Calm triggers Flurry's return from discard. If already in Calm, play Inner Peace first for the draw.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. Drawing 4 instead of 3 significantly improves the chance of finding specific combo pieces.
+
+**Calm draw -> Eruption burst:** Draw 3-4 cards in Calm, then play Eruption to exit Calm (+2E) and enter Wrath for doubled attacks. The drawn cards fuel the burst.
heuristics/cards/intimidate.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Intimidate
+
+Free mass Weak. Zero downside at 0 energy cost.
+
+**When to play:** ALWAYS play when drawn in most fights. Reduces all enemy attack damage by 25% for 1-2 turns.
+
+**Decision points:** Against Gremlin Nob and Chosen, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage/Hex). Bronze Automaton: Do NOT use for Artifact stripping. SAVE for the Hyper Beam turn -- Weak reduces Hyper Beam damage by 25%. Intimidate exhausts after use, so if played early for Artifact stripping, no Weak source exists when Hyper Beam fires.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Worth upgrading when the deck has few other debuff sources. 1->2 Weak doubles duration.
+
+**Synergies:** Letter Opener (free 5 damage + mass Weak), Corruption (free AND exhausts), Feel No Pain (exhaust triggers 3 block).
+
+**Matchups:** Guardian -- Weak reduces 32-damage attack to 24. Gremlin Leader -- mass Weak on all gremlins + Leader. Bronze Automaton -- SAVE for Hyper Beam turn. Weak on Hyper Beam reduces 51 damage to 38.
heuristics/cards/iron-wave.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Iron Wave
+
+Dual-purpose card. Does two things at once for 1 energy.
+
+**When to play:** When you need both offense and defense but only have 1 energy to spare. Against Chosen with Hex (Attack card, no Dazed penalty). During Guardian Sharp Hide (block absorbs retaliation, then deal damage).
+
+**Decision points:** Is splitting between 5 damage and 5 block better than fully committing to one? Usually yes when you need both. With Strength, only the damage scales. Block is affected by Frail and Dexterity, not Strength.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 5/5->7/7 is a 40% improvement on both halves.
+
+**Synergies:** Metallicize (passive block + Iron Wave block = solid defense per turn), Strength (damage half scales).
heuristics/cards/judgment.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Judgment
+
+Conditional instant-kill finisher. Sets enemy HP to 0 if at or below 30/40 HP threshold.
+
+**When to play:** Save for when an enemy is near the HP threshold. Calculate total damage from other cards first -- if they bring the enemy to 30/40 or below, play attacks first then finish with Judgment.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 30->40 HP threshold is significant. Many boss phases end around 40 HP, making upgraded Judgment a reliable finisher.
+
+**Ignores block:** If the enemy has block but HP is at or below threshold, Judgment still kills. Do not waste other cards removing block before Judgment.
+
+**Useless above threshold:** Against enemies above 30/40 HP, Judgment does literally nothing. Do not waste 1E playing it on a full-health enemy.
+
+**Boss fights:** Excellent finisher. Against Hexaghost, getting the boss to 30-40 HP then using Judgment avoids 1-2 additional turns of Burns damage.
heuristics/cards/just-lucky.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Just Lucky
+
+Free card that does a little of everything: damage, block, and Scry. Always playable at 0E.
+
+**When to play:** Play before ending your turn for free damage, block, and draw pile filtering. At 0E, there is never a reason not to play it.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Low-medium. 3->4 damage, 2->3 block. Small improvements across the board.
+
+**Wave of the Hand synergy:** The 2-3 block triggers Wave of the Hand's Weak application. Playing Just Lucky after Wave of the Hand = free Weak application. This is a key interaction for the Watcher's Weak strategy.
+
+**Scry 1:** Small but consistent deck filtering. Discard Burns or Curses from the top of the draw pile.
+
+**Shuriken/Kunai:** Counts as an Attack for relic triggers despite its small damage.
+
+**Play order:** Play after Wave of the Hand if active (to trigger Weak). Otherwise, play freely as a bonus before ending turn.
heuristics/cards/leap.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Leap
+
+Straightforward block card. 9 block for 1E is good value (better than Defend's 5).
+
+**When to play:** When you need block. No special mechanics. Fills the Defect's need for reliable turn-by-turn block before Frost orbs + Focus are online.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Decent. 9->12 block. Lower priority than Focus cards or Coolheaded.
+
+**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers Enrage. Against Nob, only play if survival requires it.
heuristics/cards/limit-break.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Limit Break
+
+Strength multiplier. Converts existing Strength into exponential scaling.
+
+**When to play:** After Inflame or Demon Form has provided base Strength. Mid-fight when Strength is already 3+ for meaningful doubling. Does NOTHING if Strength is 0.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** The most impactful upgrade in Str-scaling decks. Limit Break+ (no exhaust) enables repeated doubling for infinite scaling.
+
+**Flex+ combo:** Limit Break+ doubles ALL current Str including temporary from Flex+. Play Flex+ (4 temp Str) then Limit Break+ to convert into permanent. Example: 1 base + 4 from Flex+ = 5 total, doubled to 10. End of turn lose 4 = 6 permanent.
+
+**Synergies:** Vajra (+1) + Inflame (+2) + Spot Weakness (+3) = Str 6 base. Limit Break+ doubles to 12, then 24. Heavy Blade at Str 6 deals 32 (or 44 upgraded). Demon Form + Limit Break dominates long fights but requires 3+ setup turns.
+
+**Weaknesses:** Requires setup (Inflame or Demon Form first), a Skill card (triggers Hex/Enrage), at low HP vs fast enemies the setup window does not exist. As a Neow random rare, dead until a Str source is found.
+
+**Deck building note:** Always pair with immediate damage/block sources for fights where the engine cannot activate.
heuristics/cards/loop.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Loop
+
+Doubles the passive output of the first orb slot. Value scales directly with Focus.
+
+**When to play:** Play after channeling an orb you want to keep in slot 1. Since new orbs push older ones rightward, the first slot is the most stable position. Place the orb type you want Loop to trigger (usually Frost for defense) in slot 1 before playing.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 1E->0E makes Loop a free permanent passive boost.
+
+**Focus scaling:** At Focus 3, a Frost orb passive gives 5 block. Loop adds another 5 at start of turn = 10 passive block/turn from a single orb.
+
+**Emotion Chip interaction:** If Emotion Chip is active, the first orb triggers three times per cycle -- massive passive value.
heuristics/cards/machine-learning.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Machine Learning
+
+Permanent +1 card draw per turn. Subtle but compounds significantly in long fights.
+
+**When to play:** As early as possible in any fight expected to last 3+ turns. In hallway fights ending in 2-3 turns, the 1E cost may not be worth 1-2 extra draws.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Extremely high. At 0E Innate, Machine Learning+ is always free to play Turn 1, guaranteeing +1 draw from Turn 2 onward with zero opportunity cost.
+
+**Mummified Hand synergy:** As a Power, triggers Mummified Hand. At 0E upgraded, already free -- Mummified Hand trigger is pure bonus value.
heuristics/cards/malaise.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Malaise
+
+Silent's strongest boss debuff. Permanent Strength reduction + Weak.
+
+**Critical -- X-cost energy trap:** Malaise automatically uses ALL remaining energy. You CANNOT specify X manually. Play block cards and other spells FIRST, then Malaise LAST with only the desired energy remaining. A confirmed death occurred when Malaise consumed all 4E leaving 0E for Defend at 19 HP.
+
+**When to play:** Save for boss fights. Exhaust means single use per combat. Do not waste on hallway fights unless genuinely lethal. Play LAST after all other non-X-cost cards. Do NOT use for Artifact stripping.
+
+**Synergies:** High energy decks (more energy = larger X = bigger debuff), Wraith Form+ (reduces damage post-Intangible), Shiv burst (neuters offense while Shiv damage scales).
+
+**Draft priority:** High for boss fights. One of the best Silent cards against any single-target boss.
heuristics/cards/masterful-stab.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Masterful Stab
+
+12 damage for 0 energy. Cost increases by 1 for each HP lost in combat.
+
+**When to play:** Excellent in decks that take zero damage. Dead weight in long fights with chip damage.
+
+**Synergies:** Footwork+/high Dex builds (zero damage taken = permanent 0E), short hallway fights with no chip damage.
+
+**Anti-synergies:** Hexaghost (Burns deal unblockable damage, cost rises 2-4 per turn -- completely unplayable by Turn 12), any fight with unavoidable chip damage (Poison, Burns, Decay curse, Brutality), long fights generally.
+
+**Decision framework:** Take when the deck reliably achieves zero damage in hallway fights AND the Act 1 boss is NOT Hexaghost. If Hexaghost is the boss, Masterful Stab is a trap.
heuristics/cards/meditate.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Meditate
+
+Powerful card retrieval that ends your turn. Play LAST -- always.
+
+**When to play:** Play as the LAST card each turn. It ends your turn immediately. All attacks, blocks, and 0-cost plays must happen before Meditate.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. Retrieving 2 cards instead of 1 lets you set up both attack and defense for next turn, or retrieve two combo pieces.
+
+**Card retrieval with Retain:** Retrieved cards stay in hand through end of turn (Retain). Guarantees specific cards for next turn -- retrieve Eruption+ for burst, or Vigilance for blocking.
+
+**Enters Calm:** Sets up +2E energy bonus for next turn. Combined with retrieved Eruption+, this guarantees the full Calm energy loop.
+
+**Flurry interaction:** Entering Calm triggers Flurry of Blows return from discard. But since Meditate ends your turn, you cannot play returned Flurry this turn. However, Flurry Retains, so it is available next turn.
+
+**Common mistakes:**
+- Playing Meditate early and losing the rest of your plays for the turn.
+- Retrieving cards you do not need next turn (retrieve what solves the upcoming enemy intent).
heuristics/cards/melter.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Melter
+
+Bypasses enemy Block entirely. Play before other attacks on blocked enemies.
+
+**When to play:** Against enemies with Block (Spheric Guardian, Shield Gremlin, enemies using Defensive Stance). Without Melter, damage into Block is wasted -- Melter removes all Block then deals its damage directly to HP.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Moderate. 10->14 damage. The Block removal is the main value regardless of upgrade.
+
+**Play order:** Play Melter BEFORE other attacks on blocked enemies. Block removal applies first, then Melter's damage hits HP, then subsequent attacks hit unblocked HP.
+
+**Barrage synergy:** Melter strips Block, then Barrage's full multi-orb damage hits HP. Critical in Spheric Guardian and Gremlin Leader fights.
+
+**Decision points:** If enemy has 15 Block, a normal 10-damage attack does 0 HP damage. Melter removes 15 Block then deals 10 HP damage -- effectively 25 damage advantage over playing another attack first.
heuristics/cards/metallicize.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Metallicize
+
+Passive block every turn. The block equivalent of Strength.
+
+**When to play:** Play as early as possible in long fights. Over 10 turns: 30 free block. Two copies provide 6 block/turn. Powers do NOT trigger Gremlin Nob Enrage.
+
+**Key interaction:** Metallicize block is NOT affected by Frail. Frail only reduces block from cards. Metallicize's end-of-turn block is flat regardless of debuffs -- especially valuable against Spheric Guardian (Frail 5).
+
+**Synergies:** Iron Wave (passive + card block = solid defense), second Metallicize (6-7 block/turn is exceptional), Barricade (Metallicize block carries over and stacks -- with Barricade + 2x Metallicize, passive alone reaches 21 by Turn 3, 35 by Turn 5).
+
+**Matchups:** Guardian -- handles many attack patterns with only 1 Defend needed. Hexaghost -- critical for the 13-turn fight. Byrds -- essential for long attrition. Spheric Guardian -- Frail-immune block.
heuristics/cards/miracle.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Miracle
+
+Free +1E that Retains and Exhausts. ONLY play when you have a useful way to spend the energy.
+
+**When to play:** AFTER determining your turn plan. Count the energy needed for planned plays, then play exactly enough Miracles to cover the deficit. Excess Miracles should be held (Retain keeps them safe) for future turns where the energy matters.
+
+**Good uses:**
+- Enables playing one more block card to survive an attack turn.
+- Enables an attack + block card instead of just block.
+- Funds the Calm -> Wrath energy loop when short 1E.
+- Enables a Power setup on a free turn.
+
+**BAD use:** Playing all Miracles with no plan for the energy. A confirmed death occurred with 4 Miracles in hand -- player played all 3 Miracles for +3E, had no useful cards to spend energy on (only Meditate+ and Rushdown), and died with 0 block against Snake Plant's 21-damage attack.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** N/A (generated card, cannot be upgraded directly).
+
+**Stance interaction:** Miracle does not change stance but Retains. Available on the critical Calm-exit burst turn when extra energy is needed for attacks in Wrath.
heuristics/cards/offering.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# Offering
+
+One of the best cards in the game. Trade HP for a massive tempo burst.
+
+**When to play:** Turn 1 of any fight where you need to set up quickly (play a Power + attack in the same turn). At high HP (50+), the cost is negligible. At low HP (15-), the 6 HP loss can be lethal — never play when 6 HP puts you below incoming damage minus available block.
+
+**Synergies:**
+- Expensive cards (Bludgeon, Fiend Fire, Demon Form) — makes 3E cards playable alongside other cards
+- Unceasing Top — Offering draws 3 and gives 2E; if you play down to empty hand, Top triggers for additional draws
+- Fiend Fire — 2 extra energy means Fiend Fire still leaves energy for more plays
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Low. Base version already provides maximum value.
+
+**Key rule:** Exhausts after one use per fight. Plan the turn you play it to maximize the energy and card advantage.
heuristics/cards/overclock.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Overclock
+
+Free cycling card -- draws 2/3 cards for 0E. The Burn downside is minor.
+
+**When to play:** Play freely when cycling is needed. The Burn dilutes the deck slightly but is manageable in small decks.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. Draw 2->3 significantly improves the card.
+
+**Synergies:** Fire Breathing (Burns trigger damage on draw), Evolve (Burns draw replacement cards -- makes Burn downside upside).
+
+**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers Enrage.
heuristics/cards/phantasmal-killer.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Phantasmal Killer
+
+Grants Double Damage buff for the NEXT Attack card played. Buff persists until consumed.
+
+**When to play:** The turn BEFORE a burst turn. On a free turn (enemy buffing/defending), play PK. Next turn, play the highest-value Attack for doubled damage. With Shivs: PK then Blade Dance next turn for 3 doubled Shivs.
+
+**Synergies:** Blade Dance (3 Shivs, each doubled), Accuracy (+4/+8 per Shiv, all doubled), Finisher (as an Attack, its total is also doubled), Vulnerable (multiplicative with Double Damage).
+
+**Time Warp trick:** If counter is at 11/12, play PK as the 12th card on a free turn. Time Warp triggers, but Double Damage persists for the fresh cycle's first attack.
+
+**Draft priority:** High when deck has Blade Dance and/or Accuracy. Upgraded version (0E) is strictly better -- free setup any turn.
heuristics/cards/pommel-strike.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Pommel Strike
+
+Damage + draw. Replaces itself in hand while dealing more than Strike.
+
+**When to play:** Always. The draw is the real value -- it cycles through the deck to find key cards. Play early in the turn to draw into more options.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. The double draw (1->2) makes this one of the best cycling cards in the game.
+
+**Synergies:** Any deck that wants to find specific cards (Bash+, Fiend Fire, Feed). Snecko Oil (draw into 0-cost cards).
+
+**Matchups:** Against Byrds, the 9 damage is halved by Flight but the draw still finds better answers.
heuristics/cards/power-through.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Power Through
+
+Massive block (triple Defend) but pollutes hand with 2 unplayable Wounds.
+
+**When to play:** Must-survive turns with massive incoming damage. When you have exhaust tools (True Grit+, Fiend Fire, Second Wind) to remove the Wounds afterward. Short fights (3-4 turns) where the Wounds will not cycle back.
+
+**When it is a trap:** In long fights without exhaust tools. The Wounds reduce effective hand size, meaning fewer block AND fewer damage cards per turn. Over 8+ turns, playing Power Through twice adds 4 Wounds, reducing block density for all subsequent turns.
+
+**Synergy -- Fire Breathing:** The 2 Wounds trigger Fire Breathing when drawn, dealing 6-10 damage to all enemies per Wound. With Evolve, each Wound drawn deals damage AND draws a replacement.
+
+**Slime Boss caution:** Adds 2 Wounds on top of Slimed cards. Without exhaust tools, Wound + Slimed accumulation can make 4 of 5 cards unplayable.
+
+**Draft rule:** Do not take unless the deck has at least one exhaust tool, Fire Breathing, or the deck is fast enough to end fights in 4-5 turns.
heuristics/cards/pummel.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Pummel
+
+Multi-hit Attack. Excellent Strength-scaling finisher. Exhausts after use.
+
+**When to play:** Only when Strength is high enough to make the hits meaningful. At base 2x4 = 8 for 1E, mediocre. Save for mid-to-late fight after Inflame or Demon Form have built Strength. Since it Exhausts, you get one shot per fight -- make it count. Play on a Vulnerable target.
+
+**Decision points:** At +5 Str: (2+5) x 4 = 28 for 1E. At +7 Str: (2+7) x 4 = 36 for 1E.
+
+**Synergies:** Inflame/Demon Form (Strength multiplied across 4-5 hits), Vulnerable (1.5x on each hit), Pen Nib, Shuriken.
+
+**Anti-synergy:** Snake Plant Malleable -- each hit triggers Malleable, causing increasing block (4-hit Pummel can give 3+4+5+6 = 18 block). Avoid against enemies with Malleable.
+
+**Comparison:** Sword Boomerang has higher base per-hit damage and does not exhaust, but Pummel has more hits for better Strength scaling in single-target burst.
heuristics/cards/rage.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Rage
+
+Gain block whenever you play an Attack this turn. Lasts until end of turn only -- NOT permanent despite being a Power card.
+
+**When to play:** Play at start of turn before any attacks. Since it costs 0, it never competes with other plays. Even 2 attacks = 6 block for free. Do NOT play on a setup turn expecting block on future turns.
+
+**Critical mechanic:** Rage's block-per-attack effect EXPIRES at the end of the turn it is played. It must be replayed every turn to generate block.
+
+**Decision points:** If you draw Rage on a turn where you play few attacks, it provides minimal value.
+
+**Anti-synergy with Barricade:** Despite appearing to synergize, Rage's per-turn expiration limits the combo. You must redraw and replay Rage each turn to benefit.
+
+**Synergies:** Attack-heavy decks, Unceasing Top, Anger (0E Attack that triggers Rage for free block), Shuriken (both reward playing many Attacks per turn).
+
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Gremlin Leader (block while killing gremlins). Against The Collector, Rage+ combined with Thunderclap provided exactly the block needed to survive a lethal turn at 2 HP.
heuristics/cards/rampage.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Rampage
+
+Scaling attack that gets stronger every time you play it. +8 damage per play (cumulative). Resets each fight.
+
+**When to play:** Play every turn you can afford it. Each play increases all future plays. In short fights (3 turns), only reaches 16-24 total -- mediocre. In long fights, becomes highest damage-per-energy in the deck. On free turns, always play for the scaling even if targeting a full-HP enemy.
+
+**Scaling math:** Play 1: 8, Play 2: 16, Play 3: 24, Play 4: 32, Play 5: 40. The +8 accumulates on the BASE, then Strength and Vulnerable apply ON TOP (multiplicative scaling).
+
+**Synergies:** Double Tap (double play = double scaling acceleration), Headbutt (top-deck Rampage to keep scaling chain unbroken), Vulnerable (50% bonus on already-high damage), Inflame (compounds with scaling base).
+
+**Anti-synergy:** Havoc (can randomly exhaust Rampage permanently), unupgraded True Grit (random exhaust risk). Any random-exhaust effect is devastating because Rampage's value is cumulative.
+
+**Matchups:** Hexaghost -- exceptional in the 13-turn fight (scales to 60+ damage). Guardian -- strong for 10+ turns. 3 Cultists -- play turn 1 (free) to start scaling.
heuristics/cards/reaper.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Reaper
+
+Essential sustain card. Damage + healing in one. Exhausts after one use per fight.
+
+**When to play:** Multi-enemy fights where you heal from hitting all enemies. With Strength, both damage and healing scale. Timing is critical since it exhausts.
+
+**Decision points:** Reaper heals based on UNBLOCKED damage -- enemies with block reduce healing. In scaling fights, Shockwave+ on the first attack turn is higher priority than Reaper healing. Prevention > healing.
+
+**Critical limitation:** Cannot sustain across consecutive combat rooms. A deck relying solely on Reaper will lose 25-40 HP per fight in Act 2 and recover only 6 from Burning Blood. Pair with Feed, Blood Potion, Regen Potion, or Toy Ornithopter.
+
+**Exhaust warning:** Reaper can be permanently lost through random exhaust effects (unupgraded True Grit, Havoc). Protect Reaper from random exhaust at all costs.
+
+**Synergies:** Inflame (+2 Str boosts damage to all enemies = more healing), Vulnerable (bonus damage = bonus healing), multi-enemy fights.
+
+**Anti-synergy:** Demon Form as the sole Strength source. Reaper needs Strength NOW, not later.
+
+**Matchups:** The Collector -- 3 targets, excellent healing. Spheric Guardian -- WORST MATCHUP (Barricade block means Reaper heals zero). The Champ -- single target, limited healing; save for after Execute.
heuristics/cards/rupture.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Rupture
+
+Converts self-damage into permanent Strength scaling.
+
+**When to play:** As early as possible to maximize Strength accumulation. Rupture does nothing without self-damage cards.
+
+**Decision points:** Without Brutality or Hemokinesis, it is a dead card. With Brutality, it creates a scaling engine that makes long fights easier over time.
+
+**Synergies:** Brutality (automatic +1 Str/turn), Hemokinesis (+1 Str per play), Offering (+1 Str per play). Multiple Ruptures stack (2 Ruptures + Brutality = +2 Str/turn).
heuristics/cards/rushdown.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Rushdown
+
+Engine Power that draws 2/3 cards on every Wrath entry. Enables longer, more explosive burst turns.
+
+**When to play:** As early as possible on a safe turn (enemy not attacking or block already sufficient). Costs 1E and provides no immediate defense. On free turns (enemy buffing), Rushdown is the highest-priority play.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. Drawing 3 instead of 2 per Wrath entry significantly increases burst potential and consistency.
+
+**Triggers every Wrath entry:** Every Eruption, Crescendo, or other Wrath-entry card draws 2-3 extra cards. In a stance-cycling turn with 2 Wrath entries: 4-6 extra draws.
+
+**Flurry of Blows combo:** Wrath entry triggers both Rushdown (draw 2-3) and Flurry return (from discard). The drawn cards may include more attacks to play in Wrath at doubled damage. This combination is the Watcher's primary burst engine.
+
+**Draft priority:** High. Take Rushdown whenever offered. Core engine piece for the stance-cycling strategy.
heuristics/cards/searing-blow.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Searing Blow
+
+Can be upgraded multiple times (unique mechanic). Worth building around only if upgraded 3+ times early.
+
+**When to play:** If upgraded 3+ times, becomes a primary damage source. Otherwise, a mediocre 2E attack.
+
+**Decision points:** Only take if you plan to upgrade at every rest site. At 1-2 upgrades, worse than Carnage or Pommel Strike.
heuristics/cards/second-wind.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Second Wind
+
+Mass exhaust for block. Clears status cards while gaining significant block.
+
+**When to play:** When hand is full of status cards (Burns, Wounds, Slimed, Dazed). Each exhausted becomes 5-7 block. Also exhausts Defends and other Skills.
+
+**Decision points:** Exhausts ALL non-Attack cards including useful Skills. Check what you are losing before playing. In long attrition fights, exhausting irreplaceable Skills (Shockwave+, True Grit+) sealed losses.
+
+**Critical warning:** Only play when you are certain the exhausted Skills are no longer needed.
+
+**Synergies:** Corruption (Skills cost 0 but exhaust -- Second Wind exhausts them for block value), Dark Embrace (draw per exhaust), Barricade (block persists), Feel No Pain (each card exhausted by SW+ triggers FNP -- hand with 5 status = 25-35 block from SW+ + 15-20 from FNP = 40-55 total).
+
+**Normality curse removal:** Under Corruption, Second Wind+ can permanently exhaust Normality from combat. High-priority play.
+
+**Victory engine role:** Highest-impact single block play. Against 5 Dazes + 1 Burn, SW+ generates 48-66 total block from one 0E card under Corruption.
heuristics/cards/seeing-red.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Seeing Red
+
+Free energy. Costs 0 to play (upgraded), gains 2E, exhausts.
+
+**When to play:** Any turn where you have cards to play but not enough energy. Play early in the turn so you can use the 2E on remaining cards.
+
+**Synergies:** Expensive cards (Carnage 2E, Shockwave 2E, Fiend Fire 2E). Enables playing them alongside other cards. Excellent for enabling Bash+ 2E + another 2E card in the same turn.
heuristics/cards/seek.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Seek
+
+The most consistent card in the Defect's pool. Guarantees access to the exact card needed for the current situation.
+
+**When to play:** Turn 1 of boss fights: find Echo Form. Hallway fights: find the kill card (Barrage+ with full orbs).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Extremely high. 1 card->2 cards. Seek+ finds 2 cards for 0E -- functionally a perfect draw ignoring deck dilution.
+
+**Bottled Lightning synergy:** Making Seek innate guarantees a tutor Turn 1 of every combat. The single most consistent opener for the Defect.
+
+**Echo Form synergy:** Echo Form doubles Seek, tutoring 2 (or 4 with Seek+). Enables assembling a complete hand every turn.
+
+**Exhaust note:** One-time tutor per combat. Almost always sufficient because it sets up the engine on Turn 1.
+
+**Nob warning:** Skill card, triggers Enrage. Play only if tutored card enables a kill within 1-2 turns.
heuristics/cards/sentinel.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Sentinel
+
+Block card that refunds energy when exhausted.
+
+**When to play:** In exhaust-heavy decks. Sentinel provides block AND energy when fed to Fiend Fire, True Grit+, Second Wind, or Burning Pact.
+
+**Decision points:** Base block (5/8) is modest. The value is in the exhaust payoff. Without exhaust tools, Sentinel is just a worse Shrug It Off. With exhaust tools, it is a net-positive play: block + energy recovery.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Both halves improve significantly. Upgraded Sentinel exhausted via True Grit+ or Second Wind gives 8 block + 3 energy -- equivalent to a free Offering without the HP loss.
+
+**Synergies:** Fiend Fire (exhausted for 7+ damage AND gains 2-3E), True Grit+ (exhaust for block + energy), Dark Embrace (exhaust draws a card), Corruption (all Skills cost 0 and exhaust -- Sentinel becomes 0E for 5-8 block + 2-3E, a net energy gain).
+
+**Matchups:** Gremlin Nob -- do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage).
heuristics/cards/sever-soul.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Sever Soul
+
+High damage attack that exhausts every non-Attack card currently in hand.
+
+**When to play:** In exhaust-synergy decks with Feel No Pain or Dark Embrace. When hand is mostly Status cards and you want to purge them all while dealing damage.
+
+**Decision points:** Before playing, count how many non-Attack cards are in hand and evaluate whether you can afford to lose them ALL for the rest of this fight. Play Defends/Skills FIRST, then Sever Soul last to minimize cards exhausted. If any Defend or key Skill is in hand, do NOT play unless the fight will end this turn.
+
+**Critical warning:** Do not take this card as a generic damage source. The exhaust-all-non-attacks cost is severe. It is NOT a substitute for Hemokinesis or Carnage. Only take it in dedicated exhaust decks (Feel No Pain, Dark Embrace, Corruption).
+
+**Matchups:** In long fights (Guardian, bosses), losing Defend cards cripples block density for the rest of combat.
heuristics/cards/shockwave.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Shockwave
+
+Mass debuff. One of the most powerful cards in the game. Exhausts but returns to deck next fight.
+
+**When to play:** Play on the FIRST ATTACK TURN of multi-enemy fights. For 3 Cultists, that is turn 2. For Slavers, that is turn 1. Never save for turn 4+ -- preventing damage with early Weak far exceeds healing from Reaper. Never hold for "a harder fight" -- exhaust resets between fights.
+
+**Critical:** After Shockwave (2E), the remaining 1E MUST go to block at critical HP, NOT damage. Confirmed fatal: Shockwave + Pommel Strike instead of Shockwave + Defend at 19 HP vs Slavers resulted in Turn 1 death.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High. 2->3 turns of each debuff is 50% more duration.
+
+**Synergies:** Multi-enemy fights (debuffs apply to ALL), Dropkick (Vulnerable on all targets = free cycling).
+
+**Matchups:** Hexaghost -- Weak reduces Inferno from 42 to 28. MANDATORY for survival. Gremlin Leader -- mass debuff. Slime Boss split -- mass debuff on both slimes. The Collector -- play when Torch Heads are present.
+
+**Warning -- The Champ:** Shockwave debuffs applied BEFORE Execute are WASTED. Execute cleanses ALL debuffs. Ideally hold for after Execute.
heuristics/cards/shrug-it-off.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Shrug It Off
+
+Block + draw. Replaces itself while providing defense.
+
+**When to play:** Always. The draw means you get block without losing card advantage. Play early in the turn when possible -- the drawn card may change your plan.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. 8->11 block. After upgrading, always use 11 in calculations, not the base 8. Miscalculating SIO+ block as 8 instead of 11 costs 3 block per play -- this exact error has been the survival margin in a boss fight.
+
+**Matchups:** Against Gremlin Nob, do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage).
heuristics/cards/skewer.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Skewer
+
+X-cost attack that scales with remaining energy. High energy produces massive single-target burst.
+
+**Critical -- X-cost energy trap:** Skewer automatically uses ALL remaining energy. Play block cards and other spells FIRST, then Skewer LAST with only desired energy remaining.
+
+**When to play:** Best on free turns (enemy buffing/defending) where all energy can go to damage. Pairs with Energy Potions for massive burst (5E = 35/50 damage).
+
+**Synergies:** Vulnerable (50% more damage per hit), high energy sources.
+
+**Draft priority:** Moderate. Strong burst finisher for boss fights and elites. Less useful in hallway fights where energy must split between block and damage.
heuristics/cards/spot-weakness.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Spot Weakness
+
+Conditional Strength gain. Only works when enemy's current intent is an Attack.
+
+**When to play:** Only play when enemy intent is an Attack. On non-attack turns, this does nothing and wastes 1 energy. Against enemies where intent is hidden (Runic Dome), this is a gamble.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Worth upgrading for scaling fights. +3 -> +4 STR.
+
+**Synergies:** Multi-hit attacks (Pummel, Twin Strike, Whirlwind -- Strength applies per hit), Heavy Blade (+3 Str becomes +9/+15 due to multiplier), Limit Break (doubling after Spot Weakness gives massive scaling), long fights where Strength compounds.
+
+**Matchups:** Excellent vs Guardian (predictable attack turns, long fight). Excellent vs Hexaghost (frequent attack turns). Hexaghost Turn 2 timing: playing it alongside Inflame+ leaves only 1E for block against ~30 damage. Consider playing on Turn 3+ instead. Marginally useful vs Spheric Guardian (alternates attack/defend).
+
+**Snecko Eye synergy:** Even at 2-3E cost, the permanent Str gain is often worth it in boss fights.
heuristics/cards/strike.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Strike
+
+Baseline damage card that gets outclassed quickly.
+
+**When to play:** Early floors before better attacks are available. Safe against Gremlin Nob (Attack card).
+
+**Decision points:** Is there a better attack to play instead? Could this energy be spent on block or setup?
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Low. 6->9 damage (13 with Vulnerable). Only worth mass-upgrading via Ancient Writing event.
heuristics/cards/study.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Study
+
+Slow value engine. Generates one Insight per turn (0E, draw 2, Retain).
+
+**When to play:** On a safe turn (enemy not attacking). The 2E cost makes it unsuitable for defensive turns. Must be deployed early to accumulate enough Insights for value.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium. Adding Insight to hand instead of draw pile gives immediate access rather than waiting a draw cycle.
+
+**2E cost warning:** Spending 2E on a Power that does nothing immediately is risky on turns with incoming damage. Only play when survival is not in question.
+
+**Draft priority:** Low-medium. Too slow for most hallway fights. Only valuable in long boss fights where cumulative draw advantage matters. Skip in aggressive decks that end fights quickly.
heuristics/cards/sweeping-beam.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Sweeping Beam
+
+The Defect's best early AOE option. Hits all enemies and replaces itself with a draw.
+
+**When to play:** Always in multi-enemy fights for the AOE. In single-enemy fights, slightly below Strike in damage but the draw makes it net-positive. Safe against Gremlin Nob as an Attack.
+
+**Early Defect priority:** High. Addresses the Attack deficit and provides AOE damage, critical for Act 1 Slime Boss and Act 2 multi-enemy fights.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** 6->9 damage AOE. Solid improvement.
heuristics/cards/sword-boomerang.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Sword Boomerang
+
+Hits random enemies 3-4 times for 3 damage each. Targets are random across all enemies.
+
+**When to play:** In single-enemy fights, all hits land on the same target. With high Strength, each hit gains +Str damage. At +5 Str: (3+5) x 3 = 24 for 1E. Does not Exhaust, so it can be played every cycle.
+
+**Decision points:** At base (3x3 = 9 for 1E), mediocre. Becomes strong at Strength +3 or higher. Against multiple enemies, random targeting makes it unreliable for focus-firing a priority target.
+
+**Synergies:** Inflame/Demon Form/Brimstone (Strength multiplied across 3-4 hits), Vulnerable (1.5x on each hit), Pen Nib.
+
+**Anti-synergy:** Snake Plant Malleable -- each hit triggers Malleable, granting increasing block. Avoid against enemies with Malleable. Random targeting in multi-enemy fights is unreliable.
heuristics/cards/talk-to-the-hand.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Talk to the Hand
+
+Block Return per hit. Excellent against multi-hit enemies. Play early since it exhausts.
+
+**When to play:** Early in the fight for maximum Block Return value over multiple attack turns. Since it exhausts, apply it to the primary threat immediately -- do not save for later.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Medium-high. Block Return 2->3 is 50% more free block per hit. Against multi-hit enemies, this scales rapidly.
+
+**Snake Plant (7x3):** Block Return 2 triggers 3 times per Chomp = 6 free block per attack. Upgraded: 9 free block. Extremely strong in this matchup.
+
+**Multi-hit enemies:** The more hits an enemy does, the more value Block Return provides. Excellent against Hexaghost Inferno (6 hits), Book of Stabbing, and any multi-attack enemy.
+
+**Exhausts:** Only one use per combat. Apply to the enemy that attacks most frequently or with the most hits.
heuristics/cards/terror.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Terror
+
+99 Vulnerable = permanent 50% more damage from ALL attacks for the rest of combat.
+
+**When to play:** Play on the first turn you can spare 1E (0E upgraded), then focus on damage. Earlier application = more total bonus damage. Against Awakened One: Terror in Phase 1 accelerates kill before Curiosity Str accumulates. Terror in Phase 2 enables the kill without Powers.
+
+**Boss fight value:** Against bosses with 250-300+ HP, the 50% increase saves 3-5 turns of combat, translating to 3-5 fewer turns of incoming damage.
+
+**Dead Branch synergy:** Terror exhausts, triggering Dead Branch for a random card -- "free" proc on top of devastating debuff.
+
+**Bane synergy:** Bane deals double on Poisoned. With Terror's Vuln 99: (base * 2) * 1.5 = 3x effective damage.
+
+**Single-target limitation:** Only applies to one enemy. Prioritize the highest-HP target.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Excellent. 1E->0E makes it a free permanent 50% damage boost.
heuristics/cards/thunderclap.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Thunderclap
+
+Cheap mass Vulnerable. The best setup card for multi-enemy fights.
+
+**When to play:** Always in multi-enemy fights. Turn 1 opener to set up 50% more damage on everything. Strips 1 Flight per Byrd hit and 1 Artifact per enemy.
+
+**Decision points:** Against single enemies, worse than Bash+ (1 turn Vulnerable vs 3 turns). Against multiple enemies, one of the best cards in the game.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Worth upgrading when it is a regular play. 4->7 AOE damage with Vulnerable: ~6->10 damage each.
+
+**Synergies:** Dropkick (sets up free cycling on all Vulnerable targets), all Attack cards (50% more damage to all enemies).
+
+**Matchups:** Byrds -- THE single most important card. Strips 1 Flight from each + applies Vulnerable. Without Thunderclap, Flight 3 on three Byrds requires 9 separate hits. Having Thunderclap before Act 2 is essential. Sentries -- strips 1 Artifact from each. Slime Boss split -- mass Vulnerable on both slimes. Bronze Automaton -- strips Artifact charges (Automaton starts with Artifact 3).
heuristics/cards/true-grit.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# True Grit
+
+Upgraded version lets you CHOOSE which card to exhaust. Unupgraded version exhausts RANDOMLY.
+
+**When to play:** True Grit+ is excellent -- block + controlled deck thinning. Exhaust status cards (Slimed, Wound, Burn) while gaining block.
+
+**Decision points:** CRITICAL -- is this upgraded? Unupgraded True Grit is a ticking time bomb. In a thin deck, random exhaust may destroy your best card. True Grit+ lets you choose expendable cards (Strikes, status cards). Before playing unupgraded, check: are there cards in hand you absolutely cannot lose?
+
+**Upgrade priority:** THE most important upgrade in the game. Random->chosen exhaust completely changes the card. Upgrade priority #1 whenever True Grit is in the deck.
+
+**Synergies:** Dark Embrace (draw on exhaust), Charon's Ashes (3 AOE on exhaust). Be careful of over-exhausting.
+
+**Anti-synergy:** Unupgraded True Grit with Fiend Fire+ or any irreplaceable card. Random exhaust can destroy the deck's best card mid-combat.
+
+**Matchups:** Gremlin Nob -- do NOT play (Skill triggers Enrage). Guardian -- unupgraded True Grit is particularly dangerous; random exhaust removes key cards from a hand already under pressure.
heuristics/cards/twin-strike.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Twin Strike
+
+Multi-hit attack. Each hit applies Strength separately, making this one of the best Strength-scaling cheap attacks.
+
+**When to play:** Against Flight enemies (strips 2 Flight). For Pen Nib counting (each hit counts toward the 10-hit counter). In Strength-stacking decks -- at Str 6, Twin Strike deals (5+6)*2 = 22 for 1E (or 26 upgraded).
+
+**Decision points:** Against Flight enemies, multi-hit is more valuable than single-hit for the same total. In Strength decks, Twin Strike is often the best finisher for its energy cost.
+
+**Synergies:** Vajra/Inflame/Spot Weakness (Strength applies per hit = double benefit), Vulnerable (50% bonus on each hit), Double Tap (plays twice for massive burst).
heuristics/cards/uppercut.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Uppercut
+
+Applies BOTH Weak and Vulnerable in one card. The best single-card Artifact stripper in the Ironclad pool.
+
+**When to play:** Against single targets where both debuffs matter. Against Artifact enemies (strips 2 charges). Upgraded version applies 2 turns of each.
+
+**Decision points:** At 2E, is dual-debuff worth the cost? Against Spheric Guardian (3 Artifact), strips 2 charges efficiently. Against Gremlin Nob, safe (Attack card).
+
+**Upgrade priority:** High against Artifact enemies. Extends debuff duration to 2 turns each -- doubles value.
+
+**Synergies:** Artifact stripping (each debuff consumes 1 charge separately), Spot Weakness (Vulnerable amplifies Strength-boosted attacks), Demon Form (sustained Vulnerability keeps damage high as Strength ramps).
+
+**Matchups:** Spheric Guardian -- strips 2 of 3 Artifact charges in one play.
heuristics/cards/vault.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Vault
+
+Extra turn. Extremely powerful but expensive. One use per combat (Exhausts).
+
+**When to play:** On the highest-impact turn possible. Best on safe turns (enemy buffing/debuffing) -- the extra turn lets you deal massive damage before the enemy acts again.
+
+**Upgrade priority:** Very high. 3E->2E makes Vault playable alongside other cards on the same turn. At 3E, Vault often consumes your entire energy budget.
+
+**Extra turn value:** The additional turn provides its own draw + energy. Effectively doubles your output for one cycle -- critical for finishing bosses or surviving dangerous phases.
+
