+Use before a fight, not during. Best used early in the run when most cards are unupgraded (higher chance of upgrading something useful). At rest sites, using this potion gives a free upgrade without spending the rest site action -- use it in addition to the normal Smith action for two upgrades at one rest site.
+
+Value is moderate due to random targeting. Could upgrade a Strike (low value) or a key card like Bash (high value). Most valuable when the deck has many unupgraded cards, least valuable when most cards are already upgraded.
heuristics/potions/blood-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Blood Potion
+
+Use before a dangerous fight to increase survival odds, or mid-fight when HP is critical. Scales with Max HP -- more valuable when Feed has increased the pool. At 80 Max HP heals 16; at 100 Max HP heals 20.
heuristics/potions/cultist-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Cultist Potion
+
+Use Turn 1 of long fights (bosses, elites). The earlier it is used, the more turns of Strength accumulation. In a 9-turn boss fight used Turn 1, provides +1, +2, +3... up to +8 Str by Turn 9. Comparable to Demon Form (+2/turn) over 4 turns.
+
+Synergies: Rampage (Strength compounds with per-play scaling for multiplicative damage growth), Sword Boomerang (multi-hit multiplies Strength bonus), Shivs + Accuracy (Ritual Str stacks with Accuracy bonus on every Shiv -- by Turn 10, each 0-cost Shiv deals 23 damage).
+
+Best targets: Hexaghost (13-turn fight, massive scaling value), Bronze Automaton (long fight, DPS race), Time Eater (10-turn fight). Do NOT waste on hallway fights -- the fight ends before meaningful scaling occurs.
heuristics/potions/distilled-chaos.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Distilled Chaos
+
+Use when draw pile contains high-cost cards (Immolate+, Barricade, Demon Form) that would normally consume your entire turn's energy. The free play is incredible value. Also good when you need an extra action but have 0 energy.
+
+The card played is random (top of draw pile), so outcomes vary. Best used when draw pile is stacked with good options. After Battle Trance or other draw effects thin the draw pile, remaining cards are more predictable.
+
+Best for emergency big-block or big-damage on critical turns. Synergizes with decks that have expensive Power cards or heavy AOE in draw pile.
heuristics/potions/duplication-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Duplication Potion
+
+Use before a high-value card play. Rampage at 40 damage = 80 total. Carnage+ at 28 = 56 total. Iron Wave+ = double block + double damage (14/14 total). Best with damage cards for burst or Iron Wave+ for combined offense/defense.
+
+Under Confusion (Snecko Eye), wait for a high-value card to roll 0 cost, then duplicate for double value at zero energy cost. Best confirmed use: Duplication Potion on 0-cost Immolate+ dealt 60 AOE to both Donu and Deca. This is the highest-value play -- target cards whose cost was randomly set to 0.
heuristics/potions/elixir.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Elixir
+
+Use when hand is clogged with status cards (Burns, Wounds, Slimed, Dazed). Mass-exhaust garbage cards to improve future draws. Also useful for controlled deck thinning mid-combat (exhaust extra Strikes/Defends that are diluting key card draws).
heuristics/potions/energy-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Energy Potion
+
+Use on turns where you have more cards to play than energy allows. Enables playing expensive combos (Bash+ 2E + Shockwave 2E in one turn with only 3 base energy). Use on critical turns, not throwaway turns.
heuristics/potions/entropic-brew.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Entropic Brew
+
+Use before a boss fight or dangerous elite to maximize potion count. Best used when multiple potion slots are empty. Random potions received can include any potion type, providing flexible answers to unknown fights ahead.
heuristics/potions/essence-of-steel.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Essence of Steel
+
+Best used Turn 1 of long fights (bosses, elites). Plated Armor 4 provides 4 free block every turn. Over 9 turns = 36 total block. Functionally similar to Metallicize. The earlier it's used, the more total block generated. Note that Plated Armor degrades on unblocked hits, so pair with strong block cards to preserve the stacks.
heuristics/potions/explosive-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Explosive Potion
+
+Excellent against multi-enemy fights (Collector Torch Heads, Gremlin Leader gremlins, Darklings). Against enemies with Metallicize (Giant Head's 8 Metallicize), Explosive Potion deals damage that bypasses block entirely -- the 10 damage goes straight to HP.
+
+In close fights where the enemy is 1-10 HP from dying to poison, this potion can be the difference between victory and death. Use before the turn the enemy would otherwise survive by a razor-thin margin. Do not hoard for a future fight if the current fight is lethal. An unused Explosive Potion on a death screen is a strategic failure.
heuristics/potions/fairy-in-a-bottle.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Fairy in a Bottle
+
+Keep as safety net for boss fights or dangerous elites. Do not waste in hallway fights -- Fairy is often consumed in Centurion+Mystic hallway fights. Plan paths so Fairy is available for the most dangerous fight, not consumed early.
+
+Can be intentionally sacrificed when the math works: if an enemy's attack will trigger Fairy and your Thorns/Bronze Scales will kill the enemy from the revival, this is a legitimate strategy. Calculate exactly before committing.
+
+In scaling fights (Gremlin Leader), Fairy buys one extra turn but does NOT solve the underlying problem of insufficient damage output vs scaling. Plan to win before Fairy is needed.
heuristics/potions/fear-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Fear Potion
+
+Use when Bash+ or other Vulnerable sources are not available or not in hand. Excellent at the start of boss fights to guarantee Vulnerable on Turn 1 without spending energy on Bash. Also valuable mid-fight when Vulnerable has expired and Bash is in the discard pile.
+
+Vulnerable 3 means 3 turns of 50% increased damage. On a turn with Bludgeon (32 base), Vulnerable adds 16 damage. Over 3 turns of attacks, the total bonus can be 30-60+ damage. Synergizes with Bludgeon (48 vs 32), Immolate (31 vs 21), Reaper (more damage = more healing). Any high-damage turn benefits enormously.
heuristics/potions/fire-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Fire Potion
+
+Use to finish off an enemy or break through block. Excellent for killing Cultist in Cultist+Chosen fights (removes the scaling threat immediately). Good for killing Mystic when it has block up. At 20 damage, it one-shots many small enemies and chunks Act 2 hallway enemies significantly.
heuristics/potions/flex-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Flex Potion
+
+Use on burst turns where you play multiple Attack cards. Each Attack benefits from +2 damage, but only this turn. Best with Bash+ or high-damage cards on the same turn. Do NOT play on a turn where you only play 1 Attack -- the value is wasted. Plan to play 2-3 Attacks on the Flex turn. Much weaker than Strength Potion due to 1-turn limitation.
heuristics/potions/fruit-juice.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Fruit Juice
+
+Use immediately -- +5 Max HP is equally valuable whenever used. Best at start of combat for immediate HP buffer. No reason to hold it.
heuristics/potions/gamblers-brew.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Gambler's Brew
+
+Use when the current hand lacks what you need to survive the turn. Discard attack cards to draw block cards (or vice versa). Particularly valuable in boss fights where a specific card type is needed on a critical turn.
+
+Best used when you can identify which cards in hand are useless for the current turn. Discard all damage cards when you need block to survive, or discard all block when you have a free turn for burst. The more cards you discard, the higher the variance -- but at 2 HP needing block, maximum redraw is correct.
+
+Never discard block cards (Shrug It Off, Defend, Ghostly Armor) when facing an attack intent, even if the current plan is to burst damage. Block cards are insurance -- discarding them gambles the entire run on the redraw.
+
+Exceptional under Confusion (Snecko fight). Confusion randomizes card costs, and a bad roll can make the entire hand unplayable (all 3-cost cards with 3 energy). Gambler's Brew redraws the hand with NEW randomized costs, effectively re-rolling the Confusion RNG.
heuristics/potions/liquid-memories.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Liquid Memories
+
+Use when a key card is in the discard pile and you need it NOW. Retrieve Impervious for a big incoming attack, Bash+ to refresh Vulnerable, or a kill card for a lethal opportunity. The 0 cost makes it free value.
+
+Do not plan around using Liquid Memories to recover exhausted cards (Intimidate, Shockwave, Impervious, Reaper) -- it cannot retrieve them from the exhaust pile. It will retrieve a different card from discard instead, wasting the potion.
heuristics/potions/power-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Power Potion
+
+Best used Turn 1 of a boss fight, especially on a free turn. Powers are permanent, so early setup = maximum value. Can find Metallicize (passive block), Inflame (Strength), or other strong Powers. Always choose the permanent scaling option -- avoid Berserk (self-Vulnerable).
heuristics/potions/regen-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Regen Potion
+
+Best used Turn 1 of a long fight (boss) for maximum total healing. The earlier it's used, the more turns of healing received. Total: ~15 HP over 5 turns. Not worth saving for hallway fights that end in 3-4 turns (only get partial value).
heuristics/potions/skill-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Skill Potion
+
+Best during boss fights when you need a specific answer (mass debuff, big block). Can find Impervious (30 block), Shockwave (mass debuff), or other clutch Skills. The 0 cost means it's free value on any turn.
heuristics/potions/smoke-bomb.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Smoke Bomb
+
+Use when the fight is unwinnable and continuing will result in death. Use when estimated incoming damage on the next turn exceeds your current HP + maximum possible block. Do not save it for "a worse situation" -- dying to an avoidable fight is worse than losing rewards from one combat.
+
+Carry one into Act 2 whenever possible as insurance against dangerous hallway fights (3 Cultists, Byrds, Snake Plant) at low HP. Also valuable in Act 1 against Gremlin Gang fights at low HP -- 4 gremlins deal 22+ combined damage on Turn 1, which is lethal at sub-30% HP without strong AOE.
+
+A single Smoke Bomb can extend a run by multiple floors. An unused Smoke Bomb on a death screen is a strategic failure.
heuristics/potions/snecko-oil.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Snecko Oil
+
+Very high variance -- use as a desperation play or burst turn. Can give incredible turns (multiple 0-cost cards) or unplayable hands. Best as a last resort when the standard hand can't win, or when you need a miracle turn.
+
+Do NOT use when your survival depends on playing specific cards -- use it only when the default hand is already insufficient. At low HP, the variance can seal a loss by making block cards unplayable on critical survival turns.
+
+With Snecko Eye synergy: when already Confused, the cost randomization is redundant -- cards are already randomized each draw. The draw 5 effect becomes pure upside with zero additional downside. Use Turn 1 of a boss fight to expand the hand from 7 to approximately 10 cards, enabling massive burst openings.
heuristics/potions/speed-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Speed Potion
+
+Use before a big incoming damage turn where you play multiple block cards. Defend goes from 5 to 10 block with +5 Dex. Best when you can play 2-3 block cards on the same turn (10-15 extra block total). Use when you need to survive a massive attack (Inferno, Guardian 32). Against multi-enemy fights with high combined damage, Speed Potion on a defensive turn can be fight-saving.
heuristics/potions/strength-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Strength Potion
+
+Best used Turn 1 of boss fights (compounds over all attacks all fight). In hallway fights, use for kill turns or when Full Block needs extra damage to kill an attacker.
+
+If HP is below 35% entering a fight, use this potion IMMEDIATELY on turn 1. Do not save it. A Strength Potion shortens fights by 2-3 turns against high-HP enemies (Shelled Parasite, Centurion+Mystic), which means 2-3 fewer turns of incoming damage. An unused Strength Potion on a death screen means 10-20 damage was left on the table.
+
+Against Giant Head with Metallicize 8, even +2 Str on a Strike turns it from 6 damage (bounces off Metallicize) to 8 damage (exactly breaks through). In any fight approaching lethal, use ALL offensive potions before the window closes. Potions do not count as card plays (no Slow interaction).
heuristics/potions/weak-potion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Weak Potion
+
+Use against enemies with big attacks when no other Weak source is available. Against Hexaghost Inferno: reduces 42 to ~28. Against Lagavulin: reduces 18 to ~13. Use on the turn BEFORE a big attack lands.
heuristics/relics/anchor.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Anchor
+
+Free block on turn 1 reduces opening damage. Equivalent to a free Defend + half a Defend. Reduces the need to play block cards on turn 1, freeing energy for Bash+ or other setup. Compounds over the run -- less HP lost to turn 1 attacks in every fight.
heuristics/relics/ancient-tea-set.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Ancient Tea Set
+
+ALWAYS rest/smith at the rest site before a boss when you have this relic. The 2E bonus on turn 1 is worth more than most upgrades. With 5E turn 1: set up Metallicize + Dark Embrace + play damage, or Bash+ + 3 Attacks for massive burst. Don't waste the energy -- plan to spend ALL 5E on turn 1.
heuristics/relics/bag-of-marbles.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Bag of Marbles
+
+Free Vulnerable on Turn 1 without spending energy or a card play. Enables aggressive Turn 1 plays -- every attack card deals significantly more damage before Bash+ is even needed.
+
+SYNERGIES: Paper Phrog (1.75x damage to all enemies Turn 1 for free), Immolate+ (28 * 1.75 = 49 AOE Turn 1), Heavy Blade (Str multiplied + Vuln), any front-loaded damage strategy.
+
+One of the best common relics. Free damage amplification on Turn 1 of every combat.
heuristics/relics/bird-faced-urn.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Bird-Faced Urn
+
+Each Power played becomes a small heal. In Power-heavy decks (3+ Powers), adds 6+ HP healing per combat, partially offsetting the Burning Blood gap. Incentivizes taking and playing Power cards.
+
+SYNERGIES: Mummified Hand (free Power plays = free healing), Corruption (frees energy for Powers), multiple cheap Powers (Inflame 1E, Metallicize 1E) maximize triggers per fight.
+
+Best in long fights (bosses, elites) where multiple Powers are played. Less impactful in short hallway fights where only 1 Power is played. Does not replace real healing cards (Reaper, Feed) but supplements them.
heuristics/relics/black-star.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Black Star
+
+Doubles relic acquisition from elite fights. In a run fighting 3-4 elites, provides 3-4 extra relics -- each potentially game-changing.
+
+DECISION FRAMEWORK: Best taken at end of Act 1 (before Act 2/3 elites). Weaker after Act 2 (only Act 3 elites remain). Requires actively routing through elites to realize value -- taking Black Star and avoiding elites wastes the relic slot.
+
+RISK: Boss relics replace the starter relic. Losing Burning Blood's sustain must be weighed against long-term relic advantage. For the Defect, losing Cracked Core is relatively low-cost compared to losing Burning Blood.
heuristics/relics/bloody-idol.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Bloody Idol
+
+Provides both economy and sustain. Every combat victory that awards gold also heals 5 HP and gives 5 bonus gold. Over a full act (8-12 gold-awarding events): 40-60 bonus gold and 40-60 HP healing.
+
+Makes combat more sustainable (5 HP heal per fight stacks with Burning Blood's +6 = 11 HP recovered per fight). Incentivizes taking fights over avoiding them since each fight provides healing.
+
+SYNERGIES: Maw Bank (gold gain triggers heal), Golden Idol (+25% gold = more frequent triggers), any relic or event that awards gold.
heuristics/relics/bottled-flame.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Bottled Flame
+
+Guarantees a specific Attack on turn 1 every fight. Best target: Bash+ (guaranteed Vulnerable turn 1 in every fight). Other good targets: Carnage+ (guaranteed burst), Feed (guaranteed kill setup). The consistency of always having your best Attack turn 1 is enormous.
heuristics/relics/bottled-lightning.md
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+# Bottled Lightning
+
+Guarantees a specific Skill on Turn 1 of every fight. Provides perfect consistency for engine-defining cards.
+
+BEST TARGETS:
+- **Seek/Seek+** (Defect): Guaranteed Turn 1 tutor that finds Echo Form, Barrage+, or any needed card. Strongest known target for Defect.
+- **Offering** (Ironclad): Guaranteed Turn 1 energy burst + draw.
+- **Coolheaded+** (Defect): Guaranteed Turn 1 Frost orb + 3 draws.
+
+Confirmed dominant: Bottled Lightning on Seek+ guaranteed Echo Form+ in hand Turn 1 of every boss fight in the first Defect victory. This single relic-card combination was the foundation of the winning engine.
heuristics/relics/bottled-tornado.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Bottled Tornado
+
+Guarantees a specific Power from Turn 1 of every fight. The value depends entirely on which Power is bottled.
+
+BEST TARGETS (Silent):
+1. **After Image** (victory-proven) -- 1 block per card played from Turn 1.
+2. **Noxious Fumes+** -- Passive poison from Turn 1.
+3. **Footwork+** -- +3 Dex from Turn 1.
+
+BEST TARGETS (Ironclad):
+1. **Barricade** -- Block persists from Turn 1. Excellent with Corruption engine.
+2. **Metallicize** -- Passive block every turn from Turn 1.
+3. **Inflame** -- +2 Str from Turn 1.
+
+High draft priority when you have a Power that you always want Turn 1. After Image innate is game-defining; a situational Power is less impactful.
heuristics/relics/brimstone.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Brimstone
+
+Double-edged scaling relic. Your damage scales fast but enemies become devastating in long fights. Turns every fight into a race.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE: Deck has burst damage and can end fights in 4-5 turns. Pairs well with Weak application (Bash+, Thunderclap, Shockwave) to offset enemy Strength gains.
+
+**PURCHASE CHECKLIST:**
+1. Is Book of Stabbing a possible Act 2 elite? If yes, DO NOT BUY. Brimstone + Book of Stabbing is unsurvivable (confirmed kills in Run 091, Run 100).
+2. Can your deck end fights in 4-5 turns? If no, enemy Str gain will overwhelm you.
+3. Do you have Weak application? Weak offsets enemy Str gains.
+4. In Act 2 already? Evaluate remaining enemy pool before buying.
+
+SYNERGIES: Demon Form (+4 Str/turn combined), Reaper (heals more as Strength grows), Heavy Blade (3x/5x multiplied), Pummel/multi-hit (Strength added per hit), Limit Break (doubles accumulated Strength).
+
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Slow decks, long fights, enemies with multi-hit attacks (Book of Stabbing, Snake Plant).
+
+CRITICAL DANGER -- BOOK OF STABBING: By turn 5, incoming is 102 damage vs 42 without Brimstone (143% increase). If you have Brimstone, AVOID Book of Stabbing entirely or kill it in 3 turns.
heuristics/relics/bronze-scales.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Bronze Scales
+
+Passive damage every fight requiring no energy, no card plays, no setup. Particularly strong in multi-hit attack fights where the attacker hits many times (5-hit multi-attacks = 15 free damage per turn).
+
+KEY INTERACTIONS:
+- **Donu and Deca**: Can deliver killing blows without requiring a card play (confirmed final 6 damage to Deca at 4 HP).
+- **Multi-hit enemies**: Centurion (10x3), Book of Stabbing (6xN), Gremlin Leader minions -- each hit triggers Bronze Scales.
+
+Excellent as a finishing tool when enemies are at low HP. Scales with fight length. No downside.
heuristics/relics/burning-blood.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Burning Blood
+
+At full HP, up to 6 damage is "free" in hallway fights because Burning Blood refunds it post-combat. This means you can trade HP for tempo (e.g., skip blocking to kill faster) when at max HP. **This buffer does NOT exist if below max HP** -- taking 6 damage at 70/80 HP means you end the fight at 70/80, not 76/80.
+
+At +6/fight, it cannot compensate for fights that drain 30-50 HP (Byrds, Centurion+Mystic). Do not rely on Burning Blood alone for Act 2 sustain -- you need Reaper, Feed, or rest sites.
heuristics/relics/busted-crown.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Busted Crown
+
+Going from 3E to 4E is transformative. Every turn plays an extra card. On Turn 1, enables Bash+ (2E) + Defend (1E) + another card (1E), impossible at 3E.
+
+DOWNSIDE: Card rewards reduced from 3 to 1. Makes finding specific cards (Reaper, Impervious) much harder.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE: When the deck's core cards are already acquired (by Act 1 boss reward). The extra energy helps play those cards more effectively.
+
+WHEN TO AVOID: If the deck is still missing critical cards (healing, block scaling) and needs card rewards to find them. If Snecko Eye is also being considered, Busted Crown loses value since Snecko provides its own draw advantage.
+
+The +1E is reliable every turn and immediately impactful. The card reward reduction is real but can be mitigated by shops and Dream Catcher.
heuristics/relics/calipers.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# Calipers
+
+Turns any large block turn into sustained defense. Eliminates the "wasted block" problem where overblocking a small attack loses the excess.
+
+MATH:
+- Impervious+ (40 block): 25 retained next turn.
+- Impervious (30 block): 15 retained next turn.
+- Shrug It Off + 2 Defends (18 block): 3 retained next turn.
+
+SYNERGIES: Impervious/Impervious+ (retains massive amounts), Metallicize (passive block stacks with retention). Strongest in fights with alternating high/low damage turns where you can stack block on low turns and retain for high turns.
+
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Barricade (already retains all block -- Calipers redundant). Decks generating exactly 5-15 block per turn see no benefit (all decays away).
+
+Best in decks with Impervious or other large block sources. Does NOT replace the need for healing.
heuristics/relics/centennial-puzzle.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Centennial Puzzle
+
+Effectively gives an 8-card hand on the turn you first take damage. Extremely strong for setting up big combo turns.
+
+STRATEGY: On turn 1, if enemies are attacking, you can intentionally under-block to take a small hit and trigger the draw. In multi-turn fights, the 3 extra cards often let you play a devastating setup turn (Power + burst damage + block all in one turn). Against enemies that buff turn 1 (no damage), the trigger happens on turn 2 instead.
+
+SYNERGIES: Fiend Fire (larger hand = more damage), Metallicize (small chip damage still triggers draw), multi-enemy first attacks.
+
+ANTI-SYNERGY: Full block strategies that prevent all damage delay the trigger. Torii reduces damage to 1 but still triggers the draw since HP is still lost.
heuristics/relics/ceramic-fish.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Ceramic Fish
+
+Minor gold value. Over a typical run with 8-12 card additions, generates 72-108 extra gold. Useful for funding shop purchases (card removal, relics, potions). Low-impact relic -- take it when offered free (treasure chests) but do not prioritize.
heuristics/relics/champion-belt.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Champion Belt
+
+Turns Bash+ into a dual-debuff card (3 Vulnerable + 1 Weak) for free. Every Bash+ play both increases your damage AND reduces incoming damage simultaneously.
+
+SYNERGIES: Bash+ (main trigger), Thunderclap (mass Vuln = mass Weak to all enemies), Paper Phrog (75% Vuln + Weak = massive amplification with damage reduction).
+
+Excellent defensive relic requiring no deck changes. Since Bash+ is played in nearly every combat, provides passive Weak application for free. Reduces the need for dedicated Weak sources (Intimidate, Shockwave, Clothesline).
heuristics/relics/charon-s-ashes.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Charon's Ashes
+
+Creates an exhaust damage engine. With True Grit (exhaust per turn) and Dark Embrace (draw on exhaust), every exhaust = 3 AOE damage + draw. Over 14 turns: ~30+ total damage.
+
+DANGER: encourages over-exhausting, which can thin the deck below survival threshold. Don't exhaust cards just for 3 damage -- consider whether you need the cards for later turns.
heuristics/relics/coffee-dripper.md
@@ -0,0 +1,16 @@
+# Coffee Dripper
+
+Strong energy boost but removes the primary healing mechanism. Rest sites become upgrade-only, leaving potions, events, healing cards, and relics as remaining healing sources.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE:
+- Deck nearly complete and rest sites would be used for upgrades anyway.
+- Multiple healing sources exist (healing cards, potions, relics).
+- Energy-hungry deck benefits greatly from extra energy.
+- Ironclad with Burning Blood providing baseline healing per fight.
+
+WHEN TO AVOID:
+- Early Act 1 when HP management is uncertain.
+- No healing sources beyond rest sites.
+- Silent without healing cards or potions (no passive heal equivalent to Burning Blood).
+
+CRITICAL ANTI-SYNERGY -- COFFEE DRIPPER + ECTOPLASM: No healing from rest sites AND no gold to buy potions/healing at shops. Eliminates two of three primary recovery paths. Avoid this combination unless the deck reliably takes zero damage.
heuristics/relics/cracked-core.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Cracked Core
+
+Provides a guaranteed Lightning orb from Turn 1 of every fight without spending a card play or energy. The Lightning orb deals 3 passive damage at end of turn. Combined with Zap (0E, channel another), Defect can have 2 Lightning orbs dealing 6 passive damage per turn from Turn 1.
+
+Also means Dualcast always has a target on Turn 1 (16 burst damage if used immediately). If swapped for a boss relic, Dualcast is dead Turn 1 unless another orb is channeled first.
heuristics/relics/cursed-key.md
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+# Cursed Key
+
+Strong when the deck can handle curse dilution. +1 Energy is transformative (4E to 5E enables Barricade/Corruption + block in one turn). The curse downside is manageable: only triggers on chests (optional), curses can be removed at shops or via exhaust cards.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE:
+- Deck has exhaust tools (Second Wind+, Corruption).
+- Deck needs energy for 3E Powers (Barricade, Corruption, Demon Form).
+- Chest rooms are avoidable on the current map path.
+
+NORMALITY CURSE WARNING: The Normality curse (limit 3 card plays/turn) is the most dangerous curse from Cursed Key. Cripples Barricade + Corruption engine by limiting skill exhaust chains. Remove at first shop opportunity.
+
+SYNERGY: Omamori (negates 2 curses from chests), exhaust engines (Corruption makes curse removal trivial).
heuristics/relics/data-disk.md
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+# Data Disk
+
+Focus is the Defect's primary scaling stat. Each point adds 1 to every orb passive (Frost block, Lightning damage, Dark scaling). With 3 Frost orbs: +3 block per turn passively. Over a 10-turn fight: 30+ block from a single relic.
+
+KEY SYNERGIES:
+- **Frost orbs**: +1 passive block per orb per turn. With 5 Frost: +5 passive block/turn.
+- **Defragment**: Stacks for total Focus 2+ making orb passives immediately impactful.
+- **Loop/Emotion Chip**: Focus bonus applies to all passive triggers.
+
+Provides baseline Focus that makes Frost orb passives meaningful from Turn 1, before additional Focus scaling is established.
heuristics/relics/dead-branch.md
@@ -0,0 +1,15 @@
+# Dead Branch
+
+Transforms exhaust from a resource-spending mechanic into a resource-generating one. The more cards exhausted per fight, the more value generated.
+
+**CORRUPTION SYNERGY (strongest combo in the game):** With Corruption, every Skill costs 0E and exhausts, and Dead Branch replaces it with a random card. Hand never empties, cycling enormous numbers of cards per turn. Any generated block/damage cards are effectively free. This engine wins any fight it survives to activate.
+
+SETUP RISK: Corruption (3E unupgraded) + Dead Branch requires surviving the setup turn with no immediate block.
+
+**FEEL NO PAIN SYNERGY:** Every exhaust triggers both Dead Branch (new card) AND Feel No Pain (3 block). Provides defensive sustain during engine turns.
+
+**SILENT EXHAUST SYNERGY:** Shivs exhaust after play -- each triggers Dead Branch. Storm of Steel -> 5 Shivs -> 5 Dead Branch procs = massive card generation. Terror, Endless Agony, Adrenaline all provide repeatable triggers.
+
+When you have Dead Branch, prioritize cards that exhaust (Corruption, Second Wind, True Grit+, Fiend Fire). Corruption becomes the highest-priority card to acquire.
+
+Always a strong relic pick. Universally strong with no significant anti-synergies.
heuristics/relics/dream-catcher.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Dream Catcher
+
+Turns resting into resting + card reward. Reduces the opportunity cost of choosing Rest over Smith. Excellent when forced to rest at low HP -- you still get deck-building value. Changes rest site calculus: resting is now less punishing.
heuristics/relics/ectoplasm.md
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+# Ectoplasm
+
+Strong when gold is already abundant or irrelevant. +1 Energy/turn is transformative for energy-hungry decks. The gold loss is permanent -- no shops, no card removal, no potion purchases for the rest of the run.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE:
+- Deck is already mostly built and does not need shop purchases.
+- No other boss relic is better for the deck.
+
+WHEN TO AVOID:
+- Early in Act 1 when shops are still critical for deck building.
+- Card removal needed (Strikes/Defends polluting deck).
+- Deck is energy-efficient (3E is enough).
+
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Maw Bank, Ceramic Fish, Golden Idol, Bloody Idol (all negated). Shops in general.
+
+**CRITICAL -- Coffee Dripper + Ectoplasm**: No healing from rest AND no gold for potions/healing at shops. Eliminates two of three primary recovery paths. Confirmed lethal. Avoid unless deck reliably takes zero damage.
+
+Spend all gold BEFORE taking Ectoplasm if possible. Boss relics are offered after the boss fight, so a prior shop is the last chance.
heuristics/relics/emotion-chip.md
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+# Emotion Chip
+
+Effectively doubles orb passive output on turns after taking damage.
+
+KEY INTERACTIONS:
+- **Frost orbs + Focus**: With 5 Frost orbs at Focus 3, Emotion Chip adds 25 block at start of turn (5 x 5). Stacks with normal 25 at end of turn for 50 passive block per cycle.
+- **Lightning orbs**: Double passive damage. Less impactful but meaningful in multi-enemy fights.
+- **Dark orbs**: Triggers passive gain twice, accelerating scaling.
+
+Strongest in Frost-heavy builds with Focus scaling. Rewards taking chip damage (often unavoidable) by converting it into defensive value the following turn.
+
+LIMITATION: Only triggers if HP was lost previous turn. In fights with zero damage taken (full block discipline), provides no value. It is a recovery tool, not a proactive one.
heuristics/relics/enchiridion.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Enchiridion
+
+Free Power every fight. Can generate Metallicize, Inflame, Corruption, Dark Embrace, or other Powers. The 0 cost means it never competes with other turn 1 plays. High variance -- sometimes generates a duplicate of a Power you already have. Overall excellent value for free.
+
+DANGER -- UNCONTROLLABLE POWER SELECTION: The Power is random. Enchiridion can generate Brutality (lose 1 HP/turn), which is actively harmful in long fights or at low HP. Before playing an Enchiridion-generated Power, evaluate whether it helps or hurts in THIS specific fight. Do not autopilot "free Power = always play."
+
+DANGEROUS POWERS: Brutality (HP drain in long fights), Berserk (Vulnerable to self), Combust (HP drain per turn). Safe powers: Metallicize, Inflame, Dark Embrace, Feel No Pain, Rage.
heuristics/relics/eternal-feather.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Eternal Feather
+
+Supplements Burning Blood's flat 6 HP/fight. At 15 cards: +9 HP/fight (total 15 with Burning Blood). At 20 cards: +12 HP/fight. At 25 cards: +15 HP/fight. Rewards larger decks, which conflicts with typical deck-thinning strategy.
+
+Do not take extra cards just to increase Eternal Feather healing -- deck quality matters more than marginal HP gains. In a typical 15-20 card deck, the healing is modest (9-12 HP). Only valuable when the run already has a larger deck and no better relic options. Mediocre overall.
heuristics/relics/frozen-egg.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Frozen Egg
+
+Strong in decks that plan to take more Powers. Saves rest site upgrades for non-Power cards. Ironclad Powers include Metallicize, Inflame, Evolve, Barricade, Demon Form, Corruption, Dark Embrace, Fire Breathing -- many are high-priority upgrades.
+
+BEST SYNERGIES:
+- **Mid-run Power pickup**: Getting Frozen Egg before taking Metallicize means Metallicize+ (4 block/turn) for free.
+- **Barricade+**: 2E instead of 3E is a huge upgrade saved for free.
+- **Evolve+**: Draw 2 on Status instead of 1. Major power spike.
+- **Inflame+**: +3 Str instead of +2 for free.
heuristics/relics/fusion-hammer.md
@@ -0,0 +1,19 @@
+# Fusion Hammer
+
+Excellent when the deck is already well-upgraded. +1 Energy/turn is transformative. Rest sites become heal-only (and Ancient Tea Set always triggers).
+
+WHEN TO TAKE:
+- Most key cards already upgraded (Bash+, core block/damage cards).
+- Deck needs energy more than upgrades.
+- Blessing of the Forge potion available (one-time mass upgrade substitute).
+- Ancient Tea Set equipped (always triggers since you must rest).
+
+WHEN TO AVOID:
+- Critical cards still unupgraded (Bash, Corruption, True Grit, Limit Break).
+- Deck recently assembled and needs 3+ upgrades.
+
+SYNERGIES:
+- Ancient Tea Set (always triggers since you can only rest, not smith).
+- Armaments+ (upgrade ALL cards in hand, substitutes for smith in-combat).
+- Regal Pillow (extra healing since you always rest).
+- **Apotheosis** (becomes the single most valuable card -- provides the ONLY way to upgrade cards). Actively seek Apotheosis from colorless events when Fusion Hammer is equipped.
heuristics/relics/gambling-chip.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# Gambling Chip
+
+The mulligan effect lets you find key cards (Bash+, Inflame+, Shockwave, Whirlwind) on Turn 1. Consistently useful in every combat.
+
+FIGHT-SPECIFIC MULLIGANS:
+- **Gremlin Leader**: Discard block, keep attacks. Turn economy demands damage from Turn 1.
+- **Spheric Guardian**: Discard block, keep Artifact strippers and attacks. DPS check.
+- **Hallway fights**: Discard expensive Powers, keep Bash+ and cheap attacks.
+- **Chosen/Hex**: Discard Skills (trigger Hex adding Dazed). Keep Attacks and Powers.
+- **Boss fights**: Discard based on boss pattern.
+
+SYNERGIES: Lean decks (15-17 cards, mulligan more likely to find specific answers). Cards with fight-specific value can be safely included because they can be discarded when not needed.
+
+**SNECKO EYE SYNERGY**: When Snecko assigns expensive costs (3E) to key cards, Gambling Chip lets you discard and redraw with new cost rolls. Dramatically improves Turn 1 consistency.
heuristics/relics/ginger.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# Ginger
+
+Immunity to an entire debuff type is always valuable.
+
+KEY INTERACTIONS:
+1. **Mind Bloom "I am Healthy" becomes free.** The Doubt curse (1 Weak/turn) is completely harmless with Ginger. Choose it for a free full heal, then remove Doubt at the next shop.
+2. **Enemy Weak applications ignored.** Chosen's Hex, Collector's debuff, Time Eater's Ripple -- no effect on damage output.
+3. **Block calculations simplified.** Damage output is always at full value.
+
+SYNERGIES:
+- Shiv decks benefit most since Weak would reduce every individual Shiv hit.
+- Boss fights with Weak application (Collector, Time Eater) become safer offensively.
+
+High draft priority. The Mind Bloom interaction alone can provide 15-30 HP of free healing.
heuristics/relics/golden-idol.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Golden Idol
+
+More gold over the run means more shop purchases (card removal, relics, key cards). The value compounds over many fights. Worth the acquisition cost if you can afford the HP/Max HP loss.
heuristics/relics/gremlin-horn.md
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+# Gremlin Horn
+
+Transforms multi-enemy fights from resource-draining attrition into snowball opportunities. Against 4 Gremlins, killing 3 provides +3E and +3 cards -- enough for an entire extra turn's worth of actions.
+
+SYNERGIES:
+- **AOE damage** (Thunderclap, Cleave, Whirlwind) -- weakens multiple enemies, triggering chain kills.
+- **Slime Boss split** -- kills during the split phase refund energy for handling new slimes.
+- **Any multi-enemy fight** -- 3 Cultists, Gremlins, Slavers, Centurion+Mystic.
+
+Always take. No downside. In single-enemy fights it does nothing harmful. In multi-enemy fights (the most common dangerous Act 2 encounters), it is one of the strongest relics available.
heuristics/relics/happy-flower.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Happy Flower
+
+Passive energy gain in long fights. In a 9-turn fight: 3 extra energy total. Less impactful than Lantern but provides sustained value. Plan big plays on the turns when the extra energy arrives.
heuristics/relics/horn-cleat.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Horn Cleat
+
+Provides free 14 block on turn 2, which is often the first big attack turn from enemies. Effectively a free Defend + Shrug It Off on the turn you need it most. No action required -- automatic.
+
+14 block covers most single-enemy attacks in Act 1 and partially mitigates Act 2 attacks. Frees up energy on turn 2 to play offensive cards instead of blocking. Helpful against Cultists (buff turn 1, attack turn 2 -- Horn Cleat covers the attack).
heuristics/relics/hovering-kite.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Hovering Kite
+
+Effectively raises base energy from 3 to 4 (or 4 to 5) when combined with any discard source.
+
+KEY SYNERGIES:
+- **Tools of the Trade**: Discards at start of turn, triggering Kite automatically. +1E every turn with zero effort.
+- **Survivor/Survivor+**: Manual discard trigger. Block + energy in one play.
+- **Tactician+**: When discarded, returns 2E. Combined with Kite's +1E, a single TotT discard of Tactician+ generates +3E total.
+- **Calculated Gamble**: Discard entire hand, Kite triggers on first discard.
+
+Excellent when the deck has discard synergy (Tools of the Trade, Survivor+, Tactician+). Also useful for discarding Burns and curses -- turning dead cards into energy.
+
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Decks without discard sources get inconsistent value. Snecko Eye randomizes costs, making +1E less impactful when cards already cost 0.
heuristics/relics/incense-burner.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Incense Burner
+
+The Intangible turn is a free turn for maximum offense -- play all attacks, use no block. Track the counter to plan around it. Against bosses with predictable big-hit turns (Hexaghost Inferno, Bronze Automaton Hyper Beam), Intangible can negate the most dangerous attack entirely.
+
+Best in long fights (bosses, elites) where the counter triggers 2+ times. A 12-turn fight gets 2 Intangible turns. Combined with scaling (Rampage, Demon Form), Intangible turns enable all-offense plays that accelerate the kill.
+
+MATCHUPS: Excellent vs Hexaghost (Intangible on Inferno = 42 damage reduced to 1). Excellent vs Bronze Automaton (Hyper Beam = 45-57 reduced to 1).
heuristics/relics/juzu-bracelet.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Juzu Bracelet
+
+Extremely high value. Unknown rooms become free value (events) instead of potential death traps. Fundamentally changes Act 2 routing -- Unknown rooms become preferable to Monster rooms.
+
+In Act 2, Unknown rooms can spawn Spheric Guardian (30-41 HP drain), Byrds (36-73 HP drain), or Snake Plant (15-42 HP drain). Juzu Bracelet eliminates this risk entirely.
+
+Worth buying at ~147 gold if gold allows. Route through Unknown rooms for guaranteed safe rooms.
heuristics/relics/kunai.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Kunai
+
+Passive Dexterity scaling from playing Attacks. In long fights (bosses, elites), compounds: by turn 5-6, block cards generate 3-5 more block each, drastically improving survivability.
+
+Best in Attack-heavy decks with cheap Attacks (Anger at 0E, Pommel Strike, Twin Strike, Headbutt). Pairs naturally with Ironclad's Attack-heavy card pool. Purely upside with no drawback -- always take when offered.
+
+SYNERGY: Shuriken (same trigger -- playing 3 Attacks gives +1 Dex AND +1 Str if both relics held).
heuristics/relics/lantern.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Lantern
+
+Enables stronger openers. Play a setup Power AND still have energy for damage/block on turn 1. Weaker than Ancient Tea Set (+2E but only after rest sites) but more consistent. Use the extra energy on turn 1 -- don't waste it.
heuristics/relics/lees-waffle.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Lee's Waffle
+
+Immediate full heal plus permanent Max HP increase. At any HP percentage, this restores you to maximum. The +7 Max HP also raises all HP thresholds, giving more buffer for future fights.
+
+One of the strongest shop purchases in the game. Combines immediate value of a full heal with permanent Max HP scaling. Buy it whenever affordable and HP is below 70%. At 157g it is expensive, but preventing a death from low HP is worth the entire gold reserve.
heuristics/relics/letter-opener.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Letter Opener
+
+Turns every Defend into Defend + 5 damage. In fights where you play 2-3 Skills per turn, adds 10-15 free damage. Over a 12-turn fight: ~40-120 free damage. Conflicts with Gremlin Nob (can't play Skills) and Chosen (Skills trigger Hex). Synergizes with Intimidate (0E Skill = free 5 damage + Weak).
heuristics/relics/liquid-bronze.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Liquid Bronze
+
+Passive retaliation. Against multi-hit attacks (Hexaghost 7x6): 7*3 = 21 thorns damage from one Inferno. Against multiple attackers: 3 damage to each. However, damage reduction (Weak) is more important than damage reflection. Don't rely on thorns as a primary damage source.
heuristics/relics/lizard-tail.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Lizard Tail
+
+A safety net that heals significantly more than Fairy in a Bottle (50% Max HP vs ~30%). Does not take a potion slot. Once triggered, gone for the rest of the run.
+
+Can be deliberately "spent" by accepting lethal damage on a turn where you maximize damage output instead of blocking, knowing the revival will sustain you for killing turns. Not just a safety net -- it can be a strategic resource in DPS-race fights.
+
+Confirmed victory-enabling: revived from 0 HP to 35 HP during Time Eater fight, enabling 2 more turns of burst that killed the boss from 139 HP.
heuristics/relics/mark-of-pain.md
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+# Mark of Pain
+
+Strong boss relic. 4E per turn is a massive upgrade. The Wound downside is manageable and can become an advantage with the right cards.
+
+SYNERGIES:
+- **Evolve+ (BEST)**: Each Wound drawn triggers 2 additional draws. Playing attacks generates future card advantage. If Evolve+ is already in deck, Mark of Pain is the clear best boss relic choice.
+- **Evolve (unupgraded)**: Each Wound triggers 1 draw. Upgrade Evolve ASAP after taking Mark of Pain.
+- **Fire Breathing**: Wounds trigger Fire Breathing damage to all enemies.
+- **Second Wind**: Exhausts Wounds for 5 block each.
+- **True Grit+**: Chosen exhaust can target Wounds specifically.
+
+ANTI-SYNERGIES:
+- **No Evolve/Status management**: Wounds clog draw pile. By turn 4-5, drawing 2-3 Wounds/hand reduces effective hand to 2-3 real cards.
+- **Battle Trance**: "Cannot draw additional cards" prevents Evolve triggering on Wound draws that turn.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE: When Evolve is already in deck. A 15+ card deck dilutes Wounds better than a 10-card deck.
+
+WHEN TO AVOID: No Wound management (no Evolve, Fire Breathing, Second Wind, True Grit+) and small deck (under 12 cards).
heuristics/relics/matryoshka.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Matryoshka
+
+Pure value relic with no downside. Doubles relic rewards from the next two treasure chests. Makes treasure room pathing slightly more attractive -- if choosing between a path with a chest and one without, the chest path gains extra value. No gameplay impact during combat.
heuristics/relics/maw-bank.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Maw Bank
+
+Generates significant gold over time (12 gold x 10 floors = 120 gold). Best taken early for maximum accumulation. Consider skipping shops or only using shop card removal to preserve the gold generation. Most valuable when the run does not need shop purchases urgently.
heuristics/relics/meal-ticket.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Meal Ticket
+
+Makes shop visits double as healing stops. At low HP, pathing through shops provides 15 HP recovery in addition to purchasing options. More healing than Burning Blood (6 HP) and stacks with it. Prioritize shop paths on the map when carrying Meal Ticket and low on HP.
+
+15 HP per shop is significant -- roughly one fight's worth of damage absorption. In Act 2 where HP drain is the primary cause of death, Meal Ticket can extend a run by several floors if the map has shop nodes available.
+
+LIMITATION: Only triggers at shops (1-2 per act). Cannot be relied upon as a primary healing source, but excellent supplemental healing alongside Burning Blood.
heuristics/relics/meat-on-the-bone.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Meat on the Bone
+
+In Act 2, fights routinely drain 30-50 HP and leave the player below 50%. Meat on the Bone provides double the healing of Burning Blood (12 vs 6) when it triggers. Combined with Burning Blood, a fight ending below 50% heals 18 HP total.
+
+Does not trigger when HP is above 50% at fight end. Efficient fights where block prevents most damage get zero value. Does not help DURING combat -- only after.
+
+Partially addresses the Act 2 HP sustain problem. Not a replacement for in-combat healing (Reaper, Feed) but a solid supplementary source. Always take when offered -- unconditional upside with no drawback.
heuristics/relics/mercury-hourglass.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Mercury Hourglass
+
+Free passive AOE damage every turn. Over a 10-turn boss fight: 30 free damage to every enemy. Against multi-enemy fights (Collector + 2 Torch Heads): 3 to each = 9 total per turn.
+
+Best in long fights and multi-enemy encounters. Vulnerable on enemies amplifies it (3 * 1.5 = 4 per enemy). Can finish low-HP enemies at start of turn without triggering additional effects (killed Snake Plant without triggering Malleable block).
heuristics/relics/molten-egg.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Molten Egg
+
+Massive value if obtained early (Acts 1-2). Every Attack card from rewards, events, and transforms is automatically upgraded. Reduces the need to spend rest site upgrades on Attacks. Prioritize picking Attack cards when this relic is active. Weaker if obtained late when most Attacks are already picked/upgraded.
heuristics/relics/mummified-hand.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# Mummified Hand
+
+Excellent in Power-heavy decks. Each Power play enables a free card -- effectively +1 energy per Power. In a deck with 4-5 Powers, generates 4-5 free plays per combat.
+
+SYNERGIES:
+- **Multiple Powers**: More Powers = more free plays.
+- **Barricade + Metallicize setup turns**: Playing Barricade triggers a free Defend+ or Strike+ on the setup turn. Partially offsets Barricade's cost.
+- **Immolate+**: A free Immolate+ is 28-31 damage for zero energy.
+
+DEFECT SYNERGY: Excellent Power density (Echo Form, Loop, Capacitor, Defragment, Creative AI). Mummified Hand + Echo Form + Loop generates multiple free plays during setup, offsetting Echo Form's 3E cost.
+
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Decks with 0-1 Powers (trigger too rare). Corruption (Skills already cost 0, so free card is less impactful if it hits a Skill).
+
+Scales in value as more Powers are added. Confirmed dominant across both Ironclad and Defect Power-heavy archetypes.
heuristics/relics/mutagenic-strength.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Mutagenic Strength
+
+Provides a powerful turn-1 burst window. With +3 Strength, the combined effect across multiple cards on turn 1 can add 10-20 extra damage. After turn 1, the Strength disappears and has no further effect.
+
+Front-load damage on turn 1. Play as many Attack cards as possible before the Strength expires. Pairs especially well with AOE attacks (Immolate, Thunderclap) where the bonus applies to each enemy hit. The free relic is always worth taking from the event.
heuristics/relics/necronomicon.md
@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+# Necronomicon
+
+Doubles the first expensive Attack each turn for free. Effectively doubles damage AND secondary effects (orb channels, debuffs, draws).
+
+KEY INTERACTIONS:
+- **Barrage (2E base)**: Always triggers. With 5 orbs: 40 damage total (50 upgraded). Highest single-card damage output.
+- **Cold Snap (when 2+ cost)**: Plays twice = 12 damage + channels 2 Frost orbs.
+- **Any Attack with secondary effects**: Full replay including draw/channel/debuff.
+
+SNECKO EYE SYNERGY: Cards normally costing 0-1 can roll 2+, triggering Necronomicon. With 7 cards drawn, at least one Attack costing 2+ is very likely. Dramatically increases trigger frequency.
+
+NECRONOMICURSE: Unplayable hand clog, cannot be removed at shops. In Snecko Eye deck (7 draws), minor nuisance. In standard 5-draw, more impactful. Keep deck lean.
+
+DECISION: Take when deck has multiple 2+ cost Attacks, or Snecko Eye is active. Plan turns so highest-value Attack is played first to receive the doubling.
heuristics/relics/neow-s-lament.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Neow's Lament
+
+One of the best Neow offerings. 3 free fights = 3 card rewards + 3 gold drops + potential potions + zero HP lost. Equivalent to ~50 gold and 3 card choices with no risk. Take this whenever offered.
+
+PATHING TRAP: Lament preserves full HP through 3 fights, which creates a false sense of elite readiness. After 3 free fights, the deck may only have 1-2 card rewards added to the starter core. High HP does NOT mean the deck is ready for an elite. Sentries at Floor 5 with a near-starter deck is a confirmed death trap. When routing with Lament, ensure the card rewards from the 3 free fights actually improve the deck enough for the elite, or delay the elite until more cards are acquired.
heuristics/relics/nilrys-codex.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Nilry's Codex
+
+Provides a free card every turn, excellent in long fights where hand quality matters. Generated cards include powerful options like Impervious and Immolate from any class pool. In a boss fight lasting 8+ turns: 8+ free cards, some game-changing.
+
+High value in long fights (bosses, elites). The card generation compensates for thin decks or decks lacking specific answers. Lower value in short hallway fights (2-3 turns, only 2-3 extra cards).
heuristics/relics/nunchaku.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Nunchaku
+
+In Attack-heavy decks, provides a reliable energy boost every few turns. With Snecko Eye drawing 7 cards/turn and 4-5 being Attacks, triggers every 2-3 turns. The bonus energy enables playing one more card on the trigger turn.
+
+SYNERGIES: Attack-heavy decks (Anger adds copies, increasing density), Snecko Eye (more cards drawn = more Attacks played). Multi-hit attacks like Pummel count as one play toward the counter, not per hit.
+
+Solid common relic. Not flashy but reliable. Counter persists across combats.
heuristics/relics/odd-mushroom.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Odd Mushroom
+
+DOUBLE-EDGED relic. Defensively, reduces damage taken when you are Vulnerable. Offensively, REDUCES your damage output by ~17% against Vulnerable targets.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE: Good if your deck relies on block/defense and rarely applies Vulnerable offensively. BAD if your deck relies on Bash+/Thunderclap+ Vulnerable for burst damage (most Ironclad decks). Consider taking the 22 HP heal (Eat option) from the Mushroom event instead if your deck is Vulnerable-dependent.
+
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Bash+ (Vuln 3 is your primary damage amp), Thunderclap+ (mass Vuln for burst), any DPS-race fight (Bronze Automaton, Book of Stabbing), Hexaghost (the ~17% damage reduction extends the fight by 1-2 turns, adding more Burns -- confirmed lethal).
heuristics/relics/oddly-smooth-stone.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Oddly Smooth Stone
+
+Permanent +1 block on every block card played. Defend generates 6 block instead of 5. Shrug It Off generates 9 instead of 8. Compounds with multiple block cards per turn. Over a long fight: 10-20 extra block total.
+
+Solid common relic with no downside. Less impactful than Vajra (+1 Strength) for Ironclad because Ironclad's win condition is typically killing fast, not blocking more. But in Act 2 where chip damage accumulates, the extra block helps reduce HP drain between rest sites.
heuristics/relics/omamori.md
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+# Omamori
+
+Strong defensive relic. Curses are permanent deck pollution that clog draws. Omamori prevents 2 curses from ever entering the deck. Enables safe event choices that would otherwise add curses.
+
+WHEN TO USE:
+- Before curse-giving events (open Coffin for free relic at zero cost).
+- General insurance against random curse events in Unknown rooms.
+- Note: Bites (Vampires event) are NOT Curses -- Omamori does not help there.
+
+Confirmed: Used to safely open a Coffin event for a free relic (Ceramic Fish). The curse was negated, gaining a relic at zero cost.
heuristics/relics/ornamental-fan.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Ornamental Fan
+
+Moderate value. Provides passive block in attack-heavy turns without costing energy. Works well in decks that naturally play 3+ attacks per turn.
+
+Less useful in Corruption engine decks where most plays are Skills (which exhaust for block via Feel No Pain instead). The 4 block per 3 attacks is small relative to 30-40 block from Impervious or FNP chains.
+
+SYNERGY: High-attack decks, Snecko Eye (many cheap attacks), Anger (0E attack helps reach 3 plays).
heuristics/relics/pandoras-box.md
@@ -0,0 +1,18 @@
+# Pandora's Box
+
+Extremely high variance. Removes 10 starter cards and replaces them with random class cards. Can produce game-winning decks or unplayable ones.
+
+CRITICAL RISK -- BLOCK LOSS: Removes ALL 4 Defend cards. If replacements contain zero block cards, the deck has NO defensive capability for Act 1. Confirmed death: offense-heavy result with zero block cards died on Floor 16 to Guardian.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE:
+- From Neow: HIGH RISK. Cannot undo. Only if comfortable gambling the run.
+- After Act 1: Less risky because deck already has non-starter cards unaffected.
+
+AFTER TAKING:
+- Immediately assess: does the new deck have ANY block source?
+- If zero block cards: EMERGENCY. Prioritize acquiring block at very next shop or card reward.
+- Iron Wave (1E, 5/5) is minimum viable. Flame Barrier (2E, 12 block) is better.
+
+DEFECT NOTE: Slightly safer than other classes because Defect starters (Zap, Dualcast) are weak, and random Defect pool includes many strong orb-channeling cards.
+
+A run-defining gamble. Prefer safer Neow options (100 Gold, Remove 2 Cards) unless no other path to a strong deck exists.
heuristics/relics/pantograph.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Pantograph
+
+Strong but not game-changing. 25 HP is significant but does not remove the need to manage HP before bosses. Upgrading at rest sites before bosses is still good, but only if the 25 HP heal brings you above a survivable threshold.
+
+CRITICAL CORRECTION: Pantograph heals exactly 25 HP, NOT to full. Plan boss HP thresholds accordingly -- you need to enter bosses at no more than 25 HP below max to benefit fully. Entering a boss at 30% HP with 87 Max HP means healing to ~54%, not full.
+
+With Sozu (no potions), Pantograph is still valuable as reliable healing, but it is NOT a full heal replacement.
heuristics/relics/paper-phrog.md
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+# Paper Phrog
+
+Massively amplifies all damage against Vulnerable targets. Bash+ becomes a much stronger setup card. Over a multi-turn Vulnerable window (Bash+ = 3 turns), adds up to massive extra damage.
+
+MATH (with Paper Phrog):
+- Strike (6) vs Vulnerable: 10 (vs 9 without)
+- Immolate+ (28) vs Vulnerable: 49 (vs 42 without)
+- Twin Strike (5+5) vs Vulnerable: 17 (vs 14 without)
+
+SYNERGIES: Bash+ (3 turns amplified Vulnerable), Thunderclap (mass Vulnerable), Bag of Marbles (free Turn 1 Vulnerable), any high-damage attack card.
+
+One of the best common relics for Ironclad. Since Bash+ applies Vulnerable in every fight, provides permanent damage amplification. Stronger than most uncommon relics.
heuristics/relics/pear.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Pear
+
+Always take. +10 Max HP is enormous -- equivalent to a free heal of 10 + a permanently larger HP buffer. One of the best common relics.
heuristics/relics/pen-nib.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Pen Nib
+
+Track the counter. Time the 10th Attack to coincide with high-damage cards (Fiend Fire, Carnage+, Headbutt+) or Vulnerable targets. Pen Nib + Strike = 12 damage. Pen Nib + Fiend Fire (4 cards) = 56 damage. Plan your Attacks across fights to line up the 10th hit on the right card.
heuristics/relics/potion-belt.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Potion Belt
+
+More potions means more options for boss fights and dangerous elites. Pairs with White Beast Statue (guaranteed potion drops fill more slots) and Toy Ornithopter (more potions = more healing). Stock up for bosses.
heuristics/relics/preserved-insect.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Preserved Insect
+
+Makes every elite fight significantly easier. 25% HP reduction means fewer turns needed to kill, which means fewer turns of incoming damage. Particularly strong against high-HP elites like Book of Stabbing and Gremlin Leader.
+
+Passive benefit with no downside. Always take when offered. The 25% reduction compounds with Vulnerable (50% more damage taken) -- an elite at 75% HP taking 50% more damage effectively has ~50% of its original effective HP.
heuristics/relics/pure-water.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Pure Water
+
+Effectively +1 energy on Turn 1 of every combat. The Miracle Retains, so it can be saved for a turn where the energy matters most (e.g., a burst turn exiting Calm into Wrath).
+
+INTERACTION WITH DEUS EX MACHINA: Generates 2-3 additional Miracles when drawn. Combined with Pure Water's Turn 1 Miracle, accumulate 3-4 Miracles. Managing these (not wasting on turns with no useful plays) is critical.
+
+BOSS RELIC SWAP: Losing guaranteed +1E per combat is a real cost, especially early when few energy-generating cards exist. Keep Pure Water unless the boss relic is clearly superior (Snecko Eye, Pandora's Box, etc.).
heuristics/relics/red-mask.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Red Mask
+
+Free damage reduction every fight with zero player input. 1 Weak = 25% less enemy damage on turn 1. Against multi-enemy fights (3 Cultists, Byrds), applies to every enemy simultaneously.
+
+**Artifact stripping**: Against enemies with Artifact (Bronze Automaton Artifact 3, Spheric Guardian Artifact 3), Red Mask strips 1 Artifact charge automatically before the player's first turn, reducing the stripping requirement by 1.
+
+Against enemies that buff turn 1 (Cultists, some Fungi Beasts), the Weak is wasted since they don't attack. Provides consistent incremental value across every single fight for the rest of the run.
heuristics/relics/regal-pillow.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Regal Pillow
+
+Turns rest sites from 24 HP healing to 39 HP healing. In Act 3 where HP management is critical, can restore nearly half of max HP at each rest site. Makes it safer to choose Rest over Smith when HP is below threshold.
+
+Strong utility relic that patches healing gaps. Enabled healing from 53 HP to 80/80 (full HP) at the pre-boss rest site in the victory run, ensuring 100% HP entry that contributed to the win.
heuristics/relics/runic-capacitor.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Runic Capacitor
+
+Doubles starting orb capacity. More slots means more simultaneous passive effects and dramatically higher Barrage damage.
+
+KEY SYNERGIES:
+- **Barrage+**: Each additional filled slot adds 7 damage. 3 extra slots = +21 Barrage+ damage.
+- **Capacitor (card)**: Stacks for even more slots. Runic Capacitor + Capacitor+ = 9 orb slots.
+- **Coolheaded+**: More slots means Frost orbs persist longer before being pushed out. Frost wall is more stable.
+- **Blizzard**: More Frost orbs channeled = higher Blizzard damage.
+
+With 6 slots filled with Frost orbs at Focus 2: 24 passive block/turn. Enables truly massive orb boards.
heuristics/relics/runic-dome.md
@@ -0,0 +1,9 @@
+# Runic Dome
+
+Almost never take. The +1 Energy is powerful, but losing intent information is devastating. Intent drives every decision: when to block, when to attack, when to play Powers, when to use potions.
+
+THE CRITICAL PROBLEM: Some boss/elite attacks are near-fatal if unblocked (Bronze Automaton Hyper Beam, Guardian 32-damage, Hexaghost Inferno 42 damage). Without intent, you cannot prepare. Two deaths resulted from taking Runic Dome without enemy pattern knowledge.
+
+PREREQUISITE: Requires memorized enemy attack patterns to use safely. Until all enemy patterns are known, skip it in favor of other boss relics.
+
+WHEN IT COULD WORK: A deck with heavy passive block (Metallicize, Barricade + block engine) that doesn't need to react to intents. Or once all enemy patterns are memorized and predictable from cycle position.
heuristics/relics/self-forming-clay.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Self-Forming Clay
+
+Moderate defensive relic. Provides 3 free block the turn after taking damage. Works as a small safety net when full block fails.
+
+Good in multi-turn fights where occasional chip damage is unavoidable. Provides incremental block that helps reach full block thresholds on subsequent turns (3 block from Clay + 2 Defends = 13 block instead of 10).
+
+Weak when you take zero damage consistently (never triggers) or when a single massive hit kills you (3 block next turn is meaningless if dead this turn).
heuristics/relics/shuriken.md
@@ -0,0 +1,11 @@
+# Shuriken
+
+Incentivizes playing 3+ Attacks per turn. Over a long fight, provides 3-5+ Strength passively -- comparable to Inflame but requires no card slot or energy. High pick priority in Attack-heavy decks.
+
+SYNERGIES:
+- **Anger**: 0-cost Attack that copies itself. Playing 3x Anger triggers Shuriken for free.
+- **Demon Form**: +2 Str/turn stacks with Shuriken's passive gains. By turn 5, total Strength can be +12-15 combined.
+- **Rage**: 0-cost Power that gives block per Attack. Every Attack-heavy turn generates both block and Strength.
+- **Multi-attack turns**: Thunderclap + Strike + Anger = 3 attacks = +1 Str. Very easy to trigger naturally.
+
+Excellent in long fights (bosses, elites) where 5-8 triggers accumulate to significant Strength.
heuristics/relics/slavers-collar.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Slaver's Collar
+
+The extra energy in elite and boss fights is significant -- it allows playing an additional card per turn in the hardest fights. A 4E turn means you can play Bash+ (2E) + Rampage (1E) + Defend (1E) instead of choosing between offense and defense.
+
+High value for elite-heavy paths. If your route includes 2-3 elites and the Act boss, the relic provides value in 3-4 of the hardest fights. Less valuable if avoiding elites.
+
+Pairs well with expensive cards (Bludgeon at 3E, Reaper at 2E) that normally crowd out other plays.
heuristics/relics/sling-of-courage.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Sling of Courage
+
+Free permanent Strength in the two most important fight types. With Heavy Blade (3x multiplier), +2 Str becomes +6 damage per play. With Heavy Blade+ (5x), it becomes +10. Compounds with Spot Weakness+ and Limit Break (doubles all Str including this bonus).
+
+Excellent shop purchase for Strength-scaling decks. Every point of base Strength has outsized value due to multiplicative scaling with Heavy Blade and Limit Break.
heuristics/relics/snecko-eye.md
@@ -0,0 +1,17 @@
+# Snecko Eye
+
+Drawing 7 cards per turn means reliably finding key combo pieces every turn. Cost randomization averages 1.5E/card, but high-cost cards (2E+) benefit enormously -- 25% chance of costing 0E each turn. Present in both consecutive Ironclad victories.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE: Deck has multiple 2E+ cards. The more expensive the average card cost, the better. A deck with Immolate+, Heavy Blade, Impervious, Flame Barrier, Bash+ has 5 cards that can become free.
+
+WHEN TO AVOID: Decks built around 0-cost cards (Anger, Rage) or X-cost cards (Whirlwind) get worse. 0-cost cards randomized to 1-3 makes them worse.
+
+KEY SYNERGIES:
+- **Gambling Chip**: Mulligan bad cost rolls for new ones. Dramatically improves Turn 1.
+- **Necronomicon**: Cards randomized to 2+ trigger doubling. Dramatically increases trigger frequency.
+- **Headbutt**: Top-decked cards get new cost rolls.
+- **Nunchaku**: 7 cards drawn = more Attacks played per turn.
+
+S-tier for Strength-scaling decks with expensive power cards. The extra draws solve consistency problems and cost randomization provides massive energy efficiency. Take as Act 2 boss relic whenever deck has high-cost cards.
+
+DEFECT VALUE: Strong because Defect's orb-based defense partially compensates for bad cost rolls on block cards (orbs generate block automatically without playing cards).
heuristics/relics/sozu.md
@@ -0,0 +1,13 @@
+# Sozu
+
+The extra energy (3 -> 4 per turn) is extremely powerful, but the potion lockout is a severe tradeoff. Losing ALL potion access means no safety net for bad draws and no supplemental healing.
+
+ANTI-SYNERGIES: Toy Ornithopter (completely dead), Potion Belt (worthless), White Beast Statue (worthless). Healing potions as sustain plan is unavailable -- healing cards (Reaper, Feed) become even MORE critical.
+
+SILENT-SPECIFIC DANGER (3 deaths confirmed): The Silent lacks innate healing and relies on potions as emergency saves. Pattern: Sozu removes the safety net that would have saved the run in a single critical turn. For the Silent, **strongly prefer other boss relics** unless the deck has Wraith Form+ and multiple block sources independent of potions.
+
+WHEN TO TAKE:
+- Skip all potion-related relics and events.
+- Conservative play is more important -- no emergency saves.
+- The extra energy partially compensates by enabling more block per turn.
+- Strong pick if confident in finding healing cards.
heuristics/relics/stone-calendar.md
@@ -0,0 +1,12 @@
+# Stone Calendar
+
+Free 52 AOE damage with no energy or card investment in long fights. In fights that end in 4-5 turns, the relic does nothing.
+
+KEY MATCHUPS:
+- **Centurion+Mystic**: Ideal -- fight naturally lasts 7+ turns while killing Mystic through block. 52 damage lands on Centurion.
+- **Boss fights**: Most last 10+ turns, so the nuke always fires. 52 free damage is significant.
+- **Shelled Parasite**: 52 damage overkills through remaining Plated Armor block.
+- **Spheric Guardian**: 52 damage into 65 block only removes block -- does NOT kill. Insufficient against high-block enemies.
+- **Short fights**: Useless against enemies that die in 3-5 turns.
+
+Strong in decks that expect long fights (defensive/block-heavy builds). Weak in burst-focused decks that aim to kill quickly.
heuristics/relics/strawberry.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Strawberry
+
+Always take. 7 Max HP is free permanent value -- equivalent to a free heal of 7 + larger HP buffer for the rest of the run.
heuristics/relics/sundial.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Sundial
+
+Strong in longer fights (7+ turns) where the deck shuffles multiple times. Pairs well with smaller decks (more frequent shuffles). The bonus 2 energy can enable full kill + block turns that would otherwise be impossible.
heuristics/relics/torii.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# Torii
+
+Exceptional against chip damage. Burns (2 damage each) become 1 damage. Multi-hit attacks where each hit is small (5 or less after block) are drastically reduced. Against Hexaghost, Torii halves Burns attrition. Against Sentries, small hits are reduced to 1. Does NOT help against large single hits (Guardian 32, Hexaghost Inferno hits at 6 each are already above 5 per hit without Weak).
heuristics/relics/toy-ornithopter.md
@@ -0,0 +1,7 @@
+# Toy Ornithopter
+
+Turns potion usage into healing. Each potion heals an extra 5 HP. With Potion Belt and aggressive potion acquisition, can heal 25-35 HP across a run.
+
+HEALING GAP SOLUTION: When Reaper and Feed are not offered, Toy Ornithopter + aggressive potion usage becomes the backup healing plan. Buy healing potions at shops, use liberally in tough fights. Three potion uses in a single boss fight = 15 HP healed, equivalent to a medium Reaper hit.
+
+ANTI-SYNERGY: Completely dead with Sozu (prevents obtaining potions). Do NOT take Toy Ornithopter if you have Sozu.
heuristics/relics/unceasing-top.md
@@ -0,0 +1,8 @@
+# Unceasing Top
+
+Creates a draw engine with any card that empties your hand. One of the strongest relics for exhaust-focused decks. Less useful in decks with large hands that never empty.
+
+KEY SYNERGIES:
+- **Fiend Fire** (CRITICAL): Exhausts hand -> empty -> draw -> play -> empty -> draw. Infinite draw engine limited only by energy and draw pile size.
+- **0-cost cards**: Bloodletting (gain 2 energy, hand empties), Rage (play for free, hand empties), Inflame/powers (play, hand empties) all trigger additional draws.
+- **Offering**: Lose 6 HP but gain 2 energy + draw 3. Combined with Top, massive turns.
heuristics/relics/vajra.md
@@ -0,0 +1,10 @@
+# Vajra
+
+Permanent, unconditional +1 damage to every Attack play for every fight. Modest on its own, but compounds with Strength-scaling cards and effects. Over a 5-turn fight playing 2 Attacks/turn: +10 total damage. Multi-hit cards benefit especially: Twin Strike gains +2 total, Pummel gains +4-5 total.
+
+SYNERGIES:
+- **Heavy Blade** -- +1 Str becomes +3 damage (or +5 upgraded) due to the multiplier.
+- **Limit Break** -- doubling Strength that starts at 1 instead of 0 is the difference between the card doing nothing and doing something.
+- **Reaper** -- more Strength = more damage to all enemies = more healing.
+
+Safe value from Neow or early chests. No downside. Particularly strong when building toward Strength scaling (Inflame, Limit Break, Heavy Blade).
heuristics/relics/whetstone.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Whetstone
+
+Equivalent to two rest site upgrades for Attacks specifically, but with no control over which cards are chosen. Best when the deck has multiple high-priority Attack upgrades (Heavy Blade 3x->5x, Carnage 20->28, Twin Strike, Pommel Strike). Worst when key Attacks are already upgraded or only Strikes remain.
+
+Strong early (more Attacks in deck = higher chance of hitting good targets, plus upgrades compound over more fights). Weaker late. Always acceptable as a free relic with no downside.
heuristics/relics/white-beast-statue.md
@@ -0,0 +1,3 @@
+# White Beast Statue
+
+Guarantees a steady potion supply. With full potion slots, every fight generates a potion. Extremely valuable for boss preparation -- enter with a full potion belt. Pairs well with Potion Belt (+2 slots) and Toy Ornithopter (+5 HP per potion used).
heuristics/relics/wing-boots.md
@@ -0,0 +1,5 @@
+# Wing Boots
+
+Useful for avoiding dangerous fights or reaching specific paths (shops, rest sites, elites) at low HP.
+
+Use sparingly -- only 3 charges for the entire run. Best saved for Act 2-3 where hallway fights are deadlier and pathing is more critical. Do not waste charges in Act 1 unless absolutely necessary for survival.
heuristics/strategy.md
@@ -0,0 +1,726 @@
+# Strategy
+
+High-level strategic principles across all characters.
+
+**CURRENT STATUS (176 runs, 6 WINS):** Three Ironclad victories (Runs 147, 148, 150). Two Silent victories (Run 158 vs Time Eater, Run 162 vs Awakened One). One Defect victory (Run 172 vs Time Eater -- FIRST DEFECT WIN). Eighteen Silent runs total (Runs 151-169). Four Defect runs (Run 170 died Floor 7, Run 171 died Floor 28, Run 172 VICTORY Floor 51). Four Watcher runs (Runs 174-177, all defeats -- 3 died to Hexaghost at Floor 16, 1 reached Floor 31). FIVE proven winning formulas now exist plus Watcher engine discovery in progress: (1) Ironclad Snecko Eye + Immolate+ + Limit Break, (2) Ironclad Barricade + Corruption + FNP + Entrench + Body Slam, (3) Silent Accuracy + After Image + Wraith Form+ + Phantasmal Killer + Shiv burst, (4) Silent Dead Branch + exhaust engine, and (5) Defect Echo Form+ + Seek+ + Frost/Barrage orb engine (Mummified Hand for tempo, Bottled Lightning for Seek+ consistency).
+
+**SCORECARD (runs 101-158):**
+- Act 1 boss wins: ~73%. All three Act 1 bosses beaten across runs.
+- Act 2 boss kills: 6. Act 2 boss reaches: 12+.
+- **Act 3 reached nine times (Runs 145, 147, 148, 149, 150, 151, 158, 162, 167).** Run 145 died to Transient. Run 149 died to Giant Head elite. Run 151 (Silent) died to Awakened One Phase 2 at Floor 50. Run 167 (Silent) died to Giant Head elite at Floor 40 -- deepest Silent elite reach in Act 3, GH survived at 1 HP with 2 unused potions. Runs 147, 148, 150 (Ironclad) and Runs 158, 162 (Silent) all WON.
+- **Best floor: 51 -- VICTORY (Runs 147, 148, 150, 158, 162 all reached Floor 51).**
+- **6 wins in 172 runs (3.5% win rate).** Ascension 0. Three Ironclad wins. Two Silent wins (18 attempts: Run 162 VICTORY vs Awakened One -- Dead Branch exhaust engine, Run 158 VICTORY vs Time Eater -- Accuracy Shiv burst, Run 168 died Floor 16 Hexaghost -- no poison scaling, Burns overwhelm in 16-turn fight, Run 167 died Floor 40 Giant Head elite -- GH survived at 1 HP with 2 unused potions, Run 151 reached Act 3 boss, Runs 152/153/156/157 reached Act 2 boss, Run 154 died Act 1 boss Floor 16, Run 155 died Act 2 elite Floor 23, Run 159 quick death, Run 160 died Floor 28 to Hex deck clog, Run 161 died Floor 22 to Unknown room combat at 21% HP, Run 163 died Floor 19 to Malaise X-cost energy trap at 19 HP, Run 164 died Floor 16 to Guardian -- early Wraith Form + unupgraded CaD, Run 165 died Floor 27 to Cultist+Chosen Hex at 9 HP, Run 166 died Floor 29 to Gremlin Leader at 4 HP -- strong poison engine but map forced brutal combat sequence with no rest).
+- **Run 162: Silent VICTORY Floor 51 vs Awakened One.** Dead Branch MVP. Phase 1 Power dump (all Powers played early, Curiosity Str resets on death). Phase 2 zero Powers -- Dead Branch + exhaust cards for infinite value. Lizard Tail consumed Phase 2 Turn 1 (41 dmg vs 20 block + 18 HP). Final HP 23/67.
+- **Run 172: Defect VICTORY Floor 51 vs Time Eater.** Echo Form+ MVP. Pandora's Box replaced all starters with Seek x2, Coolheaded, Chill, Overclock, Barrage, Melter, Sweeping Beam. Picked up Echo Form+ in Act 2, Mummified Hand for Power tempo, Bottled Lightning for Seek+. Beat The Guardian (Act 1), The Champ at 2 HP (Act 2), Time Eater in 9 turns (Act 3). Final HP 27/85. Lizard Tail consumed during Time Eater fight.
+- **Five distinct winning formulas confirmed.** See THE WINNING FORMULAS below.
+
+**THE WINNING FORMULAS (confirmed by 5 victories across 4 distinct engines):**
+
+**Formula 1 (Ironclad): Snecko Eye + Strength Scaling (Runs 147-148).** Snecko Eye (Act 2 boss relic, draw 7, cost randomization) + Immolate+ (AOE) + Limit Break (Str doubler) + Heavy Blade / high-Str finishers. Key components: Snecko Eye as Act 2 boss relic, Immolate+ as primary AOE, Limit Break for Strength scaling. Works by rolling cheap costs on expensive cards and doubling Strength for massive finisher damage.
+
+**Formula 2 (Ironclad): Barricade + Corruption Engine (Run 150).** Barricade (block persists) + Corruption (all Skills free but exhaust) + Feel No Pain (block per exhaust) + Entrench (double block) + Body Slam+ (damage = block). Key components: Barricade as the foundation (block persists between turns), Corruption making Skills free (and triggering FNP on exhaust), Entrench doubling accumulated block, Body Slam+ converting block to damage for 0E. This engine builds an impenetrable block wall (100-200+ block) while dealing massive damage through Body Slam+. Apotheosis is critical when Fusion Hammer prevents normal upgrades -- upgrading everything in one play (Barricade 3E to 2E, FNP 3 to 4, Impervious 30 to 40).
+
+**Formula 3 (Silent): Accuracy Shiv Burst (Run 158 -- FIRST SILENT WIN).** Accuracy x2 (+8 Shiv damage) + After Image (innate via Bottled Tornado, 1 block/card played) + Wraith Form+ (3 Intangible for emergency survival) + Phantasmal Killer (double damage burst turn) + Malaise (X Weak + X Str Down for boss debuff) + Blade Dance (3 Shivs for 1E) + Finisher (damage per attack played this turn). Key components: Accuracy stacking makes every 0-cost Shiv deal 12+ damage; After Image as innate guarantees block from Turn 1 of every fight; Wraith Form+ provides 3 turns of Intangible when HP drops to crisis levels; Phantasmal Killer doubles the next attack for massive Shiv burst turns; Malaise neuters boss offense for the final stretch. Cultist Potion (Ritual 1) provides permanent Str scaling that compounds with Accuracy -- by Turn 10, Shivs dealt 23 each (4 base + 8 Accuracy + 11 Str). This formula defeats Time Eater because each Shiv is a single card play but deals massive damage, making the 12-card Time Warp counter manageable (12 cards can output 200+ damage per cycle). Lizard Tail provided death insurance for the revival that enabled the final burst.
+
+**Formula 4 (Silent): Dead Branch Exhaust Engine (Run 162 -- SECOND SILENT WIN, vs Awakened One).** Dead Branch (relic: random card on exhaust) + exhaust-heavy Silent deck (Terror, Piercing Wail, Adrenaline, Storm of Steel, Endless Agony, Distraction, Crippling Cloud, Shivs) + After Image (1 block/card played) + Footwork x2 (Dex 7 for massive Skill block) + Well-Laid Plans (card retention). Key components: Dead Branch transforms every exhaust into a new card, creating infinite card advantage; Terror provides Vuln 99 for permanent 50% damage boost; Storm of Steel discards hand for Shivs that exhaust into Dead Branch procs; After Image + high Dex provides blocking without Powers. **This formula specifically defeats the Awakened One** because it generates card advantage in Phase 2 without playing Powers (zero Curiosity triggers). Phase 1: dump ALL Powers (Curiosity Str resets on death). Phase 2: play ZERO Powers, rely entirely on Dead Branch + exhaust for card generation. Lizard Tail provides death insurance. Mercury Hourglass adds 3 passive damage/turn. Clockwork Souvenir provides Artifact 1 at combat start.
+
+**Formula 5 (Defect): Echo Form+ Orb Engine (Run 172 -- FIRST DEFECT WIN, vs Time Eater).** Echo Form+ (first card each turn plays twice) + Seek+/Bottled Lightning (find key cards every turn) + Coolheaded+ (Frost + draw 3, doubled = 14 block + 2 Frost + draw 6) + Barrage+/Barrage (damage per orb, doubled = 84+ damage per cycle) + Mummified Hand (free card per Power played) + Loop (front orb passive at start of turn) + Data Disk (+1 Focus innate). Key components: Echo Form+ is the engine foundation -- doubling the first card each turn means Coolheaded+ provides 14 block + 2 Frost channels + draw 6, and Barrage+ deals 42-84 damage per play. Seek+ bottled via Bottled Lightning guarantees finding Echo Form+ or Coolheaded+ on Turn 1 of every fight. Mummified Hand discounts a card each Power play, enabling 3-4 card turns on Power setup turns despite the energy cost. Against Time Eater, card count discipline is critical: Echo Form copies DO count toward the 12-card Time Warp counter (each doubled card = 2 toward counter). The deck plays 5-6 effective cards per turn while outputting 80+ damage per cycle via Barrage with 6-8 orbs. Lizard Tail provided death insurance for the Time Eater's 39-damage attack turn.
+
+**What all formulas share:** (1) zero UI misplays, (2) zero arithmetic errors, (3) strong Act 1 performance, (4) proper map pathing, (5) a complete scaling engine (not individual strong cards). The key insight: pursue whichever engine the card/relic offerings support, not force one formula over the other.
+
+**IMMEDIATE PRIORITIES (in order):**
+1. **FULL BLOCK EVERY HALLWAY FIGHT.** The goal every turn is ZERO damage taken. Enumerate paths to zero (kill attackers + block remainder, pure block, debuff + block).
